Project {
  database_type: 'Databricks Unity Catalog'
  Note: 'Lakehouse data model for Chemical Mfg - Version 1'
}

Table rawmaterial.material_master {
  material_master_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each material master record.']
  cas_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.cas_registry. Business justification: Material master records belong to a single CAS entry; linking to cas_registry centralizes chemical identifiers and removes duplicate CAS fields.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Material Cost Allocation Report requires linking each material to a cost center for budgeting and variance analysis; finance allocates material purchase costs by cost center.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Purchasing posts material purchase invoices to a GL expense account; linking material_master to gl_account enables drill‑down from material to GL for audit and reporting.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability analysis per material line item uses profit_center_id to attribute revenue and cost to the appropriate profit center in financial statements.']
  batch_number_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a batch number must be assigned for traceability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the material master record was created.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the material record is considered active.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the material record is no longer valid.']
  hazard_class_ghs varchar [note: 'Globally Harmonized System hazard classification (e.g., H220, H315).']
  humidity_requirement_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum relative humidity allowed for storage.']
  incompatibility_flag boolean [note: 'True if the material is known to be chemically incompatible with certain other substances.']
  is_controlled_substance boolean [note: 'True if the material is subject to controlled substance regulations (e.g., DEA).']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is classified as hazardous under GHS/OSHA.']
  is_restricted_substance boolean [note: 'True if the material is restricted under REACH, TSCA, or other jurisdictional lists.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle stage of the material within the enterprise.']
  light_sensitivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material degrades when exposed to light.']
  material_class varchar [note: 'Broad functional classification of the material within manufacturing processes.']
  material_group varchar [note: 'SAP‑defined grouping used for procurement and inventory planning.']
  material_master_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable primary name of the material (e.g., common trade name).']
  min_order_quantity decimal(12,4) [note: 'Smallest quantity that can be ordered from a supplier.']
  molecular_formula varchar [note: 'Symbolic representation of the material\'s elemental composition (e.g., C6H12O6).']
  molecular_weight decimal(12,4) [note: 'Molar mass of the material expressed in grams per mole.']
  physical_state varchar [note: 'State of the material under standard temperature and pressure conditions.']
  procurement_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average lead time from purchase order to receipt.']
  quality_control_required boolean [note: 'True if the material must undergo QC testing before use.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the material meets all applicable regulatory requirements.']
  retest_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after which the material must be re‑qualified or tested.']
  sap_material_number varchar [note: 'Material number as defined in SAP S/4HANA Materials Management module.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days the material can be stored before expiration.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the primary warehouse or storage area for the material.']
  storage_temperature_max_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Highest permissible storage temperature for the material.']
  storage_temperature_min_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Lowest permissible storage temperature for the material.']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or vendor‑assigned commercial name for the material.']
  tsca_regulation_status varchar [note: 'U.S. TSCA compliance status of the material.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for ordering, inventory, and consumption (e.g., kg, L, mol).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the material master record.']

  Note: 'Single source of truth for all raw material master records in the chemical manufacturing enterprise. Captures the full identity of each raw material, chemical feedstock, intermediate, or input substance including CAS number reference, IUPAC name, common trade name, molecular formula, molecular weight, physical state at STP, material class (feedstock, intermediate, solvent, catalyst, additive, excipient), SAP material number, material group, unit of measure, shelf life, storage temperature range, humidity requirements, light sensitivity, retest interval, material lifecycle status (new, active, phase-out, obsolete), material category classification, and incompatibility flags. Serves as the authoritative registry for all raw material identities referenced by procurement, production planning, formulation, quality, and EHS domains. Each material has exactly one material_master record regardless of how many suppliers provide it.'
}

Table rawmaterial.cas_registry {
  cas_registry_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each CAS registry entry.']
  autoignition_temp_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Temperature at which the substance ignites without an external flame.']
  boiling_point_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Standard boiling point at atmospheric pressure.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'Unique Chemical Abstracts Service registry number identifying the substance.']
  chemical_family varchar [note: 'Broad chemical family or class (e.g., alkanes, aromatics).']
  common_name varchar [note: 'Widely used trade or common name for the chemical substance.']
  epa_regulation_status varchar [note: 'Current EPA regulatory classification (e.g., hazardous waste, non‑hazardous).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the regulatory status is considered expired.']
  flash_point_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Lowest temperature at which vapors ignite in air.']
  ghs_pictograms varchar [note: 'Pipe‑delimited list of GHS pictogram codes applicable to the substance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GHS01|GHS02|GHS03|GHS04|GHS05|GHS06|GHS07|GHS08|GHS09 — promote to reference product]']
  hazard_classification varchar [note: 'Primary GHS hazard class for the substance.']
  health_hazard_statement varchar [note: 'Standardized health hazard statement (e.g., H300).']
  inchi_key varchar [note: 'Standardized hashed representation of the International Chemical Identifier.']
  is_controlled_substance boolean [note: 'Indicates if the substance is subject to controlled substance regulations.']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the substance is considered hazardous.']
  is_prohibited boolean [note: 'True if the substance is prohibited for manufacture, sale, or use.']
  is_restricted_use boolean [note: 'True if the substance may be used only under special permits or conditions.']
  iupac_name varchar [note: 'Standardized chemical name according to IUPAC nomenclature.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the CAS record.']
  melting_point_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Standard melting point at atmospheric pressure.']
  molecular_formula varchar [note: 'Empirical formula representing the composition of the molecule.']
  molecular_weight decimal(12,4) [note: 'Exact molecular weight of the substance expressed in g/mol.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the substance.']
  odor_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the substance\'s odor.']
  physical_state varchar [note: 'Standard state of the substance at ambient conditions.']
  precautionary_statement varchar [note: 'Standardized precautionary statement (e.g., P210).']
  purity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Typical purity of the material expressed as a percentage.']
  reach_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory status of the substance under the European REACH regulation.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CAS registry record was first created.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the CAS registry record.']
  regulatory_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory compliance review.']
  smiles varchar [note: 'Simplified Molecular Input Line Entry System string describing the structure.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system (e.g., SAP MM, LIMS).']
  storage_pressure_bar decimal(6,2) [note: 'Recommended storage pressure in bar.']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Recommended storage temperature in degrees Celsius.']
  substance_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the substance as pure, mixture, polymer, or UVCB.']
  synonyms varchar [note: 'Pipe‑delimited list of alternative names and synonyms for the substance.']
  tsca_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory status of the substance under the U.S. Toxic Substances Control Act.']
  vapor_pressure_mmhg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Equilibrium vapor pressure at 25 °C expressed in mm Hg.']

  Note: 'Authoritative CAS (Chemical Abstracts Service) number registry for all chemical substances managed within the enterprise. Each record links a unique CAS number to its canonical chemical identity including IUPAC name, synonyms, molecular formula, InChI key, SMILES notation, chemical family, substance type (pure substance, mixture, polymer, UVCB), regulatory status under TSCA, REACH, and other jurisdictions, and cross-references to internal material master records. Supports regulatory compliance, SDS authoring, REACH registration, and TSCA inventory reporting.'
}

Table rawmaterial.sds_record {
  sds_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for sds_record']
  cas_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.cas_registry. Business justification: Safety Data Sheets (SDS) are prepared for specific chemical substances identified by CAS number. The sds_record currently has cas_number as a STRING field, which is a denormalized reference. Adding ca']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the raw material master record.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: SDS documents in chemical manufacturing are supplier-provided and must be maintained per vendor. Replacing denormalized supplier_name and supplier_contact text fields with a proper vendor FK enables S']
  author_contact varchar [note: 'Contact details for the SDS author (phone or email).']
  author_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person or entity that authored the SDS.']
  composition_ingredients varchar [note: 'Structured description of chemical constituents and their concentrations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SDS record was first created in the system.']
  disposal_considerations varchar [note: 'Guidance on safe disposal methods and waste handling.']
  document_checksum varchar [note: 'SHA‑256 hash of the SDS file for integrity verification.']
  document_file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the SDS file in bytes.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the electronic SDS file stored in the document repository.']
  ecological_information varchar [note: 'Environmental impact data, including biodegradability and aquatic toxicity.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the SDS becomes effective.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the SDS is no longer valid.']
  ghs_pictograms varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of GHS pictogram identifiers displayed on the SDS.']
  hazard_classifications varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of GHS hazard classes applicable to the material (e.g., flammable|corrosive).']
  hazard_statements varchar [note: 'Full text of GHS hazard statements (H‑phrases) for the material.']
  is_current_version boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SDS entry is the latest active version.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code indicating the regulatory region the SDS complies with.']
  language varchar [note: 'ISO language code of the SDS content.']
  physical_chemical_properties varchar [note: 'Key physical and chemical data (e.g., boiling point, pH, flash point).']
  precautionary_statements varchar [note: 'Full text of GHS precautionary statements (P‑phrases) for the material.']
  regulatory_information varchar [note: 'Applicable regulations (e.g., REACH, TSCA, OSHA) and compliance notes.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number for the SDS document.']
  sds_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SDS record.']
  sds_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the SDS document (e.g., 1.0, 2.1).']
  signal_word varchar [note: 'GHS signal word (Danger or Warning) for the material.']
  stability_reactivity varchar [note: 'Information on chemical stability, incompatibilities, and hazardous reactions.']
  toxicological_information varchar [note: 'Data on acute, chronic, and other toxicological effects.']
  transport_information varchar [note: 'Regulatory transport classification, UN number, packing group, and handling instructions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the SDS record.']

  Note: 'Safety Data Sheet (SDS/MSDS) master records for each raw material, maintained in compliance with GHS (Globally Harmonized System) and OSHA HazCom standards. Each record captures the 16-section GHS SDS structure including supplier identity, hazard identification, composition/ingredient information, first-aid measures, fire-fighting measures, accidental release measures, handling and storage, exposure controls/PPE, physical and chemical properties, stability and reactivity, toxicological information, ecological information, disposal considerations, transport information, regulatory information, and other information. Tracks SDS version number, effective date, expiry date, issuing supplier, language, and regulatory jurisdiction. Linked to material master and GHS classification records. Supports EHS compliance, worker safety training, and emergency response.'
}

Table rawmaterial.rawmaterial_ghs_classification {
  rawmaterial_ghs_classification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each GHS classification entry.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the raw material to which this classification applies.']
  rawmaterial_sds_record_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associated Safety Data Sheet.']
  supplier_material_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier providing the raw material.']
  classification_authority varchar [note: 'Organization or agency that approved the classification.']
  classification_basis varchar [note: 'Method used to derive the GHS classification.']
  classification_date date [note: 'Calendar date the classification was determined.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the classification record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Start date the classification is valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Expiration date of the classification; null if indefinite.']
  hazard_category varchar [note: 'Category level within the hazard class (1 = most severe).']
  hazard_class varchar [note: 'Primary GHS hazard class (e.g., Flammable Liquid, Acute Toxicity).']
  hazard_statement_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of H‑codes describing the hazards.']
  hazard_statement_text varchar [note: 'Narrative description of each hazard statement (H‑code).']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'True if the material carries any GHS hazard classification.']
  material_cas_number varchar [note: 'Standardized CAS registry number identifying the chemical substance.']
  material_supplier_code varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the supplier for this raw material.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or observations about the classification.']
  pictogram_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of pictogram identifiers required on the label.']
  pictogram_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of each pictogram required.']
  precautionary_statement_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of P‑codes providing safety measures.']
  precautionary_statement_text varchar [note: 'Narrative description of each precautionary statement (P‑code).']
  rawmaterial_ghs_classification_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the classification entry.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Regulatory regime governing the classification.']
  signal_word varchar [note: 'Signal word required on the label (Danger or Warning).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the classification information.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version of the classification record for audit purposes.']

  Note: 'GHS (Globally Harmonized System) and CLP (Classification, Labelling and Packaging) hazard classification records for each raw material substance. This product is the exclusive SSOT for GHS-framework hazard data: hazard class (flammable liquid, acute toxicity, skin corrosion, respiratory sensitizer, carcinogen, environmental hazard, etc.), hazard category (Category 1, 2, 3, etc.), signal word (Danger/Warning), hazard statements (H-codes), precautionary statements (P-codes), pictogram codes (GHS01-GHS09), classification basis (test data, read-across, expert judgment, calculation method for mixtures), classification date, regulatory jurisdiction (GHS Rev 9, EU CLP, OSHA HazCom 2012), and classification authority. Non-GHS hazard frameworks (NFPA, DOT, EPA, OSHA PEL) are owned by material_hazard_profile. Supports SDS Section 2 authoring, label generation, transport classification, and REACH/CLP compliance reporting.'
}

Table rawmaterial.material_specification {
  material_specification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each material specification record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Specification development and maintenance (QC lab, R&D, regulatory affairs) are charged to cost centers in chemical manufacturing. Required for QC/regulatory budget variance reporting and overhead all']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: New material specification development in chemical manufacturing is funded via R&D or regulatory internal orders. Links specification creation cost to the authorizing project order for spend tracking ']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Material specifications describe a specific material; linking to material_master eliminates duplicate CAS number and enables direct access to material attributes.']
  rawmaterial_sds_record_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to the associated Safety Data Sheet.']
  analytical_method varchar [note: 'Primary analytical technique used to verify the specification.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the person or department that approved the specification.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the specification was approved.']
  assay_max_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable assay/purity percentage for the material.']
  assay_min_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable assay/purity percentage for the material.']
  color_aph varchar [note: 'Color measurement according to the APHA (American Public Health Association) scale.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the specification record was first created.']
  density_max_g_per_cm3 decimal(6,3) [note: 'Maximum acceptable density.']
  density_min_g_per_cm3 decimal(6,3) [note: 'Minimum acceptable density.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the specification becomes active for incoming material acceptance.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the specification is no longer valid (nullable for open‑ended specifications).']
  hazard_classification varchar [note: 'GHS hazard class applicable to the material.']
  heavy_metals_ppm_limit decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum permissible concentration of heavy metals expressed in parts per million.']
  material_specification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the specification.']
  microbiological_limit varchar [note: 'Acceptable microbiological criteria (e.g., total viable count, specific pathogens).']
  moisture_max_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable moisture content.']
  moisture_min_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable moisture content.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the specification.']
  particle_size_max_microns decimal(7,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable particle size.']
  particle_size_min_microns decimal(7,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable particle size.']
  ph_max decimal(4,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable pH value for the material.']
  ph_min decimal(4,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable pH value for the material.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the material with applicable regulations.']
  residual_solvent_limit_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable residual solvent concentration.']
  specification_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the material specification.']
  specification_version varchar [note: 'Version label of the specification (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the specification record.']
  viscosity_max_cp decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable viscosity in centipoise.']
  viscosity_min_cp decimal(8,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable viscosity in centipoise.']

  Note: 'Incoming raw material quality specifications defining the acceptance criteria for each material purchased and received. Each specification record captures the material, specification version, effective date, specification owner, and a full set of quality parameters including assay/purity limits (min/max), moisture content limits, heavy metals limits (PPM), pH range, color (APHA/Gardner), viscosity range, density range, particle size distribution, residual solvent limits, microbiological limits, and any material-specific analytical parameters. Includes specification status (draft, approved, superseded), approval authority, and links to the associated test methods. Serves as the acceptance standard for incoming QC testing and COA review.'
}

  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Recommended storage condition (e.g., temperature, humidity).']
  supplier_part_number varchar [note: 'Supplier-assigned part number for the material.']

  tsca_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory status of the material under the U.S. Toxic Substances Control Act.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for ordering and inventory tracking of the material.']

  Note: 'Approved Supplier List (ASL) records capturing qualified supplier-to-material relationships for raw material procurement. Each record represents a specific supplier\'s offering of a raw material including supplier trade name, part number, approved manufacturer, country of origin, lead time, MOQ, qualification status (approved, conditional, probationary, disqualified), qualification date, last audit date, preferred supplier flag, and supply risk tier. Manages the source list for each raw material and supports SAP MM source list and info record management. Enables procurement to identify qualified sources and supports supply chain risk management.'
}

  vendor_qualification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor_qualification. Linking material_qualification to vendor_qualification enables traceability b']
  decision_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the qualification decision becomes effective for production use.']

  production_trial_result varchar [note: 'Result of the production‑scale trial confirming material suitability for routine manufacturing.']

  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the qualification record.']
  regulatory_review_outcome varchar [note: 'Outcome of the regulatory compliance review (e.g., EPA, REACH).']
  requalification_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after which the material must be re‑qualified.']
  technical_review_outcome varchar [note: 'Decision of the technical review after evaluating trial data.']

  Note: 'Tracks the formal qualification lifecycle of raw materials and supplier-material combinations through the approved supplier qualification process. Each record captures the qualification request date, qualification type (new material, new supplier, change qualification, requalification), qualification scope, qualification protocol reference, lab-scale evaluation results, pilot-scale trial results, production-scale trial results, technical review outcome, regulatory review outcome, qualification decision (approved, rejected, conditional), qualification effective date, requalification interval, and responsible quality engineer. Supports the MOC (Management of Change) process for raw material changes and ensures only qualified materials enter production.'
}

Linking incoming_inspection to material_specification identifies which specificatio']
  po_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.po_line. Business justification: Incoming inspection in chemical manufacturing is performed at PO line item granularity — each line may contain different materials with different inspection requirements. Linking inspection to po_line']

  supplier_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.supplier_material. Business justification: Incoming inspection is performed on material received from a specific approved supplier. Linking to supplier_material (the ASL record) allows direct traceability from inspection results to the specifi']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier providing the material.']
  appearance varchar [note: 'Observed appearance description (e.g., clear, cloudy).']
  assay_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Assay result expressed as percentage.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Associated production batch number if material allocated to batch.']
  capap_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates if a CAPA workflow was triggered due to rejection.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'Unique CAS registry number for the material.']
  color varchar [note: 'Color description of the material.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑text comments entered by inspector.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspection record was created in the system.']
  delivery_note_number varchar [note: 'Delivery note (ASN) number from supplier.']
  density_kg_m3 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Density in kilograms per cubic meter.']
  disposition_instructions varchar [note: 'Instructions for material disposition based on decision.']
  hold_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for placing material on hold, if applicable.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date when the inspection was performed.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'Unique inspection lot number assigned by SAP QM.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection record.']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of inspection completion.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Type of inspection performed as per ANSI/ASQ Z1.4.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot or batch number of the received material.']
  moisture_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Moisture content percentage.']
  oos_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if any test result was out of specification.']
  oot_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if any trend deviation was observed.']
  overall_decision varchar [note: 'Overall inspection decision.']
  particle_size_um decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average particle size in micrometers.']
  ph_value decimal(4,2) [note: 'Measured pH value of the material.']
  quality_flag varchar [note: 'Simplified quality flag derived from decision.']
  received_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material received in base unit.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when material was released from hold.']
  sample_plan varchar [note: 'Sampling plan used for the inspection (e.g., AQL level).']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of units sampled from the lot.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Warehouse or tank farm location where material is stored.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for received quantity (e.g., kg, L).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the inspection record.']
  viscosity_cp decimal(10,2) [note: 'Viscosity measured in centipoise.']

  Note: 'Records of incoming quality inspection events performed on received raw material lots at the plant gate, warehouse, or tank farm. Each inspection record captures the inspection lot number (SAP QM), material, supplier, purchase order reference, delivery note number, received quantity, sample plan and sample size, inspection date, inspection type (identity test, full COA review, skip lot, reduced, tightened per ANSI/ASQ Z1.4), individual test results against specification parameters (assay, pH, moisture, appearance, color, viscosity, density, particle size, etc.), overall inspection decision (accept, reject, hold, conditional release), disposition instructions, inspector ID, inspection completion timestamp, and any OOS (Out of Specification) or OOT (Out of Trend) findings. Triggers CAPA workflows on rejection. Serves as the quality gate controlling raw material entry into unrestricted inventory and is the SSOT for the accept/reject decision at the rawmaterial domain boundary — downstream quality investigations and CAPAs are owned by the quality domain.'
}

  Note: 'Lot-level traceability records for each received batch or lot of raw material. Supports FIFO/FEFO inventory management, lot traceability for production batch records, and regulatory recall management. Serves as the foundational traceability record linking procurement to production.'
}

  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the material in the enterprise master data.']

Business justification: Certificate of Analysis documents in chemical manufacturing are received per purchase order shipment. Linking CoA to PO enables PO-level quality documentation retrieval, supplier quality audits, and r']
  supplier_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.supplier_material. Business justification: A COA document is received from a specific supplier for a specific material, corresponding to an approved supplier-material (ASL) record. Adding supplier_material_id to coa_document enables direct lin']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier in the enterprise master data.']
  analytical_method varchar [note: 'Name or code of the analytical technique used for testing (e.g., HPLC, GC-MS).']
  assay_pass boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assay result meets the specification limit.']
  assay_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured assay value expressed as a percentage of the active ingredient.']
  assay_spec_limit_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable deviation for assay percentage as defined in the specification.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'Unique numeric identifier assigned to the chemical substance.']
  coa_document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the COA document.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the COA record was first entered into the system.']
  document_file_hash varchar [note: 'SHA‑256 hash of the COA file for integrity verification.']

  document_number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned identifier printed on the COA.']
  document_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the COA document, often includes material and lot information.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the material is no longer considered usable.']
  heavy_metals_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total concentration of regulated heavy metals expressed in parts per million.']
  heavy_metals_spec_limit_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable concentration of heavy metals.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the COA was issued by the supplier\'s laboratory.']
  lab_location varchar [note: 'Physical location (city, country) of the testing laboratory.']

  manufacturing_date date [note: 'Date the material batch was manufactured by the supplier.']
  material_name varchar [note: 'Common name of the raw material covered by the COA.']
  moisture_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured moisture content expressed as a percentage.']
  moisture_spec_limit_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable moisture percentage.']
  overall_pass_fail boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the material meets all specified criteria.']
  overall_result varchar [note: 'High‑level pass/fail/conditional conclusion for the COA.']
  ph_pass boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pH measurement falls within the specified range.']
  ph_spec_max decimal(4,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable pH value as per specification.']
  ph_spec_min decimal(4,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable pH value as per specification.']
  ph_value decimal(4,2) [note: 'Measured pH of the material or solution.']
  purity_pass boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the purity result meets the specification limit.']
  purity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured purity of the material expressed as a percentage.']
  purity_spec_limit_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable deviation for purity percentage.']
  residual_solvents_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured concentration of residual solvents in parts per million.']
  residual_solvents_spec_limit_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum permissible level of residual solvents.']
  signatory_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the authorized signatory.']
  supplier_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the supplier that issued the COA.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the COA record.']

  Note: 'Certificate of Analysis (COA) documents received from suppliers for each raw material lot shipment. Each COA record captures the supplier name, supplier lot number, material name, CAS number, manufacturing date, expiry date, test parameters and results as reported by the supplier (assay, purity, moisture, pH, heavy metals, residual solvents, appearance, etc.), analytical methods used, specification limits, pass/fail status per parameter, overall COA conclusion, issuing laboratory, authorized signatory, COA issue date, and document file reference. Supports incoming inspection, lot release decisions, and regulatory traceability. Enables COA-to-specification comparison for incoming QC.'
}

Business justification: REACH registration activities (ECHA fees, dossier preparation, testing) are charged to regulatory affairs cost centers. Chemical manufacturers must track REACH compliance spend per cost center for reg']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: REACH registration projects are managed as internal orders in chemical manufacturing (project-based spend for ECHA submission costs, testing, legal fees). Enables per-substance registration cost track']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: REACH registrations are held by a specific legal entity. Chemical manufacturers with multiple EU entities must track which legal entity holds each registration for compliance liability, regulatory rep']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: REACH registration dossiers are maintained for specific chemical substances managed as raw materials in the enterprise. Linking reach_registration to material_master establishes the critical connectio']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate key referencing the party that holds the registration.']
  vendor_qualification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor_qualification. Business justification: REACH compliance verification is a mandatory component of vendor qualification in chemical manufacturing. Linking reach_registration to vendor_qualification enables tracking which qualification assess']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the registration was approved by ECHA.']
  authorisation_status varchar [note: 'Authorisation outcome for substances listed under Annex XIV (SVHC).']
  co_registrant_flag boolean [note: 'True if the entity participates as a co‑registrant in the dossier.']
  compliance_deadline date [note: 'Regulatory deadline for meeting compliance obligations related to the registration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the registration record was first created in the data lake.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that supplied the registration data.']
  dossier_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the REACH dossier.']
  downstream_use_notification varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of notified downstream uses of the substance.']
  ec_number varchar [note: 'European Community (EC) number assigned to the substance.']
  echa_registration_number varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the European Chemicals Agency for the REACH registration dossier.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the registration becomes legally effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the registration expires or is no longer effective (nullable for open‑ended).']
  evaluation_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the ECHA Committee for Risk Assessment (CoRAP) evaluation.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date the registration expires and must be renewed.']
  joint_submission_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for a joint registration submission with other registrants.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last compliance review of the registration.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the registration record.']
  registration_identifier varchar [note: 'Business identifier used internally to reference the REACH registration dossier.']
  registration_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by compliance staff regarding the registration.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the REACH registration within ECHA.']
  registration_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the entity is the lead registrant, a member registrant, or a co‑registrant.']
  registration_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the registration dossier, incremented on each amendment.']
  restriction_status varchar [note: 'Status of any restriction under Annex XVII.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date the registration dossier was submitted to ECHA.']
  substance_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the substance (e.g., hazardous, non‑hazardous) according to GHS/TSCA.']
  substance_name varchar [note: 'Common or trade name of the chemical substance covered by the REACH registration.']
  svhc_candidate_date date [note: 'Date the substance was added to the SVHC candidate list.']
  svhc_candidate_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the substance is currently a SVHC candidate.']
  tonnage_band varchar [note: 'Annual tonnage range for which the substance is registered.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the registration record.']

  Note: 'REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals) registration dossier and compliance records for chemical substances manufactured or imported into the EU/EEA. Each record captures the substance identity (CAS, EC number), ECHA registration number, registration dossier reference, tonnage band, registration holder (lead/member), co-registrant status, joint submission reference, substance evaluation status (CoRAP), authorisation status (if SVHC/Annex XIV), restriction status (Annex XVII), SVHC (Substance of Very High Concern) candidate list date, dossier update date, downstream use notifications, and compliance deadline dates. This product is the SSOT for EU REACH dossier-specific detail — the consolidated cross-jurisdictional compliance view is owned by material_regulatory_status. Supports ECHA regulatory reporting, supply chain communication obligations (Article 33), and import/export compliance for EU/EEA markets.'
}

Table rawmaterial.material_composition {
  material_composition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each material composition record.']
  component_cas_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.cas_registry. Business justification: Each component in a material composition record is identified by a CAS number (component_cas_number STRING exists on material_composition). The cas_registry table is the authoritative CAS number regis']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the raw material master record that this composition describes.']
  cbi_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the component is a trade‑secret ingredient.']
  component_function varchar [note: 'Role of the component within the mixture. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active_ingredient|solvent|stabilizer|impurity|contaminant|carrier|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  component_iupac_name varchar [note: 'Standard IUPAC chemical name of the component.']
  component_percentage_of_total decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated percentage of this component relative to the total composition weight.']
  composition_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code assigned to the composition version for tracking and regulatory reporting.']
  composition_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the composition, e.g., "Standard epoxy resin blend".']
  composition_type varchar [note: 'Category of the material composition based on its physical/chemical nature.']
  concentration_max decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum allowable concentration for safety and regulatory limits.']
  concentration_min decimal(10,4) [note: 'Minimum allowable concentration for compliance and quality.']
  concentration_nominal decimal(10,4) [note: 'Typical concentration of the component in the composition.']
  concentration_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the concentration values.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the composition record was first created.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this composition version becomes effective for production and compliance.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the composition must no longer be used unless re‑approved.']
  ghs_classification varchar [note: 'Globally Harmonized System hazard classification for the component.']
  hazard_statement varchar [note: 'Standard GHS hazard statement associated with the component.']
  material_composition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the composition record.']
  precautionary_statement varchar [note: 'Standard GHS precautionary statement for safe handling.']
  reachexempt_flag boolean [note: 'True if the component is exempt from REACH registration.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Indicates if the row reflects the current active composition or a historical version.']
  regulatory_review_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the internal regulatory compliance review for this composition.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that supplied the composition data.']
  total_components varchar [note: 'Count of distinct component substances defined for this composition.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version identifier for changes to the composition.']

  Note: 'Detailed chemical composition records for raw materials that are mixtures, blends, or multi-component substances. Each record captures the parent material, component substance (CAS number, IUPAC name), component concentration (nominal, minimum, maximum), concentration unit (% w/w, % v/v, PPM), component function (active ingredient, solvent, stabilizer, impurity, contaminant), confidential business information (CBI) flag for trade secret components, and the composition version and effective date. Supports SDS Section 3 (Composition/Information on Ingredients), REACH mixture classification, formulation compatibility assessment, and regulatory disclosure obligations.'
}

Table rawmaterial.material_regulatory_status {
  material_regulatory_status_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the material regulatory status record.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the raw material master record.']
  compliance_deadline date [note: 'Date by which compliance actions must be completed.']
  compliance_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the current compliance status became effective.']
  compliance_last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance review for this material.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes regarding compliance considerations, exemptions, or special conditions.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance determination for the material in the jurisdiction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the system.']
  ghs_pictograms varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of GHS pictogram codes applicable to the material.']
  hazard_classification varchar [note: 'GHS hazard class assigned to the material.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory status record.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current registration or listing status of the material within the regulatory framework.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory regime or framework governing the material. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: REACH|TSCA|CLP|OSHA|EPA_TRI|DOT|IECSC|CSCL|K_REACH|OTHER — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  restriction_applicability varchar [note: 'Indicates whether any usage restrictions apply to the material.']
  restriction_level varchar [note: 'Level of regulatory restriction (e.g., high, medium, low) for the material.']

  Note: 'Consolidated cross-jurisdictional regulatory compliance summary for each raw material, providing a single-pane view of a material\'s global regulatory standing across ALL applicable frameworks (REACH, TSCA, CLP, OSHA, EPA TRI, DOT, China IECSC, Japan CSCL, Korea K-REACH, and others). Each record captures substance identity, regulatory framework, jurisdiction, registration/listing status, compliance status (compliant, non-compliant, pending, exempt), restriction applicability, compliance deadlines, and responsible regulatory affairs manager. This product is the SSOT for the consolidated regulatory compliance decision (\'can we use this material in jurisdiction X?\') — it aggregates status from jurisdiction-specific detail products (reach_registration for EU REACH dossier data, tsca_inventory for US EPA TSCA data) but adds coverage for jurisdictions without dedicated detail products and provides the single authoritative compliance summary used by procurement, import/export, and market access teams.'
}

Table customer.account {
  account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the customer account record in the chemical manufacturing enterprise data platform (Silver Layer). Primary key for all downstream joins.']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.bank_account. Business justification: Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT) payments require linking a customer account to its bank account record for payment processing.']
  business_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.business_unit. Business justification: Accounts in chemical manufacturing are assigned to business units (e.g., Agricultural Solutions, Performance Materials) for BU-level P&L reporting, sales planning, and customer profitability analysis.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Needed for the \'Primary Product\' field used in account forecasting and pricing reports to identify the main chemical a key account purchases.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost Allocation Report requires each customer account to be assigned a cost center for internal expense tracking and profitability analysis.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Customer accounts in chemical manufacturing are assigned to legal entities for VAT determination, intercompany invoicing, and tax reporting. A finance controller expects direct account→legal_entity li']
  parent_account_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the parent account record for corporate hierarchy management. Enables roll-up of subsidiary accounts to ultimate parent for consolidated reporting, credit exposure, and enterprise-level relationship management.']
  preferred_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Preferred carrier selection for each account is used in carrier performance and rate negotiation reports; linking enables direct lookup of the carrier entity.']
  preferred_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: Preferred Vendor Management per account; procurement strategy reports require linking each account to its designated vendor.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability dashboards map revenue per customer to profit centers, so each account needs a profit_center_id FK.']
  revenue_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Revenue Recognition process uses a default GL account per customer; linking account to gl_account enables automated posting.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: Linking account to its segment normalizes segment data and eliminates the redundant segment code stored on account.']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Territory‑assignment report requires each Account to be linked to its Sales Territory for quota and coverage planning.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the customer account. Active: transacting customer. Inactive: no recent transactions. Prospect: under evaluation. Suspended: temporarily halted due to credit or compliance issues. Blocked: SAP-level block applied (e.g., credit block, delivery block). Closed: account permanently deactivated.']
  account_tier varchar [note: 'Commercial segmentation tier assigned to the account based on revenue contribution, strategic importance, and growth potential. Strategic: top-tier key accounts with dedicated resources. Preferred: high-value accounts with preferred pricing. Standard: regular commercial accounts. Transactional: low-frequency or spot-buy customers.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the primary business relationship type of the customer account. Direct Industrial: buys chemicals for own manufacturing use. Distributor: resells to downstream customers. Toll Processor: processes materials on behalf of Chemical Mfg or third parties. OEM: original equipment manufacturer integrating chemical products. End User: final consumer of the product. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct_industrial|distributor|toll_processor|oem|end_user — promote to reference product]']
  annual_revenue_band varchar [note: 'Banded range of the customer organization\'s annual revenue used for segmentation and strategic account planning (e.g., \'<$10M\', \'$10M-$100M\', \'$100M-$1B\', \'>$1B\'). Sourced from Dun & Bradstreet or CRM qualification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: <$10M|$10M-$100M|$100M-$1B|>$1B — promote to reference product]']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the customer\'s billing location. Used for invoice generation, tax jurisdiction determination, and financial reporting under FASB and SOX.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City of the customer\'s billing address. Used for tax jurisdiction assignment, invoice compliance, and geographic sales reporting.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the customer\'s billing address. Used for international tax compliance, REACH/ECHA regulatory applicability, export control (TSCA), and currency determination.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the customer\'s billing address. Used for tax rate lookup, geographic segmentation, and logistics zone assignment.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the customer\'s billing address. Used for sales tax determination, regulatory jurisdiction mapping (EPA, OSHA state programs), and regional sales analytics.']
  blocked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer account has an active block in SAP S/4HANA preventing order processing, delivery, or invoicing. Blocks may be applied for credit risk, compliance violations, or sanctions screening failures.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the customer account record was first created in the enterprise data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle management.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum approved credit exposure in the account\'s billing currency authorized for this customer account. Managed in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO credit management. Drives order release decisions and credit block triggers.']
  credit_limit_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the credit limit is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency credit exposure management.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Internal or external credit risk rating assigned to the customer account (e.g., A, B, C, D or numeric score). Derived from Dun & Bradstreet data or internal credit assessment. Drives credit limit setting and payment term eligibility.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'The nine-digit Data Universal Numbering System (DUNS) number assigned by Dun & Bradstreet to uniquely identify the customer business entity globally. Used for credit risk assessment, corporate hierarchy linkage, and supplier/customer due diligence.']
  employee_count_band varchar [note: 'Banded range of the customer organization\'s total employee headcount used for account sizing and segmentation (e.g., \'1-50\', \'51-500\', \'501-5000\', \'>5000\'). Sourced from Dun & Bradstreet or CRM qualification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1-50|51-500|501-5000|>5000 — promote to reference product]']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'The default International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code governing delivery responsibility and risk transfer for shipments to this customer. Used in SAP S/4HANA SD order processing and Oracle Transportation Management for freight planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_order_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent confirmed sales order placed by this customer account. Used for customer activity monitoring, churn risk identification, and account health scoring.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'The full registered legal name of the customer organization as it appears on official business registration documents, contracts, and invoices. Used for regulatory reporting, SOX compliance, and legal correspondence.']
  naics_code varchar [note: 'The six-digit North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code identifying the customer\'s industry sector. Used for market analysis, customer segmentation, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'The date on which the customer account was formally onboarded and activated in the enterprise systems (SAP S/4HANA and Salesforce CRM). Used for customer tenure analysis, cohort reporting, and relationship lifecycle management.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'SAP payment terms key (e.g., NT30, NT60, 2/10NT30) defining the agreed payment due date and any early payment discount conditions for this customer. Drives accounts receivable aging and cash flow forecasting.']
  reach_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this customer requires REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals) compliance documentation for supplied substances. Applicable for EU-based customers or those exporting to EU markets. Drives regulatory document management.']
  requires_coa_on_delivery boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer requires a Certificate of Analysis (COA) to accompany each product lot shipment. COA documents quality test results against specification. Driven by customer quality agreements and GMP requirements.']
  requires_sds_on_delivery boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer requires a Safety Data Sheet (SDS) to accompany every chemical shipment delivery. Mandatory for GHS-compliant chemical distribution. Drives document generation in SAP S/4HANA SD and EHS workflows.']
  salesforce_account_code varchar [note: 'The native record identifier assigned by Salesforce CRM for this account. Used to reconcile and synchronize customer master data between the lakehouse and the CRM system of record.']
  sap_customer_number varchar [note: 'The customer master number assigned in SAP S/4HANA SD (Sales and Distribution) module. Used for order-to-cash processing, credit management, and financial postings in the ERP system.']
  shipping_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the customer\'s primary shipping destination. Used for export compliance, DOT hazardous materials transport classification, REACH applicability, and logistics routing.']
  shipping_mode varchar [note: 'The customer\'s preferred mode of transportation for product delivery. LTL (Less Than Truckload) and FTL (Full Truckload) are common for bulk chemical shipments. Drives logistics planning and DOT hazmat transport compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ltl|ftl|parcel|rail|ocean|air|intermodal — promote to reference product]']
  sic_code varchar [note: 'The four-digit Standard Industrial Classification (SIC) code that categorizes the customer\'s primary business activity. Used for market segmentation, regulatory reporting, and industry analytics.']
  tax_number varchar [note: 'The government-issued tax identification number (EIN, VAT number, or equivalent) for the customer entity. Required for tax reporting, invoice compliance, and regulatory filings under FASB and SOX.']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'The operating or \'doing business as\' (DBA) trade name of the customer, which may differ from the legal entity name. Used in commercial communications and sales reporting.']
  tsca_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this customer requires TSCA (Toxic Substances Control Act) compliance certification for chemical products supplied. Applicable for US-based industrial customers subject to EPA chemical substance regulations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the customer account record was most recently modified in the enterprise data platform. Used for change data capture, data quality monitoring, and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Core B2B customer account master record representing industrial customers, distributors, and end-user organizations. Stores the authoritative identity of each customer entity including legal business name, DUNS number, tax ID, SIC/NAICS industry classification, account tier, account status, primary business type (direct industrial, distributor, toll processor, OEM), annual revenue band, employee count, parent account reference for corporate hierarchy, Salesforce CRM account ID, SAP customer number, credit rating, account manager assignment, onboarding date, and shipping/delivery preferences at the account level. This is the SSOT for all customer identity in the chemical-manufacturing enterprise.'
}

Table customer.contact {
  contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the contact person record in the customer domain silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent customer account this contact is associated with. Links the contact to the B2B industrial customer, distributor, or end-user account.']
  account_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific customer site or plant location this contact is associated with. A customer account may have multiple plant sites; this links the contact to their operational location for logistics and technical service routing.']
  coa_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Preferred delivery method for Certificate of Analysis (COA) and Certificate of Compliance (COC) documents for this contact. Drives automated document distribution workflows in the quality management process.']
  contact_role varchar [note: 'Functional role classification of the contact in relation to Chemical Mfg business interactions. Drives workflow routing for technical sales, EHS (Environment Health and Safety) compliance, logistics coordination, and quality inquiries. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: technical|commercial|ehs|logistics|quality|procurement|finance|executive — promote to reference product if additional roles are needed]']
  contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contact record. \'active\' indicates the contact is reachable and engaged; \'inactive\' indicates the contact is no longer in the role; \'do_not_contact\' enforces communication suppression; \'pending_verification\' indicates the record awaits validation.']
  contact_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contact by their relationship to Chemical Mfg. \'internal\' denotes Chemical Mfg employees; \'external\' denotes customer personnel; \'distributor\' denotes channel partner contacts; \'end_user\' denotes final consumer contacts; \'consultant\' denotes third-party advisors.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contact record was first created in the silver layer data product. Supports audit trail requirements, GDPR data retention tracking, and data lineage for the customer domain.']
  crm_contact_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this contact record in Salesforce CRM, the system of record for customer contact management. Used for bi-directional synchronization between the lakehouse and Salesforce.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the contact\'s purchase order authority limit (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures correct monetary interpretation across multi-currency customer accounts.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department or functional area the contact belongs to within the customer organization (e.g., Procurement, Engineering, Quality Assurance, Environment Health and Safety, Logistics, R&D). Supports targeted communication routing.']
  do_not_contact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact has opted out of or been suppressed from all outbound communications. When True, no marketing, sales, or non-essential operational communications should be sent. Enforces GDPR and CCPA consent management requirements.']
  do_not_contact_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the do_not_contact_flag is set to True. Supports regulatory audit trails for GDPR right-to-erasure and CCPA opt-out compliance. Null when do_not_contact_flag is False.']
  ehs_contact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is the designated EHS (Environment Health and Safety) coordinator or responsible person at the customer site. EHS contacts receive hazardous material notifications, incident reports, and regulatory compliance updates.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary business email address of the contact. Used for order confirmations, technical documentation delivery, Safety Data Sheet (SDS) distribution, Certificate of Analysis (COA) transmission, and regulatory correspondence.']
  email_secondary varchar [note: 'Alternate or secondary email address for the contact. Used as a fallback communication channel when the primary email is undeliverable, or for routing specific document types (e.g., invoices to a separate accounts payable email).']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the contact person. Used in personalized communications, technical sales outreach, and customer service workflows.']
  is_decision_maker boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact holds purchasing or approval authority within their organization. Used by technical sales teams to prioritize engagement and qualify opportunities in the chemical sales cycle.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is the designated primary contact for the associated customer account. Only one contact per customer account should be flagged as primary. Used to determine the default recipient for account-level communications.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title or position of the contact within their organization (e.g., Procurement Manager, Plant Engineer, Quality Manager, EHS Coordinator, Technical Buyer). Used for routing communications and identifying decision-makers.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred language for communications with this contact, expressed as an IETF BCP 47 language tag (e.g., \'en\', \'de\', \'fr\', \'zh-CN\'). Ensures SDS, COA, and regulatory documents are delivered in the correct language per REACH and GHS requirements.']
  last_interaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent recorded business interaction with this contact (e.g., sales call, technical inquiry, order placement, site visit). Used to measure contact engagement recency and identify dormant contacts for re-engagement campaigns.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name or surname of the contact person. Combined with first_name to form the full display name for business correspondence and CRM records.']
  linkedin_profile_url varchar [note: 'LinkedIn public profile URL for the contact. Used by technical sales and account management teams for relationship intelligence, pre-call research, and identifying decision-maker networks within B2B industrial customer organizations.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or initial of the contact person. Used for disambiguation when multiple contacts share the same first and last name within a customer organization.']
  phone_direct varchar [note: 'Direct office or desk phone number for the contact in E.164 international format. Used for urgent technical support, order management, and regulatory compliance communications.']
  phone_fax varchar [note: 'Fax number for the contact in E.164 international format. Relevant for regulatory document transmission (e.g., SDS, COA, purchase orders) in industries and regions where fax remains a compliance-accepted channel.']
  phone_mobile varchar [note: 'Mobile or cell phone number for the contact in E.164 international format. Used for SMS notifications, emergency EHS (Environment Health and Safety) alerts, and field-level technical support.']
  preferred_communication_channel varchar [note: 'The contact\'s preferred channel for receiving business communications from Chemical Mfg. Drives outbound communication routing in Salesforce CRM and customer service workflows.']
  purchase_order_authority_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary value in the customer\'s base currency that this contact is authorized to approve on a Purchase Order (PO). Used by the technical sales team to identify the appropriate contact for order approvals and to escalate large orders to higher-authority contacts.']
  regulatory_notification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact should receive regulatory compliance notifications, including TSCA updates, REACH substance restriction alerts, EPA reporting deadlines, and product reformulation notices. Supports proactive regulatory communication workflows.']
  relationship_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this contact\'s association with the customer account was terminated (e.g., due to role change, departure, or account closure). Null for currently active contacts. Used for historical record retention and compliance audit trails.']
  relationship_start_date date [note: 'Date on which this contact was first established as an active contact for the customer account. Used to calculate relationship tenure and for historical analysis of contact network evolution.']
  salutation varchar [note: 'Formal salutation or honorific prefix for the contact (e.g., Mr., Ms., Dr., Prof.). Used in formal business correspondence, regulatory submissions, and technical documentation.']
  sds_distribution_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact has consented to receive Safety Data Sheet (SDS) and Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) documents electronically. Required for OSHA HazCom 2012 and GHS compliance in chemical product distribution.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating operational system from which this contact record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking and master data management reconciliation between Salesforce CRM and SAP S/4HANA SD.']
  technical_service_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is designated to receive technical service support, including application engineering assistance, formulation guidance, process troubleshooting, and Certificate of Analysis (COA) requests.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this contact record in the silver layer. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail compliance.']

  Note: 'Individual contact persons associated with a customer account, including procurement managers, plant engineers, quality managers, EHS coordinators, and technical buyers. Captures full name, job title, department, direct phone, mobile, email, preferred communication channel, contact role (technical, commercial, EHS, logistics), LinkedIn profile, language preference, do-not-contact flag, and Salesforce CRM contact ID. Supports technical sales and customer service workflows.'
}

Table customer.site {
  site_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the customer site or plant location record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the site entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent corporate account (customer master) that owns this site. One account may have many sites. Supports customer hierarchy navigation.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Site‑level operating cost allocation reports need each site tied to a cost center for accurate expense tracking.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Customer delivery sites in chemical manufacturing are associated with specific legal entities for VAT/customs determination, country-specific invoicing, and hazmat transport compliance. A tax or logis']
  parent_site_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier for the parent site in a site hierarchy (e.g., a campus or complex with multiple sub-sites). Enables multi-level site hierarchy modeling for large industrial customers.']
  preferred_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: Site‑level preferred supplier for deliveries; logistics planning uses this link to route shipments.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_location. Business justification: Required for Delivery Planning Report that maps each customer site to the warehouse location used for shipments, enabling logistics scheduling and compliance with delivery windows.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the customer site. Used for shipment routing, hazardous materials transport documentation (DOT), and regulatory permit filings.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address detail for the customer site (suite, building, dock number, gate reference). Supplements address_line1 for precise delivery routing.']
  bulk_receipt_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site has infrastructure (tank farm, unloading arms, pumps) to receive bulk liquid or solid chemical shipments via tanker truck or rail car.']
  capacity_mt decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum chemical storage capacity of the site expressed in metric tons. Used for supply planning, inventory replenishment modeling, and tank farm utilization analytics.']
  city varchar [note: 'City in which the customer site is located. Used for freight zone determination, tax jurisdiction, and regulatory reporting (EPA TRI, OSHA PSM).']
  coa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer site requires a Certificate of Analysis (COA) with each chemical product shipment. Supports quality assurance, incoming inspection, and GMP compliance.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the customer site location (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN). Drives export compliance, REACH/TSCA applicability, and international freight documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the site record was first created in the system (ISO 8601 format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports data lineage, audit trail, and GDPR data retention compliance.']
  delivery_window_end varchar [note: 'Latest time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour) at which the site accepts chemical deliveries. Used for carrier scheduling, route optimization, and on-time delivery KPI measurement.']
  delivery_window_start varchar [note: 'Earliest time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour) at which the site accepts chemical deliveries. Used for carrier scheduling, route optimization, and on-time delivery KPI measurement.']
  dock_restrictions varchar [note: 'Free-text description of physical dock constraints at the site (e.g., maximum trailer length, dock height limitations, no live unload, appointment required). Used by Oracle TMS and carrier dispatch.']
  ehs_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the designated EHS contact at the customer site. Required for hazardous chemical deliveries, SDS acknowledgment, emergency response coordination, and OSHA PSM compliance.']
  ehs_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Direct phone number for the site EHS contact. Used for emergency chemical incident response, hazmat delivery coordination, and regulatory inspection notifications.']
  fax varchar [note: 'Fax number for the customer site. Used for transmission of SDS, COA, COC, and regulatory documentation where electronic fax is the required delivery channel.']
  hazmat_receiving_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site is certified and equipped to receive hazardous chemical materials per DOT and OSHA regulations. Governs which chemical products can be shipped to this site.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'ICC Incoterms code defining the delivery responsibility and risk transfer point for chemical shipments to this site (e.g., DAP, DDP, FOB). Drives freight cost allocation and SAP SD billing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate of the customer site in decimal degrees (WGS 84). Used for route optimization in Oracle Transportation Management, emergency response planning, and geospatial analytics.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate of the customer site in decimal degrees (WGS 84). Used for route optimization, emergency response planning, and geospatial analytics.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Textual description of the site\'s standard operating hours for receiving deliveries (e.g., \'Mon-Fri 06:00-18:00 CST\'). Used by Oracle TMS and SAP EWM for delivery window scheduling.']
  packaging_preference varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preferred packaging format for chemical product receipt at this site (e.g., bulk tanker, 55-gallon drum, IBC tote, bag). Drives order fulfillment, SKU selection, and GHS labeling requirements.']
  permit_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the site\'s primary regulatory operating or environmental permit. Triggers renewal alerts and may restrict chemical deliveries if permit lapses.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the customer site. Used for delivery coordination, emergency contact, and carrier communication.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the customer site. Used for freight rate calculation, carrier zone assignment, and geographic analytics.']
  preferred_carrier varchar [note: 'Name or code of the customer\'s preferred freight carrier for deliveries to this site. Used in Oracle TMS carrier selection and rate negotiation.']
  reach_registration_number varchar [note: 'ECHA REACH registration number applicable to the customer site for EU-based locations. Required for chemical supply compliance under REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006.']
  regulatory_permit_number varchar [note: 'Primary environmental or operating permit number issued to the customer site by a regulatory authority (EPA, state agency, OSHA). Required for hazardous chemical delivery authorization and compliance documentation.']
  salesforce_site_code varchar [note: 'External system identifier for this site record in Salesforce CRM (system of record). Used for data lineage, cross-system reconciliation, and integration with Salesforce-driven sales and service workflows.']
  sds_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site requires a GHS-compliant Safety Data Sheet (SDS) to accompany every chemical shipment. Mandatory for hazardous chemical deliveries under OSHA HazCom and GHS standards.']
  shipping_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation used for chemical product deliveries to this site. Drives freight planning, DOT hazmat packaging requirements, and carrier assignment. LTL = Less Than Truckload, FTL = Full Truckload.']
  site_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the site, sourced from SAP S/4HANA customer master or Salesforce CRM. Used in order management, shipping documents, and COA/COC references.']
  site_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the customer site or plant location (e.g., \'Baytown Refinery\', \'Chicago Distribution Center\'). Used on shipping labels, SDS delivery documentation, and customer communications.']
