  site_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the customer site record. Controls whether orders, deliveries, and EHS documentation can be processed against this site.']
  site_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical site by its primary operational function. Drives logistics planning, hazmat receiving rules, and EHS compliance requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: manufacturing_plant|distribution_center|rd_lab|warehouse|terminal|blending_facility|storage_depot|other — promote to reference product]']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the customer site. Used for tax jurisdiction, DOT hazmat routing restrictions, and state-level environmental permit compliance.']
  tank_farm_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer site has a dedicated tank farm for bulk liquid chemical storage. Determines eligibility for bulk tanker delivery and long-term supply agreements.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site requires temperature-controlled storage or delivery conditions for chemical products (e.g., cryogenic, refrigerated, or heated materials).']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the customer site (e.g., \'America/Chicago\', \'Europe/Berlin\'). Used for delivery window scheduling, order cutoff time calculation, and global reporting normalization.']
  un_locode varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE five-character location code identifying the customer site\'s port, city, or logistics node. Used in international shipping documentation, ASN processing, and TMS routing.']
  unloading_equipment varchar [note: 'Description of unloading equipment available at the site (e.g., forklift, pump, conveyor, crane, vacuum system). Ensures carrier and driver preparedness for chemical product offloading.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the site record (ISO 8601 format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture, audit trail, and master data governance.']

  Note: 'Physical customer site or plant location where chemical products are delivered, consumed, or processed. Captures site name, site type (manufacturing plant, distribution center, R&D lab, warehouse), street address, city, state, country, GPS coordinates, UN/LOCODE, site capacity, operating hours, dock restrictions, hazmat receiving capability, tank farm availability, bulk vs. packaged receipt capability, preferred carrier, shipping mode (LTL, FTL, bulk tanker, rail), packaging preference, delivery window constraints, unloading equipment availability, temperature-controlled requirements, site EHS contact, and site-level regulatory permits. Distinct from the account (corporate entity) — one account may have many sites.'
}

Table customer.account_hierarchy {
  account_hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each account hierarchy relationship record in the customer domain. Primary key for the account_hierarchy data product.']
  child_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the child account in the hierarchy relationship. References the subordinate node (e.g., subsidiary, division, or plant-level account) beneath the parent account.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Account hierarchies in chemical manufacturing align with legal entity structures for consolidated credit exposure, intercompany billing, and group-level financial reporting. The legal_entity_flag on a']
  parent_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent account in the hierarchy relationship. References the superior node (e.g., global ultimate, domestic ultimate, or intermediate holding entity) above the child account.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: The `account_hierarchy` product contains `account_segment: STRING` — a denormalized segment classification applied at the hierarchy level (e.g., a corporate group may be classified as Automotive or Sp']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the business approver who authorized this hierarchy relationship record (e.g., global account manager, credit manager, or master data governance owner). Required for SOX-compliant master data change management.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hierarchy relationship record was formally approved by the designated business approver. Supports SOX-compliant audit trail for master data governance.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason code explaining why this hierarchy relationship was created or last modified. \'acquisition\' = parent acquired child; \'divestiture\' = child was divested; \'restructuring\' = internal reorganization; \'correction\' = data correction; \'initial_setup\' = first-time configuration; \'merger\' = entities merged. Supports audit trail and MOC (Management of Change) compliance.']
  consolidation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hierarchy relationship is used for financial consolidation purposes (e.g., rolling up revenue, credit exposure, or receivables to the parent account). True = included in financial consolidation rollup; False = excluded (e.g., commercial grouping only).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country of domicile or registration of the child account in this hierarchy relationship. Used for REACH compliance jurisdiction determination, export control, and geographic revenue reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this account hierarchy relationship record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX-compliant master data governance.']
  credit_rollup_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the child account\'s credit exposure is aggregated to the parent account for enterprise-level credit limit management. True = child credit exposure rolls up to parent; False = child is assessed independently. Critical for managing credit risk across multinational chemical buyers and conglomerates.']
  customer_group_code varchar [note: 'SAP customer group classification code assigned to the child account, used for pricing, reporting, and statistical analysis. Examples include industrial buyer, distributor, OEM, or end-user segments in the chemical manufacturing context.']
  domestic_ultimate_duns varchar [note: 'Dun & Bradstreet D-U-N-S number of the domestic ultimate parent entity (highest-level entity within the same country). Used for country-level credit aggregation and domestic account consolidation.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Dun & Bradstreet Data Universal Numbering System (D-U-N-S) nine-digit identifier for the child account. Used for corporate linkage verification, credit risk assessment, and third-party data enrichment. Enables matching to D&B global corporate hierarchy for multinational chemical buyers.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this parent-child hierarchy relationship becomes or became effective. Used for time-based revenue rollup, credit exposure aggregation, and historical hierarchy analysis.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date on which this parent-child hierarchy relationship ceases to be effective. Null indicates an open-ended, currently active relationship. Used for historical hierarchy reconstruction and audit trails.']
  global_ultimate_duns varchar [note: 'Dun & Bradstreet D-U-N-S number of the global ultimate parent entity in the corporate ownership chain. Used to link the hierarchy record to the top-level worldwide parent for enterprise credit exposure and global account management.']
  hierarchy_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this hierarchy relationship, as assigned in SAP S/4HANA SD customer hierarchy configuration or Salesforce CRM. Used for cross-system reconciliation and reporting.']
  hierarchy_depth varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of the child account node within the full hierarchy tree, where 1 represents a direct child of the root (global ultimate). Enables efficient tree traversal queries and level-based revenue rollup in enterprise account analytics.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Designates the structural tier of the child account within the corporate hierarchy. \'global_ultimate\' is the top-level worldwide parent; \'domestic_ultimate\' is the highest-level entity within a country; \'subsidiary\' is a legally distinct owned entity; \'division\' is an internal business unit; \'plant\' or \'site\' is a specific manufacturing or distribution location. Aligns with Dun & Bradstreet corporate linkage levels used in B2B credit and sales analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: global_ultimate|domestic_ultimate|subsidiary|division|plant|site — promote to reference product]']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Materialized path string representing the full ancestry chain from the root account to the child account (e.g., \'/10001/10045/10089\'). Enables fast subtree queries and hierarchy visualization without recursive joins. Populated by the ETL/data pipeline from SAP or Salesforce hierarchy data.']
  hierarchy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the hierarchy relationship record. \'active\' indicates the relationship is currently in effect; \'inactive\' indicates it has been suspended; \'pending\' indicates it is awaiting approval or activation; \'terminated\' indicates the relationship has been formally ended.']
  hierarchy_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the parent-child relationship. \'ownership\' reflects legal corporate ownership structure; \'commercial\' reflects sales and account management grouping; \'billing\' reflects consolidated invoicing structure; \'reporting\' reflects management reporting rollup; \'logistics\' reflects shipping and delivery consolidation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ownership|commercial|billing|reporting|logistics — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  hierarchy_version varchar [note: 'Incrementing version number for this hierarchy relationship record, supporting optimistic concurrency control and change history tracking. Incremented each time the hierarchy relationship is modified (e.g., reclassification, ownership change).']
  industry_sector_code varchar [note: 'Industry classification code for the child account\'s primary business sector (e.g., automotive, construction, electronics, consumer goods, agriculture). Aligns with SAP industry sector configuration and NAICS/SIC codes. Used for market segment analytics and product portfolio alignment.']
  legal_entity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the child account in this hierarchy relationship represents a distinct legal entity (e.g., incorporated subsidiary) as opposed to an internal division or operational site. Relevant for REACH registration, TSCA compliance, and financial consolidation under FASB ASC 810.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional business context about this hierarchy relationship, such as special commercial arrangements, pending ownership changes, or exceptions to standard rollup rules. Populated by global account managers or master data stewards.']
  pricing_rollup_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether volume-based pricing and rebate agreements negotiated at the parent account level apply to this child account. True = child account inherits parent pricing terms; False = child account has independent pricing. Relevant for chemical distributor networks and multinational industrial buyers.']
  primary_relationship_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the global account manager or key account owner responsible for managing the overall parent-child relationship. Sourced from Salesforce CRM account ownership. Used for global account management and escalation routing.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Internal sales or operational region code assigned to the child account (e.g., AMER, EMEA, APAC, or sub-regional codes). Used for geographic revenue rollup, territory management, and regional account reporting.']
  relationship_role varchar [note: 'Describes the role of the child account in the context of this specific hierarchy relationship. \'parent\' indicates the account acts as a superior node; \'child\' indicates a subordinate node; \'sibling\' indicates a peer relationship under the same parent; \'co_parent\' indicates shared ownership or joint venture structures.']
  revenue_rollup_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the child account\'s revenue is included in the parent account\'s enterprise revenue rollup for global account management, rebate calculation, and volume pricing agreements. True = revenue rolls up; False = revenue is tracked independently.']
  sales_org_code varchar [note: 'SAP Sales Organization code associated with the child account in this hierarchy relationship. Determines pricing procedures, order management rules, and revenue attribution within the SAP S/4HANA SD module.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this hierarchy relationship was sourced. \'SAP_SD\' = SAP S/4HANA Sales and Distribution customer hierarchy; \'SALESFORCE_CRM\' = Salesforce CRM account hierarchy; \'MANUAL\' = manually entered by master data team; \'DUNS_ENRICHMENT\' = derived from Dun & Bradstreet corporate linkage data.']
  source_system_key varchar [note: 'The natural key or record identifier for this hierarchy relationship in the originating source system (e.g., SAP customer hierarchy node ID or Salesforce Account relationship ID). Used for cross-system traceability and delta load reconciliation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this account hierarchy relationship record was last modified in the data platform. Used for incremental data loads, change detection, and audit trail maintenance.']

  Note: 'Corporate hierarchy relationships between customer accounts, capturing parent-child relationships for multinational chemical buyers, conglomerates, and distributor networks. Stores parent account, child account, hierarchy level (global ultimate, domestic ultimate, subsidiary, division, plant), effective date, end date, hierarchy type (ownership, commercial, billing), and consolidation flag. Enables enterprise-level revenue rollup, credit exposure aggregation, and global account management for large industrial customers.'
}

Table customer.segment {
  segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the customer segment record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the customer_segment data product.']
  business_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.business_unit. Business justification: Segments in chemical manufacturing are owned by specific business units (e.g., Coatings segment → Performance Materials BU). BU-level segment performance reporting and budget allocation require direct']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Customer segments in chemical manufacturing are scoped to legal entities (e.g., EU agricultural segment under the European legal entity) for statutory reporting, transfer pricing, and segment-level co']
  payment_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment_term. Business justification: Customer segments in chemical mfg define default payment terms applied to all accounts in the segment (e.g., strategic accounts get net 60, standard accounts net 30). The existing default_payment_term']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Customer segments in chemical manufacturing (e.g., Automotive Coatings, Agrochemicals) map to profit centers for segment-level P&L reporting. Management accounting requires segment→profit_center linka']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the commercial or marketing leader who formally approved the creation or last revision of this customer segment definition. Supports governance and audit trail requirements.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer segment definition was formally approved by the designated authority. Used for governance tracking and audit compliance under SOX and ISO 9001 document control requirements.']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'The internal business unit or division of Chemical Mfg responsible for serving this customer segment (e.g., Performance Materials, Agricultural Solutions, Specialty Chemicals, Industrial Chemicals). Maps to SAP FI/CO profit center and Salesforce CRM business unit hierarchy.']
  coa_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers in this segment require a Certificate of Analysis (COA) to accompany each shipment, confirming that the product meets specified quality parameters. True = COA mandatory per shipment; False = COA provided on request. Relevant for pharmaceutical excipient, food-grade, and high-purity electronic chemical segments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this customer segment record was first created in the system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.']
  credit_risk_category varchar [note: 'Aggregate credit risk classification for the customer segment based on historical payment behavior, financial health of segment participants, and industry credit risk benchmarks. Informs credit limit setting and payment term defaults in SAP FI/CO. Classified as confidential.']
  crm_segment_code varchar [note: 'External identifier for this customer segment as maintained in Salesforce CRM, the system of record for customer segmentation. Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing between the Silver Layer lakehouse and Salesforce CRM.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this customer segment definition becomes effective and applicable for customer classification, pricing, and commercial operations. Supports temporal validity tracking for segment evolution and historical analysis.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which this customer segment definition expires or is superseded. Null indicates an open-ended segment with no planned end date. Used for segment lifecycle management and transition planning when segment structures are revised.']
  gmp_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers in this segment require chemical products to be manufactured under Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) conditions, as applicable to pharmaceutical excipient, API intermediate, and food-contact chemical segments. True = GMP manufacturing and documentation required; False = standard industrial quality.']
  growth_potential_rating varchar [note: 'Commercial assessment of the revenue growth potential for this customer segment over a 3-5 year horizon: high (significant expansion opportunity), medium (stable moderate growth), low (mature or declining market), emerging (nascent segment with high uncertainty but upside potential). Used in commercial strategy and capital allocation decisions.']
  hazmat_shipping_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether products shipped to customers in this segment are classified as hazardous materials requiring DOT/IATA/IMDG compliant shipping documentation, placarding, and carrier certification. True = hazmat shipping protocols apply; False = non-hazardous standard shipping. Drives logistics configuration in Oracle TMS.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'Default Incoterms code applicable to commercial transactions with customers in this segment, defining the allocation of costs, risks, and responsibilities between buyer and seller (e.g., DAP for delivered at place, FOB for free on board, DDP for delivered duty paid). Configured in SAP SD delivery terms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  industry_vertical varchar [note: 'The primary end-market industry vertical served by this customer segment, expressed as a NAICS (North American Industry Classification System) sector name or code description (e.g., Automotive Parts Manufacturing, Agricultural Chemical Distribution, Pharmaceutical Manufacturing, Electronic Component Manufacturing). Aligns with NAICS 2022 classification.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal review of this customer segment definition, including assessment of segment criteria, customer assignments, and commercial strategy alignment. Supports periodic governance review cycles.']
  min_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) applicable to customers in this segment, expressed in the standard unit of measure for the segment\'s primary product portfolio (e.g., kg, MT, liters). Enforced in SAP SD order entry to ensure commercial viability of transactions.']
  min_order_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the minimum order quantity field (e.g., kg = kilograms, MT = metric tonnes, L = liters, gal = gallons, drum = 200L drum, IBC = intermediate bulk container, bag = 25kg bag). Aligns with SAP MM unit of measure configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kg|MT|L|gal|drum|IBC|bag — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  naics_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit NAICS code representing the primary industry classification of the customer segment. Used for regulatory reporting (EPA TRI, TSCA), market analysis, and alignment with industry benchmarks. Enables cross-segment analytics by industry sector.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal review of this customer segment definition. Ensures segment definitions remain current with market conditions, regulatory changes, and commercial strategy evolution.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the commercial leader (e.g., Business Development Manager, Market Segment Manager) accountable for the segment strategy, performance targets, and customer relationships within this segment. Aligns with Salesforce CRM account ownership model.']
  owner_email varchar [note: 'Corporate email address of the segment owner for routing commercial inquiries, escalations, and reporting. Used in automated workflow notifications from Salesforce CRM and SAP SD.']
  preferred_packaging_type varchar [note: 'Standard packaging format preferred by customers in this segment for chemical product delivery (e.g., 200L drums, 1000L IBC totes, bulk tanker, 25kg bags, 1kg laboratory bottles). Influences supply chain planning, warehouse operations in SAP EWM, and logistics configuration in Oracle TMS.']
  pricing_tier_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the pricing tier applicable to customers in this segment, referencing the SAP SD pricing procedure and condition type configuration. Determines base price lists, discount structures, and rebate eligibility. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity.']
  primary_product_portfolio varchar [note: 'Description of the primary chemical product lines, formulations, or product families applicable to this customer segment (e.g., Epoxy Resins, Polyurethane Intermediates, Crop Protection Actives, Electronic-Grade Solvents). Drives product recommendation logic and technical service scoping.']
  reach_registration_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers in this segment are located in the European Economic Area and therefore require REACH registration documentation for all chemical substances supplied above 1 tonne per year. True = REACH compliance documentation required; False = not applicable. Drives regulatory documentation workflows.']
  regulatory_compliance_profile varchar [note: 'Description of the primary regulatory frameworks applicable to customers in this segment that Chemical Mfg must support (e.g., REACH compliance documentation for EU customers, TSCA Section 6 for US industrial customers, GHS SDS requirements, FDA food-contact compliance for food-grade chemical customers). Drives documentation and compliance reporting obligations.']
  sales_region varchar [note: 'Geographic sales region primarily associated with this customer segment (e.g., North America, EMEA, APAC, Latin America). Used for territory planning, quota allocation, and regional commercial reporting in SAP SD and Salesforce CRM.']
  sap_customer_group varchar [note: 'SAP SD customer group code (field KDGRP) associated with this segment, used for pricing condition determination, rebate processing, and sales statistics grouping in SAP S/4HANA. Enables direct mapping between the segment definition and SAP SD commercial configuration.']
  sds_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers in this segment require Safety Data Sheet (SDS) documentation to be provided with all chemical shipments as a standard commercial requirement. True = SDS mandatory for all orders; False = SDS provided on request only. Aligns with GHS and OSHA Hazard Communication Standard (HCS) requirements.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the customer segment (e.g., AUTO_OEM, AGRO_DIST, SPEC_COAT, PHARMA_EXC, ELEC_MFG). Used as the business key in Salesforce CRM and SAP SD customer classification. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the customer segment, including the types of customers included, their primary use cases for chemical products, and the commercial rationale for the segment definition.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the customer segment (e.g., Automotive OEM, Agrochemical Distributor, Specialty Coatings, Pharma Excipient, Electronics). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Used in commercial strategy, pricing tiers, and technical service allocation.']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the customer segment definition: active (in use for commercial operations), inactive (no longer applied to new customers), under_review (being evaluated for revision), deprecated (replaced by a newer segment structure). Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  segment_tier varchar [note: 'Commercial priority tier assigned to the segment, reflecting strategic importance and resource allocation level. Tier 1 represents the highest-priority segments (e.g., strategic global accounts), while Tier 4 represents transactional or low-volume segments. Drives pricing authority, technical service allocation, and account management coverage.']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the segment by customer relationship type: end_user (direct consumer of chemicals), distributor (reseller/channel partner), oem (original equipment manufacturer), toll_manufacturer (contract processor), trading_company (commodity trader), government (public sector). Serves as the primary CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE for this entity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: end_user|distributor|oem|toll_manufacturer|trading_company|government|broker|cooperative — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  strategic_account_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers in this segment are classified as strategic accounts requiring dedicated key account management, executive sponsorship, and customized commercial terms. True = strategic segment; False = standard commercial segment. Drives resource allocation in Salesforce CRM and SAP SD pricing.']
  target_revenue_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual revenue target in US Dollars (USD) set for this customer segment as part of the commercial planning cycle. Used in SAP FI/CO profitability analysis (CO-PA) and Salesforce CRM quota management. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity. This is a planning input, not a calculated aggregate.']
  technical_service_level varchar [note: 'Level of technical service and application support allocated to customers in this segment: full_service (dedicated technical account manager, on-site support, custom formulation assistance), standard (remote technical support, standard SDS/COA documentation), self_service (online portal access only), none (no technical service). Drives resource planning for technical sales teams.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this customer segment record. Used for change tracking, data synchronization with Salesforce CRM, and audit compliance. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.']
  wallet_share_estimate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated percentage of the total addressable spend within this segment that Chemical Mfg currently captures (0.00 to 100.00). Represents the MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE principal quantitative fact for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Used in commercial strategy to identify penetration gaps and prioritize growth investments. Classified as confidential due to competitive sensitivity.']

  Note: 'Market segmentation classification for customer accounts used in commercial strategy, pricing, and technical service allocation. Captures segment code, segment name (e.g., Automotive OEM, Agrochemical Distributor, Specialty Coatings, Pharma Excipient, Electronics), segment tier, industry vertical (NAICS-based), strategic account flag, growth potential rating, wallet share estimate, segment owner, and applicable product portfolio. Drives targeted marketing, pricing tiers, and technical service prioritization.'
}

Table customer.credit_profile {
  credit_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the customer credit profile record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer master record for whom this credit profile is maintained. Links the credit profile to the customer entity in the customer domain.']
  bad_debt_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Bad debt provisioning in chemical manufacturing requires posting credit loss reserves to specific GL accounts (allowance for doubtful accounts). Credit teams reference the GL account for bad debt rese']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Credit profiles in chemical manufacturing are managed per legal entity (credit_control_area maps to legal entity in SAP). Consolidated credit exposure reporting and credit insurance management require']
  payment_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment_term. Business justification: Customer credit profiles in chemical mfg specify approved payment terms (net 30, net 60, etc.) as part of credit risk management. The existing payment_terms_code is a denormalized reference; a proper ']
  credit_block_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is currently subject to a credit block in SAP SD (KNKK-CTLPC / KNVV-AUFSD). When True, new sales orders for this customer are blocked from delivery and billing until the block is resolved by the credit department. Critical control for order-to-cash risk management.']
  credit_block_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the customer credit block is active. \'overlimit\' indicates exposure exceeds approved limit; \'payment_overdue\' indicates past-due invoices; \'credit_review\' indicates periodic review in progress; \'legal_dispute\' indicates an active dispute; \'sanctions_hold\' indicates a regulatory/compliance hold; \'manual_hold\' indicates a discretionary hold by the credit analyst.']
  credit_control_area varchar [note: 'SAP credit control area code (KKBER) that governs the credit limit and exposure tracking for this customer. Defines the organizational unit responsible for credit management, typically aligned to a company code or regional finance entity.']
  credit_exposure_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current total credit exposure for the customer, representing the sum of open receivables, open sales orders, and open deliveries not yet invoiced. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA credit exposure calculation (KNKK-SKFOR). Used to assess proximity to credit limit and trigger credit blocks.']
  credit_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer account is on a general credit hold, which may be broader than a specific SAP credit block. A credit hold may prevent new orders, shipments, or both, and is managed by the credit department independently of the automated SAP block logic.']
  credit_insurance_coverage_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum insured amount under the trade credit insurance policy for this customer. Represents the portion of the credit exposure that is covered by the insurer in the event of customer default. Used to calculate the net uninsured credit exposure.']
  credit_insurance_policy_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the trade credit insurance policy covering this customer\'s receivables. Credit insurance is commonly used in chemical distribution to mitigate counterparty default risk on large B2B accounts. Enables linkage to the insurer\'s policy documentation for claims processing.']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum approved credit exposure (in base currency) extended to the customer by the credit department. Stored in SAP S/4HANA as KNKK-KLIMK. Drives order block logic in SAP SD when exposure approaches or exceeds this threshold. Critical for order-to-cash risk management in chemical distribution.']
  credit_limit_approval_level varchar [note: 'Authorization level required to approve or modify the credit limit for this customer, based on the credit limit amount and risk category. Enforces the company\'s credit approval authority matrix and SOX segregation of duties controls.']
  credit_limit_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the credit limit amount is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures correct multi-currency credit exposure calculation across global chemical distribution operations.']
  credit_limit_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the current approved credit limit became effective. Supports audit trail requirements and enables historical analysis of credit limit changes over time. Aligns with the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_FROM category.']
  credit_limit_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current approved credit limit expires and requires renewal or re-approval. Nullable for open-ended credit arrangements. When populated, triggers automated alerts to the credit analyst for timely renewal. Aligns with the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category.']
  credit_notes_text varchar [note: 'Free-text field for credit analyst notes, observations, and commentary related to the customer\'s credit profile. May include context on credit decisions, customer communications, or risk factors not captured in structured fields. Restricted to credit department personnel.']
  credit_profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the customer credit profile. \'active\' indicates the profile is in use for order-to-cash processing; \'suspended\' indicates temporary hold; \'under_review\' indicates a scheduled or triggered credit review is in progress; \'pending_approval\' indicates a new or revised limit awaiting authorization.']
  credit_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent formal credit review was conducted for this customer. Used to track compliance with the credit department\'s periodic review schedule and to assess the currency of the credit assessment.']
  credit_risk_category varchar [note: 'Categorical risk classification assigned to the customer by the credit department, summarizing the overall credit risk level. Drives credit limit approval thresholds, review frequency, and escalation procedures. \'critical\' indicates customers requiring executive-level approval for any credit extension.']
  credit_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segmentation classification from a credit management perspective. \'strategic\' and \'key_account\' customers may receive preferential credit terms; \'distributor\' reflects the B2B distribution channel common in chemical manufacturing; \'new_customer\' triggers enhanced due diligence and conservative initial limits.']
  credit_utilization_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the approved credit limit currently utilized, calculated as (credit_exposure_amount / credit_limit_amount) × 100. Provides a normalized risk indicator for credit analysts and automated alerting when utilization exceeds defined thresholds (e.g., 80%, 90%, 100%).']
  dso_actual_days varchar [note: 'Actual current days sales outstanding (DSO) for this customer, representing the average number of days receivables remain outstanding. Compared against dso_target_days to identify payment performance trends and trigger credit review actions.']
  dso_target_days varchar [note: 'Target number of days sales outstanding (DSO) established for this customer by the credit department. Represents the expected average collection period for receivables from this customer. Used as a benchmark for monitoring actual collection performance and identifying deteriorating payment behavior.']
  dun_bradstreet_number varchar [note: 'The nine-digit Data Universal Numbering System (DUNS) number assigned by Dun & Bradstreet to uniquely identify the customer\'s business entity. Used to retrieve and link external credit reports and financial risk data for the customer.']
  dun_bradstreet_rating varchar [note: 'External credit rating assigned by Dun & Bradstreet (D&B) for the customer organization. Includes the D&B PAYDEX score or composite credit appraisal code. Used to supplement internal credit assessment with third-party financial risk data for B2B industrial customers and distributors in chemical distribution.']
  internal_credit_rating varchar [note: 'Internally assigned credit rating for the customer, determined by the credit department based on payment history, financial statements, and risk assessment. Typically expressed as a letter or alphanumeric grade (e.g., A1, B2, C3) aligned to the company\'s internal credit scoring methodology. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2|D — promote to reference product]']
  last_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the credit limit or credit terms were last formally approved by an authorized approver. Supports SOX audit trail requirements and enables tracking of the age of the most recent credit authorization.']
  last_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or user ID of the individual who last approved the credit limit or credit terms for this customer. Provides an audit trail for credit authorization decisions in compliance with SOX internal control requirements.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal credit review of this customer. Determined by the credit review cycle (e.g., annual, semi-annual, triggered by risk events). Used by credit analysts to manage their review workload and ensure timely reassessment of credit limits.']
  oldest_overdue_days varchar [note: 'Number of days since the oldest overdue invoice for this customer became past due. Used to assess the severity of payment delinquency and to trigger escalation actions such as collections referral or credit block.']
  overdue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total value of invoices that are past their due date for this customer, expressed in the credit limit currency. A key indicator of payment risk and a primary trigger for credit block activation and credit review escalation.']
  payment_behavior_score varchar [note: 'Internally calculated payment behavior score for the customer, derived from historical payment patterns including on-time payment rate, frequency of late payments, and average days late. Expressed as a numeric score (e.g., 1–100). Used as an input to the internal credit rating and credit limit decisions.']
  profile_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credit profile record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker for this entity, supporting SOX compliance and data lineage requirements in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  profile_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the credit profile record. Used for change tracking, data freshness assessment, and incremental load processing in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline.']
  sanctions_check_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent sanctions and restricted party screening was performed for this customer. Used to ensure screening is current and to schedule re-screening at required intervals per compliance policy.']
  sanctions_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of the most recent sanctions and restricted party screening check performed for this customer. Chemical manufacturers must screen customers against OFAC, EU, and UN sanctions lists. \'flagged\' triggers immediate credit hold and compliance review.']
  sap_credit_account_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known SAP credit management account number (KNKK-KNKLI) assigned to the customer in SAP S/4HANA SD. Used as the business identifier for cross-system reconciliation with the ERP credit management module.']
  special_credit_terms varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any special or negotiated credit terms applicable to this customer that deviate from standard payment terms. Examples include extended payment schedules, seasonal credit adjustments, or terms negotiated as part of a strategic account agreement in chemical distribution.']

  Note: 'Customer credit standing and financial risk profile managed by the credit department. Stores credit limit (in base currency), credit exposure, payment terms code (Net 30, Net 60, COD, Prepay), credit rating (internal and external Dun & Bradstreet), credit hold flag, credit hold reason, credit review date, next review date, credit analyst, credit insurance policy reference, days sales outstanding (DSO) target, and credit block status in SAP SD. Critical for order-to-cash risk management in chemical distribution.'
}

Table customer.regulatory_profile {
  regulatory_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the customer regulatory and compliance profile record in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account for whom this regulatory profile applies. Links to the customer master record in Salesforce CRM / SAP S/4HANA SD.']
  hazmat_storage_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.hazmat_storage_rule. Business justification: Ensures Regulatory Compliance Mapping, linking a customer\'s regulatory profile to the specific hazmat storage rule applied for safe handling and reporting.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: REACH registrations, TSCA compliance obligations, and export licenses in chemical manufacturing are held by specific legal entities. Regulatory compliance reporting and audit trails require direct lin']
  operating_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.operating_permit. Business justification: Customer regulatory compliance review process: before shipping regulated chemicals, sales and regulatory teams verify the customer\'s site operating permit is valid and covers the chemical class. This ']
  quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_specification. Business justification: A customer\'s regulatory profile (REACH, TSCA, RoHS, GHS applicability) determines which quality specification version must govern shipments to that customer. This link supports compliance reporting: i']
  required_safety_data_sheet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.safety_data_sheet. Business justification: SDS distribution compliance process: a customer\'s regulatory profile mandates a specific SDS version and language (OSHA HazCom, REACH Article 31). Linking to the actual SDS record replaces the denorma']
  sds_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.sds_record. Business justification: Customer SDS Compliance Distribution: regulatory_profile.sds_version_required is a denormalized reference to a specific SDS record. Chemical manufacturers must link each customer\'s regulatory profile ']
  approved_product_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of chemical product categories or families that are approved for sale to this customer under the current regulatory profile (e.g., solvents, adhesives, specialty polymers). Restricts order processing to compliant product types.']
  audit_rights_agreed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has agreed to grant the supplier audit rights to verify compliance with regulatory requirements, safe handling practices, and end-use declarations for supplied chemicals.']
  audit_rights_agreement_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer formally agreed to audit rights provisions. Used to track agreement currency and renewal obligations.']
  coa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Certificate of Analysis must accompany each shipment to this customer, confirming that the chemical product meets specified quality and composition standards.']
  coc_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Certificate of Compliance is required with each shipment, attesting that the product conforms to applicable regulatory and customer-specified requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory profile record was first created in the system, establishing the audit trail for compliance record-keeping purposes.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the primary country of destination for chemical product shipments to this customer. Drives applicable regulatory framework selection (e.g., REACH for EU, TSCA for USA).']
  destination_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or trade bloc of the customer destination (e.g., European Union, North America, Asia-Pacific) used for regional regulatory framework grouping and reporting.']
  downstream_user_notification boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this customer qualifies as a REACH downstream user and has been notified of their obligations under REACH, including use conditions, exposure scenarios, and risk management measures.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this regulatory profile becomes binding and governs chemical product sales and documentation obligations for the customer.']
  ehs_certification_expiry date [note: 'Expiry date of the customer\'s EHS certification. Triggers re-validation workflow when approaching expiry to ensure continued compliance eligibility.']
  ehs_certification_number varchar [note: 'Certificate number or registration identifier for the customer\'s EHS management system certification (e.g., ISO 14001 certificate number), used for audit trail and verification.']
  ehs_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the customer\'s EHS certification relevant to handling and using chemical products (e.g., ISO 14001, ISO 45001, OSHA VPP). Determines eligibility for certain product categories.']
  end_use_declaration varchar [note: 'Customer\'s declared intended end-use application for the chemical products purchased (e.g., industrial solvent, agricultural formulation, polymer additive, pharmaceutical intermediate). Required for REACH downstream user obligations and export control compliance.']
  end_use_sector varchar [note: 'Industry sector classification of the customer\'s end-use application for purchased chemicals. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: automotive|construction|electronics|agriculture|pharmaceuticals|consumer_goods|coatings|textiles|food_contact|industrial — promote to reference product]']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this regulatory profile expires and must be renewed or re-validated. Null indicates an open-ended profile with no scheduled expiry.']
  export_license_number varchar [note: 'Export license or authorization number issued by the originating country\'s regulatory authority for controlled chemical substances, required for dual-use or restricted chemicals.']
  ghs_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Globally Harmonized System of Classification and Labelling of Chemicals applies to products sold to this customer, requiring GHS-compliant Safety Data Sheets and labels.']
  hazmat_transport_class varchar [note: 'DOT/IATA/IMDG hazardous materials transport classification applicable to chemical products shipped to this customer (e.g., Class 3 Flammable Liquid, Class 8 Corrosive). Governs packaging, labeling, and shipping documentation requirements.']
  import_license_number varchar [note: 'Customer\'s import license or permit number issued by the destination country\'s regulatory authority, required for controlled or restricted chemical substances. Must be validated before shipment.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory compliance audit conducted at or for this customer. Used to schedule next audit and assess compliance currency.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this regulatory profile record. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and downstream data synchronization.']
  msds_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the legacy MSDS format (pre-GHS) is required in addition to or instead of the current SDS format, applicable for customers in jurisdictions that have not fully adopted GHS.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this regulatory profile to ensure continued accuracy, completeness, and alignment with current regulatory requirements.']
  profile_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this regulatory profile, used in correspondence, audits, and regulatory submissions. Format: REGP-YYYYMMDD-CountryCode.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the customer regulatory profile. Active indicates all requirements are met and sales may proceed; pending_review indicates documentation is under assessment; suspended indicates a compliance hold; expired indicates the profile requires renewal.']
  profile_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the customer relationship type that determines applicable regulatory obligations and documentation requirements for chemical product sales.']
  prop65_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether California Proposition 65 (Safe Drinking Water and Toxic Enforcement Act) warning requirements apply to products sold to this customer based on end-use state or distribution territory.']
  reach_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the European Chemicals Agency REACH regulation applies to chemical products sold to this customer, based on destination country and product use.']
  regulatory_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the designated regulatory affairs or EHS contact at the customer organization for compliance documentation, SDS distribution, and regulatory notifications.']
  regulatory_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the designated regulatory affairs or EHS contact person at the customer organization responsible for managing compliance documentation and regulatory correspondence.']
  restricted_product_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of chemical product categories that are restricted or prohibited from sale to this customer based on regulatory, end-use, or jurisdictional constraints. Triggers order block in SAP S/4HANA SD.']
  restricted_substance_ack_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer formally acknowledged the supplier\'s Restricted Substance List. Used to track acknowledgment currency and trigger renewal when the RSL is updated.']
  restricted_substance_acknowledgment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has formally acknowledged receipt and acceptance of the supplier\'s Restricted Substance List, confirming awareness of substances that are prohibited or restricted in supplied products.']
  rohs_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether EU RoHS (Restriction of Hazardous Substances) directive requirements apply to chemical products or materials sold to this customer, particularly relevant for electronics-sector customers.']
  sds_language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) specifying the required language for Safety Data Sheets provided to this customer (e.g., en, de, fr, zh-CN).']
  special_labeling_requirements varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any customer-specific or jurisdiction-specific labeling requirements beyond standard GHS/SDS requirements, such as additional language requirements, customer-specific hazard statements, or country-specific pictogram standards.']
  svhc_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer requires proactive notification when any supplied product contains Substances of Very High Concern (SVHC) above 0.1% w/w threshold, as mandated under REACH Article 33.']
  tsca_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the US EPA Toxic Substances Control Act requirements apply to chemical products sold to this customer.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Primary UN number identifying the hazardous substance or article for transport documentation purposes. Required on shipping papers, labels, and placards for regulated chemical shipments to this customer.']

  Note: 'Customer regulatory and compliance profile capturing jurisdiction-specific requirements that govern how chemical products are sold, labeled, and documented for that customer. Stores applicable regulatory frameworks (REACH, TSCA, GHS, RoHS, PROP65), required documentation (SDS version, COA, COC, MSDS language), country of destination, import/export license numbers, end-use declaration, restricted substance list acknowledgment, customer EHS certification status, and audit rights agreement. Ensures regulatory compliance in chemical sales.'
}

The existing `approved_by_customer_contact` field is a denor']

Finance controllers']
  customer_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific customer plant, facility, or site location where the qualified product is approved for use. Qualifications are site-specific in chemical supply to automotive, pharma, and electronics sectors.']

Business justification: Customer qualification projects in chemical manufacturing are managed as SAP internal orders to capture all associated costs (lab materials, personnel, regulatory fees). This is standard SAP CO practi']

Business justification: Customer qualifications in chemical manufacturing are plant-specific — a product made at Plant A may be qualified while Plant B is not. This is a standard customer qualification scope requirement, ena']

Business justification: Customer product qualifications in chemical manufacturing are granted against a specific quality specification version. This link is essential for spec-change notification workflows: when a quality sp']
  safety_data_sheet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.safety_data_sheet. Business justification: Customer product qualification process: each qualification must reference the exact SDS version acknowledged by the customer for that product-site combination. This FK replaces denormalized sds_versio']

  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: Customer product qualifications in chemical manufacturing are vendor-source-specific: a customer qualifies a chemical grade from a specific supplier. If the vendor changes, re-qualification is mandato']
  apl_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the qualification record, typically assigned by the customer or jointly by the supplier and customer. Used in customer correspondence, COA references, and audit trails.']
  application_use_case varchar [note: 'The specific end-use application or process for which the chemical product has been qualified at the customer site (e.g., automotive coating adhesion, semiconductor etch bath, pharmaceutical excipient, agricultural formulation carrier).']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the customer product qualification. \'approved\' = fully qualified for commercial supply; \'conditionally_approved\' = approved with restrictions or deviations; \'pending_review\' = under evaluation; \'suspended\' = temporarily halted pending investigation; \'revoked\' = permanently withdrawn; \'expired\' = past expiry date without renewal.']
  approved_by_internal_user varchar [note: 'The name or employee ID of the internal Chemical Mfg representative (e.g., technical sales, quality manager) who managed and approved the qualification from the supplier side.']
  change_notification_lead_days varchar [note: 'The minimum number of calendar days advance notice required to be given to the customer before implementing any change to the qualified product. Populated only when change_notification_required_flag is True.']
  change_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer requires formal notification (e.g., PCN - Product Change Notification) before any change to the qualified product formulation, process, or raw material source. Critical in automotive and electronics supply chains.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this qualification record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance purposes. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  crm_qualification_code varchar [note: 'The qualification record identifier in Salesforce CRM, the system of record for customer-facing qualification activities. Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the operational CRM.']
  customer_part_number varchar [note: 'The customer\'s own internal part number or material code assigned to this qualified chemical product. Used for cross-referencing in purchase orders, COA requests, and EDI transactions.']
  customer_spec_number varchar [note: 'The customer\'s internal specification document number that defines the quality requirements for this qualified product. Referenced on COA and COC documents during shipment.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this qualification record is binding and commercial supply may commence. May differ from qualification_date if there is a ramp-up or transition period.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date until which this qualification is contractually binding for commercial supply. Null for open-ended qualifications. Used in supply chain planning and order management validation.']
  exclusive_supply_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Chemical Mfg is the sole qualified supplier for this product at this customer site. True = exclusive supply arrangement; False = customer has multiple qualified suppliers.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the customer qualification expires and must be renewed or re-qualified. Null indicates an open-ended qualification with no defined expiry. Critical for supply continuity planning.']
  industry_segment varchar [note: 'The end-market industry segment of the customer for which this qualification applies. Drives regulatory requirements, COA content, and qualification rigor. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: automotive|electronics|pharmaceutical|agriculture|construction|consumer_goods|industrial|aerospace|food_beverage|coatings — promote to reference product]']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this qualification record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  lims_qualification_reference varchar [note: 'The qualification record identifier in LabWare LIMS, used to cross-reference lab trial data, test results, and analytical reports that supported the qualification decision.']
  max_approved_volume_kg decimal(18,3) [note: 'The maximum annual or periodic supply volume in kilograms approved under this qualification. Volumes exceeding this threshold require customer notification or re-qualification.']
  min_approved_volume_kg decimal(18,3) [note: 'The minimum annual or periodic supply volume in kilograms approved under this qualification. Volumes below this threshold may require re-qualification or customer notification.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional qualification conditions, restrictions, or contextual information not captured in structured fields. Examples: special handling requirements, packaging restrictions, labelling requirements specific to this customer.']
  qualification_date date [note: 'The date on which the product qualification was formally approved and granted by the customer. This is the effective start date for commercial supply under this qualification.']
  qualification_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the product was qualified for use at the customer site. \'lab_trial\' = bench-scale testing at customer or supplier lab; \'plant_trial\' = full-scale production trial at customer facility; \'third_party_cert\' = qualification via third-party certification body; \'desk_review\' = documentation-only review; \'customer_audit\' = qualification via customer site audit; \'equivalency_study\' = qualification via equivalency to an already-approved product.']
  qualification_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the geographic or organizational scope of the qualification. \'site_specific\' = applies only to the named customer site; \'global_account\' = applies across all sites of the customer account; \'regional\' = applies to a defined regional cluster of sites; \'product_family\' = applies to a family of related products.']
  reach_registration_number varchar [note: 'The European Chemicals Agency (ECHA) REACH registration number for the qualified substance. Required for supply into EU markets. Format: 01-XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX-XX-XXXX.']
  regulatory_compliance_standard varchar [note: 'The primary regulatory or quality standard under which this qualification was conducted (e.g., IATF 16949 for automotive, ICH Q7 for pharmaceutical API, IPC-7711 for electronics, ISO 9001 for general industrial). Drives audit and documentation requirements.']
  renewal_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days before the expiry date that the renewal process must be initiated to ensure supply continuity. Used to trigger renewal workflow alerts in the quality management system.']
  renewal_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this qualification requires periodic renewal before the expiry date. True = renewal process must be initiated; False = qualification is perpetual or renewal is not contractually required.']
  spec_deviation_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of any customer-approved deviations from the standard product specification included in this qualification. Populated only when spec_deviation_flag is True.']
  spec_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this qualification includes customer-approved deviations from the standard product specification. True = one or more approved deviations exist; False = qualification is to standard specification.']
  volume_period varchar [note: 'The time period to which the approved volume range (min/max) applies. Defines whether volume limits are annual, quarterly, monthly, or per-order constraints.']

  Note: 'Customer-approved chemical products and grades that have been qualified for use at a specific customer site or application. Captures customer account, site, approved SKU/product code, grade, application use case, qualification date, qualification method (lab trial, plant trial, third-party cert), approval status, expiry date, approved volume range, and any customer-specific specification deviations. Critical in automotive, pharma, and electronics chemical supply where customer qualification is mandatory before commercial supply.'
}

Table customer.interaction {
  interaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each customer interaction record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the interaction data product.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer (account) involved in this interaction. Links to the customer master data product.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service case or complaint record linked to this interaction, if applicable. Supports complaint resolution tracking and Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) workflows.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Supports support interaction logs tying each customer interaction to the specific chemical product discussed, required for service quality metrics.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific customer contact person who participated in this interaction. Links to the customer contact data product.']
  customer_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific customer site or plant location where the interaction occurred or is relevant to. Supports site-level account management and technical service tracking.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales opportunity linked to this interaction, if applicable. Enables tracking of interactions that advance a specific sales pursuit.']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order. Business justification: REQUIRED: Interaction Log per Order ties customer/technical interactions to a specific sales order, enabling SLA measurement and order‑level support metrics.']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote. Business justification: Customer interactions in chemical sales frequently involve quote presentations, price negotiations, and quote follow-ups. Linking interaction to quote enables CRM tracking of which quotes were discuss']
  safety_data_sheet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.safety_data_sheet. Business justification: SDS request fulfillment process: customer interactions flagged sds_request_flag=true represent formal SDS distribution events (OSHA HazCom 2012, REACH). Linking the interaction to the specific SDS doc']
  channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel through which the interaction was conducted. Supports omnichannel analytics and customer engagement reporting.']
  coa_request_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer requested a Certificate of Analysis (COA) during this interaction. Supports quality document fulfillment tracking and customer quality management.']
  complaint_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this interaction involves or originated from a customer complaint. Triggers linkage to the complaint/case management workflow and Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this interaction record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  crm_activity_code varchar [note: 'External identifier from Salesforce CRM activity record. Used to trace the interaction back to the system of record for reconciliation and lineage.']
  customer_participant_count varchar [note: 'Number of customer-side contacts who participated in the interaction. Indicates the breadth of customer engagement and stakeholder involvement.']
  customer_participants varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of customer contact names or identifiers who participated in the interaction. Confidential business relationship data.']
  customer_satisfaction_score varchar [note: 'Numeric satisfaction rating provided by the customer or assessed by the account manager following the interaction, typically on a 1-5 or 1-10 scale. Supports Key Performance Indicator (KPI) tracking for customer relationship health.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The total elapsed time of the interaction in minutes. Used to assess engagement depth, resource utilization, and account planning efficiency.']
  follow_up_due_date date [note: 'The target date by which the required follow-up action must be completed. Used for task management and account manager accountability tracking.']
  follow_up_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the specific follow-up actions committed to during the interaction. Captures commitments made to the customer for accountability and continuity.']
  follow_up_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up action or subsequent interaction is required as a result of this interaction. Drives task creation and account planning workflows.']
  interaction_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the customer interaction occurred. Used for day-level scheduling, account planning, and reporting.']
  interaction_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the interaction content, discussion points, and context. Captures technical topics discussed, products referenced, and any regulatory or compliance matters raised.']
  interaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the interaction record. Indicates whether the interaction was completed as planned, cancelled, or requires rescheduling.']
  interaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which the customer interaction commenced. Supports time-zone-aware analytics and SLA measurement.']
  interaction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the nature or purpose of the customer interaction. Distinguishes between sales-driven, technical service, complaint handling, and digital engagement touchpoints. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sales_call|technical_visit|complaint_call|email_exchange|webinar|trade_show|portal_login|video_conference — promote to reference product]']
  internal_participant_count varchar [note: 'Number of internal Chemical Mfg employees who participated in the interaction. Used for resource utilization and engagement intensity analysis.']
  internal_participants varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of internal employee names or identifiers who participated in the interaction (e.g., account manager, technical service representative, application chemist). Supports multi-participant interaction tracking.']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the interaction took place (e.g., customer plant site, trade show venue, video conference URL, Chemical Mfg office). Relevant for in-person and on-site technical visits.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this interaction record was last updated in the system. Supports change tracking, data quality monitoring, and audit compliance.']
  next_interaction_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next planned customer interaction as agreed during this touchpoint. Supports account planning cadence and proactive customer engagement management.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'The result or disposition of the interaction as assessed by the internal participant. Supports win/loss analysis, customer satisfaction tracking, and account health scoring.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the interaction, reflecting urgency of follow-up or strategic importance of the customer engagement.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'The Chemical Mfg product line or portfolio segment discussed during the interaction (e.g., performance materials, agricultural solutions, specialty chemicals). Supports product-level engagement analytics.']
  regulatory_topic_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the interaction involved discussion of regulatory or compliance topics such as REACH, TSCA, GHS, SDS requirements, or EPA/OSHA obligations. Supports regulatory engagement tracking and compliance reporting.']
  sales_territory_code varchar [note: 'The sales territory code associated with this interaction, derived from the account owner\'s territory assignment. Supports territory-level performance reporting and account planning.']
  sample_request_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer requested a product sample during this interaction. Triggers linkage to the sample request fulfillment process in the sales domain.']
  sds_request_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer requested a Safety Data Sheet (SDS) or Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) during this interaction. Supports regulatory document fulfillment tracking per GHS and OSHA HazCom requirements.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating operational system from which this interaction record was sourced. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and multi-system integration governance.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief title or subject line summarizing the topic of the interaction. Mirrors the Salesforce CRM activity subject field and supports keyword search and categorization.']
  technical_topic varchar [note: 'The primary technical subject matter discussed during the interaction (e.g., formulation support, application development, regulatory compliance, Safety Data Sheet (SDS) review, Certificate of Analysis (COA) query). Supports technical service analytics.']

  Note: 'Log of all customer-facing interactions and touchpoints including sales calls, technical visits, complaint calls, email exchanges, webinar attendance, trade show meetings, and portal logins. Captures interaction type, channel (phone, email, in-person, video, portal), date and time, duration, participants (internal and customer contacts), subject, outcome, follow-up actions, and linked opportunity or case. Sourced from Salesforce CRM activity records. Supports customer relationship management and account planning.'
}
