  pricing_tier varchar [note: 'Pricing tier classification for this grade, reflecting the quality level and market positioning. Higher purity and specialty grades typically command premium pricing.']
  reach_registered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this grade is registered under the European REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals) regulation for sale in the EU.']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory classification or standard applicable to this grade (e.g., GMP-compliant, USP Grade, EP Grade, FCC Grade, ACS Reagent). May reference multiple standards separated by semicolons.']
  shelf_life_months varchar [note: 'Expected shelf life of this grade in months under proper storage conditions, used for inventory management (FEFO - First Expired First Out) and lot tracking.']
  storage_temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum storage temperature in degrees Celsius required to maintain grade specifications and product stability.']
  storage_temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum storage temperature in degrees Celsius required to maintain grade specifications and product stability.']
  tsca_listed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this grade is listed on the EPA TSCA (Toxic Substances Control Act) inventory for commercial use in the United States.']
  usp_grade_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this grade meets United States Pharmacopeia (USP) monograph standards for pharmaceutical use.']

  Note: 'Defines the quality grades and purity tiers available for a chemical product (e.g., Technical Grade, Reagent Grade, Food Grade, Pharmaceutical Grade, Electronic Grade, Agricultural Grade). Captures grade code, grade name, grade description, minimum purity percentage, applicable regulatory standards (GMP, USP, EP, FCC), intended application sector, and grade-specific handling requirements. A single chemical product may have multiple grades, each with distinct specifications and pricing. Owned by the product domain as part of the product catalog.'
}

Table product.product_specification {
  product_specification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product specification record. Primary key for the product specification entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer for whom this specification is defined. Nullable for standard specifications. Populated for customer-specific specifications that define unique quality requirements per contract or technical agreement.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the chemical product, grade, or Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) that this specification applies to. Links to the product master catalog.']
  grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grade. Business justification: product_specification defines technical specifications for a chemical product, grade, or SKU (per its description). It already has chemical_product_id. Adding nullable grade_id enables grade-specific ']
  sds_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sds. Business justification: product_specification has an `sds_section_reference` STRING field that references a specific section within an SDS document. Adding sds_id FK normalizes the reference to the SDS master record, while k']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: product_specification\'s description explicitly states it covers \'a chemical product, grade, or SKU.\' Adding nullable sku_id enables SKU-level specifications — customer-specific specs (customer_specifi']
  superseded_specification_product_specification_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous specification version that this version replaces. Nullable for initial specifications. Supports version history tracking and regulatory audit trail for specification changes.']
  tds_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.tds. Business justification: product_specification defines technical parameters with test methods and acceptance criteria. These specifications are often derived from or aligned with the Technical Data Sheet (TDS). Adding nullabl']
  acceptance_criteria varchar [note: 'Textual description of the acceptance rule for this parameter. Used for qualitative assessments or complex criteria that cannot be expressed as simple numeric ranges (e.g., \'clear colorless liquid\', \'no visible particulates\', \'conforms to reference standard\').']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Role or individual responsible for approving this specification version. Typically Quality Assurance (QA) manager, regulatory affairs director, or technical director. Required for audit trail and compliance with Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) and ISO 9001.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this specification version was formally approved. Establishes the audit trail for specification lifecycle management and supports Management of Change (MOC) processes.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved this specification version. Captured for regulatory audit trail and compliance with Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) documentation requirements.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for creating this specification version. Documents the reason for specification changes as required by Management of Change (MOC) processes. Examples include regulatory update, process improvement, customer requirement, Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) response.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this specification record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and regulatory compliance with Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) documentation requirements.']
  criticality_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the parameter\'s importance to product quality and safety. Critical parameters directly impact safety or efficacy and trigger immediate batch rejection if Out of Specification (OOS). Major parameters affect product performance. Minor parameters are informational or aesthetic.']
  customer_specific_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this specification is tailored for a specific customer contract or represents the standard product specification. True if customer-specific, false if standard. Customer-specific specifications may have tighter limits or additional parameters.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the full specification document stored in the document management system. Provides access to detailed test procedures, sampling instructions, and supporting documentation.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this specification version becomes binding for quality control testing and release decisions. Used to determine which specification applies to a given production batch or lot.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which this specification version is no longer valid for new testing. Nullable for specifications with indefinite validity. Superseded specifications retain expiry date for audit purposes.']
  ghs_classification_relevance varchar [note: 'Describes how this specification parameter relates to Globally Harmonized System (GHS) hazard classification and Safety Data Sheet (SDS) content. For example, pH specifications may determine corrosivity classification; VOC content affects environmental hazard labeling.']
  iso_standard_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the International Organization for Standardization (ISO) standard that governs this specification parameter or test method. Examples include ISO 9001 for quality management, ISO 14001 for environmental parameters, ISO 17025 for testing competence.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified this specification record. Required for audit trail and compliance with Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this specification record. Tracks changes for audit trail and supports Management of Change (MOC) processes.']
  lims_test_code varchar [note: 'Test code or identifier in the Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) used to execute this specification parameter. Links this specification to LabWare LIMS for test execution, result capture, and Certificate of Analysis (COA) generation.']
  maximum_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Upper acceptance limit for the quality parameter. Test results above this value indicate Out of Specification (OOS) condition. Nullable for parameters with only lower limits.']
  minimum_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Lower acceptance limit for the quality parameter. Test results below this value indicate Out of Specification (OOS) condition. Nullable for parameters with only upper limits.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments, instructions, or context for this specification parameter. May include special handling instructions, historical context, or clarifications for laboratory technicians and Quality Control (QC) personnel.']
  parameter_name varchar [note: 'Name of the quality parameter being specified. Examples include assay, pH (Potential of Hydrogen), viscosity, moisture content, color, density, particle size, heavy metals concentration, residual solvents, Volatile Organic Compound (VOC) content, and other chemical or physical properties.']
  reach_registration_relevance varchar [note: 'Describes how this specification parameter supports Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH) registration and compliance. For example, impurity limits may be required to demonstrate substance identity and composition per REACH dossier.']
  regulatory_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this parameter is mandated by regulatory authorities such as Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA), Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH), Toxic Substances Control Act (TSCA), or Food and Drug Administration (FDA). True if regulatory-mandated, false if internal quality control only.']
  sampling_plan varchar [note: 'Description of the sampling strategy for this parameter. Defines sample size, sampling frequency, and sampling points (e.g., \'three samples from top, middle, bottom of batch\', \'composite sample from each reactor vessel\'). Links to Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) requirements.']
  sds_section_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the section of the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) or Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) where this specification parameter is documented. Supports linkage between quality specifications and safety documentation.']
  specification_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the specification document. Externally-known unique code used in Certificate of Analysis (COA) and Certificate of Compliance (COC) generation.']
  specification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the specification. Active specifications are used for Certificate of Analysis (COA) generation and Quality Control (QC) release decisions. Superseded specifications are retained for audit trail.']
  specification_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the specification based on its purpose in the quality control lifecycle. Release specifications define final product acceptance criteria; in-process specifications govern intermediate checkpoints; shelf-life and stability specifications define storage and aging limits.']
  target_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Ideal or nominal value for the quality parameter. Used for Statistical Process Control (SPC) and process optimization. Nullable for parameters defined only by acceptance ranges.']
  test_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which this parameter must be tested. Per-batch and per-lot testing occur for every production run. Periodic testing (daily, weekly, monthly) applies to stability and shelf-life specifications. On-demand testing is triggered by specific events or investigations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: per-batch|per-lot|daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|annual|on-demand — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  test_method_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the standardized or internal test method used to measure this parameter. May reference ASTM (American Society for Testing and Materials), ISO (International Organization for Standardization), USP (United States Pharmacopeia), or internal Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) codes.']
  tsca_compliance_relevance varchar [note: 'Describes how this specification parameter supports Toxic Substances Control Act (TSCA) compliance and reporting. For example, residual monomer content may be required for TSCA inventory listing or Chemical Data Reporting (CDR).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the parameter value is expressed. Examples include Parts Per Million (PPM), percent, pH units, centipoise (cP) for viscosity, grams per cubic centimeter (g/cm³) for density, microns for particle size. Must align with test method reference.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the specification to support lifecycle changes and regulatory audit trail. Follows major.minor versioning convention.']

  Note: 'Authoritative technical specification record for a chemical product, grade, or SKU, defining the quality parameters and acceptance criteria used in COA/COC generation and QC release. Captures specification version, effective date, expiry date, specification type (release, in-process, shelf-life, stability), parameter name (assay, pH, viscosity, moisture, color, density, particle size, heavy metals, residual solvents), test method reference (ASTM, ISO, USP, internal), minimum value, maximum value, target value, unit of measure, specification status, and approval authority. Versioned to support lifecycle changes and regulatory audit trail. Links to LabWare LIMS for test execution and to quality domain for COA generation.'
}

Table product.tds {
  tds_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the technical data sheet record. Primary key.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent chemical product for which this TDS is published. Links to the chemical_product master data.']
  sds_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sds. Business justification: tds (Technical Data Sheet) has a denormalized `sds_document_number` STRING field that references the associated SDS document. The sds table is the authoritative SDS master record. TDS and SDS are comp']
  appearance varchar [note: 'Visual appearance characteristics of the product such as color, clarity, and texture. Aids in quality verification and customer identification.']
  application_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of recommended applications and use cases for the chemical product. Guides customers on appropriate usage scenarios.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the technical authority who approved this TDS version for publication. Ensures technical accuracy and regulatory compliance.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this TDS version received final technical approval. Part of document control and audit trail.']
  boiling_point_unit varchar [note: 'Temperature unit for boiling point measurement, either Celsius or Fahrenheit.']
  boiling_point_value decimal(6,2) [note: 'Temperature at which the product transitions from liquid to vapor phase at standard atmospheric pressure. Important for process design and safety.']
  compatibility_notes varchar [note: 'Information on material compatibility and incompatibility with other chemicals, substrates, or container materials. Prevents adverse reactions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this TDS record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for data governance.']
  document_file_path varchar [note: 'Internal file system or content management system path to the TDS document. Used for document retrieval and version control.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique document number assigned to this TDS for customer and distributor reference. Follows company TDS numbering convention.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Web URL or URI path to the published TDS document file (PDF or other format). Enables digital distribution and customer self-service access.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this TDS version becomes the authoritative technical reference for the product. Used to manage version transitions and customer communication.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which this TDS version is no longer valid and should not be referenced. Nullable for current versions without planned obsolescence.']
  flash_point_unit varchar [note: 'Temperature unit for flash point measurement, either Celsius or Fahrenheit.']
  flash_point_value decimal(6,2) [note: 'Lowest temperature at which vapors ignite when exposed to an ignition source. Critical safety parameter for flammable materials.']
  handling_precautions varchar [note: 'Safety precautions and best practices for handling the product including personal protective equipment (PPE) requirements and exposure controls.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code indicating the language in which this TDS is published. Supports multi-language TDS distribution for global markets.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this TDS record was last modified. Tracks data currency and supports change detection.']
  odor varchar [note: 'Characteristic odor or scent of the chemical product. Important for safety identification and customer expectations.']
  performance_data_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to supporting performance test data, technical bulletins, or case studies that demonstrate product efficacy and application results.']
  ph_value decimal(4,2) [note: 'Measured pH value of the product indicating acidity or alkalinity. Critical chemical property for application compatibility and safety.']
  physical_form varchar [note: 'Physical state or form of the chemical product at standard conditions. Critical for handling and storage guidance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: liquid|solid|powder|granular|paste|gel|gas|emulsion|suspension — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  product_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the chemical product, its composition, and key characteristics as presented to customers in the TDS.']
  recommended_use_concentration varchar [note: 'Guidance on typical or recommended concentration ranges for product application. May include dilution ratios or usage rates.']
  regulatory_compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Summary of key regulatory compliance information including REACH registration status, TSCA listing, FDA approvals, and other jurisdiction-specific clearances.']
  revision_notes varchar [note: 'Summary of changes made in this TDS version compared to the previous version. Supports change management and customer communication.']
  solubility_description varchar [note: 'Description of product solubility characteristics in various solvents (water, organic solvents). Guides formulation and application decisions.']
  specific_gravity decimal(6,4) [note: 'Ratio of product density to water density at specified temperature. Used for volume-to-weight conversions and quality control.']
  storage_conditions varchar [note: 'Recommended storage conditions including temperature range, humidity control, and container requirements to maintain product quality and safety.']
  tds_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the TDS document. Controls visibility and usage in customer-facing channels.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this TDS document. Incremented with each revision to track document evolution and ensure customers reference the correct edition.']
  viscosity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the viscosity value. Common units include centipoise (cP), centistokes (cSt), and Pascal-seconds.']
  viscosity_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured viscosity of the product indicating flow resistance. Critical for processing, pumping, and application equipment selection.']

  Note: 'Technical Data Sheet (TDS) document record for a chemical product, capturing the authoritative technical and application information published to customers and distributors. Stores TDS document number, version, effective date, language, product applications, physical and chemical properties summary, handling and storage guidance, recommended use concentrations, compatibility notes, performance data references, and document URL/path. Versioned and linked to the parent chemical_product. Supports customer technical sales and regulatory submissions.'
}

Table product.sds {
  sds_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Safety Data Sheet record. Primary key for the SDS master data.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the chemical product for which this SDS is issued. Links to the product master catalog.']
  source_sds_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.sds_record. Business justification: Product SDS authoring in chemical manufacturing requires reference to raw material SDS records for hazard data (toxicology, ecological info, physical-chemical properties). SDS authors explicitly cross']
  accidental_release_measures varchar [note: 'Personal precautions, protective equipment, emergency procedures, and methods for containment and cleanup of spills or leaks, as per GHS Section 6.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this SDS was formally approved for distribution and use.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or authority who approved this SDS for release.']
  boiling_point_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Temperature at which the substance transitions from liquid to gas at standard atmospheric pressure, measured in degrees Celsius.']
  california_prop65_listed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the chemical is listed under California Proposition 65 as known to cause cancer, birth defects, or reproductive harm.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'CAS Registry Number uniquely identifying the chemical substance. Format: XXXXXX-XX-X. May be a primary CAS or mixture identifier.']
  color varchar [note: 'Visual color or appearance of the chemical product.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the jurisdiction to which this SDS applies (e.g., USA, DEU, GBR).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SDS record was first created in the system.']
  disposal_considerations varchar [note: 'Recommended methods for safe disposal of the substance and contaminated packaging, including waste treatment methods and applicable regulations, as per GHS Section 13.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique document number for the SDS, used for regulatory filing and customer reference. Format: SDS-XXXXXX.']
  document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SDS document: Draft (under preparation), Active (current official version), Superseded (replaced by newer version), Archived (historical record).']
  document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the full PDF or electronic version of the SDS document stored in the document management system.']
  dot_hazard_class varchar [note: 'US DOT hazard class for transportation of dangerous goods (e.g., Class 3 Flammable Liquid, Class 8 Corrosive).']
  dot_packing_group varchar [note: 'DOT packing group indicating the degree of danger: I (high danger), II (medium danger), III (low danger).']
  dot_un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN number assigned to the substance for international transport of dangerous goods. Format: UNXXXX.']
  ec_number varchar [note: 'EC Number (EINECS, ELINCS, or NLP) assigned by the European Chemicals Agency for substance identification under REACH. Format: XXX-XXX-X.']
  ecological_information varchar [note: 'Environmental impact data including aquatic toxicity (LC50, EC50), persistence and degradability, bioaccumulative potential, and mobility in soil, as per GHS Section 12.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this SDS revision becomes the official version for regulatory and operational use.']
  exposure_limits varchar [note: 'Occupational exposure limits including OSHA PEL (Permissible Exposure Limit), ACGIH TLV (Threshold Limit Value), and other applicable exposure standards, as per GHS Section 8.']
  firefighting_measures varchar [note: 'Suitable extinguishing media, unsuitable extinguishing media, and special hazards arising from the substance or mixture during fire, as per GHS Section 5.']
  first_aid_measures varchar [note: 'Summary of first aid instructions for exposure routes (inhalation, skin contact, eye contact, ingestion) as per GHS Section 4.']
  flash_point_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Lowest temperature at which vapors of the substance will ignite when exposed to an ignition source, measured in degrees Celsius. Critical for flammability classification.']
  ghs_classification varchar [note: 'Complete GHS hazard classification string for the chemical product, including hazard classes and categories (e.g., Flam. Liq. 2, Acute Tox. 3, Skin Corr. 1B).']
  handling_precautions varchar [note: 'Safe handling advice including measures to prevent fire, explosion, and exposure, as per GHS Section 7.']
  hazard_statements varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of GHS hazard statement codes (H-codes) describing the nature of the hazards (e.g., H225, H302, H314, H400).']
  iata_shipping_name varchar [note: 'Proper shipping name for air transport as per IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations.']
  imdg_shipping_name varchar [note: 'Proper shipping name for sea transport as per IMDG Code.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the language in which the SDS is written (e.g., EN, DE, FR, ES).']
  melting_point_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Temperature at which the substance transitions from solid to liquid at standard atmospheric pressure, measured in degrees Celsius.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SDS record was last modified in the system.']
  odor varchar [note: 'Characteristic odor of the chemical product (e.g., pungent, sweet, odorless).']
  ph_value decimal(4,2) [note: 'pH value of the chemical product or its aqueous solution, indicating acidity or alkalinity. Range: 0.00 to 14.00.']
  physical_state varchar [note: 'Physical form of the chemical product at ambient conditions (e.g., solid, liquid, gas, powder).']
  pictogram_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of GHS pictogram codes representing hazard symbols (e.g., GHS02 Flame, GHS05 Corrosion, GHS07 Exclamation Mark, GHS09 Environment).']
  ppe_requirements varchar [note: 'Recommended personal protective equipment for handling the substance, including respiratory protection, hand protection, eye protection, and skin protection, as per GHS Section 8.']
  precautionary_statements varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of GHS precautionary statement codes (P-codes) describing recommended measures to minimize or prevent adverse effects (e.g., P210, P280, P305+P351+P338).']
  prepared_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or department responsible for preparing or updating this SDS.']
  reach_registration_number varchar [note: 'REACH registration number issued by ECHA for substances manufactured or imported in the EU in quantities of 1 tonne or more per year.']
  regulatory_information varchar [note: 'Additional regulatory information including national and regional regulations (TSCA, REACH, DSL, ENCS, IECSC, KECL, PICCS, AICS) and safety, health, and environmental regulations, as per GHS Section 15.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date when this revision of the SDS was issued or last updated. Critical for regulatory compliance and version control.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number of the SDS document. Increments with each update to the safety data sheet.']
  sara_313_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the chemical is subject to SARA Title III Section 313 Toxics Release Inventory (TRI) reporting requirements.']
  signal_word varchar [note: 'GHS signal word indicating the severity level of the hazard: Danger (more severe) or Warning (less severe).']
  solubility_in_water varchar [note: 'Description of the substances solubility in water (e.g., soluble, insoluble, partially soluble, miscible) and quantitative data if available.']
  specific_gravity decimal(6,4) [note: 'Ratio of the density of the substance to the density of water at a specified temperature. Dimensionless value.']
  storage_requirements varchar [note: 'Conditions for safe storage including incompatibilities, temperature limits, ventilation, and container specifications, as per GHS Section 7.']
  supersedes_date date [note: 'Date of the previous SDS version that this revision replaces. Used for audit trail and version history.']
  toxicological_information varchar [note: 'Summary of toxicological data including acute toxicity (LD50, LC50), skin corrosion/irritation, eye damage/irritation, sensitization, mutagenicity, carcinogenicity, reproductive toxicity, and target organ effects, as per GHS Section 11.']
  tsca_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory status of the chemical substance under the US EPA TSCA Inventory. Indicates whether the substance is listed, exempt, or requires notification.']
  vapor_pressure_mmhg decimal(10,4) [note: 'Pressure exerted by the vapor of the substance in equilibrium with its liquid or solid form at a given temperature, measured in millimeters of mercury (mmHg).']
  voc_content_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage by weight of volatile organic compounds in the product. Critical for environmental and air quality regulations.']

  Note: 'Safety Data Sheet (SDS/MSDS) master record for a chemical product, compliant with GHS/OSHA HazCom 2012 and REACH Article 31. Captures SDS document number, revision number, revision date, GHS hazard classification, signal word (Danger/Warning), hazard statements (H-codes), precautionary statements (P-codes), pictogram codes, first aid measures, firefighting measures, accidental release measures, handling and storage requirements, exposure controls/PPE, physical and chemical properties, toxicological information, ecological information, disposal considerations, transport information (DOT, IATA, IMDG), regulatory information (TSCA, REACH, SARA 313), and SDS language/country. System of record: Intelex EHS Management.'
}

Table product.product_ghs_classification {
  product_ghs_classification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the GHS classification record.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the chemical product being classified under GHS.']
  rawmaterial_ghs_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.rawmaterial_ghs_classification. Business justification: GHS/CLP mixture classification rules require product hazard classifications to be derived from and traceable to component raw material GHS classifications. EHS and regulatory teams use this link for S']
  sds_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this classification to the corresponding Safety Data Sheet document in the document management system.']
  superseded_by_classification_product_ghs_classification_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer GHS classification record that supersedes this one, enabling classification version history tracking.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'CAS Registry Number uniquely identifying the chemical substance (e.g., 67-64-1 for acetone).']
  classification_authority varchar [note: 'Entity responsible for the classification: self-classification by manufacturer, ECHA harmonized classification, competent authority, or third-party assessor.']
  classification_basis varchar [note: 'Methodology or data source used to determine the classification: experimental test data, read-across from analogous substances, QSAR (Quantitative Structure-Activity Relationship) modeling, expert judgment, literature review, or bridging principles.']
  classification_date date [note: 'Date when the GHS classification was performed or last updated.']
  classification_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context, rationale, or special considerations for the classification decision.']
  classification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the GHS classification record.']
  classification_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the GHS classification (e.g., GHS Rev 7, GHS Rev 8) indicating which revision of the Globally Harmonized System was applied.']
  classifier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or team responsible for performing the GHS classification.']
  clp_notification_number varchar [note: 'Notification number assigned by ECHA for substances notified under the CLP Regulation.']
  cmr_classification varchar [note: 'Specific CMR classification categories if applicable (e.g., Carc. 1A, Muta. 1B, Repr. 2), indicating carcinogenic, mutagenic, or reproductive toxicity hazards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this GHS classification record was first created in the system.']
  ec_number varchar [note: 'European Community number (EINECS, ELINCS, or NLP number) identifying the substance in the EU.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this GHS classification becomes effective and must be used for labeling and SDS purposes.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'Date until which this GHS classification remains valid; nullable for current active classifications.']
  environmental_hazard_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of environmental hazard categories corresponding to the classes (e.g., Acute 1, Chronic 2, Chronic 3).']
  environmental_hazard_classes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of environmental hazard classes assigned (e.g., Hazardous to the Aquatic Environment, Hazardous to the Ozone Layer).']
  hazard_statement_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of GHS hazard statement codes (e.g., H225, H302, H315, H319, H411) describing the nature and severity of hazards.']
  health_hazard_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of health hazard categories corresponding to the classes (e.g., Category 1, Category 2, Category 1A, Category 1B).']
  health_hazard_classes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of health hazard classes assigned (e.g., Acute Toxicity, Skin Corrosion, Carcinogenicity, Reproductive Toxicity, Respiratory Sensitization).']
  label_generation_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a GHS-compliant label must be generated for this classification.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this GHS classification record was last modified.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of the GHS classification to ensure ongoing compliance.']
  physical_hazard_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of physical hazard categories corresponding to the classes (e.g., Category 2, Category 1A, Category 3).']
  physical_hazard_classes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of physical hazard classes assigned (e.g., Flammable Liquids, Explosives, Oxidizing Liquids, Corrosive to Metals).']
  pictogram_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of GHS pictogram codes (e.g., GHS01, GHS02, GHS05, GHS07, GHS09) representing the hazard symbols required on labels.']
  precautionary_statement_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of GHS precautionary statement codes (e.g., P210, P280, P301+P312, P501) describing recommended measures to minimize or prevent adverse effects.']
  reach_registration_number varchar [note: 'REACH registration number if the substance is registered under REACH regulation, linking the classification to the REACH dossier.']
  regulatory_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or jurisdiction for which this classification applies (e.g., EU, USA, Canada, China, Australia), as GHS implementation may vary by region.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the GHS classification was last reviewed for accuracy and regulatory compliance.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or team who reviewed and approved the GHS classification.']
  signal_word varchar [note: 'GHS signal word indicating the relative level of severity of hazard: Danger (more severe), Warning (less severe), or None.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system from which this classification record originated (e.g., Intelex EHS, SAP EHS, LIMS).']
  test_data_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific test reports, study numbers, or LIMS records that support the classification (e.g., study IDs, OECD guideline numbers).']
  tsca_inventory_status varchar [note: 'Status of the substance on the TSCA Chemical Substance Inventory: active (commercial use), inactive (no current commercial use), or not listed.']

  Note: 'GHS (Globally Harmonized System) hazard classification record for a chemical product, capturing the official classification outcome per GHS/CLP regulation. Stores classification version, classification date, classifying authority (self-classification, ECHA harmonized), physical hazard classes and categories (flammable liquid Cat 2, explosive, oxidizer), health hazard classes (acute toxicity, skin corrosion, CMR categories), environmental hazard classes (aquatic toxicity), hazard statement codes, precautionary statement codes, signal word, pictogram list, and classification basis (test data, read-across, QSAR). Supports REACH, CLP, and GHS label generation. Linked to Intelex EHS.'
}

Table product.regulatory_status {
  regulatory_status_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the regulatory compliance status record.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the chemical product subject to this regulatory status record.']
  material_regulatory_status_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_regulatory_status. Business justification: Product regulatory compliance status (TSCA, REACH, jurisdiction) is directly informed by constituent raw material regulatory status. Regulatory affairs teams link product compliance records to raw mat']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory authority officially approved or accepted the registration or notification.']
  authorization_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the granted authorization expires and renewal application is required.']
  authorization_number varchar [note: 'Authorization number granted by the regulatory authority for continued use of a restricted or SVHC substance under specific conditions.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'Unique numerical identifier assigned by the Chemical Abstracts Service to every chemical substance described in the open scientific literature. Format: XXXXXX-XX-X.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes, clarifications, or context regarding this regulatory status record, including any special conditions or pending actions.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of the product under the specified regulation and jurisdiction. Registered (fully compliant and registered), Notified (notification submitted), Exempt (exemption granted), Restricted (use restrictions apply), Prohibited (banned), Pending (application in progress).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory status record was first created in the system.']
  ec_number varchar [note: 'Seven-digit identifier assigned to chemical substances for regulatory purposes within the European Union. Format: XXX-XXX-X.']
  ghs_classification varchar [note: 'GHS hazard classification codes assigned to this substance (e.g., H302, H315, H319). Multiple codes may be listed separated by commas.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic jurisdiction or regulatory region where this compliance status applies. USA (United States), EUR (European Union), CHN (China), JPN (Japan), KOR (South Korea), CAN (Canada).']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when this regulatory status record was last reviewed and verified for accuracy and currency.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory status record was last modified or updated.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this regulatory status record to ensure ongoing compliance.']
  pfas_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product contains PFAS (per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances), subject to emerging regulatory restrictions.']
  pmn_number varchar [note: 'EPA-assigned number for a Pre-Manufacture Notice submitted for a new chemical substance not on the TSCA Inventory.']
  prop_65_listing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this substance is listed under California Proposition 65 as known to cause cancer, birth defects, or reproductive harm.']
  reach_registration_type varchar [note: 'Type of REACH registration dossier submitted. Full (standard registration), Intermediate (on-site or transported isolated intermediate), Transported Isolated (intermediate transported between sites), Not Applicable (non-REACH regulation).']
  registration_date date [note: 'Date when the product was officially registered or notified under the applicable regulatory framework.']
  registration_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current registration expires and renewal or re-notification is required. Nullable for regulations without expiry.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration or notification number issued by the regulatory authority for this product under the specified regulation.']
  regulation_type varchar [note: 'Type of regulatory framework applicable to this product. REACH (Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals), TSCA (Toxic Substances Control Act), SARA 313 (Superfund Amendments and Reauthorization Act Section 313), Prop 65 (California Proposition 65), RoHS (Restriction of Hazardous Substances), SVHC (Substance of Very High Concern).']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the government agency or regulatory body that issued or oversees this compliance status (e.g., EPA, ECHA, METI, MEE).']
  responsible_party_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the responsible party for regulatory compliance inquiries and notifications.']
  responsible_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or organizational unit responsible for maintaining this regulatory status record and ensuring ongoing compliance.']
  restriction_conditions varchar [note: 'Detailed description of any restrictions, limitations, or special conditions imposed on the manufacture, import, use, or sale of this product under the applicable regulation.']
  rohs_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product complies with RoHS Directive restrictions on hazardous substances in electrical and electronic equipment.']
  sara_313_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this substance is subject to SARA Section 313 Toxics Release Inventory (TRI) reporting requirements.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date when the registration, notification, or compliance dossier was submitted to the regulatory authority.']
  substance_role varchar [note: 'Role of the organization in the supply chain for this substance under the regulatory framework. Manufacturer (produces the substance), Importer (imports into jurisdiction), Only Representative (non-EU manufacturer representative), Downstream User (uses the substance in formulations).']
  svhc_candidate_list_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this substance is included on the ECHA Candidate List of Substances of Very High Concern for potential authorization under REACH.']
  tonnage_band varchar [note: 'Annual production or import volume band for REACH registration purposes, measured in metric tonnes per year. Categories: 1-10 tonnes, 10-100 tonnes, 100-1000 tonnes, over 1000 tonnes, or not applicable for non-REACH regulations.']
  tsca_inventory_status varchar [note: 'Status of the substance on the EPA TSCA Chemical Substance Inventory. Active (commercially active), Inactive (not currently manufactured or imported), Not on Inventory (requires PMN), Not Applicable (non-US regulation).']

  Note: 'Regulatory compliance status record for a chemical product across applicable regulatory frameworks and jurisdictions. Captures regulation type (REACH, TSCA, SARA 313, California Prop 65, RoHS, SVHC, PFAS), jurisdiction (EU, US, China, Japan, Korea, Canada), registration or notification number, registration date, registration expiry, tonnage band (for REACH), substance role (manufacturer, importer, only representative), compliance status (registered, notified, exempt, restricted, prohibited), restriction conditions, and last review date. Enables regulatory reporting to EPA, ECHA, and ACC Responsible Care. System of record: Intelex EHS Management.'
}

Table product.hierarchy {
  hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product hierarchy node. Primary key for the product hierarchy entity.']
  parent_node_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent node in the hierarchy structure. Null for top-level division nodes. Enables recursive hierarchy traversal and roll-up aggregations for EBITDA reporting and sales analytics.']
  application_category varchar [note: 'Primary end-use application or industry segment for products in this node. Examples: Automotive Coatings, Construction Materials, Electronics Manufacturing, Consumer Goods, Agriculture Crop Protection. Supports market segmentation and application-based analytics.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval status for hierarchy changes. Draft: under construction. Pending Approval: submitted for review. Approved: active and released. Rejected: change request denied. Supports Management of Change (MOC) processes.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who approved this hierarchy node or hierarchy change. Used for accountability and compliance with MOC procedures.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hierarchy node or change was approved. Used for audit trail and MOC compliance reporting.']
  business_unit_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the business unit responsible for this product hierarchy node. Used for P&L reporting, EBITDA analysis by business unit, and organizational accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hierarchy node record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA distribution channel code for products in this node. Examples: 10=Direct Sales, 20=Distributor, 30=E-Commerce. Supports channel-specific pricing and analytics.']
  division_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the top-level division. Examples: PERF (Performance Materials), AGRI (Agricultural Solutions), SPEC (Specialty Chemicals). Used for executive-level portfolio reporting.']
  ebitda_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hierarchy node is used for EBITDA reporting and financial performance tracking. True for nodes that represent P&L responsibility centers.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this hierarchy node is no longer active for new product assignments. Null for open-ended nodes. Historical products retain their classification for reporting continuity.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this hierarchy node becomes active and available for product classification. Supports time-based hierarchy changes for organizational restructuring and portfolio realignment.']
  external_hierarchy_code varchar [note: 'External system or customer-facing hierarchy code used for EDI transactions, customer portals, or third-party integrations. May differ from internal SAP codes.']
  ghs_hazard_category varchar [note: 'Predominant GHS hazard classification for products in this hierarchy node. Used for safety reporting, hazmat storage planning, and EHS compliance. Examples: Flammable Liquids Category 2, Acute Toxicity Category 3, Corrosive to Metals.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level of the node within the hierarchy structure. Typically: 1=Division, 2=Business Unit, 3=Product Family, 4=Product Line, 5=Product Group. Supports drill-down analytics and aggregation logic.']
  hierarchy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the hierarchy purpose. Commercial: sales and EBITDA reporting. Technical: product formulation and R&D. Regulatory: TSCA, REACH, GHS grouping. Application: end-use industry segmentation. Geographic: regional product portfolio management.']
  is_leaf_node boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this node is a leaf node (has no children) in the hierarchy. True for the lowest level nodes where individual SKUs are assigned. Used for validation and hierarchy integrity checks.']
  legacy_hierarchy_code varchar [note: 'Historical product hierarchy code from legacy ERP systems prior to SAP S/4HANA implementation. Retained for data lineage, historical reporting, and migration traceability.']
  level_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the hierarchy level. Standard values: division, business_unit, product_family, product_line, product_group, sub_group. Used for reporting labels and user interface display.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this hierarchy node record. Used for change tracking and data governance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hierarchy node record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization.']
  node_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the hierarchy node. Used in SAP S/4HANA product hierarchy configuration and external reporting systems. Alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the node within the hierarchy structure.']
  node_depth varchar [note: 'Calculated depth of the node from the root of the hierarchy tree. Root nodes have depth 0. Used for hierarchy traversal algorithms and reporting level filtering.']
  node_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the hierarchy node including scope, product categories included, and business purpose. Provides context for classification decisions and portfolio management.']
  node_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the hierarchy node. Used for display in reports, dashboards, and user interfaces. Examples: Performance Materials, Agricultural Solutions, Specialty Chemicals, Industrial Polymers.']
  node_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the hierarchy node. Active: in use for classification and reporting. Inactive: temporarily disabled. Pending: awaiting approval. Deprecated: scheduled for retirement. Archived: historical reference only.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments about the hierarchy node. Used for documenting classification decisions, special handling instructions, or historical context.']
  path varchar [note: 'Full path from root to this node using node codes separated by forward slashes. Example: DIV01/BU05/FAM12/LINE34. Enables fast ancestor/descendant queries and breadcrumb navigation.']
  product_family_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the product family within the business unit. Groups related product lines with similar chemistry, applications, or market segments.']
  product_line_manager varchar [note: 'Name of the product line manager or business owner responsible for this hierarchy node. Used for accountability, approval workflows, and organizational reporting.']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory status category for products in this hierarchy node. Used for TSCA submissions, REACH compliance reporting, and regulatory grouping. Supports filtering for regulatory audits and compliance analytics.']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA sales organization code responsible for products in this hierarchy node. Used for sales analytics, commission calculations, and regional performance reporting.']
  sap_product_hierarchy_code varchar [note: '18-character SAP S/4HANA product hierarchy code (PRODH field). Concatenated representation of the full hierarchy path used in SAP MM and SD modules for material grouping and sales reporting.']
  sort_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric value controlling the display order of sibling nodes within the same parent. Lower values appear first. Used for consistent presentation in reports and user interfaces.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this hierarchy node record in SAP S/4HANA. Used for audit trail and data governance.']

  Note: 'Multi-level product classification hierarchy for commercial reporting, portfolio management, and SAP material grouping. Captures hierarchy node code, node name, hierarchy level (division > business unit > product family > product line > product group), parent node reference, hierarchy type (commercial, technical, regulatory), effective date, and node status. Supports EBITDA reporting by product line, sales analytics by business unit, regulatory grouping for TSCA/REACH submissions, and product application categorization. Managed in SAP S/4HANA product hierarchy configuration.'
}

Table product.packaging_config {
  packaging_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the packaging configuration record. Primary key.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the chemical product or SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) that this packaging configuration applies to.']
  filling_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.equipment. Business justification: Packaging configurations (drum fill, IBC, bulk tanker) are tied to specific filling/packaging equipment lines. In chemical manufacturing, equipment constraints determine which packaging configs can ru']
  maintenance_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.plant. Business justification: Packaging configurations are plant-specific — a 55-gallon drum fill line or bulk loading station exists only at certain plants. This drives order routing, production scheduling, and ensures hazmat-cer']
  capacity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the nominal capacity. Common units include L (liters), GAL (gallons), M3 (cubic meters), KG (kilograms), LB (pounds).']
  closure_type varchar [note: 'Type of closure or sealing mechanism used on the packaging container (e.g., screw cap, bung, valve, crimp seal, tamper-evident seal).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this packaging configuration record was first created in the system.']
  customer_specific_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this packaging configuration is designed for a specific customer or customer segment with unique packaging requirements.']
  dot_label_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether DOT hazardous materials transportation labels are required on this packaging configuration.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this packaging configuration is no longer effective and should not be used for new orders. Null indicates no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this packaging configuration becomes effective and available for use in order fulfillment and warehouse operations.']
  fill_volume_liters decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum fill volume of the chemical product in liters that can be loaded into this packaging configuration.']
  fill_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum fill weight of the chemical product in kilograms that can be loaded into this packaging configuration.']
  food_grade_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this packaging configuration is certified for food-contact chemicals and meets FDA (Food and Drug Administration) food-grade requirements.']
  ghs_label_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GHS hazard communication labels are required on this packaging configuration.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight in kilograms of the packaging configuration when filled, including product, container, and closure. Used for transportation and logistics planning.']
  hazmat_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this packaging configuration is certified for transporting hazardous materials under DOT, IATA, and IMDG regulations.']
  height_mm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Height of the packaging container in millimeters. Used for warehouse slotting, stacking calculations, and transportation planning.']
  iata_label_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether IATA dangerous goods air transport labels are required on this packaging configuration.']
  imdg_label_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether IMDG Code maritime transport labels are required on this packaging configuration.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this packaging configuration record was last modified or updated.']
  length_mm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Length of rectangular packaging (IBCs, totes, boxes) in millimeters. Used for warehouse slotting and transportation planning.']
  material_of_construction varchar [note: 'Material used to construct the packaging container. Options include HDPE (High-Density Polyethylene), steel, stainless steel, fiber, aluminum, and lined containers.']
  nominal_capacity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Nominal or rated capacity of the packaging container, expressed in the unit specified by capacity_unit_of_measure.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional information about the packaging configuration, including special handling instructions, compatibility notes, or regulatory remarks.']
  outer_diameter_mm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Outer diameter of cylindrical packaging (drums, cylinders, pails) in millimeters. Used for warehouse slotting and space planning.']
  packaging_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the packaging configuration, used in SAP EWM (Extended Warehouse Management) and order fulfillment systems.']
  packaging_config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the packaging configuration. Active configurations are available for order fulfillment; inactive/obsolete are phased out; pending approval are under review; restricted require special authorization.']
  packaging_group varchar [note: 'UN Packaging Group classification indicating the degree of danger: I (high danger), II (medium danger), III (low danger). Determines packaging performance requirements.']
  packaging_type varchar [note: 'Type of packaging container used for the chemical product. Includes drum, IBC (Intermediate Bulk Container), bulk tanker, bag, cylinder, pail, tote, flexitank, and isotank. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: drum|ibc|bulk_tanker|bag|cylinder|pail|tote|flexitank|isotank — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pallet_type varchar [note: 'Type of pallet used for this packaging configuration (e.g., EUR pallet, standard 48x40, block pallet, chemical-resistant pallet).']
  pharmaceutical_grade_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this packaging configuration meets pharmaceutical-grade standards for API (Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient) and pharmaceutical chemical packaging.']
  preferred_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the preferred or default packaging configuration for the associated product SKU (Stock Keeping Unit).']
  region_restriction varchar [note: 'Geographic regions where this packaging configuration is approved or restricted for use, based on local regulatory requirements (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX, EUR, CHN). Pipe-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes.']
  returnable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this packaging configuration is returnable (e.g., returnable drums, IBCs, cylinders) requiring deposit tracking and reverse logistics.']
  stack_height_max varchar [note: 'Maximum number of pallet layers that can be safely stacked vertically in warehouse storage, considering packaging strength and product stability.']
  tare_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Weight of the empty packaging container in kilograms, excluding product. Used for net weight calculations and transportation compliance.']
  un_packaging_code varchar [note: 'United Nations packaging certification code indicating the packaging meets UN performance standards for hazardous materials transport. Examples: 4G (fiberboard box), 1A1 (steel drum), 1H1 (plastic drum), 31HA1 (IBC composite).']
  units_per_pallet varchar [note: 'Number of packaging units (drums, IBCs, bags, etc.) that can be loaded on a single pallet for warehouse storage and transportation.']
  width_mm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Width of rectangular packaging (IBCs, totes, boxes) in millimeters. Used for warehouse slotting and transportation planning.']

  Note: 'Packaging configuration master defining all approved packaging formats for chemical product SKUs. Captures packaging type (drum, IBC, bulk tanker, bag, cylinder, pail, tote, flexitank, isotank), material of construction (HDPE, steel, stainless steel, fiber, aluminum, lined), nominal capacity, fill weight, fill volume, tare weight, closure type, UN packaging certification code (4G, 1A1, 1H1, 31HA1), packaging group (I, II, III), label requirements (GHS, DOT, IATA), pallet configuration (units per pallet, pallet type, stack height), and packaging status. Supports DOT/IATA/IMDG hazmat packaging compliance, warehouse slotting in SAP EWM, and customer order fulfillment.'
}

  lot_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.lot_record. Business justification: GMP batch traceability and recall management require linking each product composition record to the specific raw material lot consumed during manufacturing. Regulatory audits and recall investigations']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Required for BOM: links each product composition entry to the specific raw material master record, enabling formulation, cost, and regulatory reporting.']
  material_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_specification. Business justification: Product formulation approval requires each raw material component to meet a specific material specification version. Change control and quality management processes track which specification was in ef']
  supplier_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.supplier_material. Business justification: Approved Supplier Lists (ASL) in chemical manufacturing require product compositions to specify not just the raw material but the approved supplier source. Change control processes and supplier qualif']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier providing the component.']
  component_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the component received regulatory approval.']
  component_concentration_range_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the concentration is expressed as a range.']
  component_concentration_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the component concentration.']
  component_concentration_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric concentration of the component.']
  component_ec_number varchar [note: 'EC number identifying the component within European regulatory lists.']
  component_expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the component is considered expired.']
  component_function varchar [note: 'Role of the component within the product formulation.']

  component_regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory status of the component under applicable chemical regulations.']
  component_trade_name varchar [note: 'Commercial trade name used for the component.']
  composition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the composition record.']
  confidentiality_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the component information is protected as confidential business information.']
  disclosure_threshold varchar [note: 'Regulatory threshold that triggers mandatory disclosure of the component.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the composition became effective for the product.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the composition was retired or superseded.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes about the composition.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the composition record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the composition record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the composition data.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the composition record.']

  Note: 'Chemical composition disclosure record for a product, capturing the ingredient-level breakdown required for SDS Section 3 (Composition/Information on Ingredients) and REACH substance identification. Stores component CAS number, IUPAC name, trade name, EC number, concentration (exact or range), concentration unit, function in mixture (active ingredient, solvent, stabilizer, surfactant, filler), classification of component, disclosure threshold (OSHA, REACH, SARA 313), and confidentiality flag (CBI — Confidential Business Information). Supports SDS authoring, REACH mixture notification, and TSCA reporting.'
}

Table product.reach_dossier {
  reach_dossier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each REACH registration dossier record.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Each REACH dossier pertains to a specific chemical product; linking enables traceability and reporting. No existing reverse FK, so direction dossier → product is appropriate.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: REACH registration costs (dossier preparation, SIEF membership, testing) are charged to regulatory affairs cost centers in chemical mfg. Required for REACH compliance budget tracking and cost allocati']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: REACH dossier preparation is managed as a discrete internal order project in chemical mfg, enabling budget control and actual cost settlement for each substance registration. Regulatory project cost t']
  reach_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.reach_registration. Business justification: ECHA REACH compliance requires product dossiers to reference the upstream substance registration of constituent raw materials. Regulatory affairs teams link product REACH dossiers to raw material REAC']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: REACH regulatory compliance requires identifying which vendor is the lead registrant or SIEF co-registrant for each substance dossier. Chemical manufacturers must track registrant identity for ECHA su']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the chemical substance assigned by the CAS registry.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes on compliance actions, exemptions, or special conditions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dossier record was first created in the system.']
  csr_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference or file identifier for the associated Chemical Safety Report document.']
  csr_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a full Chemical Safety Report is required for this substance.']
  dossier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the REACH dossier.']
  dossier_update_date date [note: 'Most recent date the dossier information was updated.']
  ec_number varchar [note: 'European Community identifier for the substance.']
  echa_submission_date date [note: 'Date the dossier was submitted to the ECHA portal.']
  echa_submission_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code for the submission package in the ECHA REACH-IT portal.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the REACH registration expires or must be renewed.']
  exposure_scenarios_count varchar [note: 'Total number of exposure scenarios defined within the dossier.']
  hazard_classification varchar [note: 'GHS hazard class assigned to the substance.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High‑level business category of the chemical product.']
  registrant_role varchar [note: 'Role of the registrant in the REACH dossier (lead registrant, member, or coordinator).']
  registration_date date [note: 'Date the REACH registration was initially submitted to ECHA.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official REACH registration number assigned by ECHA.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Overall regulatory status of the substance across applicable frameworks.']
  risk_assessment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a risk assessment has been completed for the dossier.']
  sief_membership varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the dossier is part of a Substance Information Exchange Forum (SIEF).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that originated the dossier record (e.g., Intelex EHS).']
  substance_iupac_name varchar [note: 'Systematic chemical name of the substance as defined by IUPAC.']
  tonnage_band varchar [note: 'Annual tonnage range for which the substance is registered.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the dossier record.']

  Note: 'REACH registration dossier record — the single source of truth (SSOT) for substance registration under EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, including the full Chemical Safety Report (CSR) and all exposure scenarios. Captures REACH registration number, substance identity (IUPAC name, CAS, EC number), registrant role (lead registrant, member), SIEF membership, tonnage band, registration date, dossier update date, ECHA submission reference, CSR requirement flag, and dossier status. Contains all exposure scenarios as child records: scenario title, use descriptor codes (SU, PC, PROC, AC, ERC), contributing scenario name, operational conditions (temperature, duration, frequency, amount used), risk management measures (PPE, ventilation, containment), DNEL values, PNEC values, risk characterization ratios (RCR), and scenario validity dates. Supports extended SDS (eSDS) generation for downstream users. Managed in Intelex EHS and submitted to ECHA REACH-IT. This entity is the sole owner of exposure scenario data — there is no separate exposure scenario entity.'
}

Table product.order {
  order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the sales order record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who placed the order.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: REQUIRED: Order Entry captures the buying contact who placed the order, supporting sales attribution, commission calculations, and contact‑order reporting.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a blanket or framework contract, if applicable.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for Cost Allocation Report: each sales order must be charged to an internal cost center for accurate expense tracking and financial reporting.']
  customer_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.customer_allocation. Business justification: During allocation periods in chemical manufacturing (supply constraints, force majeure, raw material shortages), product orders are placed against customer allocations. The order must reference the al']
  distributor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.distributor. Business justification: Links orders placed through a distributor; required for distributor performance and rebate calculations.']
  forecast_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.forecast_version. Business justification: Order Planning Audit uses the forecast version that drove demand planning for the sales order, required for regulatory reporting and demand accuracy analysis.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Project Accounting process requires associating a sales order with an internal order to track project‑related costs and budgets.']
  maintenance_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.plant. Business justification: Product orders are fulfilled from specific plants. The existing plain column \'plant_code\' is a denormalized plant reference. Proper FK to maintenance.plant enables capacity planning, regulatory compli']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Tracks Opportunity win to Order creation for pipeline‑to‑revenue analysis.']
  price_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.price_agreement. Business justification: Ensures each order references the price agreement applied, needed for pricing compliance audit.']
  primary_product_order_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the address used for invoicing.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Needed for Profitability Analysis: linking orders to profit centers enables revenue attribution and margin reporting per business unit.']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote. Business justification: Required for Quote‑to‑Order conversion report; orders are created from approved sales quotes.']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Assigns each order to a sales territory for regional sales reporting and quota tracking.']
  sds_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the attached Safety Data Sheet or regulatory compliance document.']
  shipping_address_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the destination address for delivery.']
  supply_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Back-to-back and make-to-order scenarios in chemical manufacturing require linking a customer sales order to the supply purchase order it triggered. Role-prefix \'supply_\' distinguishes this from line_']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the order record was first persisted.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code of the transaction currency.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount applied to the order.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the product is sold (e.g., wholesale, retail).']
  division varchar [note: 'Business division responsible for the product line.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert foreign currency to company reporting currency.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Responsibility for freight costs.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total order value before taxes, discounts, and freight.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order contains regulated hazardous substances.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms defining delivery responsibilities.']
  is_backorder boolean [note: 'True when any line items are pending due to stock shortage.']
  is_split boolean [note: 'True when the order is fulfilled in multiple shipments.']
  line_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct line items on the order.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount payable after taxes, discounts, and freight.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'External order number visible to customers and used in communications.']
  order_source varchar [note: 'System or channel through which the order was created.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the order.']
  order_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the order was entered into the system.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the order based on business process and fulfillment rules.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Credit terms agreed with the customer (e.g., NET30).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level used for scheduling and resource allocation.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the customer expects the order to be delivered.']
  sales_org varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for the sale.']
  sales_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region assigned to the sales organization.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation selected for delivery.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Customer‑provided notes for handling, packaging, or compliance.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status, e.g., reason for cancellation.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax calculated for the order.']
  total_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Aggregate volume of all line items in cubic meters.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Aggregate weight of all line items in kilograms.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the order record.']

  Note: 'Core master record for a customer sales order capturing the full order header: customer account, order type (standard, rush, blanket, consignment, return), requested delivery date, incoterms, payment terms, total order value, currency, plant assignment, sales organization, distribution channel, and division. Serves as the authoritative SSOT for all customer order commitments in the order-to-cash lifecycle. Links to quotation (source), blanket order (if call-off), and downstream confirmations, deliveries, and status events. Maps to SAP SD transaction VA01/VA02.'
}

Table product.line_item {
  line_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for line_item']
