  contract_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.contract_price. Business justification: Contract price compliance auditing and revenue recognition in chemical manufacturing require linking each order line item to the specific contract_price record governing its pricing. Auditors and comm']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Detailed Costing report allocates line‑item costs to specific cost centers for granular expense analysis.']
  cost_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_element. Business justification: Cost element classification on order line items is required in chemical mfg controlling to distinguish material costs, freight surcharges, and hazmat handling fees. Enables cost-element-level variance']
  customer_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.customer_allocation. Business justification: In chemical manufacturing during supply constraints, order line items are fulfilled against customer allocations. The line item must reference the allocation it consumes to track allocation utilizatio']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.equipment. Business justification: MES production scheduling assigns each order line to specific equipment for batch manufacturing, required for traceability and capacity planning.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.functional_location. Business justification: Production planning links each order line to the functional location (plant/area) where it will be produced, essential for allocation and compliance reporting.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: GL Posting process: each line item must map to a GL account for revenue recognition and financial statement generation.']
  grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grade. Business justification: line_item has a denormalized `grade` STRING field capturing the quality grade of the ordered product. The grade table is the authoritative record for quality grades and purity tiers. Adding grade_id F']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: In chemical mfg, campaign or project-based sales line items are settled against internal orders for cost tracking and budget consumption monitoring. Required for internal order settlement reporting an']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Chemical mfg sales order line items are fulfilled from specific lots for GMP batch traceability, CoA attachment (coa_required_flag already on line_item), and REACH/FDA regulatory compliance. Domain ex']
  maintenance_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.plant. Business justification: Line items have a plain \'plant\' column (denormalized plant code). Each line item is fulfilled from a specific plant, driving inventory allocation, hazmat compliance documentation, and shipping point d']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Required for Order Fulfillment to retrieve material specifications, hazard data, and compliance info for each line item.']
  opportunity_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity_product. Business justification: Links each order line to the opportunity product that generated it, supporting win‑loss and forecasting reports.']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent sales order to which this line belongs.']
  planned_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.planned_order. Business justification: Order line fulfillment tracking links each sales order line to the MRP‑generated planned production order that will satisfy it, essential for production scheduling and order status reporting.']
  po_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.po_line. Business justification: Back-to-back order processing in chemical manufacturing requires tracing each sales order line item to the specific purchase order line that fulfills it. This is essential for delivery commitment trac']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.production_schedule. Business justification: In chemical manufacturing, order line items are pegged to specific production schedule entries for ATP/CTP (available-to-promise/capable-to-promise) confirmation. This peg enables delivery date commit']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Product-line P&L reporting in chemical mfg requires each order line item to be attributed to a profit center (e.g., specialty chemicals vs. agrochemicals). Enables contribution margin analysis by prod']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Needed for procurement allocation process to map each sales order line to the PO that supplies the material.']
  quote_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote_line. Business justification: Maps order line items to their originating quote lines for margin and variance analysis.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: A line item represents a specific orderable SKU configuration. The sku table is the authoritative record for each orderable/shippable product configuration. line_item already has chemical_product_id f']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: Supports supplier allocation per line for sourcing decisions and vendor performance tracking.']
  batch_determination_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether batch allocation is required for this line (True) or not (False).']
  coa_required_flag boolean [note: 'True if a COA must be attached to the shipment for regulatory or customer reasons.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity confirmed by the supply chain after availability check.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the line record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the price fields.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Discount value applied to this line item.']
  ehs_hazard_statement varchar [note: 'Standard GHS hazard statement associated with the material.']
  ehs_precautionary_statement varchar [note: 'Standard GHS precautionary statement for safe handling.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the product batch is no longer sellable.']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'GHS hazard class for the product. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|2|3|4|5|6|7|8|9 — promote to reference product]']
  is_gmp_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the product is manufactured under Good Manufacturing Practice standards.']
  last_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the line record.']
  line_comment varchar [note: 'Free‑form comment entered by order entry personnel.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the order, starting at 1.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the line item.']
  line_total_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net price multiplied by confirmed quantity before tax.']
  material_gl_account_code varchar [note: 'GL account used for revenue recognition of this material.']
  material_sds_url varchar [note: 'Link to the electronic Safety Data Sheet for the material.']
  moq_enforced_flag boolean [note: 'True when the line must meet the customer\'s minimum order quantity requirement.']
  net_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net price per unit before taxes and discounts, in the order currency.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity requested by the customer, expressed in the unit of measure.']
  partial_delivery_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'True if the line can be split across multiple deliveries.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the line item meets all applicable chemical regulations (REACH, TSCA, EPA).']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the customer requested for delivery of this line item.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the product remains usable from the production date.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Warehouse or storage location identifier for inventory allocation.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Tax amount applicable to this line item.']
  temperature_control_flag boolean [note: 'True if the product must be stored or transported at a controlled temperature.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the ordered quantity (kilograms, liters, metric tons, drums).']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a customer sales order representing a specific chemical product SKU, grade, or formulation being ordered. Captures line number, material number (SAP), product description, ordered quantity, unit of measure (kg, L, MT, drums), confirmed quantity, net price, pricing conditions, requested delivery date per line, plant, storage location, batch determination flag, COA/COC requirement flag, and special handling instructions (e.g., hazmat class, temperature-controlled). Supports MOQ enforcement and partial delivery rules.'
}

Table product.order_confirmation {
  order_confirmation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the order confirmation record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who placed the order.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Order confirmations in chemical manufacturing must reference the specific lot shipped to support CoA/CoC document generation (coa_attached, coc_attached already on order_confirmation) and customer-fac']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original sales order that this confirmation relates to.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Confirmed orders in chemical mfg are attributed to profit centers for segment revenue reporting. Finance controllers require profit center on order confirmations to produce product-line revenue report']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the logistics carrier responsible for delivery.']
  coa_attached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Certificate of Analysis is attached to the confirmation.']
  coc_attached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Certificate of Compliance is attached to the confirmation.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'External confirmation number issued to the customer for this order confirmation.']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the confirmation was issued to the customer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the confirmation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  delivery_date_confirmed date [note: 'Delivery date confirmed by the supplier.']
  delivery_date_requested date [note: 'Delivery date originally requested by the customer.']
  dot_classification varchar [note: 'U.S. Department of Transportation classification for hazardous material transport. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: class_1|class_2|class_3|class_4|class_5|class_6|class_7|class_8|class_9 — promote to reference product]']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the product lot, if applicable.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Incoterm governing the shipment responsibilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|FOB|CFR|CIF|DAP|DDP — promote to reference product]']
  ghs_hazard_class varchar [note: 'GHS hazard classification for the product. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|2|3|4|5|6|7|8|9 — promote to reference product]']
  is_deviation boolean [note: 'True if the confirmation deviates from the original customer request (e.g., quantity, packaging).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any extra information or remarks.']
  order_confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the order confirmation.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Agreed payment terms for the order.']
  price_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Unit price agreed for the confirmed quantity.']
  quantity_confirmed decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of product confirmed for delivery.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates compliance status with applicable regulations (e.g., REACH, TSCA).']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Method selected for transporting the confirmed order.']
  tax_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Applicable tax amount for the confirmed order.']
  total_discount_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total discount applied to the order confirmation.']
  total_gross_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Gross monetary amount before discounts, taxes, and adjustments.']
  total_net_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Net amount payable after discounts and taxes.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier‑provided tracking identifier for the shipment.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the confirmed quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the confirmation record.']

  Note: 'Official order acknowledgment document sent to the customer confirming acceptance of a sales order. Captures confirmation number, confirmed order quantities, confirmed delivery dates, confirmed pricing, plant of supply, batch allocation status, COA/COC commitment, special handling confirmations (GHS hazard class, DOT classification), and any deviations from the original customer request. Serves as the contractual commitment record between Chemical Mfg and the customer.'
}

  order_confirmation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.order_confirmation. Business justification: In the order fulfillment lifecycle, an outbound delivery is created to execute the physical shipment after an order confirmation is issued. Linking outbound_delivery to order_confirmation enables trac']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales order that triggered this delivery.']
  sds_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sds. Business justification: Outbound deliveries of hazardous chemicals must include the current SDS for regulatory compliance (OSHA HazCom 2012, EU CLP). Chemical manufacturers attach SDS to delivery documents for customer recei']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer or party receiving the shipment.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Outbound delivery picking references a specific stock position; linking supports picking optimization and audit of shipped inventory.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date when the delivery was actually completed.']
  coa_attachment_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether a COA document is attached to the delivery.']
  coc_attachment_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether a COC document is attached to the delivery.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the outbound delivery record was first created in the system.']

  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for the delivery.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'United Nations identification number for hazardous material, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the outbound delivery record.']

  Note: 'Outbound delivery document created in SAP EWM/SD to execute the physical shipment of chemical products against a sales order. Captures delivery number, delivery date, ship-to party, shipping point, total weight, total volume, hazardous material indicator, UN number, DOT hazard class, packing instructions, batch numbers assigned, COA/COC attachment status, and goods issue posting date. Integrates with Oracle Transportation Management for carrier assignment and freight execution.'
}

Table product.status_event {
  status_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for status_event']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.line_item. Business justification: status_event is an immutable event log for order fulfillment milestones. While it already references product_order_id for order-level events, many fulfillment events (partial shipment, line cancellati']
  order_confirmation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.order_confirmation. Business justification: status_event captures every status transition in the sales order lifecycle. Order confirmation events (confirmation issued, confirmation revised, deviation flagged) are key milestones that should refe']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales order to which this status event belongs.']
  outbound_delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.outbound_delivery. Business justification: status_event tracks every fulfillment milestone. Delivery-level events (goods issued, in-transit, delivered, delivery exception) are key milestones that should reference the specific outbound_delivery']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Order status events are triggered by maintenance work orders — PSM-covered equipment breakdowns cause order delays, and work order completion triggers order resumption. Chemical manufacturers track th']
  actual_date date [note: 'Date on which the milestone was actually completed.']
  event_notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments, observations, or instructions associated with the status change.']
  event_source_reference varchar [note: 'Logical name of the system or process that generated the event.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the status change event occurred.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of the status event, e.g., order_created, credit_check_passed, production_scheduled, goods_issued, delivered, invoiced, cancelled.']
  is_manual boolean [note: 'True if the event was recorded by a user; false if generated automatically by a system.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'Order status after this event was applied.']
  planned_date date [note: 'Target date that was originally scheduled for this milestone.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'Order status before this event was applied.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the event record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the event record.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Business party or role accountable for the event execution.']
  triggering_system varchar [note: 'Name of the automated system or service that caused the status transition.']
  variance_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the actual date deviated from the planned date; positive indicates delay.']

  Note: 'Immutable event log capturing every status transition and fulfillment milestone in the sales order lifecycle from creation through delivery, invoicing, and closure. Records event timestamp, previous status, new status, milestone type (order created, order confirmed, credit check passed, production scheduled, batch released, quality released, goods issued, picked up, in transit, customs cleared, delivered, POD received, invoiced), planned date, actual date, variance in days, triggering user or system, event source (SAP SD, OTM, MES, WMS, manual), responsible party, and event notes. Supports full order lifecycle traceability, SLA monitoring, on-time delivery (OTD) KPI calculation, proactive customer communication, customer inquiry resolution, and regulatory audit trail for controlled substance shipment tracking.'
}

Table product.product_hold {
  product_hold_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for hold']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who placed the order.']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.line_item. Business justification: A product hold can be placed at the order header level (product_order_id already exists) or at the individual line item level for granular hold management. In chemical manufacturing, holds are often p']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Product holds in chemical manufacturing are frequently triggered by lot-level quality failures, contamination events, or regulatory issues (FDA, REACH). Linking product_hold to the specific lot enable']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales order to which this hold is applied.']
  safety_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.safety_incident. Business justification: Product holds in chemical manufacturing are frequently triggered by safety incidents (process safety events, contamination, exposure). Linking product_hold to safety_incident enables EHS-to-commercial']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Product holds in chemical manufacturing are triggered by quality investigations that generate maintenance work orders (equipment contamination, off-spec production). The hold is released when the work']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount associated with a credit or financial hold.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the hold record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the hold amount.']
  hold_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the hold, used in communications and reporting.']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the hold.']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Category of the hold, indicating the underlying business reason.']
  is_regulatory_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hold satisfies all applicable regulatory requirements.']
  placed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hold was initially placed on the order.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the hold for escalation handling.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the specific reason for the hold.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing details about why the hold was placed.']
  release_authorization varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or system that authorized the hold release.']
  release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hold was released or cleared.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Notes documenting actions taken to resolve or release the hold.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the hold record.']

  Note: 'Record of a hold placed on a sales order preventing further processing or shipment. Captures hold ID, hold type (credit hold, quality hold, regulatory hold, export compliance hold, customer dispute hold), hold reason code, hold placed by, hold placed timestamp, hold release timestamp, release authorization, and resolution notes. Supports credit management integration with SAP FI/CO and quality hold integration with QM module. Tracks order blockage duration for SLA and customer service reporting.'
}

Table product.returns_order {
  returns_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated surrogate key uniquely identifying the return order record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who initiated the return.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.case. Business justification: In chemical manufacturing, a returns order is typically created as a resolution action for a customer quality or complaint case. Linking returns_order to case enables CAPA closure tracking, credit mem']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Returns in chemical mfg trigger credit memo postings to returns/allowances GL accounts. Direct FK required for automated reversal journal entries and returns accrual reporting. Auditors expect returns']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.line_item. Business justification: A customer return (RMA) is typically initiated against a specific line item from the original sales order, not just the order header. returns_order already has product_order_id for the order header re']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Returns are processed per lot to manage quality quarantine and regulatory compliance; linking enables the Returns Quality Inspection workflow.']
  order_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the original sales order linked to this return.']
  outbound_delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.outbound_delivery. Business justification: A customer return (RMA) is typically initiated against a specific outbound delivery — the customer received goods via a specific delivery and is returning them. returns_order already has product_order']
  waste_disposal_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.waste_disposal_record. Business justification: Returned hazardous chemical products require RCRA-compliant waste disposal. The returns_order triggers a waste disposal workflow; linking to waste_disposal_record enables traceability from customer re']
  actual_return_date date [note: 'Date the returned goods were actually received.']
  coa_attached boolean [note: 'True if a Certificate of Analysis (COA) is attached to the return record.']
  coc_attached boolean [note: 'True if a Certificate of Compliance (COC) is attached to the return record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this return order record was first captured in the lakehouse.']
  credit_memo_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the return qualifies for a credit memo to the customer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter currency code for monetary values (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  disposal_instruction varchar [note: 'Business instruction for how the returned product should be handled after receipt.']
  expected_return_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the returned goods are expected to arrive at the plant.']
  inspection_result_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the outcome of the inspection.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the quality inspection performed on the returned product.']
  logistics_provider varchar [note: 'Name or code of the carrier responsible for transporting the return.']
  net_refund_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Refund amount after tax and any applicable deductions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or instructions related to the return.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or warehouse receiving the returned goods.']
  quality_issue_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of any quality problems discovered.']
  quality_issue_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a quality issue was identified during inspection.']
  refund_amount_gross decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the return before taxes and deductions.']
  return_condition varchar [note: 'Physical condition of the returned product.']
  return_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the return order was created or submitted by the customer.']
  return_order_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier assigned to the return order by the source system.']
  return_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of product being returned, expressed in the unit of measure.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing why the product is being returned.']
  returns_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the return order.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for the return shipment.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the refund.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier‑provided tracking identifier for the return shipment.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the return quantity (e.g., kg, L, pcs).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this return order record.']

  Note: 'Customer return order (RMA) capturing the authorization and processing of product returns from customers. Captures return order number, original sales order reference, return reason code (OOS, damaged, wrong product, excess inventory, quality complaint), return quantity, return condition, plant receiving the return, credit memo eligibility, disposal instructions (restock, destroy, rework), and return logistics details. Maps to SAP SD Returns Order (VA01 with order type RE) and reverse logistics workflow.'
}

Table product.delivery_line {
  delivery_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for this delivery line record.']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the sales order line item being fulfilled by this delivery.']
  outbound_delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the outbound delivery document that physically ships this line item.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date when this specific line item was actually delivered. In split deliveries, different line items on the same outbound delivery document may have different actual delivery dates due to multi-stop routes or partial unloading.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Specific production batch number assigned to this delivery line. In chemical manufacturing, batch traceability is regulatory-critical. A single order line may be fulfilled from multiple batches across multiple deliveries, making this a relationship-level attribute.']
  coa_attached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a COA document is attached for this specific delivery line and batch combination. COA attachment is per-batch and per-delivery, not per-order-line or per-delivery globally.']
  coc_attached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a COC document is attached for this specific delivery line and batch combination. COC attachment is per-batch and per-delivery, not per-order-line or per-delivery globally.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this delivery line record was created in the system, typically during delivery document creation or wave planning.']
  delivered_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Actual quantity of the line item shipped on this specific delivery. Critical for partial fulfillment tracking and cannot be stored on either parent table because one line item can have different quantities across multiple deliveries.']
  delivery_line_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this specific delivery line execution. Tracks the operational state of fulfilling this line item on this delivery, independent of the overall delivery status or order line status.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(18,3) [note: 'Gross weight including packaging for this delivery line in kilograms. Required for hazmat documentation and freight calculation at the line level.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this delivery line record, tracking changes during picking, packing, or shipment execution.']
  net_weight_kg decimal(18,3) [note: 'Net weight of the delivered quantity for this line in kilograms. Required for hazmat documentation and freight calculation at the line level.']
  number_of_packages varchar [note: 'Count of physical packages (drums, bags, totes) for this delivery line. Required for hazmat documentation and warehouse handling.']
  packing_type varchar [note: 'Type of packaging used for this delivery line (drums, IBC totes, bulk tanker, bags). Packing decisions are made at delivery execution time and may vary for the same line item across different deliveries.']
  temperature_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this specific delivery line experienced temperature excursions during transport. Critical for temperature-sensitive chemicals and belongs to the delivery execution of this specific line, not to the delivery or line item globally.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the delivered quantity on this delivery line (kg, L, MT, drums). May differ from the order line UOM due to conversion during fulfillment.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the physical execution of a sales order line item through an outbound delivery in chemical manufacturing. It captures the many-to-many relationship where a single outbound delivery can ship multiple order line items (consolidated shipment), and a single order line item can be split across multiple deliveries due to hazmat transport limits, tank capacity constraints, batch availability, or partial fulfillment. Each record links one outbound delivery to one line item with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific delivery execution: delivered quantity, batch numbers assigned, COA/COC attachment status per line, and temperature deviation tracking.. Existence Justification: In chemical manufacturing operations, the relationship between outbound deliveries and sales order line items is genuinely many-to-many. A single outbound delivery routinely consolidates multiple order line items from one or more sales orders into a single shipment (many line items to one delivery). Conversely, a single order line item is frequently split across multiple deliveries due to hazmat transport quantity limits, production batch availability, tank truck capacity constraints, or customer-requested partial fulfillment (one line item to many deliveries). This is the standard SAP SD/EWM delivery item concept.'
}

  inspection_lot_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection lot.']

Table quality.test_method {
  test_method_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each analytical test method.']
  safety_data_sheet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.safety_data_sheet. Business justification: Test methods for chemical analysis must reference SDS for safe handling of reagents, required PPE, and waste disposal procedures. ISO 17025 and GLP require documented safety procedures linked to test ']
  accuracy_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Typical accuracy of the method expressed as a percentage.']
  applicable_product_family varchar [note: 'Product families for which the method is valid.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the method received formal approval.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory or quality approval state of the method.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person or authority that approved the method.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test method record was created.']
  detection_limit varchar [note: 'Lowest concentration reliably measurable by the method (e.g., "0.01 mg/L").']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the method became officially usable.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the method is no longer valid (nullable).']
  instrument_type varchar [note: 'Primary analytical instrument required for the method.']
  is_gmp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the method complies with Good Manufacturing Practice.']
  is_iso17025_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the method meets ISO/IEC 17025 laboratory accreditation requirements.']
  method_code varchar [note: 'Business code uniquely identifying the test method.']
  method_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the analytical test method.']
  method_type varchar [note: 'Category of analytical technique used by the method.']
  precision_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Typical repeatability (precision) of the method expressed as a percentage.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision count for the method.']
  safety_considerations varchar [note: 'Safety notes and precautions associated with the method.']
  sample_preparation_steps varchar [note: 'Detailed procedural steps to prepare a sample before analysis.']
  stock_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Current inventory quantity of the reference standard.']
  test_method_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the test method.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the test method record.']
  validation_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person or group that performed the validation.']
  validation_date date [note: 'Date when the method was last validated against standards.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the method definition.']

  Note: 'Master record defining standardized analytical test methods and associated reference standards/reagents used in quality control and laboratory testing. Includes method code, method name, method type (titration, chromatography, spectroscopy, gravimetric, microbiological), applicable product families, instrument type, reagent requirements, sample preparation steps, applicable standard (ASTM, ISO, USP, internal), version number, effective date, and approval status. Embeds reference standard master data: standard ID, standard name, CAS number, standard type (primary, secondary, working, certified reference material), supplier, lot number, purity/concentration, certificate number, receipt date, expiry date, storage condition, current stock quantity, reorder level, and traceability chain (NIST, ECHA, pharmacopoeia). Single source of truth for analytical methodology and the certified reference materials that support measurement traceability per ISO/IEC 17025 and GMP requirements.'
}

Linked to inspection plans and drives qc_result recording.'
}

Table quality.inspection_plan {
  inspection_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inspection plan.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.chemical_product']

  grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grade. Business justification: Inspection plans in chemical manufacturing are grade-specific — pharmaceutical grades require tighter AQL, more frequent sampling, and additional test methods than technical grades. Grade-to-inspectio']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Inspection plans are created per material; the plan definition references the Material Master for specification lookup.']
  material_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_specification. Business justification: Inspection plans are built from material specifications — acceptance criteria (assay limits, pH range, moisture limits) come directly from material_specification. This link enables specification-drive']
  pm_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.pm_plan. Business justification: Integrated inspection planning in chemical manufacturing coordinates quality inspection plans with preventive maintenance plans for equipment-based inspections (pressure vessels, reactors, heat exchan']
  safety_data_sheet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.safety_data_sheet. Business justification: Inspection plans for chemical products must reference SDS to define safe handling procedures, required PPE, and hazard controls for the inspection process. GMP and PSM standards in chemical manufactur']
  sampling_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.sampling_plan. Business justification: An inspection plan defines which sampling plan to use for sample collection. inspection_plan currently has sample_size_code, sampling_scheme_type, and sampling_standard as denormalized STRING attribut']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: SKU-specific inspection plans cover packaging-related criteria (container integrity, fill weight, label verification) that vary by pack size and packaging type. Regulatory and customer audit requireme']
  test_method_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality.test_method']
  acceptance_number varchar [note: 'Maximum number of defectives allowed for acceptance.']
  aql decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable percentage of defective items.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) the plan must satisfy.']
  control_lcl decimal(12,4) [note: 'Lower statistical control limit for SPC.']
  control_ucl decimal(12,4) [note: 'Upper statistical control limit for SPC.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspection plan record was created.']
  decimal_precision varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places for numeric results.']
  dynamic_modification_rules varchar [note: 'Textual rules allowing plan modifications based on lot size or other triggers.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the inspection plan becomes valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the inspection plan expires (null if open‑ended).']
  gmp_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the characteristic is GMP‑critical.']
  inspection_level varchar [note: 'Inspection level code determining sample size per AQL.']
  inspection_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection plan.']
  inspection_stage varchar [note: 'Stage of production where the inspection occurs.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent plan review.']
  lot_size_max varchar [note: 'Largest lot size for which this sampling plan applies.']
  lot_size_min varchar [note: 'Smallest lot size for which this sampling plan applies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the inspection plan.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned to the inspection plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the plan based on its applicability (material, product, or process).']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version label of the inspection plan, allowing multiple revisions.']
  rejection_number varchar [note: 'Minimum number of defectives that triggers rejection.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the plan\'s review process.']
  spec_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the specification limits.']
  specification_lsl decimal(12,4) [note: 'Lower bound of the acceptable specification range.']
  specification_usl decimal(12,4) [note: 'Upper bound of the acceptable specification range.']
  target_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Desired target measurement for the characteristic.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the inspection plan.']
  usage_context varchar [note: 'Business context in which the plan is executed.']

  Note: 'Master quality inspection plan defining the complete template for material/product quality evaluation. Includes plan number, version, material/product reference, plant, usage context (goods receipt, in-process, final release), inspection stage, and the full set of inspection characteristics (parameter definitions with target values, specification limits LSL/USL, control limits LCL/UCL for SPC, characteristic types, test method references, decimal precision, GMP-critical flags). Embeds statistical sampling plan details: sampling scheme type (single, double, sequential), inspection level, AQL (Acceptable Quality Level), lot size range, sample size code letter, acceptance/rejection numbers, dynamic modification rules, and applicable sampling standard (ISO 2859, MIL-STD-1916). Serves as the single authoritative template driving inspection lot creation, sample size determination, and characteristic assignment in SAP QM.'
}

Table quality.sampling_plan {
  sampling_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the sampling plan.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Sampling plans in chemical manufacturing are defined for specific chemical products based on hazard class, regulatory requirements, and historical quality data. Product-level sampling plan assignment ']
  grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grade. Business justification: Sampling frequency and sample size differ by grade — pharmaceutical grades require tighter sampling than industrial grades. Grade-specific sampling plans are a GMP requirement in chemical manufacturin']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Sampling plans can be material‑specific to meet regulatory sampling frequency; linking provides direct material context.']
  safety_data_sheet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.safety_data_sheet. Business justification: Sampling plans for hazardous chemicals must reference SDS to define safe sampling procedures, required PPE, and containment requirements. OSHA HazCom and GMP standards in chemical manufacturing requir']
  acceptance_number varchar [note: 'Maximum number of defective items allowed for the lot to be accepted.']
  applicable_material_class varchar [note: 'Material class to which the sampling plan applies.']
  applicable_process_stage varchar [note: 'Manufacturing process stage where the plan is used.']
  aql_unit varchar [note: 'Unit for the AQL value (typically percent).']
  aql_value decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric AQL value defining the acceptable defect rate.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sampling plan record was created.']
  dynamic_modification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sampling plan can be modified dynamically during execution.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the sampling plan expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the sampling plan becomes effective.']
  inspection_level varchar [note: 'Inspection level code defining the sampling intensity as per ISO 2859.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the sampling plan was last reviewed for relevance.']
  lot_size_max varchar [note: 'Maximum lot size (in units) for which this plan is applicable.']
  lot_size_min varchar [note: 'Minimum lot size (in units) for which this plan is applicable.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next review of the sampling plan.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the sampling plan.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the sampling plan in operational systems.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the sampling plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the plan based on the sampling scheme (single, double, or sequential).']
  regulatory_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard governing the sampling plan (e.g., ISO 2859, MIL‑STD‑1916).']
  rejection_number varchar [note: 'Minimum number of defective items that cause the lot to be rejected.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of units to be sampled from each lot.']
  sample_size_code varchar [note: 'Letter code representing the sample size according to the sampling table.']
  sampling_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which sampling is performed.']
  sampling_method varchar [note: 'Statistical method used to select samples (e.g., random, stratified).']
  sampling_plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the purpose, scope, and application of the plan.']
  sampling_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sampling plan.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the sampling plan.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the sampling plan for change control.']

  Note: 'Statistical sampling plan defining sample size, sampling frequency, acceptance/rejection criteria, and AQL (Acceptable Quality Level) for a given inspection stage and material class. Captures sampling scheme type (single, double, sequential), inspection level, lot size range, sample size code letter, acceptance number, rejection number, dynamic modification rules, and applicable regulatory standard (ISO 2859, MIL-STD-1916). Referenced by inspection plans to determine how many samples to draw.'
}

Table quality.usage_decision {
  usage_decision_id varchar [pk, note: 'System generated unique identifier for the usage decision record.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Ties release/hold decisions to the product, required for release‑authorization workflow and audit trail.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Release/rework cost allocation: usage decisions (release, rework, hold) generate cost entries that must be posted to the responsible cost center.']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.goods_receipt. Business justification: Usage decision (release/reject/reprocess) is the disposition outcome for received goods. Direct FK to goods_receipt enables incoming material disposition reporting, return-to-vendor processing, and GM']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inspection lot that this decision applies to.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Usage decisions release material from a specific lot; linking to inventory lot enables accurate stock deduction and compliance.']
  lot_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.lot_record. Business justification: Usage decisions (release, reject, reprocess) are made on specific raw material lots after incoming inspection. Direct lot_record linkage enables lot disposition tracking and regulatory audit trails fo']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Usage decisions apply to a specific raw material. Direct material_master linkage supports material-level disposition reporting and regulatory compliance dashboards without requiring joins through insp']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_order. Business justification: Usage decisions (release/reject/reprocess) in chemical manufacturing directly affect order fulfillment. Linking usage decision to product order enables automated order release, backorder management, a']
  quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the quality specification that governs the inspection criteria.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the audit trail entry capturing the decision workflow.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier for the production batch associated with the inspection lot.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑text comments entered by the decision maker or quality engineer.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the decision complies with GMP and ISO 9001 requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the usage decision record was first created in the system.']
  decision_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the final disposition of the inspection lot after evaluation.']
  decision_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the usage decision was made.']
  defect_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of material identified as defective in the inspection lot.']
  digital_signature varchar [note: 'Base‑64 encoded digital signature of the decision maker for GMP compliance.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the material, if applicable.']
  follow_up_action_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the follow‑up action triggered by the decision.']
  hold_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of material placed on hold or blocked.']
  is_conditional_release boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the decision includes a conditional release of the material.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code of the manufacturing plant where the inspection took place.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Code describing the primary reason for the usage decision.']
  release_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the material was released to inventory.']
  release_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of material approved for unrestricted release.']
  reprocess_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of material earmarked for reprocessing as a result of the decision.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record where the usage decision originated.']
  stock_posting_instruction varchar [note: 'Instruction for how the material should be posted in inventory after the decision.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the usage decision record.']
  usage_decision_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the usage decision record.']

  Note: 'Quality usage decision record capturing the final disposition of an inspection lot after all test results are evaluated. Records decision code (accept, reject, conditional release, reprocess, scrap, return to vendor), decision date, decision maker, stock posting instruction (unrestricted, blocked, returns), defect quantity, follow-up action triggered, and digital signature for GMP compliance. Drives downstream inventory stock transfer in SAP QM/EWM.'
}

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the notification.']

  Note: 'Quality deviation and notification record capturing all types of quality events requiring investigation: OOS (Out of Specification), OOT (Out of Trend), non-conformance reports (NCR), customer complaints, internal deviations, and supplier quality issues. Single source of truth for all quality events requiring investigation, triggering CAPA workflow and linking to hold decisions.'
}

  severity varchar [note: 'Severity rating of the issue prompting the CAPA.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the corrective or preventive action should be completed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the CAPA record.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify that the CAPA is effective.']
  verification_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the verification activity.']

  Note: 'Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record managing the full lifecycle of quality improvement actions triggered by OOS events, quality notifications, audit findings, or customer complaints. Captures CAPA number, CAPA type (corrective, preventive), root cause description, RCI method (5-Why, Fishbone, FMEA), proposed actions, responsible owner, target completion date, actual completion date, effectiveness verification date, effectiveness outcome, and GMP compliance status. Central to ISO 9001 and GMP compliance.'
}

Adding this FK l']
  grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grade. Business justification: CoC records must certify grade compliance (e.g., \'This product meets USP grade specifications\'). Grade is a mandatory field on CoC documents for pharmaceutical and food-grade chemicals, required for c']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: A Certificate of Conformance/Analysis (COC/COA) is generated for a specific inspection lot after quality release. coc_record has batch_number and lot_number as STRING attributes but no FK to the inspe']

  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Certificates of Conformance are issued for specific inventory lots in chemical manufacturing. lot_number on coc_record is a denormalized text field. Direct FK enables automated CoC generation from lot']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Sales order identifier associated with the batch.']

Addi']
  safety_data_sheet_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SDS document for the product.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: In chemical manufacturing, a Certificate of Conformance is issued per shipment and must accompany the delivery. Linking coc_record directly to shipment enables shipment release validation, customer do']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Certificate of Conformance records in chemical manufacturing are issued for specific SKUs (the actual packaged unit shipped). Customer receiving, regulatory submission, and traceability audits all req']
  usage_decision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.usage_decision. Business justification: A COC/COA is issued upon quality release, which is formally captured in a usage decision record. Adding usage_decision_id to coc_record links the certificate to the specific release decision that auth']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certificate was approved for release.']
  archive_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certificate was moved to archive.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch or lot number to which the certificate applies.']
  batch_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of product produced in the batch.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique business‑assigned identifier for the certificate.']
  certificate_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the document is a Certificate of Analysis (COA) or Certificate of Compliance (COC).']
  coc_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certificate.']
  compliance_statement varchar [note: 'Textual statement affirming compliance with applicable specifications and regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certificate record was first created in the system.']
  distribution_list varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of internal or external recipients of the certificate.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored PDF or electronic version of the certificate.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the product is no longer considered fit for use.']
  failed_test_count varchar [note: 'Number of tests that did not meet specification limits.']
  hazard_class varchar [note: 'GHS hazard class of the product.']
  is_archived boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the certificate record has been archived.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates if the certificate contains confidential information.']
  is_customer_specific boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the certificate includes customer‑specific requirements or format.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the certificate was issued to the customer or internal stakeholder.']
  manufacturing_date date [note: 'Date the batch was produced.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any extra remarks or observations.']
  overall_result varchar [note: 'Overall pass/fail outcome of the certificate based on specifications.']
  passed_test_count varchar [note: 'Number of tests that met specification limits.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Internal code identifying the chemical product or grade.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the chemical product.']
  qa_signatory_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Quality Assurance person who approved the certificate.']
  regulatory_compliance_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of regulatory codes (e.g., REACH, TSCA) applicable to the batch.']
  released_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certificate status changed to released.']
  revision_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for re‑issuing or revising the certificate.']
  total_test_count varchar [note: 'Number of analytical tests performed for this batch.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for batch quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the certificate record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the certificate document, incremented on re‑issue.']

  Note: 'Quality certificate master record (COA or COC) generated for a product batch upon quality release. Supports Certificate of Analysis (COA) listing individual test parameters with specification limits and actual measured values, and Certificate of Compliance (COC) attesting overall conformance to specifications without detailed test data. Captures certificate number, certificate type (COA/COC), batch/lot number, product code/grade, manufacturing and expiry dates, test results summary, applicable specification reference, compliance statement, release status, QA signatory, customer-specific variant flag, issue date, document version, and distribution history. Serves as the single authoritative source for all outbound quality certificates supporting customer order fulfillment, regulatory submissions, and audit requirements.'
}

Table quality.quality_hold {
  quality_hold_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the quality hold record.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Associates holds with the specific product, supporting inventory control and compliance hold reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Holding cost allocation: material hold incurs storage and opportunity costs charged to the owning cost center.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: A quality hold is frequently triggered by a failed or pending inspection lot. quality_hold currently references quality_deviation but has no direct FK to the inspection_lot that triggered the hold. Ad']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Quality holds in chemical manufacturing incur holding costs (extended storage, retesting, potential disposal of held quantity). Internal orders capture these costs for financial reporting. quantity_he']
  inventory_target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.inventory_target. Business justification: Inventory target calculations consider active quality holds to adjust safety stock and reorder points, requiring a direct link.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Finance must block or flag invoices for batches under quality hold, ensuring no payment is collected before release.']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.line_item. Business justification: Quality holds in chemical manufacturing are placed on specific order line items (a particular SKU quantity on a specific order). Line-item-level hold tracking is required for order management, custome']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Quality holds block usage of a specific lot; FK to inventory lot enables automated quarantine handling.']
  lot_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.lot_record. Business justification: Quality holds are placed on specific raw material lots pending investigation. Direct lot_record linkage enables lot-level quarantine management and regulatory reporting on held raw material inventory ']
  manufacturing_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.manufacturing_order. Business justification: Quality hold is applied to a manufacturing order; linking supports hold/release workflow and regulatory reporting.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the material subject to the hold.']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order. Business justification: Quality hold linked to a sales order enables the Order Hold Management workflow to prevent shipping non‑conforming goods.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.production_schedule. Business justification: Quality holds placed on batches must be traced to the specific production schedule run to assess schedule impact and trigger rescheduling. Chemical manufacturing planners require this link to determin']
  quality_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Link to the quality notification (e.g., OOS, OOT) that generated the hold.']
  quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_specification. Business justification: A quality hold is placed when material fails to meet a quality specification. Adding quality_specification_id to quality_hold links the hold to the specific specification that was violated or is under']
  safety_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.safety_incident. Business justification: Regulatory traceability: when a quality hold is placed due to a safety incident, the hold must reference the incident for compliance reporting.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Hold Management process tracks shipments placed on hold due to quality issues, linking hold records to the affected shipment.']
  usage_decision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.usage_decision. Business justification: A quality hold is released based on a usage decision. Adding usage_decision_id to quality_hold links the hold record to the formal release decision that authorized lifting the hold. This is essential ']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_location. Business justification: Quality holds specify where held material must be physically stored (quarantine zone, segregated area). storage_location on quality_hold is a denormalized text field. FK to warehouse_location enables ']
  waste_disposal_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.waste_disposal_record. Business justification: Held chemical inventory condemned after quality hold must be disposed of as hazardous waste under RCRA. Linking quality_hold to waste_disposal_record ensures condemned material is properly manifested ']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Quality holds are often held until a maintenance work order is completed; linking coordinates release decisions with maintenance schedules.']
  actual_release_date date [note: 'Date the hold was actually released; null if still active.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier of the material under hold.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text notes entered by users regarding the hold.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the hold record was first created in the system.']
  ehs_incident_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of an associated environmental, health, or safety incident, if applicable.']
  expected_release_date date [note: 'Planned date for releasing the hold, based on investigation or process schedule.']
  hold_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the hold was placed.']
  hold_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the hold, used in operational communications and reports.']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the hold based on where the material is in the value chain.']
  quality_hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the hold record.']
  quantity_held decimal(18,3) [note: 'Amount of material placed on hold, expressed in the unit of measure.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the material was placed on hold.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation of the hold reason providing additional context.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hold is required for regulatory compliance (true) or internal quality control (false).']
  release_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the hold disposition after investigation.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the held quantity (e.g., kilograms, liters).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the hold record.']

  Note: 'Quality hold record placing a material batch, lot, or inventory quantity under restricted status pending quality investigation or disposition. Captures hold number, hold type (incoming, in-process, finished goods, customer return), affected material, batch/lot, quantity, storage location, hold reason, hold initiator, hold date, expected release date, actual release date, release authority, and hold outcome (released, rejected, reprocessed, scrapped). Integrates with SAP QM blocked stock and EWM.'
}

Table quality.spc_control {
  spc_control_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for spc_control']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: SPC control charts in chemical manufacturing are maintained for specific chemical products to monitor process capability. Product-level SPC tracking is required for process validation, FDA/regulatory ']
  emission_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.emission_source. Business justification: SPC control charts monitoring process parameters at emission sources enable real-time detection of process upsets causing permit exceedances. Linking SPC charts to emission sources supports continuous']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.equipment. Business justification: SPC control charts in chemical manufacturing are defined per equipment unit (e.g., reactor R-101 viscosity chart, extruder E-201 melt index chart). Linking spc_control to equipment enables equipment-l']
  inspection_characteristic_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_characteristic. Business justification: An SPC control chart is configured to monitor a specific quality characteristic. spc_control currently stores characteristic_code and characteristic_name as denormalized STRING attributes but has no F']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: SPC charts are maintained for raw material incoming quality parameters (e.g., tracking assay % trends for a specific material over time). Material-level process capability analysis for incoming raw ma']
  measurement_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.measurement_point. Business justification: SPC control charts in chemical manufacturing are populated from process historian data sourced at specific equipment measurement points (PI tags, DCS sensors). Linking spc_control to measurement_point']
  quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_specification. Business justification: SPC control limits are derived from and governed by quality specifications. spc_control has no FK to quality_specification currently. Adding quality_specification_id links the SPC chart configuration ']
  chart_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the chart purpose, scope, and configuration notes.']
  chart_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SPC chart.']
  chart_type varchar [note: 'Type of control chart used for monitoring the quality characteristic.']
  control_limit_cl decimal(12,4) [note: 'Target or nominal value for the quality characteristic.']
  control_limit_lcl decimal(12,4) [note: 'Statistical lower control limit for the chart.']
  control_limit_ucl decimal(12,4) [note: 'Statistical upper control limit for the chart.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SPC chart record was initially created.']
  last_recalculation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when control limits were last recomputed.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by investigators or operators.']
  process_step_code varchar [note: 'Unique code for the process step (e.g., STEP_01, REACTOR_A).']
  process_step_name varchar [note: 'Name of the manufacturing process step where the SPC chart is applied.']
  sampling_frequency varchar [note: 'How often samples are collected for the SPC chart.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that supplied the violation data.']
  subgroup_size varchar [note: 'Number of consecutive samples grouped for each SPC calculation.']
  trend_analysis_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the violation contributed to a longer‑term trend analysis.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the characteristic values (e.g., °C, kg, ppm).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the SPC chart record.']
  warning_limit_lcl decimal(12,4) [note: 'Threshold that triggers a warning before reaching the LCL.']
  warning_limit_ucl decimal(12,4) [note: 'Threshold that triggers a warning before reaching the UCL.']

  Note: 'Statistical Process Control (SPC) chart configuration, control limits, and violation event management for monitored quality characteristics at specific process steps or production units. Captures chart type (X-bar R, X-bar S, I-MR, p-chart, c-chart, u-chart), characteristic reference, process step, control limits (UCL, LCL, CL), warning limits, subgroup size, sampling frequency, chart status, last recalculation date. Includes all violation event records: rule violations (Western Electric / Nelson rules 1-8), violation type, measured value, control limit breached, detection timestamp, operator notification, investigation required flag, disposition (false alarm, assignable cause identified, process adjusted), and trend analysis. Single source of truth for both SPC chart configuration and all control chart violation events. Drives real-time SPC monitoring via LabWare LIMS and Aveva PI integration, feeding quality deviation creation for out-of-control conditions.'
}

Table quality.quality_specification {
  quality_specification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the quality specification record.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Quality specifications in chemical manufacturing are defined at the chemical product level and govern all downstream inspection, CoA, and regulatory compliance activities. A domain expert would expect']
  coa_template_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the COA template used for reporting against this specification.']
  grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grade. Business justification: Quality specifications are grade-specific in chemical manufacturing — USP, ACS, FCC, EP grades each have distinct specification limits. The grade determines which quality_specification applies for bat']
  hazmat_storage_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.hazmat_storage_rule. Business justification: Quality specifications for hazardous chemicals must reference applicable hazmat storage rules (OSHA, EPA, GHS compliance). In chemical manufacturing, the quality spec defines the chemical\'s hazard pro']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to rawmaterial.material_master']
  material_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_specification. Business justification: Quality specifications are derived from raw material specifications. Linking them enables specification change management — when material_specification is updated, the corresponding quality_specificat']
  process_safety_info_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.process_safety_info. Business justification: Quality specifications for PSM-covered chemicals must reference process safety information to ensure specification limits (LSL/USL) are consistent with safe operating limits. EPA RMP and OSHA PSM requ']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Customer-specific or packaging-specific quality specifications (e.g., different fill tolerance for drum vs. IBC) are tied to specific SKUs. This drives customer CoA generation and order-specific compl']
  applicable_process varchar [note: 'Manufacturing process type (e.g., batch, continuous) to which the specification applies.']
  applicable_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region(s) where the specification is valid (e.g., NA, EU).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the specification was formally approved.']
  approving_authority varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person/role that approved the specification.']
  change_control_number varchar [note: 'Identifier linking the specification change to the change‑control system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the specification record was first created in the system.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level indicating data sensitivity and handling requirements.']
  default_batch_size decimal(15,2) [note: 'Typical batch size for production runs governed by this specification.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the specification becomes effective.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the specification is no longer valid (nullable if open‑ended).']
  is_ghs_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with the Globally Harmonized System of Classification and Labelling.']
  is_gmp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the specification meets Good Manufacturing Practice requirements.']
  is_iso9001_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with ISO 9001 quality management standards.']
  is_reach_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with REACH regulatory requirements.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal review of the specification.']
  lsl_value decimal(15,5) [note: 'Numeric lower bound for the specification parameter.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next scheduled review of the specification.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Business department responsible for maintaining the specification.']
  quality_specification_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the specification purpose and scope.']
  quality_specification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the specification.']
  regulatory_standard varchar [note: 'Applicable regulatory or industry standard governing the specification.']
  revision_reason varchar [note: 'Reason documented for the latest revision of the specification.']
  sds_reference varchar [note: 'Reference (ID or URL) to the associated Safety Data Sheet.']
  specification_category varchar [note: 'Broad category indicating whether the spec applies to raw material, intermediate, or finished product.']
  specification_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the specification, used for external reference.']
  specification_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the specification source: internal, customer‑provided, regulatory, or pharmacopoeia.']
  target_value decimal(15,5) [note: 'Target or nominal value for the specification parameter.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for numeric specification limits (e.g., kg, ppm, pH).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last updated the specification record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the specification record.']
  usl_value decimal(15,5) [note: 'Numeric upper bound for the specification parameter.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the specification, incremented on each change.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the specification record.']

  Note: 'Master quality specification record defining the approved limits, grades, and acceptance criteria for a finished product, intermediate, or raw material. Captures specification number, specification version, material/product code, grade, specification type (internal, customer, regulatory, pharmacopoeia), parameter list with LSL/USL/target values, effective date, expiry date, approval status, approving authority, and applicable regulatory standard (ISO, ASTM, EP, USP). Serves as the authoritative specification against which COA results are evaluated.'
}
