  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Required storage condition code for the material (e.g., ambient, refrigerated, frozen, temperature-controlled, humidity-controlled, inert atmosphere). Validates that the destination bin meets material storage requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ambient|refrigerated|frozen|temperature_controlled|humidity_controlled|inert_atmosphere|flammable_cabinet — promote to reference product]']
  to_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible alphanumeric transfer order number assigned by SAP EWM. This is the business-facing identifier used in warehouse operations, printed on pick/put-away documents, and referenced in MES and WMS communications.']
  transfer_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the transfer order within SAP EWM. \'created\' = issued but not yet started; \'in_progress\' = operator has begun movement; \'partially_confirmed\' = partial quantity confirmed; \'confirmed\' = fully executed and goods movement posted; \'cancelled\' = voided before execution.']
  transfer_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Planned quantity of material to be moved as specified on the transfer order at creation time. Expressed in the base unit of measure of the material (e.g., KG, L, EA, MT).']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the warehouse movement purpose. \'replenishment\' = restocking a pick face from reserve storage; \'putaway\' = placing newly received goods into a bin; \'pick\' = retrieving goods for outbound delivery; \'internal_transfer\' = bin-to-bin move for reorganization or hazmat segregation; \'return_to_stock\' = returning goods from staging or production back to storage.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for the transfer and confirmed quantities (e.g., KG = kilograms, L = litres, MT = metric tonnes, EA = each, M3 = cubic metres). Aligns with SAP MM material master UOM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|L|EA|MT|G|M3|LB|GAL — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Warehouse transfer order issued within SAP EWM to physically move stock from one storage bin or location to another within the same warehouse complex. Captures transfer order number, source bin, destination bin, material, lot, quantity, transfer type (replenishment, putaway, pick, internal transfer), priority, assigned operator, confirmation status, and execution timestamps. Distinct from inter-plant stock transfer orders which are logistics-domain events.'
}

Table inventory.quarantine_hold {
  quarantine_hold_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the quarantine or quality hold record. Primary key for this entity.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.batch_record. Business justification: Quarantine holds in chemical manufacturing are directly triggered by batch failures or deviations. Linking quarantine_hold to batch_record enables direct traceability from inventory hold to the produc']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.capa. Business justification: Quarantine holds in chemical manufacturing are resolved through CAPAs. capa_reference_number is a denormalized text field. A proper FK enables CAPA effectiveness tracking against inventory disposition']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.case. Business justification: In chemical mfg, customer complaints (cases) directly trigger quarantine holds on implicated inventory lots. The quarantine_hold table already stores customer_complaint_number as a plain text denormal']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Quarantine holds are placed on specific chemical products\' stock for QC failures, regulatory events, or EHS incidents. Linking to chemical_product enables product-level hold reporting, CAPA management']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Quality cost accountability: quarantine holds incur costs (storage, testing, disposal, CAPA) charged to cost centers. Chemical manufacturing quality departments track hold-related costs by cost center']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.goods_receipt. Business justification: Quarantine holds in chemical manufacturing are frequently initiated upon goods receipt when incoming material fails inspection. Linking quarantine_hold to the triggering goods_receipt enables quality ']
  incoming_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.incoming_inspection. Business justification: Quarantine holds in chemical manufacturing are frequently triggered by failed incoming inspections. Linking quarantine_hold to the triggering incoming_inspection record is essential for CAPA root caus']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: In chemical manufacturing, quarantine holds are directly triggered by QM inspection lots (goods receipt, in-process). The lims_inspection_lot_number plain-text field is a denormalized reference to ins']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: quarantine_hold is placed on a specific lot/batch. The lot_number STRING column is denormalized; adding lot_id FK to inventory.lot normalizes this and enables direct join to lot attributes (expiration']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record for the raw material, intermediate, finished good, or packaging material placed under quarantine hold.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.production_schedule. Business justification: A quarantine hold on a raw material or intermediate directly threatens scheduled production runs in chemical manufacturing. Planners need to identify which production schedules are at risk due to held']
  returns_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.returns_order. Business justification: In chemical manufacturing, customer returns trigger quarantine holds on returned material pending QC inspection and disposition (GMP requirement). Linking quarantine_hold to returns_order enables end-']
  safety_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.safety_incident. Business justification: Quarantine holds in chemical manufacturing are frequently initiated as a direct consequence of safety incidents (contamination events, spills, chemical exposure). Linking quarantine_hold to the trigge']
  sds_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.sds_record. Business justification: Quarantine disposition decisions for hazardous materials require the current SDS to determine safe handling, storage, and disposal. quarantine_hold.sds_number is a plain-text denormalization; a proper']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: A quarantine hold is placed on a specific stock position (the exact quantity at a specific location). Adding stock_position_id FK to inventory.stock_position enables direct linkage between the hold an']
  usage_decision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.usage_decision. Business justification: Quarantine hold disposition (release/reject/reprocess/destroy) is formally recorded as a QM usage decision in chemical manufacturing. This FK links the inventory quarantine resolution to the quality a']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier/vendor master record when the quarantined material was externally procured. Used for supplier quality performance tracking and return-to-supplier disposition workflows.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse or tank farm location where the quarantined stock is physically stored. Links to the warehouse master in SAP EWM.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Quarantine holds in chemical manufacturing are frequently initiated by equipment failures and resolved through corrective maintenance work orders. CAPA and disposition workflows require traceability f']
  actual_resolution_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the quarantine hold was resolved through release, rejection, or administrative closure. Compared against target resolution date for performance reporting.']
  affected_quantity_rejected decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the originally held material that was condemned, destroyed, or returned to supplier following disposition. Together with affected_quantity_released, accounts for the full hold quantity.']
  affected_quantity_released decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the originally held material that was ultimately released for use or shipment following disposition. May be less than hold_quantity if partial release was approved (e.g., after rework or resampling).']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Production batch number associated with the quarantined material, as recorded in the Manufacturing Execution System (MES) or SAP PP. May differ from lot number when multiple batches are consolidated into a single lot.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the quarantine hold record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final disposition outcome for the quarantined material: \'released\' = approved for use or shipment; \'rejected\' = condemned and removed from usable stock; \'rework\' = returned to production for reprocessing; \'retest\' = additional testing required before final decision; \'return_to_supplier\' = returned to vendor; \'destroy\' = material to be destroyed per EHS protocols; \'conditional_release\' = released with documented restrictions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: released|rejected|rework|retest|return_to_supplier|destroy|conditional_release — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  disposition_justification varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative documenting the technical and business rationale for the disposition decision. Required for GMP compliance and regulatory audit readiness.']
  disposition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the disposition decision was formally approved and recorded. Distinct from actual resolution date; captures the precise moment of authorization.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date on which the quarantine hold was escalated to senior management or regulatory affairs. Null if escalation_flag is false.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quarantine hold has been escalated to senior QA management, regulatory affairs, or executive leadership due to severity, regulatory implications, or breach of target resolution date.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration or retest date of the quarantined lot as assigned during manufacturing or receipt. Critical for FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory management and determining if material has expired during the hold period.']
  ghs_hazard_class varchar [note: 'GHS hazard classification of the quarantined material (e.g., \'Flammable Liquid Cat 2\', \'Acute Toxicity Cat 3\', \'Corrosive\'). Required for safe storage and handling during quarantine and for EHS compliance reporting.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quarantined material is classified as hazardous under GHS, DOT, or OSHA standards. Drives special handling, storage segregation, and disposal requirements during the hold period.']
  hold_date timestamp [note: 'The date and timestamp when the quarantine hold was formally placed on the material or lot. Represents the principal business event time for this transaction.']
  hold_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the quarantine hold was active, calculated from hold_date to actual_resolution_date. Used for inventory aging analysis, SLA compliance reporting, and process improvement KPIs.']
  hold_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable reference number assigned to the quarantine hold at initiation (e.g., QH-2024-000123). Used in cross-functional communications, LIMS, and EHS systems.']
  hold_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of material placed under quarantine hold. Represents the principal quantitative fact of this hold record. Must be expressed in the base unit of measure for the material.']
  hold_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the hold quantity (e.g., KG for kilograms, L for liters, MT for metric tons, DRUM for drum containers). Aligns with SAP base unit of measure (MEINS). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|LB|L|GAL|MT|TON|EA|BAG|DRUM|IBC — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  hold_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary reason for the quarantine hold. OOS = Out of Specification; OOT = Out of Trend; CUSTOMER_COMPLAINT = customer-reported quality issue; REGULATORY_HOLD = regulatory agency directive; DAMAGE = physical damage; CONTAMINATION = cross-contamination or foreign material; LABEL_ERROR = incorrect or missing labeling; PENDING_COA = Certificate of Analysis (COA) not yet received. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OOS|OOT|CUSTOMER_COMPLAINT|REGULATORY_HOLD|DAMAGE|CONTAMINATION|LABEL_ERROR|PENDING_COA — promote to reference product]']
  hold_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the specific reason for placing the material under quarantine hold. Provides context beyond the hold reason code, including test failure details, complaint summary, or regulatory directive reference.']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the quarantine hold. \'open\' indicates active restriction; \'released\' indicates disposition approved for use/shipment; \'rejected\' indicates material condemned or destroyed; \'pending_review\' awaits disposition decision; \'escalated\' requires senior QA or regulatory review; \'closed\' is administratively finalized.']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the quarantine hold by originating function: \'quality\' for QC/QA-initiated holds; \'regulatory\' for holds triggered by EPA, OSHA, or ECHA directives; \'safety\' for EHS-initiated holds; \'customer\' for customer complaint-driven holds; \'environmental\' for environmental compliance holds; \'administrative\' for documentation or system-related holds.']
  initiating_system varchar [note: 'Source system that triggered or originated the quarantine hold (e.g., LIMS = LabWare LIMS for OOS results; EHS = Intelex EHS for safety incidents; SAP_QM = SAP Quality Management for inspection lot failures; MES = Manufacturing Execution System; MANUAL = manually initiated by QA personnel). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: LIMS|EHS|SAP_QM|MES|MANUAL|SCADA|ERP — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  initiator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person who initiated the quarantine hold. Retained for audit trail and regulatory reporting purposes where employee ID alone is insufficient.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing or storage facility where the quarantined material is physically located (e.g., \'PLANT01\', \'TX02\').']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase Order (PO) number associated with the quarantined material if it was received from an external supplier. Used for supplier quality management, return-to-supplier disposition, and debit memo processing.']
  regulatory_hold_reference varchar [note: 'External regulatory agency reference number or directive identifier when the hold is initiated by a regulatory body (e.g., EPA enforcement action number, OSHA citation reference, FDA warning letter number, ECHA restriction notice). Null for non-regulatory holds.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'SAP storage location (LGORT) or warehouse bin where the quarantined material is physically held. Includes tank farm designations, warehouse zones, and quarantine bays.']
  target_resolution_date date [note: 'Target date by which the quarantine hold disposition must be resolved (released or rejected). Used for SLA tracking, escalation management, and inventory planning.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the quarantine hold record was last modified. Tracks changes to disposition, status, or hold details.']

  Note: 'Record of a quarantine or quality hold placed on a specific lot or stock quantity, preventing it from being issued or shipped until disposition is resolved. Captures hold reference number, material, lot, quantity affected, hold reason (OOS result, customer complaint, regulatory hold, damage), hold initiator, hold date, target resolution date, CAPA reference, and release or rejection outcome. Integrates with LabWare LIMS and Intelex EHS for hold initiation triggers.'
}

Table inventory.physical_inventory_count {
  physical_inventory_count_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each physical inventory count record in the lakehouse silver layer. Maps to SAP MM physical inventory document item.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Period-end close and financial reporting: physical inventory counts are scheduled and posted within specific fiscal periods. Linking to fiscal_period enables automated period-close validation, audit t']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: A physical inventory count is performed at the lot level in chemical manufacturing (FIFO/FEFO compliance requires lot-level counting). Adding lot_id FK to inventory.lot enables direct linkage between ']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Physical inventory count should reference the stock position record that aggregates quantity and location; removes duplicated attributes.']
  approval_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the approver explaining the rationale for approving the variance write-off. Supports audit documentation and Root Cause Investigation (RCI) for recurring discrepancies.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the variance write-off was approved by the authorized approver. Part of the SOX-compliant approval chain audit trail for inventory reconciliation.']
  book_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'System-recorded (book) quantity of the material at the storage location at the time the count document was created, as per SAP MM inventory records. Represents the expected quantity before physical verification.']
  count_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the physical count was performed. Used for inventory valuation cutoff, regulatory reporting, and SOX period-end reconciliation.']
  count_document_item varchar [note: 'Line item number within the SAP physical inventory document, distinguishing individual material-storage location combinations within the same count document.']
  count_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP MM physical inventory document number (MI01/MI04) that uniquely identifies the count event in the source system. Used for reconciliation and audit trail back to SAP.']
  count_method varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the count was performed blind (counter not shown book quantity, reducing bias) or informed (counter shown book quantity). Blind counting is preferred for SOX-compliant inventory reconciliation.']
  count_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the count document: open (count not yet performed), counted (count entered, pending posting), recounted (recount completed), posted (variance posted to inventory), cancelled (count voided).']
  count_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the physical count was recorded, including timezone offset. Used for intraday reconciliation and audit trail in continuous process manufacturing environments.']
  count_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the count event: cycle_count (periodic scheduled count of a subset of materials), annual_wall_to_wall (full facility count), triggered_count (initiated due to discrepancy or event), or recount (second count to resolve variance).']
  counted_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual physical quantity of the material counted and recorded by the counter. This is the primary measurement outcome of the count event.']
  counter_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee who performed the physical count. Stored for audit readability and SOX compliance reporting without requiring a join to HR systems.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the physical inventory count record was first created in the source system (SAP MM). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOX record retention compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the inventory valuation amount (e.g., USD, EUR). Supports multi-currency operations across global chemical manufacturing sites.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration or shelf-life expiry date of the lot/batch being counted. Critical for FEFO (First Expired First Out) lot rotation compliance and identification of expired stock during physical counts.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Accounting period (month) within the fiscal year during which the count was performed (e.g., 001 through 012). Used for period-end inventory cutoff and financial close processes.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the physical inventory count was performed. Used for period-end financial reporting, SOX compliance, and year-over-year inventory accuracy trend analysis.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material being counted is classified as hazardous under GHS, OSHA, or DOT regulations. Drives additional safety protocols during physical counting and storage compliance checks.']
  inventory_valuation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the inventory variance calculated as variance_quantity multiplied by the standard or moving average price of the material. Used for financial impact assessment and COGS adjustment. Expressed in local currency.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the physical inventory count record. Supports incremental data loading in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer and change data capture tracking.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Short description of the material being counted as maintained in the SAP material master. Provides human-readable context for reporting without requiring a join to the material master.']
  material_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP material document number generated when the inventory variance is posted (Transaction MI07). Links this count record to the resulting inventory_adjustment material document for financial reconciliation.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Accounting date on which the inventory variance was posted to the general ledger in SAP FI/CO. Critical for period-end cutoff, COGS calculation, and SOX financial reporting compliance.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Status of the variance posting to the SAP general ledger: not_posted (pending), posted (successfully posted), posting_failed (error during posting), reversed (posting reversed for correction).']
  quarantine_status varchar [note: 'Stock status of the material at time of count: unrestricted (available for use), quarantine (held pending investigation), blocked (restricted from use), quality_inspection (held for QC testing). Aligns with SAP MM stock categories.']
  recount_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a recount has been triggered for this count item, typically due to variance exceeding the tolerance threshold. True = recount required or performed; False = no recount needed.']
  recount_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating which recount iteration this record represents (1 = first recount, 2 = second recount, etc.). Zero or null indicates the original count. Supports multi-round recount audit trails.']
  special_stock_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates the ownership or allocation type of the stock being counted: own (company-owned), consignment (customer-owned at our facility), project (allocated to a project), sales_order (reserved for a sales order), vendor_consignment (vendor-owned at our facility).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure in which the book, counted, and variance quantities are expressed (e.g., KG, L, MT, EA, DR for drums). Aligns with SAP base unit of measure from the material master.']
  variance_percentage decimal(10,4) [note: 'Variance quantity expressed as a percentage of book quantity. Used to determine whether variance exceeds tolerance thresholds requiring management approval or recount. Key KPI for inventory accuracy reporting.']
  variance_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Difference between counted quantity and book quantity (counted_quantity minus book_quantity). Positive value indicates surplus; negative value indicates shortage. Drives inventory adjustment postings.']
  variance_tolerance_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Allowable variance percentage threshold configured for this material or material group. Variances exceeding this threshold require management approval for write-off. Supports SOX-compliant variance authorization controls.']
  warehouse_number varchar [note: 'SAP EWM warehouse number identifying the logical warehouse complex. Used when the count is managed at the warehouse management level rather than simple storage location level.']
  within_tolerance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the variance percentage is within the configured tolerance threshold. True = variance is acceptable and can be auto-posted; False = variance exceeds tolerance and requires manual approval.']

  Note: 'Record of a physical inventory count event (cycle count, annual wall-to-wall count, or triggered count) for a specific material at a specific storage location or bin. Captures count document number, count date, counter identity, book quantity at time of count, counted quantity, variance quantity, variance percentage, recount flag, count method (blind vs. informed), posting status, and approval chain for variance write-off. Supports regulatory and SOX-compliant inventory reconciliation and feeds inventory_adjustment records for variance postings. Integrates with SAP MM physical inventory module.'
}

Table inventory.hazmat_storage_rule {
  hazmat_storage_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each hazardous material storage compatibility and segregation rule record. Primary key for the hazmat_storage_rule reference entity. [_canonical_skip_reason: Entity role classified as REFERENCE_LOOKUP — defines a codified regulatory/operational rule set governing chemical storage compatibility; no per-role minimum categories apply, but comprehensive coverage is still provided for enterprise utility.]']
  cas_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.cas_registry. Business justification: Hazmat storage rules in chemical manufacturing are authored for specific chemical substances identified by CAS number. Linking hazmat_storage_rule to cas_registry enables automated rule lookup during ']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this storage rule was formally approved by the designated EHS authority. Part of the MOC documentation chain. Required for OSHA PSM compliance audits and ISO 9001 document control records.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the EHS Manager, Process Safety Engineer, or Regulatory Affairs Officer who formally approved this storage rule for operational use. Required for MOC (Management of Change) documentation and regulatory audit trails per OSHA PSM and ISO 45001.']
  classification_system varchar [note: 'Primary hazard classification system under which this storage rule is defined (e.g., GHS for global chemical classification, NFPA for US fire codes, UN for transport, IMDG for maritime, CLP for EU). Determines which regulatory framework governs the rule\'s applicability. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GHS|NFPA|UN|DOT|IMDG|IATA|CLP|WHMIS — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this hazmat storage rule record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for rule origination. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per enterprise data standards.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this storage rule version becomes enforceable in warehouse operations. Rules with a future effective date are loaded into SAP EWM in draft status and activated on the effective date. Supports regulatory transition periods.']
  epa_regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'Specific EPA regulatory citation (CFR part and section) that mandates or informs this storage rule (e.g., \'40 CFR Part 112\', \'40 CFR Part 68 RMP\', \'40 CFR Part 264 Subpart I\'). Used in environmental compliance reporting and audit documentation submitted to EPA.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this storage rule version expires or is superseded. Null indicates the rule is open-ended with no planned expiry. When populated, the system automatically transitions the rule to \'superseded\' status and activates the replacement rule version.']
  fire_suppression_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an automatic fire suppression system (sprinkler, deluge, foam, or dry chemical) is required at the storage location for chemicals governed by this rule. Drives facility engineering requirements and influences NFPA 400 maximum allowable quantity multipliers.']
  fire_zone_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fire zone or control area type to which the maximum quantity limit applies. NFPA 400 and IFC allow higher MAQ in sprinklered buildings versus unprotected control areas. Drives differentiated quantity limits in SAP EWM storage type configuration.']
  ghs_hazard_statement_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of GHS H-statement codes applicable to chemicals governed by this rule (e.g., H225,H302,H314). Drives SDS Section 2 alignment and label generation. Used in REACH and CLP regulatory reporting.']
  grounding_bonding_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether electrical grounding and bonding equipment is required at the storage location for chemicals governed by this rule. Mandatory for flammable liquids with flash points below 100°F to prevent static discharge ignition per NFPA 77 and OSHA standards.']
  hazard_class varchar [note: 'Primary GHS/UN hazard class of the chemical governed by this rule (e.g., Class 3 Flammable Liquids, Class 5.1 Oxidizing Substances, Class 8 Corrosives, Class 6.1 Toxic Substances). Aligns with UN Model Regulations and GHS classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Class 1|Class 2|Class 3|Class 4|Class 5.1|Class 5.2|Class 6.1|Class 6.2|Class 7|Class 8|Class 9 — promote to reference product]']
  hazard_sub_risk varchar [note: 'Secondary hazard classification or division providing additional specificity within the primary hazard class (e.g., Division 1.1 for mass explosion risk, Packing Group I/II/III, sub-risk 6.1 on a Class 3 flammable). Captures multi-hazard chemical profiles per GHS and UN regulations.']
  incompatibility_consequence varchar [note: 'Primary hazardous consequence that would result from co-locating the two incompatible hazard classes defined in this rule. Used in risk assessment, PHA/HAZOP documentation, and emergency response planning. Informs the severity weighting in automated putaway violation alerts.']
  incompatible_hazard_class_a varchar [note: 'First hazard class in the incompatible pair defined by this segregation rule. Together with incompatible_hazard_class_b, forms the segregation matrix entry that prohibits co-location of chemicals from these two hazard classes in the same storage zone. Aligns with NFPA 400 Table 10.3.2 and UN segregation table.']
  incompatible_hazard_class_b varchar [note: 'Second hazard class in the incompatible pair defined by this segregation rule. Together with incompatible_hazard_class_a, forms the segregation matrix entry. The automated putaway validation engine in SAP EWM checks both directions of this pair to prevent co-location of incompatible chemicals.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this hazmat storage rule record was last updated. Supports change detection in downstream ETL pipelines and provides audit trail for rule modifications. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per enterprise data standards.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date on which this storage rule was last reviewed for continued regulatory accuracy and operational relevance. ISO 14001 and ISO 45001 require periodic review of EHS controls. Triggers a review workflow in Intelex EHS Management when overdue.']
  max_quantity_per_fire_zone_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum allowable quantity in kilograms of chemicals governed by this rule that may be stored within a single fire zone or control area. Derived from NFPA 400 maximum allowable quantities (MAQ) tables and local fire code. Enforced by SAP EWM inventory capacity checks.']
  max_quantity_per_fire_zone_l decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum allowable quantity in liters of liquid chemicals governed by this rule that may be stored within a single fire zone or control area. Complements the kilogram-based limit for liquid hazmat categories where volume is the regulatory basis (e.g., NFPA 30 flammable liquids).']
  max_storage_temp_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for chemicals governed by this rule. Exceeding this threshold may cause decomposition, polymerization, or ignition. Monitored via Aveva PI System historian and triggers automated EHS alerts in Intelex.']
  min_storage_temp_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for chemicals governed by this rule. Below this threshold, the chemical may solidify, become unstable, or lose efficacy. Monitored via Aveva PI System historian for real-time compliance alerting.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Date by which this storage rule must next be reviewed for regulatory currency and operational accuracy. Calculated based on review frequency policy (typically annual for high-hazard rules). Drives compliance calendar in Intelex EHS Management.']
  nfpa_flammability_rating varchar [note: 'NFPA 704 fire diamond flammability rating (0–4) for chemicals governed by this rule. Rating 4 indicates materials that will rapidly vaporize at normal pressure and temperature. Used in fire zone maximum quantity calculations and emergency response planning.']
  nfpa_health_rating varchar [note: 'NFPA 704 fire diamond health hazard rating (0–4) for chemicals governed by this rule. Rating 4 indicates materials that can cause death or major residual injury on short exposure. Used in PPE requirements and emergency response planning.']
  nfpa_reactivity_rating varchar [note: 'NFPA 704 fire diamond instability/reactivity rating (0–4) for chemicals governed by this rule. Rating 4 indicates materials capable of detonation or explosive decomposition at normal temperatures and pressures.']
  nfpa_special_hazard_code varchar [note: 'NFPA 704 special hazard symbol code for chemicals governed by this rule (e.g., W = water-reactive, OX = oxidizer, SA = simple asphyxiant, BIO = biological hazard, CRY = cryogenic). Governs special storage precautions and emergency response procedures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: W|OX|SA|BIO|COR|POI|RAD|CRY|ACID|ALK — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  osha_regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'Specific OSHA regulatory citation (CFR part and section) that mandates or informs this storage rule (e.g., \'29 CFR 1910.119 PSM\', \'29 CFR 1910.106 Flammable Liquids\', \'29 CFR 1910.1200 HazCom\'). Used in PSM compliance documentation and OSHA inspection readiness.']
  packing_group varchar [note: 'UN packing group designation (I = great danger, II = medium danger, III = minor danger) for the hazardous material governed by this rule. Determines packaging requirements for transport and influences storage quantity limits and segregation requirements.']
  reach_regulation_reference varchar [note: 'REACH regulation article or annex reference applicable to this storage rule (e.g., \'REACH Annex XVII Restriction Entry 3\', \'REACH Article 56 Authorization\'). Required for EU operations and ECHA compliance reporting. Populated for chemicals on the SVHC candidate list.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this storage rule within the enterprise rule catalog (e.g., HSR-001, FLAM-OX-SEG-01). Used as the human-readable business key in SAP EWM putaway strategies and EHS management systems.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the storage rule, including the rationale for the segregation or storage condition requirement, the chemical hazard interaction being prevented, and any operational guidance for warehouse personnel.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive short name for the storage rule (e.g., \'Flammable-Oxidizer Segregation\', \'Corrosive-Reactive Incompatibility\'). Used in warehouse management UI labels and EHS compliance reports.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the storage rule record. \'active\' rules are enforced in automated putaway validation. \'superseded\' rules have been replaced by a newer version. \'under_review\' rules are pending regulatory or internal re-assessment.']
  rule_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this storage rule record (e.g., v1.0, v2.3). Supports rule versioning when regulatory updates require rule revisions. Enables audit trail of rule changes over time and ensures warehouse systems reference the current approved version.']
  sds_section_references varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of SDS section numbers that provide the source data for this storage rule (e.g., \'7,8,10,14\'). Section 7 = Handling and Storage, Section 8 = Exposure Controls, Section 10 = Reactivity, Section 14 = Transport. Enables traceability from rule to SDS source documentation per GHS.']
  segregation_distance_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Minimum physical separation distance in meters required between storage locations holding chemicals from the two incompatible hazard classes defined in this rule. Used by SAP EWM bin assignment logic to enforce spatial separation within the warehouse or tank farm.']
  storage_location_type varchar [note: 'Type of physical storage location required for chemicals governed by this rule. Drives SAP EWM storage type determination during putaway. \'tank_farm\' applies to bulk liquid chemicals. \'refrigerated_room\' applies to temperature-sensitive hazmat. Validated against warehouse master data.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'UN four-digit identification number for the hazardous substance or article governed by this rule (e.g., UN1203 for gasoline, UN1824 for sodium hydroxide solution). Used in DOT transport documentation, emergency response, and cross-referencing with the UN Model Regulations segregation table.']
  ventilation_class varchar [note: 'Required ventilation classification for storage areas holding chemicals governed by this rule. \'local_exhaust\' indicates dedicated fume extraction is required. \'explosion_proof\' indicates ATEX/NEC-rated ventilation systems are mandatory. Aligns with OSHA industrial hygiene requirements and NFPA 30.']

  Note: 'Reference record defining hazardous material storage compatibility and segregation rules per GHS, NFPA, and UN hazard classification systems. Captures hazard class, sub-risk, NFPA fire diamond rating, incompatible hazard class pairs (segregation matrix), required storage conditions (temperature range, ventilation class, secondary containment type), maximum storage quantity per fire zone, EPA/OSHA regulatory citation, and applicable SDS section references. Governs automated putaway validation ensuring incompatible chemicals are never co-located in the same storage zone.'
}

Table inventory.storage_bin {
  storage_bin_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the storage bin master record in the SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) system. Serves as the primary key for all bin-level inventory tracking and directed putaway/picking operations.']
  hazmat_storage_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.hazmat_storage_rule. Business justification: Hazardous storage rules are defined centrally; link bin to rule and remove duplicated class info.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_location. Business justification: Storage bin belongs to a warehouse location; replace free‑text warehouse_number with FK for proper hierarchy.']
  aisle varchar [note: 'Aisle identifier within the warehouse where the storage bin is physically located. Used for warehouse navigation, slotting optimization, and pick path sequencing.']
  allowed_material_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of SAP material type codes (e.g., ROH for raw materials, FERT for finished goods, HALB for semi-finished, VERP for packaging) permitted to be stored in this bin. Enforces material segregation rules for chemical compatibility and regulatory compliance.']
  bin_access_type varchar [note: 'Type of material handling equipment required to access this storage bin. Determines equipment assignment during transfer order creation and supports warehouse resource planning. Critical for safety compliance in chemical warehouses where certain hazmat bins require specialized handling equipment.']
  bin_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the physical storage bin within a warehouse number and storage type. Corresponds to the SAP EWM Lgpla field. Used in directed putaway, picking, and bin-level inventory queries.']
  bin_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the storage bin providing additional context about its physical characteristics, special use restrictions, or operational notes. May include information about dedicated chemical families, special handling requirements, or historical usage notes.']
  bin_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name or label for the storage bin, used in warehouse operations, signage, and reporting. May include aisle, row, and level descriptors (e.g., \'Aisle-A Row-03 Level-02\') to aid physical navigation.']
  bin_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the storage bin. Active bins are available for putaway and picking. Blocked bins are temporarily unavailable. Quarantine status is applied when contents are under quality hold per QM procedures. Maintenance status indicates the bin is undergoing physical inspection or repair.']
  bin_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical bin configuration. Fixed bins are dedicated to specific materials (e.g., hazardous chemicals); random bins accept any compatible material; bulk bins hold large quantities; tank bins represent liquid storage vessels in tank farms; floor bins are ground-level pallet positions; rack bins are elevated rack locations.']
  containment_type varchar [note: 'Type of secondary containment infrastructure present at this storage bin to prevent chemical spills from reaching drains or the environment. Secondary bund and tank bund are used for bulk liquid chemical storage; spill pallets for drum storage; lined floor for general chemical areas. Required for EPA SPCC compliance and ISO 14001 environmental management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the storage bin master record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for bin lifecycle management and supports data governance requirements.']
  current_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Current number of handling units (pallets, drums, IBCs) physically present in the bin. Reflects the real-time stock count as maintained by SAP EWM goods movements.']
  current_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Current total volume in cubic meters occupied by stock in the bin. Compared against max_volume_m3 to determine available capacity for putaway decisions.']
  current_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Current total gross weight in kilograms of all stock currently stored in the bin. Updated upon each goods movement. Used to monitor bin load against maximum weight capacity and support structural safety compliance.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date on which the storage bin was or is scheduled to be deactivated and removed from active warehouse operations. Null for currently active bins. Supports bin lifecycle management and warehouse restructuring projects.']
  fire_protection_zone varchar [note: 'NFPA fire protection zone or compartment designation assigned to the storage bin. Used to enforce fire load limits, sprinkler system coverage mapping, and emergency response planning for flammable and combustible chemical storage areas.']
  is_fixed_bin boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this bin is a fixed bin permanently assigned to a specific material or material group, as opposed to a random bin available for any compatible material. True = fixed bin assignment; False = random bin. Drives SAP EWM putaway strategy determination.']
  is_hazmat_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage bin has been formally approved and certified for the storage of hazardous materials in compliance with OSHA PSM, EPA RMP, and GHS regulations. True = approved for hazmat storage; False = not approved.']
  is_quarantine_bin boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this bin is designated as a quarantine storage location for materials under quality hold, out-of-specification (OOS) status, or pending Certificate of Analysis (COA) release. True = quarantine bin; False = standard bin. Integrates with SAP QM quality inspection workflows.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical safety or structural inspection of the storage bin, including rack integrity checks, containment integrity, and hazmat compliance verification. Supports OSHA PSM and ISO 45001 occupational health and safety compliance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the storage bin master record. Used for change tracking, data lineage, and audit trail purposes in compliance with ISO 9001 document control requirements.']
  last_physical_inventory_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent physical inventory count or cycle count was performed for this storage bin. Used to track inventory accuracy compliance, schedule future counts, and support SOX financial reporting requirements for inventory valuation.']
  max_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum number of handling units (pallets, drums, IBCs, or containers) that can be stored in the bin simultaneously. Enforced during directed putaway to prevent overfilling.']
  max_stacking_height_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable stacking height in meters for materials stored in this bin. Enforced to prevent structural overload and comply with OSHA stacking safety regulations. Particularly relevant for drum and IBC storage of liquid chemicals.']
  max_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this bin. Prevents thermal degradation, polymerization, or hazardous decomposition of stored chemicals. Critical for compliance with SDS storage requirements.']
  max_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum storage volume in cubic meters available within the bin. Used for volumetric capacity planning, putaway feasibility checks, and tank farm liquid volume management.']
  max_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum allowable gross weight in kilograms that the storage bin can safely hold, including packaging and handling units. Critical for structural safety compliance and overload prevention in chemical warehouses storing dense raw materials or bulk liquids.']
  min_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this bin. Enforced for temperature-sensitive chemical raw materials, intermediates, and finished goods to prevent degradation, crystallization, or phase separation.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory safety or structural inspection of the storage bin. Drives preventive maintenance scheduling and ensures continuous compliance with OSHA, NFPA, and ISO 45001 inspection frequency requirements.']
  occupancy_status varchar [note: 'Real-time occupancy state of the storage bin indicating whether the bin is empty, fully occupied, partially occupied, or reserved for an incoming transfer order. Drives directed putaway logic in SAP EWM and supports inventory accuracy reporting.']
  picking_point varchar [note: 'SAP EWM picking point bin code associated with this storage bin. For bulk or reserve storage bins, identifies the forward pick face bin from which order picking is executed. Supports two-step picking workflows common in chemical distribution operations.']
  putaway_strategy varchar [note: 'SAP EWM putaway strategy assigned to this bin that determines how incoming stock is directed to storage locations. Fixed strategy assigns stock to pre-defined bins; open storage allows any compatible bin; bulk strategy consolidates large quantities; near-picking places fast-movers close to pick areas; addition-to-existing consolidates partial bins.']
  rotation_strategy varchar [note: 'Inventory lot rotation strategy applied to this bin for picking sequence determination. FIFO (First In First Out) is standard for most chemicals; FEFO (First Expired First Out) is mandatory for materials with expiration dates to prevent use of expired lots; LIFO and LEFO are used in specific bulk storage scenarios. Aligns with GMP and ISO 9001 inventory management requirements.']
  row_number varchar [note: 'Row number within the aisle that identifies the horizontal position of the storage bin. Combined with aisle and level, provides the full physical coordinate of the bin within the warehouse.']
  storage_bin_level varchar [note: 'Vertical level or tier of the storage bin within a rack structure. Level 1 is ground floor; higher numbers indicate elevated rack positions. Used for equipment assignment (e.g., forklift vs. reach truck) and weight distribution planning.']
  storage_section_code varchar [note: 'SAP EWM storage section (Lgber) code that subdivides a storage type into logical groupings, such as fast-moving, slow-moving, or temperature-controlled sections. Used to optimize putaway strategies for chemical raw materials and finished goods.']
  storage_type_code varchar [note: 'SAP EWM storage type (Lgtyp) code that classifies the physical area of the warehouse where this bin resides, such as high-rack storage, bulk storage, tank farm, or hazardous material area. Drives putaway and picking strategies.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature-controlled storage zone classification for the bin. Ambient bins operate at room temperature; refrigerated bins maintain 2–8°C for temperature-sensitive chemicals or intermediates; heated bins prevent solidification of high-melting-point materials; controlled bins maintain a specific validated temperature range per product specification.']
  ventilation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage bin requires dedicated ventilation or is located in a ventilated area to manage volatile organic compound (VOC) emissions or hazardous air pollutant (HAP) concentrations. True = ventilation required; False = no special ventilation needed. Supports EHS compliance with EPA and OSHA air quality standards.']

  Note: 'Master record for individual storage bins within a warehouse location, representing the smallest addressable unit of storage in SAP EWM. Captures bin code, warehouse number, storage type, storage section, bin capacity (weight and volume), bin type (fixed, random, bulk, tank), current occupancy status, maximum weight, and allowed material types. Enables precise bin-level inventory tracking and directed putaway/picking strategies.'
}

Table inventory.stock_reservation {
  stock_reservation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for the stock reservation record in the inventory management system.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Supports Customer Allocation Report by linking each inventory reservation to the customer account that requested the reservation, enabling order fulfillment tracking.']
  customer_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.customer_allocation. Business justification: In chemical manufacturing supply constraints, customer allocations authorize specific reservation quantities per customer. Stock reservations must reference the customer allocation that authorized the']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Project budget consumption monitoring: stock reservations for maintenance shutdowns, R&D, or capital projects are created against internal orders. Linking enables real-time budget consumption tracking']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.line_item. Business justification: In chemical manufacturing, stock reservations are created at sales order line item level for lot-controlled and hazmat materials (SAP MRP reservation process). Linking reservation to line_item enables']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific inventory lot or batch being earmarked. Supports FIFO/FEFO lot rotation and prevents double-allocation of expiring chemical stock.']
  manufacturing_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.manufacturing_order. Business justification: Stock reservations are fundamentally created against manufacturing orders in chemical plant MRP/ERP systems (SAP movement type 261). Direct FK enables manufacturing order material availability confirm']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record for the chemical, raw material, intermediate, finished good, or packaging material being reserved.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Needed for allocation reports: reserving stock for a specific sales opportunity ensures availability and supports the Opportunity‑to‑Inventory allocation process.']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production order, customer delivery order, or maintenance work order that will consume the reserved stock. Establishes the demand source driving the reservation.']
  price_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.price_agreement. Business justification: Contract-priced reservation valuation: in chemical manufacturing, stock reserved for contract customers must be valued and allocated under the governing price agreement. This link enables accurate res']
  process_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.process_order. Business justification: Stock reservations in chemical manufacturing are created against process orders to ensure material availability before production starts. Direct FK enables material availability checks and reservation']
  quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_specification. Business justification: Stock reservations for chemical products must specify the required quality specification (grade, purity, regulatory compliance). In chemical manufacturing, customers order to specific quality specs; r']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote. Business justification: Quote-driven inventory reservation: when a customer accepts a quote, chemical manufacturers reserve stock against that quote prior to order conversion. This link enables quote-to-reservation traceabil']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: A stock reservation earmarks a specific quantity from a specific stock position. Adding stock_position_id FK to inventory.stock_position enables direct linkage between reservations and the position be']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse location or tank farm where the reserved stock is physically stored.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Reservation of inventory for a specific maintenance work order; needed for parts reservation and work order cost tracking.']
  auto_release_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reservation should be automatically released back to available stock upon reaching the expiry date without fulfillment. Supports automated inventory management and prevents indefinite stock lock-up.']
  coa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Certificate of Analysis must be verified and attached before the reserved stock can be issued to the consuming order. Enforces quality gate compliance for regulated chemical products.']
  consuming_order_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the type of the consuming order document. Distinguishes between production orders, process orders (for batch chemical manufacturing), sales delivery orders, maintenance orders, and project orders to support domain-specific analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the stock reservation record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, SLA tracking, and data lineage.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which the reservation is no longer valid and the earmarked stock is automatically released back to available inventory. Critical for preventing indefinite stock lock-up of expiring chemical materials.']
  fulfilled_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cumulative quantity of the reservation that has already been issued or withdrawn against the consuming order. Used to calculate the remaining open quantity and determine partial fulfillment status.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reserved material is classified as hazardous. When true, additional handling, storage compliance, and transport regulations apply per OSHA, DOT, and REACH requirements.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the stock reservation record was most recently modified, such as a quantity change, status update, or date revision. Supports change tracking and data reconciliation.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'The ERP material number (SAP material number) of the chemical or material being reserved. Serves as the externally-known business identifier for cross-system reference and reporting.']
  movement_type_code varchar [note: 'SAP goods movement type code that will be used when the reservation is fulfilled (e.g., 261 for goods issue to production order, 601 for goods issue to customer delivery). Determines the accounting and inventory impact of the eventual withdrawal.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special handling instructions, or comments entered by the planner or warehouse operator at the time of reservation creation or update.']
  oos_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reserved lot has been flagged as Out of Specification (OOS) by the quality management system. An OOS reservation is blocked from fulfillment pending CAPA resolution.']
  partial_fulfillment_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consuming order can accept a partial goods issue against this reservation, or whether the full reserved quantity must be issued in a single transaction. Relevant for batch chemical processes with minimum charge quantities.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or distribution center where the reserved stock is located and where the consuming event will occur. Aligns with SAP organizational structure.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking of the reservation used to resolve allocation conflicts when available stock is insufficient to fulfill all open reservations. Lower values indicate higher priority (e.g., 1 = highest priority).']
  quarantine_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reserved lot is currently under quarantine hold (e.g., pending quality release, Out of Specification investigation, or regulatory hold). A quarantined reservation cannot be fulfilled until the hold is lifted.']
  reach_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reserved material lot has been verified as compliant with REACH regulation requirements at the time of reservation. Prevents issuance of non-compliant chemical stock to production.']
  requirement_date date [note: 'The date on which the reserved material is required for consumption by the planned event (production start date, delivery date, or maintenance execution date). Drives FEFO/FIFO lot selection and warehouse picking scheduling.']
  reservation_date date [note: 'The business date on which the stock reservation was created and the stock was earmarked. Represents the principal real-world event time for the reservation transaction.']
  reservation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reservation document number assigned by the ERP or WMS system (e.g., SAP reservation number). Used for cross-system reference and communication with warehouse operations.']
  reservation_reason_code varchar [note: 'Business reason code explaining why the reservation was created (e.g., scheduled production run, emergency maintenance, customer priority order, safety stock replenishment). Supports demand analysis and inventory planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled_production|emergency_maintenance|customer_priority|safety_stock|quality_sample|r_and_d_trial|regulatory_hold — promote to reference product]']
  reservation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the stock reservation. \'open\' indicates no goods movement has occurred; \'partially_fulfilled\' indicates some quantity has been issued; \'fulfilled\' indicates full quantity has been issued; \'cancelled\' indicates the reservation was voided before consumption.']
  reservation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the demand event driving the reservation. Distinguishes between production order consumption, customer delivery fulfillment, maintenance work order material staging, project-based withdrawal, or manually created reservations.']
  reserved_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of the material and lot earmarked for the planned consumption event, expressed in the base unit of measure. Prevents double-allocation of limited or expiring chemical stock.']
  sds_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the current Safety Data Sheet (SDS) for the reserved material has been verified as up-to-date and accessible prior to reservation fulfillment. Required for hazardous chemical handling compliance.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record that originated the reservation document (e.g., SAP EWM, SAP MM, SAP PP, SAP SD, SAP PM, or manually created). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the lakehouse.']
  special_stock_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates the stock category from which the reservation will be fulfilled. Distinguishes between unrestricted-use stock, quality inspection stock, blocked stock, restricted-use stock, consignment stock, or project stock. Critical for chemical inventory where quarantine and quality holds are common.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'SAP storage location code within the plant identifying the specific physical area (e.g., raw material warehouse, tank farm, hazmat storage, quarantine area) where the reserved stock is held.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for the reserved and fulfilled quantities (e.g., KG, L, MT, EA, BAG, DRUM). Aligns with the material master unit of measure in SAP MM.']
  valuation_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the valuation price is expressed (e.g., USD, EUR). Required for multi-currency inventory valuation in global chemical manufacturing operations.']
  valuation_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The standard or moving average price per unit of measure used to value the reserved stock at the time of reservation creation. Used for inventory valuation reporting and COGS calculation in SAP FI/CO.']

  Note: 'Record of a stock reservation that earmarks a specific quantity of a material and lot for a future planned consumption event such as a production order, customer delivery, or maintenance work order. Captures reservation number, material, lot, reserved quantity, unit of measure, requirement date, consuming order or delivery reference, reservation status (open, partially fulfilled, fulfilled, cancelled), and expiry date. Prevents double-allocation of limited or expiring chemical stock.'
}

Table inventory.tank_farm_level {
  tank_farm_level_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each tank farm level measurement record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Tank farm level readings track bulk liquid chemical inventory by product — essential for reorder point management, EPA/OSHA regulatory reporting, and REACH tonnage band compliance. Domain experts univ']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.equipment. Business justification: Link tank level measurements to the tank equipment record for maintenance monitoring and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  notification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.notification. Business justification: Tank level alarm breaches (high/low level alarms) in chemical plants automatically generate maintenance notifications in the CMMS via DCS/historian integration. Linking tank_farm_level readings to the']
  primary_tank_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record identifying the bulk liquid chemical currently stored in the tank at the time of measurement.']
  production_plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or site where the tank farm is located. Supports multi-site inventory tracking.']
  quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_specification. Business justification: Bulk chemical tank farm levels must be monitored against quality specifications (purity, concentration, pH limits). In chemical manufacturing, continuous quality monitoring of bulk storage is required']
  standard_cost_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.standard_cost. Business justification: Bulk liquid inventory valuation: tank farm inventory_value_usd must be computed from standard cost records for balance sheet reporting. Chemical manufacturing domain experts expect tank farm contents ']
  supply_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.supply_plan. Business justification: Tank farm levels for bulk liquid chemicals (solvents, acids, monomers) are the primary operational signal for supply plan execution. Planners monitor tank levels against supply plan replenishment trig']
  tank_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the storage tank asset record that this level measurement belongs to. Links to the tank master data entity in the inventory or asset domain.']
  below_reorder_point_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the current tank inventory mass is at or below the defined reorder point threshold (True = replenishment required, False = adequate stock). Enables real-time inventory replenishment alerting and integration with SAP MM MRP processes.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'The Chemical Abstracts Service (CAS) registry number uniquely identifying the chemical substance stored in the tank. Critical for regulatory reporting (TSCA, REACH, TRI), SDS linkage, and hazardous material compliance tracking.']
  data_collection_method varchar [note: 'Describes how this measurement record was collected: automatic (real-time instrument feed via PI historian), manual (operator-entered gauge reading), or interpolated (value estimated between two valid readings). Supports data quality governance and audit trail requirements.']
  density_kg_m3 decimal(10,4) [note: 'The density of the stored liquid at the measurement temperature, expressed in kilograms per cubic meter (kg/m³). Used to convert volume measurements to mass for inventory valuation and material balance. May be measured in-line or derived from temperature-corrected reference data.']
  ghs_hazard_category varchar [note: 'The GHS hazard category code for the stored chemical (e.g., Flammable Liquid Cat 1, Acute Toxicity Cat 3). Supports SDS compliance, emergency response documentation, and regulatory reporting under REACH, TSCA, and EPA requirements.']
  hazmat_storage_class varchar [note: 'The hazardous material storage class designation for the chemical stored in the tank, based on GHS/DOT classification (e.g., Class 3 Flammable Liquid, Class 8 Corrosive). Used for EHS compliance, emergency response planning, and segregation requirement enforcement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: class_1_explosive|class_2_gas|class_3_flammable_liquid|class_4_flammable_solid|class_5_oxidizer|class_6_toxic|class_7_radioactive|class_8_corrosive|class_9_misc — promote to reference product]']
  high_level_alarm boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the high-level alarm setpoint was active (True) or inactive (False) at the time of measurement. A high-level alarm signals that the tank level has reached or exceeded the high alarm threshold, requiring immediate operational attention to prevent overfill.']
  instrument_tag varchar [note: 'The P&ID instrument tag identifier for the level transmitter or gauge device that produced this measurement (e.g., LT-101, LIT-2204). Enables traceability to the physical instrument for calibration records, maintenance history, and instrument reliability analysis.']
  inventory_value_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'The estimated financial value of the bulk chemical inventory in the tank at the time of measurement, expressed in US Dollars. Calculated from mass_kg and the current standard or moving average cost from SAP FI/CO. Used for inventory valuation reporting and financial close processes.']
  level_m decimal(10,4) [note: 'The measured liquid level height in the tank expressed in meters, as reported by the level transmitter instrument and captured in the Aveva PI System historian. This is the primary raw measurement value for continuous inventory tracking.']
  level_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'The current tank fill level expressed as a percentage of the tank\'s maximum working capacity (0-100%). Provides an intuitive operational view of tank utilization for dashboards, alerts, and capacity planning without requiring knowledge of tank dimensions.']
  low_level_alarm boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the low-level alarm setpoint was active (True) or inactive (False) at the time of measurement. A low-level alarm signals that the tank level has dropped to or below the low alarm threshold, indicating potential supply shortage or pump cavitation risk.']
  mass_kg decimal(18,4) [note: 'The calculated mass of the liquid inventory in the tank expressed in kilograms, derived from volume and density. Used for material balance, production accounting, and regulatory emission calculations (e.g., EPA TRI, VOC reporting).']
  measurement_quality varchar [note: 'The data quality indicator for this measurement record as reported by the Aveva PI System historian (e.g., Good, Bad, Uncertain, Substituted). Reflects instrument health, communication status, or manual data substitution. Used to filter unreliable readings in analytics and reporting.']
  measurement_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating source system or method for this level measurement record. Distinguishes between automated PI historian readings, manual gauge readings entered by operators, DCS/SCADA system feeds, calculated values from strapping tables, or estimated values.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which the tank level measurement was recorded by the process historian or instrumentation system. This is the principal real-world event time for this observation record.']
  pi_tag_name varchar [note: 'The Aveva PI System historian tag identifier (e.g., SITE01.TK-101.LEVEL) that is the source of this measurement record. Enables traceability back to the originating instrument data stream in the process historian.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this tank farm level record was first created or ingested into the Silver Layer data lakehouse. Distinct from measurement_timestamp (the real-world event time). Used for data pipeline auditing, latency monitoring, and record lineage tracking.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this tank farm level record was last modified in the Silver Layer, for example due to a data quality correction, manual override, or reprocessing event. Supports change tracking and data lineage auditing.']
  reorder_point_kg decimal(18,4) [note: 'The inventory mass threshold in kilograms at which a replenishment order should be triggered for this tank. When mass_kg falls at or below this value, procurement or supply chain teams are alerted to initiate a purchase order or transfer. Supports continuous inventory management for bulk chemicals.']
  storage_temperature_max_c decimal(8,3) [note: 'The maximum allowable storage temperature for the chemical in this tank, expressed in degrees Celsius, as specified in the material SDS (Safety Data Sheet). Exceeding this threshold may indicate a safety hazard, product degradation risk, or regulatory non-compliance.']
  storage_temperature_min_c decimal(8,3) [note: 'The minimum allowable storage temperature for the chemical in this tank, expressed in degrees Celsius, as specified in the material SDS (Safety Data Sheet). Used to trigger temperature deviation alerts and ensure regulatory compliance with safe storage requirements.']
  tank_capacity_m3 decimal(18,4) [note: 'The nominal or maximum working capacity of the tank in cubic meters as defined in the tank design specification and strapping table. Stored with the measurement record to enable percentage calculations and capacity utilization reporting without requiring a join to the tank master.']
  tank_farm_name varchar [note: 'The name or designation of the tank farm area or storage cluster where the tank is physically located (e.g., North Tank Farm, Solvent Storage Area). Supports area-level inventory aggregation and reporting.']
  temperature_c decimal(8,3) [note: 'The temperature of the liquid in the tank at the time of measurement, expressed in degrees Celsius. Temperature is required for density correction, volume-to-mass conversion, and monitoring of temperature-sensitive chemicals to ensure safe storage conditions.']
  temperature_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the measured temperature at the time of this record was outside the defined safe storage temperature range (True = deviation detected, False = within range). Supports EHS compliance monitoring and automated alerting for temperature-sensitive chemical storage.']
  ullage_m decimal(10,4) [note: 'The available headspace (ullage) in the tank expressed in meters, calculated as the difference between the maximum allowable working level and the current measured level. Critical for safe filling operations, overfill prevention, and scheduling inbound deliveries.']
  ullage_volume_m3 decimal(18,4) [note: 'The available headspace volume in the tank expressed in cubic meters, representing the remaining capacity available for additional material. Used for logistics planning, delivery scheduling, and overfill risk management.']
  valuation_currency varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the inventory value is expressed (e.g., USD, EUR). Supports multi-currency reporting for global operations.']
  volume_liters decimal(18,4) [note: 'The calculated volume of liquid in the tank expressed in liters, derived from the level measurement and tank strapping table. Used for inventory quantity reporting and material balance calculations.']
  volume_m3 decimal(18,4) [note: 'The calculated volume of liquid in the tank expressed in cubic meters (m³). Provides SI unit representation for regulatory reporting, engineering calculations, and international trade documentation.']

  Note: 'Real-time and historical level measurement records for bulk liquid chemical storage tanks in tank farms. Captures tank identifier, material stored, measurement timestamp, level in meters, volume in liters or cubic meters, mass in kg, temperature at measurement, density, ullage (available headspace), high-level alarm status, low-level alarm status, and data source (Aveva PI System historian tag). Supports continuous inventory tracking for bulk chemicals without discrete lot tracking.'
}

Table inventory.inventory_adjustment {
  inventory_adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each inventory adjustment record in the Chemical Mfg lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.batch_record. Business justification: Inventory adjustments in chemical manufacturing are frequently triggered by batch yield variances or quality rejections. Linking to batch_record enables direct adjustment-to-batch traceability for var']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Adjustments affect financial valuation per product; linking to chemical_product enables audit trails, cost allocation, and regulatory impact analysis.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Links adjustments to cost centers for variance analysis and internal cost control reporting.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.equipment. Business justification: Adjustments caused by equipment failures or repairs; needed for audit trail linking inventory adjustments to affected equipment.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Period-end close controls: inventory adjustments must be posted in the correct fiscal period. Linking to fiscal_period enables automated period validation, prevents backdating violations, and supports']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Ensures each inventory adjustment posts to the appropriate GL account for financial statements and audit trails.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: SOX compliance and financial audit trail: every inventory adjustment (write-off, revaluation, shrinkage) generates a journal entry in chemical manufacturing. Linking enables direct traceability from i']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific production or procurement lot (batch) whose stock is being adjusted. Critical for FIFO/FEFO lot rotation compliance and traceability in chemical manufacturing.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material (raw material, intermediate, finished good, or packaging material) whose stock quantity is being adjusted. Corresponds to the SAP MM material master record.']
  mrp_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.mrp_run. Business justification: Inventory adjustments (write-offs of expired chemicals, spill losses, correction postings) change available stock and trigger MRP re-runs to recalculate net requirements. Linking adjustment to the sub']
