  original_adjustment_inventory_adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the original inventory adjustment record that this record reverses. Populated only when reversal_flag is True. Enables full reversal chain traceability for audit and SOX compliance.']
  quality_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Inventory adjustments for quality-related scrap, rework, or write-offs must reference the triggering quality deviation. This FK enables cost-of-quality reporting, regulatory traceability for material ']
  safety_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.safety_incident. Business justification: Inventory write-offs due to spills, fires, or contamination must reference the triggering safety incident for SOX compliance, insurance claims, and EHS cost tracking. The plain-text ehs_incident_refer']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: An inventory adjustment corrects a specific stock position\'s quantity. Adding stock_position_id FK to inventory.stock_position creates a direct link between adjustment records and the affected positio']
  usage_decision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.usage_decision. Business justification: Inventory adjustments triggered by QM usage decisions (scrapping rejected lots, releasing blocked stock) must reference the authorizing usage decision. This is a standard QM-MM integration point in ch']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse storage location, tank farm, or bin where the adjusted stock resides. Integrates with SAP EWM for warehouse operations and hazardous material storage compliance.']
  adjustment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the inventory adjustment record, tracking progression from initial draft through dual-authorization approval (required for SOX-controlled threshold adjustments) to final posting or reversal in SAP MM.']
  adjustment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world business event timestamp recording when the inventory adjustment was physically executed or the stock correction was applied. Distinct from the record creation audit timestamp. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inventory adjustment indicating the business reason category: write-off (stock reduction for loss/destruction), write-up (stock increase for found goods), count_variance (physical inventory count discrepancy posting), damage (goods damaged in storage or handling), obsolescence (expired or out-of-spec material), or revaluation (value correction). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: write_off|write_up|count_variance|damage|obsolescence|revaluation|transfer_correction|system_correction — promote to reference product]']
  approval_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the inventory adjustment received its final required authorization. Used for SOX compliance reporting and aging analysis of pending adjustments. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  capa_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number linking this inventory adjustment to an associated CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) record when the adjustment triggers or is part of a quality or process improvement action. Supports ISO 9001 quality management traceability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this inventory adjustment record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, SOX audit trails, and lakehouse ingestion tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the financial impact value is expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency financial reporting and consolidation.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the inventory adjustment document, typically matching the SAP MM material document number generated at the time of posting. Used for cross-referencing with ERP and audit trails.']
  financial_impact_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value impact of the inventory adjustment on the balance sheet, calculated as adjustment quantity multiplied by the standard or moving average price of the material. Positive for write-ups, negative for write-offs. Used for COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) reporting and SOX financial controls.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the material being adjusted is classified as hazardous under GHS, OSHA, or DOT regulations. When True, additional EHS (Environment Health and Safety) reporting and disposal compliance requirements apply to the adjustment.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the material being adjusted, as maintained in the SAP MM material master. Supports reporting and analytics without requiring a join to the material master table.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'SAP MM material number (alphanumeric code) identifying the specific chemical, raw material, intermediate, or finished good subject to the inventory adjustment. Enables direct cross-reference to SAP S/4HANA without relying solely on the surrogate key.']
  movement_type_code varchar [note: 'SAP MM movement type code that classifies the goods movement transaction (e.g., 551 for scrapping, 701 for physical inventory difference posting). Determines the accounting entries and stock update logic in SAP.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, instructions, or context provided by the requestor or approver regarding the inventory adjustment. Supports operational communication and audit documentation.']
  physical_inventory_document varchar [note: 'SAP MM physical inventory document number associated with this adjustment when the adjustment originates from a physical inventory count (cycle count or annual count). Null for non-count-related adjustments such as damage write-offs.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing site or distribution center where the inventory adjustment is being made. Supports multi-site inventory management and plant-level financial reporting.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting posting date on which the inventory adjustment is recorded in the general ledger and stock accounts in SAP FI/CO. May differ from the adjustment timestamp if adjustments are posted retroactively within an open period. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of material being adjusted, expressed as a positive value for stock increases (write-ups, found goods) or negative value for stock reductions (write-offs, damage, count shortages). Stored in the base unit of measure of the material.']
  quarantine_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the material lot being adjusted was in quarantine status (OOS – Out of Specification or OOT – Out of Trend hold) at the time of the adjustment. Supports QM (Quality Management) traceability and regulatory compliance.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code classifying the root cause or business justification for the inventory adjustment (e.g., PHYS_INV_DIFF for physical inventory difference, DAMAGE_STORAGE for storage damage, EXPIRY for expired material). Drives financial posting rules and regulatory reporting.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description providing additional context for the inventory adjustment beyond the standardized reason code. Captures specific circumstances such as spill details, damage cause, or discrepancy explanation for audit and CAPA purposes.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this inventory adjustment record is a reversal of a previously posted adjustment. True = this record reverses a prior adjustment; False = original adjustment. Supports audit trail integrity and double-entry accounting reconciliation.']
  sap_material_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP MM material document number generated when the inventory adjustment is posted in SAP S/4HANA. Serves as the primary cross-reference between the lakehouse silver layer record and the ERP system of record for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  sap_material_document_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year associated with the SAP MM material document number. Required because SAP material document numbers are unique only within a fiscal year; combined with sap_material_document_number provides a globally unique ERP reference.']
  sox_threshold_exceeded boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the financial impact value of this adjustment exceeds the SOX dual-authorization threshold, triggering the requirement for a secondary approver. True = dual authorization required and applied; False = single authorization sufficient.']
  sox_threshold_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary threshold value above which a second approver is required per SOX internal control policy. Stored on the record for audit traceability as thresholds may be updated over time.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'SAP storage location code (e.g., warehouse zone, tank farm area, quarantine area) within the plant where the adjusted material is stored. Supports hazardous material segregation compliance and EWM warehouse operations.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure in which the adjustment quantity is expressed (e.g., KG for kilograms, L for liters, MT for metric tons, DRUM for drum containers). Aligns with SAP MM base unit of measure for the material. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|LB|L|GAL|MT|EA|BAG|DRUM|IBC|TON — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this inventory adjustment record was last modified, supporting change tracking and SOX dual-authorization audit requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  valuation_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The unit price (standard cost or moving average price) used to calculate the financial impact value of the adjustment at the time of posting. Captured for audit traceability as prices may change over time.']

  Note: 'Record of inventory adjustments made to correct stock quantities outside of normal goods movement transactions, including write-offs, write-ups, count variance postings, and damage adjustments. Captures adjustment document number, material, lot, location, adjustment quantity (positive or negative), adjustment reason code, approver, approval date, financial impact value, and SAP MM material document reference. Requires dual authorization for adjustments above threshold values per SOX controls.'
}

Table inventory.consignment_stock {
  consignment_stock_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each consignment stock record in the Chemical Mfg inventory system. Primary key for the consignment_stock data product.']
  billing_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_schedule. Business justification: Consignment stock arrangements in chemical manufacturing are governed by billing schedules defining when consumption triggers invoicing. Linking consignment_stock to billing_schedule enables automated']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Consignment reporting and liability transfer depend on knowing the exact chemical product, supporting inventory valuation and compliance checks.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.contract. Business justification: Consignment stock arrangements in chemical manufacturing are governed by supply contracts defining holding limits, settlement terms, and liability transfer. Linking consignment_stock to the governing ']
  customer_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.customer_allocation. Business justification: Consignment stock at customer sites in chemical manufacturing is governed by customer allocation agreements that define quantity entitlements. The consignment stock record must reference the customer ']
  distributor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.distributor_agreement. Business justification: Consignment inventory management: chemical manufacturers place product at distributor locations under a specific distributor agreement governing ownership transfer, settlement terms, and liability. Th']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.goods_receipt. Business justification: Consignment stock receipts are documented via goods receipts in chemical manufacturing. Linking consignment_stock to goods_receipt enables settlement reconciliation and CoA verification; goods_receipt']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Consignment stock consumption in chemical manufacturing generates settlement invoices. consignment_stock.settlement_document_number is a denormalized invoice reference. Replacing it with a proper invo']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Consignment stock is tracked at the lot level — vendor-owned stock held at the manufacturer\'s premises is identified by lot/batch. The lot_number STRING column is denormalized; adding lot_id FK to inv']
  manufacturing_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.manufacturing_order. Business justification: Consignment stock consumed in chemical production must be linked to the manufacturing order that consumed it for liability transfer and settlement processing. Required for consignment settlement billi']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record for the chemical raw material, intermediate, or packaging material held on consignment. Corresponds to SAP MM material master.']
  price_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.price_agreement. Business justification: Consignment settlement pricing: consignment stock consumed by a distributor is invoiced under a specific price agreement. Chemical manufacturers require this link for automated consignment settlement ']
  quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_specification. Business justification: Consignment stock at customer sites must be managed against the agreed quality specification. coa_reference on consignment_stock is a plain text field. FK to quality_specification enables spec complia']
  sds_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.sds_record. Business justification: Consignment stock of hazardous chemicals held at customer sites requires a current SDS for OSHA HazCom, REACH, and local regulatory compliance. Chemical manufacturers must ensure the SDS version accom']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.site. Business justification: Needed for Consignment Stock Management to record the customer site where consigned inventory is stored, supporting inventory visibility and billing.']
  standard_cost_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.standard_cost. Business justification: Consignment stock valuation and liability recognition: consignment_value must be computed from standard cost for balance sheet and consignment settlement reporting. Chemical manufacturers must value c']
  storage_bin_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_bin. Business justification: consignment_stock has a storage_bin STRING column representing the bin where vendor-owned stock is held. Adding storage_bin_id FK to inventory.storage_bin normalizes this reference and enables bin-lev']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor (supplier) who legally owns the consignment stock until the agreed consumption trigger is met. Corresponds to the SAP MM vendor master record.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse storage location or tank farm bin where the consignment stock is physically held at the chemical manufacturer\'s premises. Corresponds to SAP EWM storage location.']
  aging_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the consignment stock has been held at the manufacturer\'s premises since receipt date. Used for vendor-managed inventory (VMI) performance monitoring, slow-moving stock identification, and consignment agreement compliance (maximum holding period enforcement).']
  coa_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the vendor-provided Certificate of Analysis (COA) accompanying the consignment lot. Confirms the material meets agreed quality specifications. Linked to LabWare LIMS for incoming quality verification.']
  consignment_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Current quantity of vendor-owned consignment stock on hand at the manufacturer\'s premises, expressed in the base unit of measure. Represents the unreceived (not yet financially owned) stock balance.']
  consignment_stock_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the consignment stock record. \'active\' = vendor-owned stock available for consumption; \'consumed\' = fully consumed and liability transferred; \'partially_consumed\' = partially drawn down; \'returned\' = returned to vendor; \'expired\' = past expiration date; \'quarantine\' = held pending quality disposition.']
  consignment_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total financial value of the current consignment stock on hand, calculated as consignment_quantity multiplied by valuation_price. Used for balance sheet disclosure of vendor-owned stock, financial reporting, and off-balance-sheet liability tracking under FASB ASC 330 and SOX.']
  consumed_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cumulative quantity of consignment stock that has been consumed (goods-issued to production or other cost centers) and for which financial liability has been transferred to the manufacturer. Used for settlement reconciliation and VMI performance reporting.']
  consumption_trigger varchar [note: 'The agreed business event that triggers financial liability transfer from vendor to manufacturer for consignment stock. Defines when ownership and payable obligation are created. Aligned with SAP MM consignment settlement process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consignment stock record was first created in the system, typically upon goods receipt posting in SAP MM. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial reporting compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the valuation price (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency financial reporting and foreign exchange revaluation under FASB and SOX.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the consignment material is no longer suitable for use per quality specifications or regulatory requirements. Drives FEFO lot rotation, quarantine triggers, and vendor return decisions. Critical for GMP and chemical stability compliance.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consignment stock material is classified as hazardous under GHS, DOT, or OSHA regulations. Drives storage compliance requirements, SDS documentation obligations, and EHS reporting under TSCA and REACH.']
  last_consumption_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent goods issue (consumption) from this consignment stock lot. Used for slow-moving stock analysis, VMI replenishment planning, and consignment agreement compliance monitoring.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the consignment stock record (e.g., quantity adjustment, status change, quarantine flag update). Supports data lineage, change audit, and Silver layer delta processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  liability_transfer_date date [note: 'Date on which financial ownership and payable liability for the consignment stock transferred from the vendor to the manufacturer, triggered by the agreed consumption event. Used for accounts payable accrual and financial period-end reporting under SOX and FASB ASC 330.']
  maximum_holding_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days the consignment stock may be held at the manufacturer\'s premises per the consignment agreement before automatic return or forced consumption. Breach of this threshold may trigger penalty clauses or mandatory settlement.']
  maximum_stock_level decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum quantity of consignment stock permitted at the manufacturer\'s premises per the VMI or consignment agreement. Prevents over-stocking of hazardous chemicals and ensures compliance with site storage limits under OSHA PSM and EPA RMP.']
  minimum_stock_level decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum quantity of consignment stock that must be maintained at the manufacturer\'s premises per the VMI agreement to ensure uninterrupted production supply. Triggers vendor replenishment when consignment_quantity falls below this threshold.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing site or facility where the consignment stock is physically located. Used for plant-level inventory reporting and regulatory site compliance.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP purchase order number associated with the consignment stock receipt. For consignment arrangements, this is typically a consignment PO (order type KE or KB in SAP MM) that governs the replenishment and settlement terms.']
  quarantine_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consignment stock lot is currently placed under quality quarantine and restricted from consumption pending laboratory testing or quality disposition. Integrates with LabWare LIMS quality hold status and SAP QM usage decision.']
  quarantine_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for placing the consignment stock under quarantine. OOS (Out of Specification), OOT (Out of Trend), vendor deviation, physical damage, contamination suspicion, pending COA (Certificate of Analysis), or regulatory hold. Supports CAPA initiation and quality investigation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oos_result|oot_result|vendor_deviation|damaged|contamination_suspect|pending_coa|regulatory_hold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date on which the vendor-owned consignment stock was physically received and placed at the manufacturer\'s premises. Used for aging calculation, FIFO/FEFO rotation, and consignment agreement compliance tracking.']
  retest_date date [note: 'Date by which the consignment lot must be retested by the quality laboratory to confirm continued conformance to specification before use. Common for chemical raw materials with defined shelf-life stability windows. Sourced from LabWare LIMS batch records.']
  returned_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of consignment stock returned to the vendor without consumption, typically due to quality rejection, expiration, or excess inventory. Recorded as a consignment return movement in SAP MM.']
  storage_temperature_max_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum permitted storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the consignment material as specified in the SDS or vendor COA. Critical for flammable, reactive, or thermally unstable chemical feedstocks.']
  storage_temperature_min_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Minimum required storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the consignment material as specified in the SDS or vendor COA. Ensures compliant storage conditions for temperature-sensitive chemicals and prevents degradation or hazardous reactions.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for the consignment quantity (e.g., KG for kilograms, L for litres, MT for metric tonnes, DRUM for drum containers). Aligned with SAP MM base UOM and chemical industry standard packaging units. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|LB|L|GAL|MT|TON|M3|BAG|DRUM|IBC — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  valuation_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Agreed unit price at which the consignment stock will be valued upon consumption and liability transfer. Used for inventory valuation, COGS calculation, and accounts payable settlement. Sourced from SAP MM purchasing info record or consignment agreement.']

  Note: 'Record tracking vendor-owned consignment stock held at the chemical manufacturer\'s premises but not yet financially owned. Captures consignment agreement reference, vendor, material, lot, quantity on consignment, storage location, receipt date, agreed consumption trigger, valuation price, liability transfer date, and aging days. Enables accurate financial reporting by distinguishing owned stock from consignment stock in SAP MM special stock management. Supports vendor-managed inventory (VMI) arrangements common in chemical feedstock supply.'
}

Table inventory.interplant_transfer {
  interplant_transfer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for interplant_transfer']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Transfer orders must reference the chemical product to calculate hazard handling, track REACH status, and generate correct shipping documentation.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Interplant transfers of chemicals require QM inspection lots at the receiving plant (goods receipt inspection). In chemical manufacturing, transferred materials must be re-inspected at the destination']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Project cost tracking for interplant movements: interplant transfers in chemical manufacturing are frequently funded by internal orders (plant turnarounds, capital projects). Linking enables cost trac']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Interplant transfers between legal entities in chemical manufacturing generate intercompany invoices for transfer pricing and cost allocation. Linking interplant_transfer to invoice enables intercompa']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Inter‑plant transfer is tied to a specific lot; replace free‑text lot_number with FK for traceability.']
  sds_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.sds_record. Business justification: DOT/ADR/IMDG hazmat transport regulations require SDS to accompany interplant transfers of hazardous chemicals. A direct FK from interplant_transfer to sds_record enables automated SDS attachment to s']
  sending_warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_location. Business justification: Inter‑plant transfer originates from a warehouse location; replace string with FK.']
  supply_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.supply_plan. Business justification: Interplant transfers in chemical manufacturing are planned supply movements between plants to balance inventory against regional demand. Transfers must reference the supply plan that authorized the mo']
  transfer_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.transfer_order. Business justification: interplant_transfer has transfer_order_number (STRING) referencing the associated warehouse transfer order. transfer_order is in the inventory domain. Adding transfer_order_id FK normalizes this refer']
  actual_goods_issue_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the goods issue posting was made in SAP MM, confirming physical departure of material from the sending plant. Used for transit time analysis and on-time shipment KPI reporting.']
  actual_goods_receipt_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the goods receipt posting was made in SAP MM at the receiving plant, confirming physical arrival and acceptance of the transferred material. Used for transit time calculation and inventory reconciliation.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Official bill of lading document number issued for the shipment. Serves as the legal contract of carriage and is required for hazardous material transport documentation under DOT regulations and customs compliance.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the logistics carrier or transport service provider responsible for moving the material between plants. Used for carrier performance tracking and freight cost allocation.']
  carrier_reference_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned reference number (e.g., bill of lading number, waybill number, or pro number) for the shipment. Used to cross-reference with Oracle Transportation Management and carrier tracking systems.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'SAP user ID of the person who created the stock transfer order in SAP S/4HANA. Required for audit trail, authorization control, and SOX compliance documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the stock transfer order was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA). Represents the business event of order initiation, not the ETL load time.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the transfer value (e.g., USD, EUR). Required for multi-currency inter-company transfer valuation and financial reporting under FASB and SOX.']
  delivery_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP outbound delivery document number created in SAP EWM/SD for the physical shipment execution of this stock transfer order. Links the STO to the warehouse execution and shipping documents.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Shelf-life expiration date of the transferred lot. Governs FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory rotation logic and ensures expired or near-expiry materials are flagged before transfer. Mandatory for regulated chemicals and pharmaceutical-grade materials.']
  hazmat_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the transferred material is classified as a hazardous material requiring special handling, labeling, and transport documentation under DOT, GHS, and IMDG regulations. Triggers mandatory SDS/MSDS documentation and emergency response procedures.']
  intransit_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of material currently in transit between the sending and receiving plants — goods issued but not yet goods-receipted. Represents the stock-in-transit position used for real-time inventory visibility and financial valuation of in-transit inventory.']
  issued_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity of material physically issued (goods issued) from the sending plant\'s inventory at the time of transfer. May differ from transfer_quantity due to partial shipments or measurement variances.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the stock transfer order record in the source system. Used for change tracking, audit trails, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Short descriptive name of the material being transferred as maintained in the SAP material master. Provides human-readable identification for operational and compliance reporting.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'SAP material master number uniquely identifying the chemical, raw material, intermediate, finished good, or packaging material being transferred. Serves as the primary material reference across SAP MM, PP, and QM modules.']
  planned_goods_issue_date date [note: 'Planned date on which goods are scheduled to be issued from the sending plant\'s inventory. Derived from the requested delivery date and transit lead time in SAP MM planning.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the stock transfer order, used to sequence fulfillment and logistics scheduling. Urgent transfers may be triggered by production line stoppages or critical inventory shortfalls.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP purchase order number associated with this stock transfer order for cross-company or third-party warehouse transfers. Links the STO to the procurement document for financial settlement and accounts payable processing.']
  quarantine_status varchar [note: 'Quality management status of the transferred material lot indicating whether it is available for unrestricted use or held under quarantine/quality hold pending inspection or testing. Integrates with SAP QM and LabWare LIMS quality disposition workflows.']
  received_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity of material confirmed as received (goods received) at the destination plant. Used to calculate transfer variances, identify transit losses, and trigger inventory reconciliation. Populated upon goods receipt posting in SAP MM.']
  receiving_plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the destination plant, distribution center, or external warehouse that will receive the transferred material. Corresponds to the receiving plant in SAP MM stock transfer processing.']
  receiving_plant_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the destination plant or facility receiving the stock transfer. Provides human-readable context for reporting and operational dashboards without requiring a join to the plant master.']
  receiving_storage_location varchar [note: 'SAP storage location code within the receiving plant where the material will be placed upon arrival. Identifies the target warehouse area, tank farm, or storage bin at the destination facility.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the transferred material is required to arrive at the receiving plant. Used for production planning, supply chain scheduling, and on-time delivery performance measurement.']
  sap_movement_type varchar [note: 'SAP MM movement type code governing the inventory and accounting postings for this transfer (e.g., 641 for goods issue for STO, 101 for goods receipt). Determines the financial and inventory impact in SAP S/4HANA.']
  sending_plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the originating plant or manufacturing facility from which the material is being transferred. Corresponds to the issuing plant in SAP MM stock transfer processing.']
  sending_plant_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the originating plant or facility issuing the stock transfer. Provides human-readable context for reporting and operational dashboards without requiring a join to the plant master.']
  special_stock_indicator varchar [note: 'SAP special stock type indicator identifying whether the transferred material is standard unrestricted stock or a special stock category such as consignment, project stock, or sales order stock. Affects inventory valuation and availability checking.']
  transfer_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Ordered quantity of material to be transferred from the sending plant to the receiving plant, expressed in the base unit of measure. Represents the planned transfer amount at order creation.']
  transfer_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason or justification for initiating the inter-plant stock transfer (e.g., production replenishment, inventory rebalancing, quality rework, customer order fulfillment). Used for operational analytics and cost allocation.']
  transfer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inter-plant stock transfer order. Tracks progression from creation through in-transit movement to receipt confirmation at the destination plant. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: created|in_transit|partially_received|received|confirmed|cancelled — promote to reference product if additional statuses are required]']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inter-plant transfer indicating the nature of the movement (e.g., plant-to-plant, plant-to-distribution center, cross-company transfer). Determines the SAP movement type and accounting treatment in FI/CO.']
  transfer_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the transferred material quantity based on the standard or moving average price at the sending plant. Used for inter-company or inter-plant inventory valuation and financial settlement in SAP FI/CO. Expressed in the company currency.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used to move the material between plants (e.g., road tanker, rail car, pipeline, barge). Relevant for hazardous material transport compliance under DOT 49 CFR and IATA/IMDG regulations for chemical shipments.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'UN four-digit identification number assigned to hazardous materials for transport classification (e.g., UN1993 for flammable liquids). Required on shipping documents and placards for hazardous chemical transport under DOT 49 CFR and IMDG regulations.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for the transfer quantity (e.g., KG for kilograms, L for liters, MT for metric tons, DRUM for drum containers). Aligns with SAP MM unit of measure configuration and chemical industry standard packaging units. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|LB|L|GAL|MT|EA|BAG|DRUM|IBC|TON — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Inter-plant or inter-warehouse stock transfer order record for moving materials between different plant locations, distribution centers, or external warehouses. Captures transfer order number, sending plant, receiving plant, material, lot, transfer quantity, unit of measure, requested delivery date, transport mode, carrier reference, in-transit quantity, and transfer status (created, in-transit, received, confirmed). Distinct from intra-warehouse transfer orders managed in SAP EWM.'
}

Table inventory.tank {
  tank_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for tank']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product.   equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.equipment. Business justification: In chemical manufacturing, every tank is a registered equipment asset in the CMMS for API 653 inspection scheduling, PSM compliance, and maintenance work order execution. A domain expert would immedia']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Fixed asset lifecycle management: storage tanks are capitalized fixed assets in chemical manufacturing. Linking enables depreciation tracking, insurance valuation, regulatory compliance (EPA/OSHA), an']
  hazmat_storage_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.hazmat_storage_rule. Adding hazmat_storage_rule_id FK to inventory.hazmat_storage_rule links each tank to its governing storag']
  manifolded_tank_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on tank (manifolded_tank_id)']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Tank farm management requires knowing which material_master record corresponds to the chemical stored in each tank for inspection scheduling, hazmat compliance, reorder triggering, and storage conditi']
  operating_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.operating_permit. Business justification: Individual tanks in chemical manufacturing are governed by specific air emission permits, storage permits, or Title V conditions. Linking each tank to its governing operating permit enables permit com']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical location or warehouse where the tank is installed.']
  capacity_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum volume the tank can hold, expressed in cubic metres.']
  certification_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the current certification.']
  cleaning_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Recommended interval between cleaning operations, in days.']
  compliance_certification varchar [note: 'Safety or environmental certification held by the tank.']
  corrosion_rate_mm_per_year decimal(6,2) [note: 'Estimated material loss due to corrosion per year.']
  current_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Current amount of material stored in the tank, in cubic metres.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the stored material, if applicable.']

  gps_longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the tank location.']
  hazardous_class varchar [note: 'Hazard classification of the material according to regulatory standards.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the tank was installed and became operational.']
  is_quarantined boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tank is under quarantine due to contamination or safety concerns.']
  last_cleaned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the tank was last cleaned.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent safety or regulatory inspection.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier of the material currently stored.']
  maintenance_status varchar [note: 'Current maintenance state of the tank.']
  next_inspection_due date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required inspection.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional remarks or observations.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Business department responsible for the tank.']
  pressure_psi decimal(10,2) [note: 'Real‑time internal pressure reading.']
  pressure_rating_psi decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable internal pressure, in pounds per square inch.']
  quarantine_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for placing the tank in quarantine.']
  safety_valve_present boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a safety relief valve is installed.']
  safety_valve_type varchar [note: 'Type of safety valve installed on the tank.']
  tank_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or label assigned to the tank.']
  tank_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the tank.']
  tank_type varchar [note: 'Category of tank based on its functional purpose.']
  temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Real‑time temperature reading inside the tank.']
  temperature_rating_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable operating temperature, in degrees Celsius.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for tank. Referenced by tank_id.'
}

Table sales.opportunity {
  opportunity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the sales opportunity record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account (industrial buyer, distributor, or end-user) associated with this opportunity. Links to the customer/account master data product.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary customer contact person (e.g., procurement manager, technical buyer) associated with this opportunity. Links to the contact master data product.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Opportunities in chemical mfg are pursued by a specific legal entity — required for export control (TSCA/REACH jurisdiction), intercompany deal routing, and statutory revenue recognition reporting.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Opportunities are tracked against profit centers for pipeline-to-revenue forecasting in chemical mfg financial planning. Finance uses this link to project expected revenue by profit center from the sa']
  quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_specification. Business justification: Opportunity qualification requires checking the product\'s quality specification to ensure regulatory compliance and customer requirements.']
  reach_dossier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.reach_dossier. Business justification: REACH compliance is a legal prerequisite for selling chemicals in the EU market. When reach_compliance_required=true on an opportunity, the sales team must verify a valid REACH dossier exists. FK to r']
  regulatory_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.regulatory_profile. Business justification: Chemical sales opportunities must be validated against the customer\'s regulatory profile (REACH registration, TSCA applicability, export licenses). Linking opportunity to regulatory_profile enables co']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Chemical sales pipeline reporting by market segment (automotive, electronics, agriculture) is standard practice. opportunity.application_segment is a denormalized text field; replacing with segment_id']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.site. Business justification: Opportunity delivery planning uses the Customer Site to calculate logistics, compliance (REACH/SDS) and shipping terms.']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales territory to which this opportunity is assigned. Supports territory-based pipeline reporting, quota management, and regional performance analytics.']
  actual_close_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the opportunity was closed (won or lost). Populated upon opportunity closure. Used for sales cycle analysis, win/loss reporting, and period attribution. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  close_probability_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated probability (0.00–100.00%) that this opportunity will be won, as assessed by the sales representative or derived from stage-based defaults. Used in weighted pipeline calculations and revenue forecasting models.']
  close_reason varchar [note: 'Structured reason code explaining why the opportunity was won or lost. Captures the primary decision criterion at closure. Used for competitive intelligence, win/loss analysis, and sales strategy refinement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Price|Technical Fit|Relationship|Competitor|Timeline|Budget|No Decision|Other — promote to reference product]']
  closed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual deal value at the time of opportunity closure (won or lost). May differ from estimated_amount due to negotiation, scope changes, or competitive pricing adjustments. Used for win/loss revenue analysis and competitive intelligence.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the opportunity record was first created in the source CRM system (Salesforce). Used for pipeline aging, sales cycle duration measurement, and audit trail. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  crm_opportunity_code varchar [note: 'Native opportunity identifier from Salesforce CRM (e.g., 006XXXXXXXXXXXXXXX). Used for cross-system traceability and reconciliation with the source system of record.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the estimated and closed deal amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency pipeline reporting and FX conversion in SAP FI/CO.']
  decision_criteria varchar [note: 'Free-text or structured description of the customer\'s primary evaluation criteria for this opportunity (e.g., price, technical performance, regulatory compliance, delivery reliability, sustainability). Supports win/loss analysis and sales strategy development.']
  estimated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total deal value (revenue) for this opportunity in the transaction currency. Represents the gross revenue expected if the opportunity is won. Used for pipeline valuation, quota tracking, and revenue forecasting. Aligns with SAP SD pricing and SAP FI/CO revenue planning.']
  estimated_volume_kg decimal(18,3) [note: 'Estimated annual or deal-period volume of chemical product(s) in kilograms associated with this opportunity. Used for supply chain capacity planning, raw material procurement forecasting, and production scheduling alignment with SAP PP.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter in which the opportunity is expected to close, formatted as Q[1-4]-YYYY (e.g., Q3-2024). Used for period-based pipeline reporting, quota attainment tracking, and financial forecasting aligned with SAP FI/CO fiscal calendar.']
  forecast_category varchar [note: 'Sales forecast classification assigned to the opportunity, indicating the confidence level for revenue recognition in the current period. Used in executive revenue forecasting and financial planning aligned with SAP FI/CO reporting.']
  is_key_account boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this opportunity is associated with a strategically designated key account (True) requiring executive sponsorship, dedicated account management, and priority resource allocation. Used for key account management reporting.']
  is_won boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the opportunity was closed as won (True) or lost/abandoned (False). Null if the opportunity is still open. Used for win rate calculations and pipeline conversion reporting.']
  last_activity_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent sales activity (call, email, meeting, task) logged against this opportunity in Salesforce CRM. Used for pipeline hygiene monitoring, stale opportunity identification, and sales rep engagement tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the opportunity record was last updated in the source CRM system (Salesforce). Used for change detection, incremental data loading, and audit trail. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  lead_source varchar [note: 'Channel or origin through which this opportunity was identified or generated. Used for marketing attribution, channel effectiveness analysis, and sales pipeline sourcing analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Inbound|Trade Show|Referral|Cold Outreach|Distributor|Web|Other — promote to reference product]']
  next_step varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the immediate next action required to advance this opportunity (e.g., \'Schedule technical demo\', \'Submit COA for customer qualification\', \'Negotiate pricing terms\'). Used by sales representatives for pipeline management and CRM activity tracking.']
  opportunity_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the opportunity context, customer requirements, technical application details, and strategic importance. Supports sales team knowledge sharing and opportunity review meetings.']
  opportunity_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the sales opportunity, typically describing the customer, product line, and deal context (e.g., \'Acme Corp - Specialty Solvents Q3 2024\'). Used for pipeline reporting and sales team communication.']
  opportunity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the opportunity by business development type. Distinguishes new customer acquisition from expansion of existing accounts, contract renewals, win-back of lost customers, and channel type (distributor vs. direct). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: New Business|Existing Business Expansion|Renewal|Win-Back|Distributor|Direct — promote to reference product]']
  product_line varchar [note: 'The primary chemical product line or portfolio segment associated with this opportunity (e.g., Specialty Solvents, Performance Polymers, Agricultural Chemicals, Adhesives). Used for product-level pipeline and revenue mix reporting.']
  reach_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the opportunity involves chemical substances subject to REACH registration, evaluation, or authorization requirements under EU regulation. Triggers regulatory compliance review and ECHA documentation workflow.']
  requires_custom_formulation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this opportunity requires a customer-specific chemical formulation or product development engagement (True) versus a standard catalogue product (False). Triggers R&D and technical sales involvement when True.']
  requires_sds boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has requested or requires a Safety Data Sheet (SDS) / Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) as part of the sales process. Relevant for GHS compliance and REACH regulatory submissions.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'The commercial channel through which this opportunity is being pursued. Distinguishes direct sales from distributor-mediated, agent-based, e-commerce, or tender/bid processes. Used for channel performance analytics and margin analysis.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this opportunity record was sourced (e.g., Salesforce CRM, SAP SD, Manual entry). Used for data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.']
  stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the opportunity in the sales pipeline lifecycle. Drives pipeline forecasting, stage-gate reviews, and funnel conversion analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Prospecting|Qualification|Needs Analysis|Proposal|Negotiation|Closed Won|Closed Lost — promote to reference product]']
  target_close_date date [note: 'Expected date by which the opportunity will be won or lost. Drives pipeline aging analysis, period-end forecasting, and sales cycle duration reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  tsca_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the opportunity involves chemical substances subject to TSCA inventory listing, reporting, or restriction requirements under US EPA regulation. Triggers regulatory compliance review.']

  Note: 'Core sales opportunity record tracking prospective deals with industrial chemical customers, distributors, and end-users. Captures opportunity stage progression, estimated deal value, probability of close, target close date, and owning sales representative. Includes opportunity-level product associations (per-product quantity, estimated revenue, competitive status) for pipeline visibility. Records structured win/loss outcome on closure (close reason, competitor displaced/displacing, decision criteria, deal value at close) for competitive intelligence. Sourced from Salesforce CRM. Primary pipeline management and forecasting entity for commercial sales.'
}

Table sales.quote {
  quote_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the price quotation record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for the quote entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or distributor to whom this quotation is issued. Links to the customer master record.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Chemical quotes are addressed to specific purchasing or technical contacts who have authority to accept pricing. Linking quote to contact enables proper document routing, COA/SDS delivery per contact ']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master pricing agreement or sales contract under which this quotation is issued, linking spot quotes to long-term contractual frameworks.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Quotes in chemical mfg are issued by a specific legal entity — critical for VAT/tax determination, export control compliance, and intercompany pricing. A domain expert would expect this link for statu']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Quote generation attaches SDS, REACH, TSCA compliance data; linking to material_master provides authoritative source for those attributes.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Salesforce CRM sales opportunity from which this quotation was generated, enabling pipeline-to-revenue traceability and win/loss analysis.']
  organization_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP SD sales organization responsible for issuing this quotation, defining the legal selling entity and pricing hierarchy.']
  price_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.price_agreement. Business justification: While quote_line already carries a price_agreement_id at the line level, a quote header may be governed by a single overarching price agreement (e.g., a long-term supply agreement or contract price). ']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Chemical mfg quotes are priced and margin-reported by profit center (e.g., Performance Materials vs. Ag Solutions). Revenue recognition and contribution margin reporting in CO-PA require knowing which']
  qualification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.qualification. Business justification: Chemical quotes must reference the customer qualification record confirming the product grade/spec is approved for that customer\'s use case. Quoting against an unqualified product-customer combination']
  quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_specification. Business justification: Chemical quotes must reference the quality specification defining purity, grade, and acceptance criteria for the product being sold. This drives CoA/CoC issuance and ensures the customer receives exac']
  regulatory_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.regulatory_profile. Business justification: Chemical quotes require regulatory validation at generation time — export control flags, REACH compliance, and SDS version requirements are driven by the customer\'s regulatory profile. This link enabl']
  sds_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sds. Business justification: Every chemical quote must reference the current SDS version for OSHA HazCom and REACH regulatory compliance. The sds_version plain-text field is denormalized; FK to sds ensures the correct SDS documen']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.site. Business justification: Quotes must reference the specific Customer Site for accurate pricing, tax, incoterms and regulatory documentation.']
  coa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer requires a Certificate of Analysis (COA) to accompany the chemical product shipment, confirming that the product meets specified quality parameters. Drives LabWare LIMS documentation workflow.']
  coc_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer requires a Certificate of Compliance (COC) confirming the product meets regulatory, contractual, or customer-specific specification requirements.']
  converted_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP SD sales order document number created when this quotation is accepted and converted to a binding sales order, enabling end-to-end quote-to-cash traceability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this quotation record was first captured in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  crm_quote_number varchar [note: 'The Salesforce CRM quote object identifier, enabling bidirectional traceability between the CRM pipeline and the SAP SD quotation document.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary values on this quotation are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  customer_po_number varchar [note: 'Customer\'s own Purchase Order (PO) reference number provided with the RFQ or acceptance, required for invoice matching and customer accounts payable reconciliation.']
  distribution_channel_code varchar [note: 'SAP SD distribution channel code indicating the route to market for this quotation (e.g., direct, distributor, e-commerce), affecting pricing and availability determination.']
  division_code varchar [note: 'SAP SD division code representing the product line or business unit (e.g., Performance Materials, Agricultural Solutions, Plastics) responsible for this quotation.']
  export_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any product in this quotation is subject to export control regulations, triggering mandatory compliance review before order conversion (e.g., EAR, ITAR, dual-use chemical controls).']
  gross_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total gross value of the quotation before application of discounts, surcharges, and taxes, expressed in the quotation currency. Supports margin analysis and revenue forecasting.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quoted products are classified as hazardous materials under DOT, IATA, or IMDG regulations, requiring special packaging, labeling, and transport documentation.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost allocation between seller and buyer for chemical product shipments.']
  incoterms_location varchar [note: 'Named place or port associated with the Incoterms code, specifying the exact point of delivery or risk transfer (e.g., \'FOB Houston Port\'). Required for complete Incoterms specification per ICC rules.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent modification to this quotation record, supporting change tracking and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  net_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net total value of the quotation after all discounts and surcharges but before tax, representing the agreed commercial value. Core metric for margin analysis at the quote level.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'SAP SD payment terms key defining the agreed payment schedule, early payment discount conditions, and due date calculation for the customer invoice (e.g., NT30, 2/10NET30).']
  price_list_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pricing basis applied to this quotation, determining which SAP SD condition records and pricing procedures are used for calculation.']
  pricing_date date [note: 'The reference date used to determine applicable price lists, condition records, and raw material index values for pricing calculation. May differ from quote_date for forward-priced contracts.']
  quote_date date [note: 'The business date on which the quotation was formally issued to the customer or distributor. Represents the principal real-world event date for this transaction.']
  quote_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the quotation, tracking progression from initial draft through customer acceptance or expiry. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|expired|converted|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  quote_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the quotation by commercial purpose: standard customer quote, spot market offer, contract pricing, distributor channel, intercompany transfer, or product sample. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|spot|contract|distributor|intercompany|sample — promote to reference product]']
  reach_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all chemical substances in this quotation are registered and compliant under REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 for the destination market, required for EU export.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason for quotation rejection or loss, populated when quote_status transitions to rejected or cancelled. Supports win/loss analysis and competitive intelligence. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: price_too_high|competitor_selected|spec_mismatch|delivery_terms|no_decision|budget_cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Customer-requested delivery date for the quoted chemical products, used for production scheduling, ATP (Available-to-Promise) checks, and logistics planning.']
  sales_district_code varchar [note: 'SAP SD sales district code identifying the geographic territory assignment for this quotation, used for territory management, sales performance reporting, and commission calculation.']
  sap_quotation_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known 10-digit SAP SD quotation document number used for cross-system reference and customer-facing communication.']
  ship_to_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the destination country for chemical product delivery. Drives export control screening, REACH compliance checks, and hazardous material transport regulations.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total tax amount applicable to the quotation, including VAT, GST, or applicable excise duties on chemical products, calculated per the customer\'s tax jurisdiction.']
  technical_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing technical sales notes, application guidance, customer-specific formulation requirements, or special handling instructions relevant to the quoted chemical products.']
  total_discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Aggregate monetary discount applied across all line items on the quotation, including volume rebates, customer-specific pricing adjustments, and promotional discounts.']
  total_surcharge_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Aggregate surcharges applied at the header level, including energy surcharges, raw material index surcharges, hazardous material handling fees, and freight surcharges.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'The start date from which the quoted prices, terms, and conditions are binding and may be accepted by the customer.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'The expiry date after which the quoted prices and terms are no longer binding. Drives automated expiry workflows and renewal alerts.']
  win_probability_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Sales representative\'s estimated probability (0.00–100.00%) that this quotation will be accepted and converted to a sales order, used for pipeline forecasting and revenue planning.']

  Note: 'Formal price quotation issued to a customer or distributor for chemical products, including header-level terms (validity, incoterms, payment terms) and line-level detail (product/grade, quantity, UOM, per-line pricing, discounts, surcharges, COA/COC requirements). Supports RFQ response workflows and technical sales negotiations. Linked to SAP SD quotation and Salesforce CRM quote object. Enables granular margin analysis at the product-grade level.'
}

  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Revenue posting requires each quote line to map to a GL account for accurate financial reporting and downstream invoicing.']
  grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grade. Business justification: Chemical grade (USP, ACS, technical) is a primary commercial differentiator on quote lines, driving price tier, regulatory compliance requirements, and COA/COC obligations. The product_grade plain-tex']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Available-to-promise quoting: in chemical manufacturing, quote lines for spot sales or batch-specific products reference a specific inventory lot to confirm availability, purity, COA/COC details, and ']
  packaging_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.packaging_config.   price_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer-specific pricing agreement, contract price list, or framework agreement that governs the unit price on this quote line. Links to long-term supply agreements and volume rebate contracts.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center.   safety_data_sheet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.safety_data_sheet.   sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Quote lines in chemical manufacturing are written at SKU level (specific pack size/configuration). The sku_code plain-text field is a denormalized reference; a proper FK to sku enables automated SDS v']
  batch_managed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the chemical product on this quote line is subject to batch management and lot traceability requirements. Batch-managed products require Certificate of Analysis (COA) per lot and full GMP traceability.']
  coa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer requires a Certificate of Analysis (COA) to accompany the shipment for this quote line. COA documents the actual quality test results for the specific production lot against specification limits.']
  coc_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer requires a Certificate of Compliance (COC) confirming the product meets all specified regulatory and contractual requirements. Common for food-contact, pharmaceutical, and automotive-grade chemicals.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'Seller-confirmed delivery date for this quote line after Available-to-Promise (ATP) check and production capacity validation. May differ from the requested delivery date.']
  contribution_margin decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross contribution margin for this quote line, calculated as line net amount minus total standard cost. Expressed in the transaction currency. Enables profitability analysis at the product-grade level during the quoting process. Commercially sensitive.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this quote line record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail, quote aging analysis, and SLA tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values on this quote line (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Enables multi-currency quoting for international chemical trade.']
  delivery_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Estimated number of calendar days from order confirmation to delivery at the agreed delivery point for this quote line. Accounts for production scheduling, inventory availability, and logistics transit time.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Absolute monetary value of the discount applied to this quote line in the transaction currency. Derived from discount_percent applied to line_gross_amount. Stored explicitly for audit and margin reporting.']
  discount_percent decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to this quote line, representing negotiated price reductions from the list price. Includes volume discounts, customer-specific pricing agreements, and promotional discounts. Commercially sensitive.']
  hazard_class varchar [note: 'Globally Harmonized System (GHS) hazard classification for the chemical product on this quote line (e.g., Flammable Liquid Class 3, Corrosive, Toxic). Drives hazmat surcharge applicability, packaging requirements, and transport documentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FLAMMABLE_LIQUID|FLAMMABLE_SOLID|OXIDIZER|TOXIC|CORROSIVE|EXPLOSIVE|COMPRESSED_GAS|ENVIRONMENTAL_HAZARD|NON_HAZARDOUS — promote to reference product]']
  hazmat_surcharge decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional surcharge applied to this quote line for hazardous chemical handling, packaging, labeling, and transport compliance costs. Applicable for products classified under DOT, IATA, or IMDG dangerous goods regulations.']
  incoterm_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between seller and buyer for this quote line. Affects freight cost allocation and logistics planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this quote line record, in ISO 8601 format. Tracks revisions during negotiation cycles and supports change audit requirements.']
  line_gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary value of this quote line before any discounts or surcharges, calculated as quantity multiplied by unit price in the transaction currency. Used as the baseline for margin and discount analysis.']
  line_net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary value of this quote line after applying all discounts and surcharges (gross amount minus discounts plus surcharges) in the transaction currency. Represents the actual quoted value to the customer for this line.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential position of this line item within the parent quote, used for ordering and referencing individual items. Corresponds to SAP SD item number (e.g., 10, 20, 30).']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of this individual quote line item. Allows partial acceptance or rejection of lines within a multi-line quote. Draft: under preparation; Open: submitted to customer; Revised: updated after customer feedback; Accepted: customer confirmed; Rejected: customer declined; Cancelled: withdrawn by seller.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'SAP material number (MATNR) identifying the specific chemical product or grade being quoted. Used for cross-referencing with SAP MM and PP modules for production planning and inventory availability.']
  price_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure basis for the unit price (e.g., price per KG, price per MT). May differ from the sales UOM when pricing is expressed per metric ton but sold in drums. Required for accurate line value calculation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|MT|L|GAL|LB|DRUM|IBC|BAG|TON|M3 — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  product_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the chemical product or grade being quoted, including trade name, grade designation, and key specification identifiers as presented to the customer.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of the chemical product being quoted on this line. Expressed in the unit of measure specified in the uom field. Supports bulk chemical quantities in kg, MT, L, or container counts.']
  reach_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the chemical product on this quote line is compliant with REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals) regulation for the destination market. Required for EU export quoting.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Customer-requested delivery date for the product quantity on this quote line. Used for production planning, Available-to-Promise (ATP) checking, and logistics scheduling.']
  sds_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Safety Data Sheet (SDS) must be provided to the customer with this quote line. Mandatory for hazardous chemical substances under GHS/OSHA HazCom 2012 and REACH regulations.']
  small_order_surcharge decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional surcharge applied when the quoted quantity falls below the Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) threshold for this product. Covers incremental handling and packaging costs for sub-MOQ orders.']
  standard_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Internal standard cost per unit of measure for the chemical product on this line, sourced from SAP CO (Controlling). Used to calculate contribution margin and Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) at the quote line level. Commercially sensitive internal data.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'SAP storage location code within the delivering plant from which the chemical product will be sourced for this quote line. Relevant for inventory reservation and warehouse management.']
  tsca_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the chemical substance on this quote line is listed on the TSCA Chemical Substance Inventory and compliant with applicable TSCA requirements for US domestic sales.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'United Nations dangerous goods number (e.g., UN1090 for Acetone) assigned to the hazardous chemical on this quote line. Required for transport documentation, hazmat labeling, and emergency response planning under DOT and IMDG regulations.']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quoted price per unit of measure for the chemical product on this line, expressed in the transaction currency. Represents the base list or negotiated price before surcharges and discounts. Commercially sensitive pricing data.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quoted quantity (e.g., KG - kilograms, MT - metric tons, L - litres, GAL - gallons, LB - pounds, DRUM - 200L drum units, IBC - intermediate bulk containers, BAG - bag units, TON - short tons, M3 - cubic meters). Critical for chemical pricing and logistics planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|MT|L|GAL|LB|DRUM|IBC|BAG|TON|M3 — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a customer quote, representing a specific chemical product, grade, or SKU with its own pricing, quantity, unit of measure (kg, MT, L, drums), and technical specification reference. Captures per-line discount, surcharges (e.g., hazmat handling, small-order), and COA/COC requirements. Enables granular margin analysis at the product-grade level.'
}

Table sales.price_agreement {
  price_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the negotiated pricing agreement record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or distributor with whom this pricing agreement is established. Links to the customer master in the sales domain.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Price agreements are legally binding contracts executed by a specific legal entity — essential for intercompany pricing, tax jurisdiction assignment, and REACH/TSCA regulatory compliance in chemical m']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Pricing contracts are material‑specific; linking price_agreement to material_master enables material‑based price terms and regulatory clauses.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: In chemical manufacturing sales, negotiated price agreements (long-term supply agreements, contract prices) are frequently originated from a specific sales opportunity. Linking price_agreement back to']
  organization_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP SD sales organization responsible for this pricing agreement, defining the legal selling entity and regional scope.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Price agreements in chemical mfg are negotiated at profit center level for margin management and revenue planning. Finance uses this link to report contracted revenue and discount exposure by business']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.site. Business justification: Chemical price agreements are frequently negotiated for specific delivery sites (bulk tank farms, manufacturing plants) with site-specific incoterms and logistics costs. Site-level pricing is standard']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: price_agreement currently stores territory_code as a denormalized STRING. Price agreements in chemical manufacturing are frequently scoped to specific sales territories (e.g., regional pricing for a p']
  agreement_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or title of the pricing agreement as referenced internally and in customer negotiations (e.g., \'FY2025 Polyethylene Supply Agreement - Acme Corp\').']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the pricing agreement, typically matching the SAP SD condition contract number or Salesforce CRM contract number. Used in customer-facing communications and purchase orders.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the pricing agreement. Draft = under preparation; Pending Approval = submitted for internal authorization; Active = in force and applicable to orders; Suspended = temporarily halted; Expired = past effective end date; Terminated = ended before expiry by mutual or unilateral action; Cancelled = voided before activation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|active|suspended|expired|terminated|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pricing agreement by commercial structure. Contract Price = fixed negotiated unit price; Volume Rebate = retrospective rebate based on volume thresholds; Long-Term Supply = multi-year supply commitment with pricing; Spot Price = short-term market-based pricing; Distributor Price = channel partner pricing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: contract_price|volume_rebate|long_term_supply|spot_price|distributor_price|framework — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  annual_volume_commitment decimal(18,3) [note: 'The total annual volume the customer has committed to purchase under this agreement, expressed in the price UOM. Used to validate volume rebate eligibility and supply planning. Corresponds to long-term supply agreement volume commitments.']
