Business justification: Distributor agreements are executed by a specific legal entity — governing_law_country and tax obligations require this link. Chemical mfg compliance teams use it for REACH downstream reporting and cr']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Distribution agreements authorize a distributor to sell particular chemicals; need FK to material_master to enforce product scope and compliance.']
  organization_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Chemical Mfg sales organization responsible for managing and executing this distributor agreement, as defined in SAP SD organizational structure.']
  price_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.price_agreement. Business justification: A distributor agreement in chemical manufacturing governs the commercial relationship including pricing terms. Linking distributor_agreement to the governing price_agreement enables traceability betwe']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Distributor agreements generate channel revenue tracked to profit centers. Chemical mfg P&L reporting by distribution channel requires this link to attribute net revenue (after rebates) to the correct']
  quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_specification. Business justification: Distributor agreements in chemical manufacturing contractually bind distributors to maintain and communicate specific quality standards (GHS, REACH, SDS obligations). The quality specification defines']
  reach_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.reach_registration. Business justification: distributor_agreement.reach_downstream_reporting_required flag confirms REACH obligations exist. Under EU REACH Articles 31-32, distributors must pass SDS and registration information downstream; the ']
  safety_data_sheet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.safety_data_sheet. Business justification: REACH Article 31 and OSHA HazCom legally require chemical manufacturers to ensure distributors receive and pass on the current SDS. Distributor agreements must formally reference the applicable SDS ve']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: distributor_agreement has territory_scope and territory_country_code as STRING fields but no FK to the territory master. Distribution agreements in chemical manufacturing are scoped to specific territ']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable agreement reference number assigned by SAP SD (Sales and Distribution) at contract creation. Used in all correspondence, purchase orders (POs), and regulatory submissions with the distributor.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the distributor agreement. Draft: under negotiation; Active: fully executed and binding; Suspended: temporarily halted (e.g., compliance breach); Terminated: ended before natural expiry; Expired: reached end date without renewal.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the distribution arrangement: exclusive (sole distributor in territory), non_exclusive (multiple distributors permitted), master (umbrella agreement with sub-agreements), sub (child of a master agreement), or trial (limited-term evaluation agreement).']
  agreement_version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the distributor agreement, incremented each time the agreement is formally amended (e.g., price renegotiation, territory expansion, MOQ revision). Version 1 is the original executed agreement.']
  amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal amendment to this distributor agreement. Null if no amendments have been made since original execution. Used with agreement_version for change tracking.']
  amendment_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the most recent amendment to this distributor agreement, summarizing the key changes made (e.g., \'Territory expanded to include CAN; MOQ increased to USD 500,000\'). Supports audit trail and Management of Change (MOC) documentation.']
  authorized_product_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of Chemical Mfg product category codes or names that the distributor is authorized to sell under this agreement (e.g., \'Performance Coatings, Agricultural Chemicals, Specialty Polymers\'). Governs which SKUs can be ordered against this agreement in SAP SD.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distributor agreement automatically renews upon expiry unless either party provides written notice of termination within the notice period. Drives renewal workflow automation in Salesforce CRM.']
  coa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Certificate of Analysis (COA) must accompany each product shipment to the distributor under this agreement, confirming that the batch meets specification. Supports GMP and ISO 9001 quality documentation requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this distributor agreement record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Populated at insert time and never updated. Supports SOX audit trail requirements.']
  credit_limit_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding accounts receivable credit extended to the distributor under this agreement, denominated in USD. Enforced in SAP SD credit management (FD32) to control financial exposure.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all financial terms of this agreement (MOQ, rebates, pricing) are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency reporting in SAP FI/CO.']
  dispute_resolution_mechanism varchar [note: 'Contractually agreed mechanism for resolving disputes arising under this distributor agreement: arbitration (binding third-party arbitration), litigation (court proceedings), mediation (non-binding third-party mediation), or negotiation (direct party negotiation).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the distributor agreement becomes legally binding and commercially operative. Aligns with SAP SD contract validity start date.']
  ehs_training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether distributor personnel are required to complete Chemical Mfg-mandated EHS (Environment Health and Safety) training on product handling, storage, and emergency response as a condition of this agreement.']
  exclusivity_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the breadth of exclusivity granted to the distributor within the authorized territory: full_portfolio (all Chemical Mfg products), product_category (specific product categories only), single_product (one product or SKU), or none (non-exclusive arrangement).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the distributor agreement naturally expires. Nullable for open-ended evergreen agreements. Used to trigger renewal workflows and compliance reviews.']
  governing_law_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction whose laws govern interpretation and enforcement of this distributor agreement (e.g., USA, DEU, GBR). Critical for dispute resolution and regulatory compliance.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between Chemical Mfg and the distributor for shipments under this agreement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  moq_annual_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum annual purchase commitment in US dollars that the distributor must achieve to maintain agreement terms and qualify for contracted pricing and rebates. Failure to meet MOQ may trigger agreement renegotiation or termination. Aligns with SAP SD minimum order value configuration.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'SAP SD payment terms key (e.g., \'NT30\', \'NT60\', \'2/10NT30\') governing invoice due dates and early payment discount conditions for orders placed under this agreement.']
  product_application_support boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Chemical Mfg provides technical sales and product application guidance support to the distributor under this agreement, including customer-specific formulation assistance and technical documentation.']
  reach_downstream_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the distributor must fulfill REACH downstream user reporting obligations, including use notifications and exposure scenario communication, as required under ECHA REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 Title V.']
  rebate_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Base rebate percentage (0.00–100.00) applied to eligible purchases under this agreement. For volume_tiered structures, this represents the base tier rate; tier thresholds are managed in the rebate schedule. Stored in SAP SD rebate agreement condition records.']
  rebate_structure_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rebate mechanism applicable to this distributor agreement: volume_tiered (rebate percentage increases with purchase volume tiers), flat_rate (fixed percentage on all purchases), growth_incentive (rebate tied to year-over-year growth targets), or none (no rebate applicable).']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Date by which a renewal decision must be made or the agreement auto-renews. Drives CRM (Salesforce) renewal opportunity creation and account manager notifications.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days prior to expiry_date by which either party must provide written notice of non-renewal or termination. Typically 30, 60, or 90 days as negotiated.']
  responsible_care_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the distributor is contractually required to adhere to the ACC (American Chemistry Council) Responsible Care program standards as a condition of this distribution agreement.']
  sap_contract_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA SD contract document number (transaction VA41/VA42) corresponding to this distributor agreement. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the CRM pipeline and ERP order management.']
  sds_distribution_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the distributor is contractually obligated to distribute Safety Data Sheets (SDS) to downstream customers for all chemical products sold under this agreement. Mandatory under GHS, REACH, and OSHA HazCom 2012 (29 CFR 1910.1200).']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date on which both parties executed (signed) the distributor agreement. May differ from effective_date when agreements are backdated or post-dated.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the agreement was terminated early (before natural expiry). Null if the agreement has not been terminated. Distinct from expiry_date which represents the scheduled end date.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for early termination of the distributor agreement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mutual_agreement|breach_of_contract|moq_failure|compliance_violation|business_exit|acquisition|other — promote to reference product]']
  territory_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code representing the primary country of the authorized distribution territory. Used for REACH downstream user reporting and EPA/TSCA jurisdictional compliance tracking.']
  territory_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic scope within which the distributor is authorized to sell Chemical Mfg products under this agreement. May be expressed as country codes (ISO 3166-1 alpha-3), regions, or named sales territories (e.g., \'USA,CAN,MEX\' or \'EMEA-West\'). Supports territory management analytics in Salesforce CRM.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this distributor agreement record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Updated on every modification. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.']

  Note: 'Formal distribution agreement governing the commercial relationship between Chemical Mfg and an authorized distributor. Captures agreement type (exclusive, non-exclusive), authorized product categories, territory scope, minimum purchase commitments (MOQ), rebate structure, compliance obligations (SDS distribution, REACH downstream user reporting), and agreement validity period.'
}

Table sales.rebate_agreement {
  rebate_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the sales rebate agreement record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or distributor party with whom this rebate agreement is established. Links to the customer master.']
  accrual_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Rebate accruals post to specific GL accounts for period-close journal entries. accrual_account_code is a denormalized plain column that should be a proper FK to gl_account for accurate financial posti']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Rebate accruals in chemical mfg are charged to specific cost centers for period-close accounting. cost_center_code is a denormalized plain column on sales_rebate_agreement that should be replaced by a']
  distributor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.distributor. Business justification: The sales_rebate_agreement product description explicitly states it covers rebate agreements with \'a customer or distributor\'. While account_id covers the customer side, there is no FK to distributor ']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Rebate settlement and accrual cycles must align with fiscal periods for period-close accuracy. Chemical mfg finance requires this link to ensure rebate postings fall in the correct accounting period.']
  hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product hierarchy or product group that defines the eligible product scope for this rebate agreement (e.g., specialty chemicals, performance materials, agricultural solutions).']
  organization_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP SD sales organization responsible for administering this rebate agreement, defining the selling entity and currency context.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Rebate settlements are tracked against profit centers for P&L impact reporting by business segment. Chemical mfg finance teams require this link to report net revenue after rebates at the profit cente']
  salesforce_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associated Salesforce CRM opportunity or contract record that originated or tracks this rebate agreement. Enables cross-system traceability between CRM pipeline and SAP SD rebate execution.']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: sales_rebate_agreement currently stores territory_code as a denormalized STRING. Rebate agreements in chemical manufacturing are scoped to specific sales territories (e.g., a regional distributor reba']
  accrual_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to recognize and accrue rebate liability over the agreement period. Accrual-based: rebate liability is recognized as sales occur; Cash basis: recognized only upon settlement payment; Manual: accruals are posted manually by finance. Aligns with SAP FI/CO accrual posting logic.']
  accrued_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative rebate liability accrued to date under this agreement based on eligible customer purchases. Updated periodically via SAP SD rebate settlement runs. Expressed in payout currency. Represents the current outstanding rebate obligation.']
  agreement_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing commercial negotiation context, special conditions, customer-specific terms, or exceptions not captured in structured fields. Used by sales and finance teams for agreement administration.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the rebate agreement, typically sourced from SAP SD rebate agreement number (BO01/BO02 agreement number field). Used in customer-facing communications and settlement documents.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the rebate agreement. Draft: under negotiation; Active: binding and accruing; Suspended: temporarily paused; Settled: final settlement completed; Expired: past end date without renewal; Cancelled: terminated before end date.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rebate agreement by commercial incentive type. Volume rebate rewards cumulative purchase volume; growth incentive rewards year-over-year growth; market development fund (MDF) supports distributor marketing activities; distributor rebate applies to channel partners; loyalty rebate rewards long-term customers; performance rebate ties payout to KPI achievement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: volume_rebate|growth_incentive|market_development_fund|distributor_rebate|loyalty_rebate|performance_rebate — promote to reference product]']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the rebate agreement was formally approved by the authorized approver. Marks the transition from draft to active status and establishes the audit trail for financial commitment.']
  baseline_period_end date [note: 'End date of the reference period used to establish the baseline purchase volume or revenue for growth incentive rebate measurement. Together with baseline_period_start, defines the comparison window.']
  baseline_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the reference period used to establish the baseline purchase volume or revenue against which growth performance is measured for growth incentive rebate agreements.']
  calculation_basis varchar [note: 'Defines the metric used to measure customer performance and calculate rebate entitlement. Revenue: net invoiced sales value; Volume: quantity of product units; Quantity: number of orders or transactions; Weight (kg): metric weight of chemical products shipped; Mixed: combination of revenue and volume thresholds.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Commercial channel through which the customer purchases eligible products. Direct: sold directly by Chemical Mfg sales force; Distributor: sold through authorized chemical distributors; Dealer: regional dealer network; OEM: original equipment manufacturer purchasing for incorporation; Ecommerce: digital channel purchases.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rebate agreement record was first created in the system, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Provides audit trail for record origination and SOX compliance.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Calendar date on which the rebate agreement becomes binding and eligible purchases begin accruing toward rebate thresholds. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Calendar date on which the rebate agreement expires and no further accruals are recognized. Nullable for open-ended agreements. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.']
  eligible_product_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the breadth of products eligible for rebate accrual under this agreement. All products: entire customer portfolio; Product group: defined SAP material group; Specific SKUs: enumerated Stock Keeping Units (SKUs); Product line: a named chemical product line; Material type: SAP material type classification (e.g., FERT, HALB).']
  growth_target_percent decimal(7,4) [note: 'Year-over-year revenue or volume growth percentage target that the customer must achieve to qualify for growth incentive rebate tiers. Applicable only when agreement_type is growth_incentive. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 10.0000 = 10%).']
  is_retroactive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rebate is applied retroactively to all eligible purchases once a higher tier threshold is crossed (retroactive/step rebate), as opposed to applying only to incremental purchases above the threshold (incremental rebate). Critical for SAP SD rebate condition type configuration.']
  is_tiered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rebate agreement uses a tiered scale structure where different rebate rates apply at different purchase volume or revenue thresholds. True: multiple tiers defined in the rebate tier child product; False: single flat rate applies.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this rebate agreement record, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  last_settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent rebate settlement was completed and credit memo or payment was issued to the customer. Used to track settlement history and validate settlement frequency compliance.']
  max_rebate_cap_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum total rebate payout amount allowed under this agreement regardless of actual purchase performance. Protects Chemical Mfg from uncapped rebate liability exposure. Null if no cap applies. Expressed in payout currency.']
  next_settlement_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next rebate settlement run for this agreement. Drives SAP SD settlement processing calendar and finance accrual review cycles.']
  payout_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the rebate settlement is denominated and paid to the customer or distributor (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency rebate programs for global chemical distribution.']
  rebate_amount_fixed decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary rebate amount payable upon achievement of agreement conditions, used for flat-fee rebate structures (e.g., market development funds with a fixed payout). Null when the agreement uses a percentage-based rate. Expressed in the payout currency.']
  rebate_rate_percent decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage rate applied to the calculation basis to determine the rebate payout amount for a given tier. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 2.5000 = 2.5%). For tiered agreements, this represents the base or entry-level rate; tier-specific rates are captured in the rebate tier product.']
  sap_rebate_agreement_number varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for this rebate agreement in SAP SD (BO01/BO02 agreement number). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the SAP S/4HANA system of record.']
  settled_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total rebate amount that has been paid or credited to the customer through completed settlement runs. The difference between accrued_amount and settled_amount represents the outstanding rebate payable. Expressed in payout currency.']
  settlement_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the accrued rebate is settled and paid to the customer or distributor. Determines the cadence of SAP SD rebate settlement runs and credit memo generation.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which the rebate is paid to the customer or distributor. Credit memo: applied against future invoices in SAP SD; Check: physical payment instrument; Bank transfer: electronic funds transfer; Product credit: rebate applied as credit toward future product purchases.']
  threshold_basis_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum cumulative purchase value (in payout currency) or volume that the customer must achieve to qualify for the rebate. Represents the entry-level threshold for single-tier agreements. For multi-tier agreements, this is the lowest tier threshold.']
  threshold_basis_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the rebate threshold basis amount. For revenue-based agreements, this is the ISO 4217 currency code. For volume-based agreements, this is the unit of measure (MT = metric ton, KG = kilogram, L = liter, EA = each) aligned with chemical product measurement standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|MT|KG|L|EA| — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Volume-based or performance-based rebate agreement with a customer or distributor, defining rebate tiers, calculation basis (revenue or volume), accrual method, settlement frequency, and eligible product scope. Captures rebate type (volume rebate, growth incentive, market development fund), threshold levels, and payout currency. Maps to SAP SD rebate processing (BO01/BO02). Distinct from price_agreement — rebates are retrospective settlements, not upfront pricing.'
}

  opportunity_product_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the opportunity-product association record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  cas_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.cas_registry. Business justification: opportunity_product.cas_number is a denormalized reference to cas_registry.   chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the chemical product or grade being proposed within this opportunity. Identifies the specific finished good, intermediate, or specialty chemical.']
  cost_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_element. Business justification: Opportunity products in chemical mfg carry standard cost estimates for margin calculation during the sales stage. Linking to cost_element enables accurate contribution margin forecasting on pipeline o']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.equipment. Business justification: In chemical manufacturing technical sales, opportunity products are tied to specific customer equipment (e.g., a catalyst for a specific reactor, a lubricant for a specific compressor). This link enab']
  grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grade. Business justification: During opportunity qualification in chemical manufacturing, the specific grade pursued determines pricing tier, GMP/USP compliance requirements, and technical review scope. The product_grade plain-tex']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent sales opportunity to which this product line belongs. Links the product-level detail back to the opportunity header.']
  packaging_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.packaging_config. Business justification: During opportunity qualification, packaging configuration affects cost modeling, logistics feasibility, and hazmat compliance. The packaging_type plain-text field is denormalized; FK to packaging_conf']
  product_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_specification. Business justification: In chemical manufacturing, customer-specific specifications drive the technical review process during opportunity qualification. FK from opportunity_product to product_specification links the commerci']
  qualification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.qualification. Business justification: In chemical manufacturing, opportunity products must reference the customer qualification record to confirm the product grade is approved for the customer\'s application. This drives sample requests, t']
  sds_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sds. Business justification: When sds_required=true on an opportunity_product, the specific SDS document must be referenced for OSHA HazCom and REACH compliance. FK to sds enables automatic SDS version tracking during opportunity']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Chemical sales opportunities are tracked at SKU level to forecast demand by specific pack configuration (e.g., 200L drum vs 1000L IBC). The sku_code plain-text field is denormalized; FK to sku enables']
  tds_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.tds. Business justification: TDS documents are central to technical sales in chemical manufacturing — customers require performance data to qualify a chemical for their process. When technical_review_required=true, the TDS should']
  annual_volume_estimate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Estimated annual purchase volume for this product line, expressed in the quantity UOM. Supports long-term demand forecasting, supply chain planning, and volume-based pricing negotiations.']
  application_end_use varchar [note: 'Specific end-use application or industry segment for which the customer intends to use this chemical product (e.g., automotive coatings, agricultural formulation, electronic cleaning). Critical for REACH downstream user notifications and technical sales support.']
  close_date date [note: 'Expected or actual date on which this product line within the opportunity is anticipated to be won or lost. Used for pipeline aging analysis and sales forecast period alignment.']
  coa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer requires a Certificate of Analysis (COA) with each shipment of this product. Drives quality documentation requirements in LabWare LIMS and SAP QM.']
  competitive_status varchar [note: 'Describes the competitive landscape for this product line within the opportunity. Indicates whether Chemical Mfg is the sole supplier, competing against others, defending incumbent business, or attempting to displace a competitor.']
  competitor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary competing supplier for this product line in the opportunity. Used for competitive intelligence, win/loss analysis, and strategic positioning.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Proposed end date of the supply contract or pricing agreement for this product line. Defines the duration of the commercial commitment being negotiated.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Proposed start date of the supply contract or pricing agreement for this product line if the opportunity is won. Feeds into contract management and SAP SD scheduling agreements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this opportunity-product association record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and pipeline aging analysis.']
  crm_opportunity_product_code varchar [note: 'External identifier for this opportunity-product line as recorded in Salesforce CRM (OpportunityLineItem ID). Used for cross-system reconciliation between CRM and SAP SD.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values on this opportunity product line (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency reporting and FX conversion.']
  delivery_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Estimated number of calendar days from order placement to delivery for this product line, used in customer negotiations and supply chain planning.']
  discount_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount offered on the list price for this product line as part of the commercial negotiation. Supports margin analysis and pricing governance.']
  estimated_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Estimated volume or quantity of the chemical product the customer intends to purchase within the opportunity period. Used for demand forecasting and production planning.']
  estimated_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total estimated revenue for this product line within the opportunity, calculated as estimated quantity multiplied by unit price. Represents the gross revenue potential before discounts.']
  first_shipment_date date [note: 'Anticipated date of the first product shipment if the opportunity is won. Used for production scheduling, raw material procurement planning, and customer commitment management.']
  formulation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this opportunity product line requires a customer-specific formulation or blending service rather than a standard catalog product. Triggers engagement with R&D and formulation teams.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this opportunity-product record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and incremental ETL processing.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number of this product within the parent opportunity, enabling ordered display and reference of multiple product lines within a single opportunity.']
  list_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Published or standard list price per unit of measure for this chemical product before any negotiated discounts or customer-specific pricing adjustments.']
  moq decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum order quantity required for this chemical product, expressed in the quantity UOM. Communicated to the customer during opportunity negotiation to set commercial expectations.']
  opportunity_product_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this specific product line within the opportunity. Allows individual product lines to be won, lost, or withdrawn independently of the overall opportunity status.']
  probability_weighted_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated revenue for this product line adjusted by the win probability percentage. Used for pipeline valuation, sales forecasting, and revenue planning at the product level.']
  quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the estimated quantity field (e.g., KG for kilograms, MT for metric tons, L for liters, DRUM for drum containers). Aligns with SAP base unit of measure for the material. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|MT|LB|L|GAL|DRUM|IBC|BAG|TON — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reach_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this chemical product is confirmed compliant with REACH regulation for the customer\'s intended use and geography. Critical for European market opportunities.']
  sales_stage varchar [note: 'Current sales pipeline stage for this product line, which may differ from the parent opportunity stage when individual products are at different negotiation phases. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: prospecting|qualification|needs_analysis|proposal|negotiation|closed_won|closed_lost — promote to reference product]']
  sample_requested boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has requested a product sample for evaluation or qualification testing as part of this opportunity. Triggers sample order creation in SAP SD.']
  sap_sd_item_number varchar [note: 'SAP SD quotation or sales order item number corresponding to this product line, used for traceability from opportunity to formal quote or order in SAP S/4HANA.']
  sds_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer requires a Safety Data Sheet (SDS) for this chemical product as part of the opportunity documentation. Mandatory for hazardous chemicals under GHS and REACH.']
  technical_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product line requires a technical review by the applications engineering or R&D team before a formal proposal can be submitted to the customer.']
  tsca_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this chemical product is listed on the TSCA Chemical Substance Inventory and compliant for commercial sale in the United States.']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Proposed or target selling price per unit of measure for this chemical product in the opportunity. Represents the commercial pricing position before final negotiation.']
  win_probability_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated probability (0–100%) that this specific product line will be won, which may differ from the overall opportunity win probability when the customer is evaluating multiple products independently.']

  Note: 'Association record linking a specific chemical product or grade to a sales opportunity, capturing estimated quantity, unit of measure, estimated revenue, probability-weighted value, and competitive status for that product line within the opportunity. Enables product-level pipeline visibility and supports demand forecasting from the sales pipeline.'
}

  organization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for sales_organization']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: In SAP-based chemical mfg, every sales organization is assigned to exactly one legal entity — fundamental organizational structure for statutory reporting, tax determination, and intercompany transact']
  maintenance_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.plant. Business justification: In SAP-based chemical manufacturing, a sales organization is assigned to one or more plants for production order routing, available-to-promise checks, and delivery scheduling. This is a foundational S']
  parent_organization_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent sales organization in the hierarchy.']
  parent_sales_organization_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on sales_organization (parent_sales_organization_id)']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Sales organizations map to profit centers for CO-PA revenue reporting in chemical mfg. Finance uses this link to aggregate sales org performance into profit center P&L statements for management report']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the sales organization.']
  annual_budget decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned annual financial budget for the sales organization.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the sales organization is located.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost‑center code associated with the sales organization for financial reporting.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the sales organization location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sales organization record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code used for the organization’s financial reporting.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the sales organization became operational.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the sales organization ceased operation (null if still active).']
  number_of_employees varchar [note: 'Total headcount employed by the sales organization.']
  organization_type varchar [note: 'Category that defines the scope or channel of the sales organization.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code for the sales organization address.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Main email address for contacting the sales organization.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Main telephone number for the sales organization.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region identifier (e.g., NA, EU, APAC) for the sales organization.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the organization sells products.']
  sales_organization_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose, focus, or notes about the sales organization.']
  sales_organization_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the sales organization.']
  sales_organization_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the sales organization.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the sales organization location.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the sales organization (e.g., America/New_York).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the sales organization record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for sales_organization. Referenced by sales_organization_id.'
}

Table supply.vendor {
  vendor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor record. Primary key.']

  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Vendors in chemical manufacturing are registered and managed per legal entity for tax reporting, payment processing, and regulatory compliance. Vendor master scoping to legal entity is required for in']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the procurement manager or executive who authorized the vendors approval and addition to the approved vendor list.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor was officially approved and added to the approved vendor list (AVL) following successful qualification and onboarding.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Primary business classification of the vendor indicating the type of goods or services they provide to the chemical manufacturing operation.']
  contract_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current master supply agreement or contract with the vendor expires requiring renewal or renegotiation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor master record was first created in the system capturing the initial data entry event.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the primary currency used for pricing and invoicing with this vendor.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit Dun & Bradstreet identifier used globally to uniquely identify business entities for credit and compliance purposes.']
  hazmat_handling_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has certified capabilities and infrastructure to handle, store, and transport hazardous chemical materials in compliance with DOT and OSHA regulations.']
  headquarters_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the vendors corporate headquarters street address including building number and street name.']
  headquarters_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the vendors corporate headquarters address for suite, floor, or additional location details.']
  headquarters_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the vendors corporate headquarters is located.']
  headquarters_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the vendors corporate headquarters is domiciled.']
  headquarters_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the vendors corporate headquarters address used for mail delivery and geographic classification.']
  headquarters_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the vendors corporate headquarters is located.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'Preferred Incoterms rule defining the division of costs, risks, and responsibilities between buyer and seller for international shipments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  iso_9001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor holds ISO 9001 certification for quality management systems at the corporate or site level.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent on-site vendor audit or qualification assessment conducted to verify compliance with quality, safety, and regulatory requirements.']
  minority_owned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is certified as a minority-owned business enterprise for supplier diversity program tracking and reporting.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to any field in the vendor master record used for change tracking and data lineage.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic vendor audit or re-qualification assessment based on audit frequency policy and risk tier.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional vendor information, special handling instructions, historical context, or operational notes relevant to procurement and supply chain teams.']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'Current stage in the vendor qualification and onboarding process including documentation review, site audits, and compliance verification.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms negotiated with the vendor specifying due date calculation (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30) for invoice settlement.']
  performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite performance scorecard rating (0-100 scale) based on on-time delivery, quality metrics, responsiveness, and compliance with contract terms.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary vendor contact used for purchase orders, ASN notifications, and business correspondence.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact at the vendor organization responsible for account management and order coordination.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Telephone number of the primary vendor contact including country code and extension where applicable.']
  reach_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor complies with European REACH regulation for chemical substance registration and safety data provision.']
  responsible_care_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor holds Responsible Care certification from the American Chemistry Council (ACC) demonstrating commitment to environmental health and safety standards.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall supply chain risk assessment rating for the vendor based on financial stability, geopolitical factors, quality performance, and business continuity capabilities.']
  sap_vendor_number varchar [note: 'Unique vendor identifier in SAP S/4HANA MM (Materials Management) module used for procurement and accounts payable transactions.']
  small_business boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor qualifies as a small business enterprise based on SBA size standards for supplier diversity and government contracting purposes.']
  tax_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identifier (EIN, VAT number, or equivalent) for the vendor used for tax reporting and compliance.']
  vendor_code varchar [note: 'Internal business identifier or short code used to reference the vendor in operational systems and reports.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the chemical supplier or raw material vendor as registered with governing authorities.']
  vendor_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the vendor indicating whether they are approved for procurement transactions and order placement.']
  vendor_tier varchar [note: 'Tiered classification reflecting the vendors strategic importance, performance history, and procurement preference level within the supply chain network.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Primary website URL for the vendor providing access to product catalogs, technical documentation, and company information.']
  woman_owned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is certified as a woman-owned business enterprise for supplier diversity program tracking and reporting.']

  Note: 'Master record for all chemical suppliers and raw material vendors including multi-site physical locations. Captures vendor identity, classification, SAP vendor number, DUNS number, tax identifiers, payment terms, currency, preferred incoterms, vendor tier (strategic/preferred/approved/conditional), Responsible Care certification status, REACH compliance flag, and onboarding status. Includes site-level detail: physical plant addresses, site types (manufacturing plant, distribution center, port of export), geographic regions, country of origin, UN/LOCODE, hazmat handling capabilities, ISO 9001/14001 certifications at site level, and site-level contacts. Single source of truth for supplier identity, locations, and site capabilities within the supply domain.'
}

Table supply.vendor_site {
  vendor_site_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor site. Primary key.']

  active_to_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor site was deactivated or decommissioned. Null for currently active sites. Used for supplier lifecycle management.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the vendor site. Used for shipping, logistics planning, and regulatory reporting.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for building, suite, or unit information.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the vendor site is located.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the vendor site location. Critical for customs, trade compliance, and country-of-origin tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor site record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  dock_hours varchar [note: 'Operating hours for receiving and shipping at the vendor site dock. Used for delivery scheduling and ASN planning.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Macro-geographic region classification for supply chain planning and risk segmentation.']
  hazmat_handling_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor site is certified and equipped to handle hazardous chemical materials per DOT and OSHA requirements. Critical for chemical raw material sourcing.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'Default Incoterm for shipments from this vendor site. Defines responsibility for freight, insurance, and risk transfer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  iso_14001_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Certificate number for the site\'s ISO 14001 certification. Used for environmental compliance verification.']

  iso_9001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor site holds ISO 9001 Quality Management System certification. Used for supplier qualification and quality assurance.']

  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent supplier audit conducted at this vendor site. Used for supplier performance tracking and re-audit scheduling.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor site record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the vendor site. Used for logistics optimization, distance calculations, and supply chain mapping.']

  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the vendor site. Used for logistics optimization, distance calculations, and supply chain mapping.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum order quantity required by this vendor site for purchase orders. Used for procurement planning and order consolidation.']
  minimum_order_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the minimum order quantity. Common chemical industry units include kilograms, liters, drums, totes, and railcars. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|LB|MT|L|GAL|M3|EA|DRUM|TOTE|RAILCAR — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next supplier audit at this vendor site. Used for compliance monitoring and audit planning.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the vendor site address. Used for logistics routing and freight cost calculation.']
  preferred_carrier varchar [note: 'Preferred freight carrier or logistics provider for shipments from this vendor site. Used for logistics planning and freight cost estimation.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary site contact for purchase order confirmations, ASN notifications, and operational communication.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person at the vendor site for procurement and logistics coordination.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary site contact for urgent procurement and logistics issues.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current supplier qualification status of the vendor site. Determines whether site can be used for new purchase orders and material sourcing.']
  responsible_care_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor site participates in the American Chemistry Council\'s Responsible Care program. Key indicator for chemical industry supplier qualification.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Supply chain risk rating for this vendor site based on factors including geographic risk, financial stability, quality history, and regulatory compliance. Used for supply risk management and dual-sourcing decisions.']
  site_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the vendor site within the vendor\'s network. Used for operational reference and system integration.']
  site_name varchar [note: 'Official business name of the vendor site or facility.']
  site_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the vendor site in the supply chain network. Controls whether site can be used for new purchase orders.']
  site_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vendor site based on its primary operational function. Determines handling capabilities and logistics planning.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region of the vendor site. Used for tax jurisdiction and regulatory compliance.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the vendor site. Used for scheduling shipments, coordinating deliveries, and ASN timing.']
  un_locode varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE for the site location. Used for international logistics, customs documentation, and port-of-export identification.']

  Note: 'Physical plant, warehouse, or shipping location associated with a vendor. Tracks site address, site type (manufacturing plant, distribution center, port of export), geographic region, country of origin, UN/LOCODE, hazmat handling capability, ISO 9001/14001 certification at site level, and site-level contact information. Supports multi-site vendor relationships common in chemical raw material sourcing.'
}

Table supply.purchase_order {
  purchase_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the purchase order record in the lakehouse. Primary key.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.contract. Business justification: Purchase orders in chemical manufacturing are frequently issued against long-term supply contracts or blanket purchase agreements. The purchase_order table stores contract_number (STRING) but has no F']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for PO expense charging; finance cost center needed for budgeting and actual spend reporting per purchase order.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Allocates PO costs to internal orders for project accounting and cost tracking in financial systems.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: POs are issued by a specific legal entity for statutory reporting, intercompany procurement, and tax compliance. company_code is a denormalized legal entity identifier; replacing with proper FK enforc']
  production_plant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the manufacturing plant or facility where the ordered materials will be received and used.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Chemical manufacturing POs are posted against profit centers for product-line P&L and raw material spend allocation. Procurement controllers require this for segment-level cost reporting and budget va']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the chemical supplier or service provider to whom this purchase order is issued.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status: not_required (no approval needed), pending (awaiting approver action), approved (all approvals obtained), rejected (approval denied).']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who provided final approval for the purchase order release.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase order received final approval and was authorized for release to the vendor.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the purchase order record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the purchase order value is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the ordered materials or services are requested to be delivered to the receiving plant or location.']
  document_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase order document was created in the system. Represents the business event timestamp for PO creation.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Freight payment responsibility: prepaid (seller pays freight), collect (buyer pays freight), or third_party (third party pays freight).']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) defining the responsibilities of buyer and seller for transportation, insurance, and risk transfer (e.g., FOB, CIF, DDP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  incoterms_location varchar [note: 'Named place or port specified in the Incoterms clause where the transfer of risk and responsibility occurs (e.g., Houston Port, Vendor Warehouse).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the purchase order record was last modified. Audit trail for record updates.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms code defining the payment schedule and discount conditions (e.g., Net 30, 2/10 Net 30). Typically a 4-character SAP payment terms key.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique purchase order document number issued to the supplier. Typically a 10-digit SAP document number from MM module.']
  po_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the purchase order: draft (being created), pending_approval (awaiting authorization), approved (authorized but not yet released), released (transmitted to vendor), partially_received (some goods received), fully_received (all goods received), closed (completed and archived), cancelled (voided before fulfillment). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|released|partially_received|fully_received|closed|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  po_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the purchase order type: standard (one-time purchase), blanket (release against framework agreement), contract (long-term agreement), consignment (vendor-owned inventory), subcontracting (material provided to vendor for processing), or service (non-material procurement).']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Buyer or purchasing team responsible for this purchase order. Typically a 3-character code identifying the procurement specialist or commodity team (e.g., raw materials, packaging, services).']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'SAP organizational unit responsible for procurement activities. Typically a 4-character alphanumeric code representing the central or regional purchasing group.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase order was officially released and transmitted to the vendor for fulfillment.']
  requisition_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the internal purchase requisition that triggered the creation of this purchase order.']
  rfq_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Request for Quotation (RFQ) document that preceded this purchase order, if applicable. Links PO to the sourcing event.']
  shipping_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the delivery address where the ordered materials should be shipped (street address, building number).']
  shipping_city varchar [note: 'City name of the delivery address for the purchase order shipment.']
  shipping_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the delivery address (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX).']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation for delivering the ordered materials: truck (road freight), rail (railway), ocean (sea freight), air (air cargo), pipeline (bulk liquid/gas), or intermodal (combination).']
  shipping_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code or ZIP code of the delivery address for the purchase order shipment.']
  shipping_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the delivery address for the purchase order shipment.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field for any special handling, packaging, labeling, or delivery instructions communicated to the vendor.']
  total_gross_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross monetary value of the purchase order including net value, taxes, freight, and all other charges.']
  total_net_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total net monetary value of the purchase order before taxes, freight, and other charges. Sum of all line item net values.']
  total_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applicable to the purchase order, including sales tax, VAT, or other applicable taxes based on jurisdiction.']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'End date of the purchase order validity period. Applicable primarily for blanket or contract purchase orders defining the expiration date.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the purchase order validity period. Applicable primarily for blanket or contract purchase orders defining the effective-from date.']
  vendor_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary vendor contact for order acknowledgment, shipping notifications, and issue resolution.']
  vendor_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person at the vendor organization for this purchase order.']

  Note: 'Core procurement transaction representing a legally binding PO issued to a chemical supplier for raw materials, intermediates, packaging, or services. Captures PO number, PO type (standard, blanket release, consignment, subcontracting), vendor, plant, purchasing organization, purchasing group, document date, delivery date, incoterms, payment terms, currency, total net value, approval status, and SAP document number. Integrates with SAP MM ME21N/ME22N.'
}

  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center.   gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: PO line account assignment includes GL account for direct cost posting of consumables and services.   material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the material master record representing the raw material or chemical substance being procured on this line.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent purchase order header. Links this line item to its containing purchase order document.']
  batch_managed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this material requires batch management and lot tracking for quality control and traceability purposes.']

  deletion_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this purchase order line has been marked for deletion and should be excluded from active procurement processing.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'The requested or confirmed delivery date for this purchase order line item. Represents when the material is expected to arrive at the receiving location.']
  ghs_hazard_category varchar [note: 'The GHS hazard classification category for this chemical substance (e.g., Flammable Liquid Category 2, Acute Toxicity Category 3). Used for safety data sheet compliance and labeling.']

  hazmat_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this material is classified as hazardous and requires special handling, storage, and transportation procedures.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'The Incoterms code defining the delivery terms and transfer of risk between buyer and supplier (e.g., FOB, CIF, DDP).']
  incoterms_location varchar [note: 'The specific location or port associated with the Incoterms delivery terms (e.g., Houston Port, supplier facility).']

  invoice_receipt_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an invoice must be received and verified before payment can be processed for this line item.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this purchase order line item record was most recently updated or changed.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line item number within the purchase order. Determines the ordering and position of this line in the PO document.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current fulfillment status of this purchase order line item in its procurement lifecycle.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Short text description of the material or chemical substance being procured, including common name and key characteristics.']
  material_group varchar [note: 'Classification code grouping similar materials together for procurement analysis and reporting (e.g., solvents, acids, polymers).']
  material_number varchar [note: 'The business material number or SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) code identifying the specific raw material or chemical being ordered.']
  net_order_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total net value of this purchase order line, calculated as ordered quantity multiplied by net price, before taxes and freight.']
  net_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The negotiated unit price for the material before taxes and additional charges. Expressed per price unit.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of material ordered on this purchase order line, expressed in the unit of measure specified.']
  outstanding_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Remaining quantity of material still to be delivered on this purchase order line, calculated as ordered quantity minus goods receipt quantity.']
  over_delivery_tolerance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acceptable percentage by which the supplier may exceed the ordered quantity without requiring approval.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The manufacturing plant or facility code where the material will be received and used in production.']
  price_unit varchar [note: 'The quantity unit to which the net price applies (e.g., price per 1 unit, per 100 units, per 1000 units).']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Code identifying the purchasing group or buyer responsible for procuring this material category.']
  quality_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether incoming material on this line must undergo quality control inspection and Certificate of Analysis (COA) verification before release to production.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'The specific storage location or warehouse area within the plant where the material will be stored upon receipt.']
  supplier_material_number varchar [note: 'The suppliers own material number or product code for this chemical substance, used for cross-referencing and order confirmation.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code determining the applicable tax rate and tax jurisdiction for this purchase order line.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN identification number for hazardous materials used in international transport and shipping documentation.']
  under_delivery_tolerance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acceptable percentage by which the supplier may deliver less than the ordered quantity without penalty.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the ordered quantity is expressed (e.g., KG for kilograms, L for liters, MT for metric tons, GAL for gallons).']

  Note: 'Individual line items within a purchase order, each representing a specific raw material or chemical substance being procured. Captures line number, material number, CAS number, material description, ordered quantity, unit of measure, net price, price unit, delivery date, storage location, batch management flag, hazmat classification, GHS hazard category, and line-level delivery status. Integrates with SAP MM EKPO table.'
}

  received_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The actual quantity of material received and accepted at the dock. This is the line quantity for the receipt transaction.']
  receiver_name varchar [note: 'The name of the warehouse or receiving dock employee who physically accepted and inspected the shipment.']

  seal_number varchar [note: 'The security seal number applied to the shipment container or truck. Verified upon receipt to ensure shipment integrity and prevent tampering.']
  stock_type varchar [note: 'The inventory stock type to which the received goods are posted. Quality inspection stock requires QC release before use; blocked stock cannot be used.']
  temperature_on_arrival_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'The measured temperature of the material shipment upon arrival at the receiving dock, recorded in degrees Celsius. Critical for temperature-sensitive chemicals and compliance with storage specifications.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'The four-digit UN identification number for hazardous materials, used for transportation and storage classification (e.g., UN1090 for acetone).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure in which the received quantity is expressed (e.g., kilograms, liters, metric tons, gallons). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|L|MT|GAL|LB|TON|M3|EA|BOX|DRUM|PALLET — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  valuation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of the goods receipt transaction, calculated based on purchase order price and received quantity. Posted to inventory and financial accounts.']

  Note: 'Records the physical receipt of raw materials and chemicals at the plant gate or warehouse dock against a purchase order. Captures GR document number, GR date, vendor, material, received quantity, unit of measure, storage location, batch number (LOT), movement type, quality inspection flag, COA received flag, temperature on arrival, and SAP material document number. Triggers QC inspection workflow in LIMS. Integrates with SAP MM MIGO/MSEG.'
}

Table supply.asn {
  asn_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Advanced Shipping Notice record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Inbound ASN carrier validation is a named process in chemical manufacturing: approved hazmat carriers must be verified before accepting delivery. Links ASN to carrier master for DOT compliance checks ']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order against which this shipment is being delivered.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the chemical supplier sending the shipment.']
  vendor_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor_site. Business justification: An ASN (Advanced Shipping Notice) originates from a specific vendor shipping site. The asn table currently stores shipping_point_code as a free-text string. Adding vendor_site_id FK normalizes this to']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the shipment physically arrived at the receiving facility.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique ASN document number provided by the supplier.']
  asn_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ASN in the receiving workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|transmitted|in_transit|arrived|receiving|completed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  asn_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ASN based on shipment characteristics and handling requirements.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'ISO standard container identification number for intermodal shipments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ASN record was first created in the system.']
  expected_delivery_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when the shipment is expected to arrive at the receiving facility.']
  expected_delivery_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the shipment is scheduled to arrive at the receiving dock.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Terms specifying who is responsible for freight payment and when title transfers.']
  hazmat_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the shipment contains hazardous materials requiring special handling.']
  hazmat_placard_code varchar [note: 'DOT hazard class placard code required for transportation (e.g., 3 for flammable liquids, 8 for corrosives).']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'Standard trade terms defining responsibilities for shipping costs and risk transfer between buyer and seller. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ASN record was last updated.']
  package_count varchar [note: 'Total number of packages, containers, or handling units in the shipment.']
  pallet_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pallets included in the shipment for warehouse planning.']
  pro_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned tracking number for Less Than Truckload (LTL) shipments.']
  receiving_dock_location varchar [note: 'Specific dock or bay location at the receiving facility designated for unloading this shipment.']
  receiving_plant_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the destination plant or facility where the shipment will be received.']
  required_temperature_max decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature threshold allowed during transportation to maintain product integrity.']
  required_temperature_min decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature threshold required during transportation to maintain product integrity.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the security seal applied to the container or trailer.']
  shipment_mode varchar [note: 'Transportation mode used for this shipment (Less Than Truckload, Full Truckload, etc.).']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text instructions for special handling requirements during transportation and receiving.']
  temperature_controlled_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the shipment requires temperature-controlled transportation.']
  temperature_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for temperature values (Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin).']
  total_gross_weight decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the entire shipment including packaging materials.']
  total_net_weight decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total net weight of the chemical materials excluding packaging.']
  total_volume decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total volume of the shipment for capacity planning and storage allocation.']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'Identification number of the trailer or truck used for transportation.']
  transmitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ASN was transmitted by the vendor to the receiving system.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN identification number for hazardous substances as per international transport regulations.']
  volume_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for volume values (liters, gallons, cubic meters, cubic feet).']
  weight_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for weight values (kilograms, pounds, metric tons, tons).']

  Note: 'Advanced Shipping Notice received from chemical suppliers prior to physical delivery, with full line-level material detail. Captures ASN number, vendor, PO reference, expected delivery date/time, carrier, bill of lading, container/trailer ID, hazmat placard codes, UN numbers. Line-level detail includes: material, CAS number, shipped quantity, unit of measure, batch/lot number, expiry date, country of origin, REACH registration number, SDS version reference, packaging type (drum, IBC, tanker, bag), and net/gross weight per line. Enables pre-receipt planning and line-level receiving reconciliation against PO lines. Integrates with Oracle TMS and SAP MM inbound delivery.'
}

Replacing the denormalized quality_specification text field with a proper FK enable']

Linking contract to the governing operating_permit enables compliance verification that c']

Direct FK replaces denormalized plant_code, enabling p']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Long-term supply contracts in chemical manufacturing are budgeted and tracked at profit center level for strategic sourcing cost management and committed spend reporting against segment P&L.']
  quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_specification. Business justification: Supply contracts in chemical manufacturing explicitly reference quality specifications the vendor must meet. Contract already has a plain text \'quality_specification\' field — proper FK replaces this d']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the chemical supplier or raw material vendor party with whom this contract is established.']
  vendor_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific vendor location or plant from which materials will be supplied under this contract.']

  approved_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the executive or manager who provided final approval for this contract.']
