  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: freight_charge_amount on shipment_line requires GL account assignment for line-level freight cost posting in chemical manufacturing ERP. Enables accurate freight expense classification by product or h']
  grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grade. Business justification: Chemical shipment lines carry a specific product grade (USP, ACS, technical). Grade determines COA requirements, handling instructions, and regulatory classification. Chemical manufacturing domain exp']
  invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice_line. Business justification: Line-level freight charge billing reconciliation: chemical manufacturers bill per shipment line (product, quantity, hazmat surcharge). Linking shipment_line to invoice_line enables line-level revenue ']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.line_item. Business justification: Shipment line items correspond to order line items; linking provides detailed fulfillment and billing reconciliation.']
  logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent shipment header.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Regulatory traceability (GHS/REACH) requires each shipment line to reference the exact inventory lot shipped.']
  lot_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.lot_record. Business justification: Inbound raw material shipment lines must link directly to lot records for chain-of-custody traceability, recall management, and quality disposition. Chemical manufacturers require lot-level traceabili']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material or product being shipped.']
  packaging_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.packaging_config. Business justification: Shipment lines ship chemicals in specific packaging configurations (drum, IBC, bulk tanker). Packaging config determines UN packaging code, hazmat certification, weight calculations, and DOT label req']
  planned_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.planned_order. Business justification: Shipment‑to‑Planned‑Order Traceability links each shipped line back to its originating Planned Order.']
  process_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.process_order. Business justification: Chemical shipment lines must trace to the originating process order for batch traceability, COA issuance, and REACH/DOT regulatory audits. A domain expert expects this link to support quality-to-logis']
  qualification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.qualification. Business justification: In chemical manufacturing, each shipment line for a qualified product must reference the customer qualification record (APL number, COA template, spec deviation flags, max approved volume). This link ']
  quote_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote_line. Business justification: Enables traceability from shipped items back to the original quoted line for compliance, billing reconciliation, and margin analysis.']
  safety_data_sheet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.safety_data_sheet. Business justification: OSHA HazCom and EU CLP require current SDS to accompany each hazardous chemical shipment line. In chemical manufacturing, the shipment_line already tracks ghs_label_required and dot_label_required; li']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Shipment lines execute at SKU level (specific pack size, packaging, grade). COA attachment, hazmat labeling, and weight calculations are SKU-specific. The existing `sku` column is a denormalized strin']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Pick‑by‑position processes need the exact stock position for each line to optimize warehouse operations.']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Code of the carrier responsible for transporting this line.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment line record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  customs_declaration_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if a customs declaration is required.']
  customs_document_number varchar [note: 'Customs document number for international shipments.']
  dot_label_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if DOT labeling is required for this line.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the material, if applicable.']
  freight_charge_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Freight charge amount for this line.']
  freight_class varchar [note: 'Freight class for LTL/FTL billing purposes.']
  ghs_label_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if GHS labeling is required for this line.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Gross weight including packaging in kilograms.']
  hazmat_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the line contains hazardous material.']
  is_freight_chargeable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether freight charges apply to this line.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence number of the line within the shipment.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the shipment line.']
  manufacturing_date date [note: 'Manufacturing date of the material.']
  net_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net weight of the shipped items (excluding packaging) in kilograms.']
  package_count varchar [note: 'Number of packages of this type in the line.']
  package_height_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Height of the package in centimeters.']
  package_length_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Length of the package in centimeters.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Type of packaging used for the line item.']
  package_width_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Width of the package in centimeters.']
  packing_group varchar [note: 'Packing group indicating degree of danger.']
  quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of the material shipped in the line.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Seal or closure identifier for verification.']
  seal_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the package seal has been verified.']
  tare_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Weight of the empty packaging in kilograms.']
  temperature_control_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if temperature control is required for the shipment.']
  temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature in Celsius for temperature‑controlled shipment.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature in Celsius for temperature‑controlled shipment.']
  un_hazard_class varchar [note: 'UN hazard class for hazardous material. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|2|3|4|5|6|7|8|9 — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shipment line record.']

  Note: 'Line-level detail for each product, material, or package included in a shipment. Records the finished chemical SKU or raw material, ordered and shipped quantities, unit of measure, lot number, batch number, hazmat indicator, UN hazard class, packing group, net and gross weight, COA reference, package count, package type (drum/IBC/tote/ISO tank), package dimensions, tare weight, GHS/DOT label requirements per 49 CFR §172.400, seal or closure verification, and packing list documentation for customs clearance. Supports multi-product chemical shipments, regulatory manifest line-item traceability, receiving verification, and international customs documentation requirements.'
}

Table logistics.bill_of_lading {
  bill_of_lading_id varchar [pk, note: 'System generated surrogate primary key for the Bill of Lading record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Bill of lading should reference carrier entity; carrier_scac is redundant once carrier_id FK is added.']
  destination_location_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the location where the shipment is destined.']
  freight_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.freight_order. Business justification: A bill of lading is the legal contract of carriage issued for a freight order execution. bill_of_lading already links to shipment and shipment_plan, but lacks a direct FK to the freight_order that it ']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: BOL freight_charge_amount and declared_value_amount require GL account assignment for freight expense and customs duty posting in chemical manufacturing. BOL is the primary financial document for frei']
  loading_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.equipment. Business justification: Bills of lading for bulk chemical shipments must document the loading equipment (loading arms, tank filling stations) used for regulatory traceability and quality chain-of-custody. Chemical manufactur']
  notify_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Bills of lading for hazardous chemical shipments require a named notify party — a specific customer contact with regulatory_notification_flag or ehs_contact_flag. This is a standard shipping document ']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_order. Business justification: BOL is generated to fulfill a customer product order in chemical manufacturing. Order-to-cash traceability requires linking BOL back to the originating product order for invoicing, dispute resolution,']
  outbound_delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.outbound_delivery. Business justification: BOL is created from an outbound delivery in chemical manufacturing ERP (SAP SD). The outbound delivery triggers BOL generation; linking them enables freight cost allocation to delivery, proof-of-shipm']
  parent_logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: A bill of lading is generated for a single shipment; adding parent_logistics_shipment_id creates the required child‑to‑parent link.']
  primary_bill_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party that is sending the shipment.']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote. Business justification: Bills of lading for chemical exports must align with the commercial quote for letter of credit compliance, export documentation accuracy, and customer billing. Customs authorities and banks require BO']
  sds_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sds. Business justification: BOLs for hazardous chemical shipments must reference the SDS for emergency response information (49 CFR 172.604). Chemical manufacturers attach SDS to BOL for first responder access. Regulatory compli']
  shipment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the planned transportation route.']
  bill_of_lading_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the Bill of Lading.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier printed on the legal transport document.']
  bol_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the document is a master BOL or a house BOL.']
  compliance_flags varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of compliance indicators (e.g., REACH|TSCA|GHS).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the Bill of Lading record was first created in the system.']
  customs_declaration_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customs filing associated with the shipment.']
  declared_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value declared for the shipment for liability and customs purposes.']
  declared_value_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the declared value.']
  dot_certification_statement varchar [note: 'Legal statement required by DOT 49 CFR confirming compliance.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the emergency contact listed on the Bill of Lading.']
  equipment_number varchar [note: 'Identifier (e.g., container number) of the equipment.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of equipment used to move the shipment.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification for export control compliance.']
  freight_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount charged by the carrier for transportation.']
  freight_charge_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the freight charge.']
  freight_class varchar [note: 'Freight classification used for rate calculation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 50|55|60|65|70|77.5|85|92.5|100|110|125|150|175|200|250|300|400|500 — promote to reference product]']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'Regulatory class of the hazardous material, per DOT.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains hazardous materials.']
  hazmat_un_number varchar [note: 'United Nations identification number for the hazardous material.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial term governing responsibility and cost allocation.']
  issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the Bill of Lading was issued to the carrier.']
  number_of_packages varchar [note: 'Total count of individual packages or units in the shipment.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Standard classification of the packaging used.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference such as purchase order or sales order number linked to the shipment.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the security seal applied to the transport unit.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free‑text instructions for handling the shipment (e.g., keep upright, no stacking).']
  temperature_control_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if the shipment must be kept within a temperature range.']
  temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the required temperature range in Celsius.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the required temperature range in Celsius.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for the shipment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the Bill of Lading record.']
  volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total volume of the shipment in cubic meters.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Gross weight of the shipment in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Legal transport document issued for each chemical shipment, serving as the contract of carriage between shipper and carrier. Captures BOL number, shipper and consignee details, carrier SCAC code, freight class, declared value, hazmat emergency contact, special handling instructions, seal numbers, and DOT 49 CFR certification statement. Required for all regulated chemical transport under DOT and IATA rules.'
}

Table logistics.carrier {
  carrier_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the carrier master record.']
  billing_party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.party. Business justification: Carrier AP invoice processing: carriers are represented as billing parties for freight invoice payment. Role prefix \'billing_\' used because carrier is a logistics entity distinct from billing.party. E']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Carriers must be qualified to transport specific chemical products based on hazmat class, temperature requirements, and regulatory approvals. Chemical manufacturers maintain carrier qualification matr']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Carrier payments in chemical manufacturing are posted to freight payable and carrier expense GL accounts. Carrier-level GL assignment enables automatic account determination in accounts payable proces']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the material master']
  payment_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment_term. Business justification: Carrier AP payment processing: chemical manufacturers negotiate payment terms per carrier contract. Linking carrier to billing.payment_term enables freight invoice due-date calculation, early payment ']
  approved_hazard_classes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of GHS/UN hazard classes the carrier is authorized to transport.']
  average_load_capacity_tons decimal(6,2) [note: 'Typical maximum payload per vehicle expressed in metric tons.']
  benchmark_comparison varchar [note: 'Result of comparing carrier performance against internal benchmarks.']
  carrier_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the carrier\'s capabilities and services.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Legal or trade name of the freight carrier.']
  carrier_type varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transport the carrier provides.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Effective end date of the carrier service contract (null if open‑ended).']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the carrier service contract.']
  corrective_action_flag boolean [note: 'True if any corrective actions were issued for the carrier during the measurement period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the carrier record was first created.']
  ctpat_certified boolean [note: 'True if the carrier is certified under the C‑TPAT program.']
  damage_claim_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of shipments resulting in damage claims.']
  dot_carrier_number varchar [note: 'Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration (FMCSA) registration number for the carrier.']
  fleet_size varchar [note: 'Total number of vehicles or units operated by the carrier.']
  hazmat_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier holds a valid hazmat certification for regulated chemical shipments.']
  hazmat_incident_count varchar [note: 'Number of hazardous‑material incidents recorded for the carrier in the measurement period.']
  insurance_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the carrier\'s insurance certificate.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the carrier\'s liability insurance coverage expires.']
  insurance_provider_name varchar [note: 'Name of the insurance carrier providing liability coverage.']
  invoice_accuracy_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of carrier invoices that match agreed rates and terms.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance or performance audit of the carrier.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the carrier record.']
  mc_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the carrier\'s operating authority.']
  measurement_period_end date [note: 'Last day of the reporting window for carrier KPIs.']
  measurement_period_start date [note: 'First day of the reporting window for carrier KPIs.']
  oracle_tms_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Carrier identifier used within Oracle Transportation Management.']
  performance_on_time_delivery_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of deliveries made on schedule during the measurement period.']
  performance_on_time_pickup_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of pickups completed within the agreed time window during the measurement period.']
  preferred_lanes varchar [note: 'Typical origin‑to‑destination lane pairs where the carrier is preferred.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Main email address used for carrier communications.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Main telephone number for carrier communications.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current qualification status (e.g., Approved, Pending, Revoked)']
  regulatory_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance standing with DOT, EPA, and other applicable regulations.']
  safety_rating_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'Composite safety score (0‑5) derived from audit results and incident history.']
  scac_code varchar [note: 'Four‑character code assigned to the carrier by the National Motor Freight Traffic Association.']
  tender_acceptance_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of carrier tenders accepted versus offered.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the carrier relationship was terminated, if applicable.']
  tier_rating varchar [note: 'Internal tier classification based on performance and strategic importance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the carrier record.']

  Note: 'Master record for all approved freight carriers used to transport chemical products, including qualification status, contractual terms, and periodic performance scorecards. Stores carrier name, SCAC code, DOT carrier number, MC number, carrier type (LTL/FTL/rail/intermodal/tanker/ISO tank), hazmat certification status, insurance certificate expiry, approved chemical hazard classes, preferred lanes, carrier tier rating, CTPAT certification status, Oracle TMS carrier ID, and performance KPIs (on-time pickup/delivery rates, damage and claim rates, hazmat compliance incidents, tender acceptance rate, invoice accuracy, measurement period, corrective action flags, benchmark comparison). Supports carrier qualification reviews, contract renewal decisions, tier classification, and compliance vetting per DOT 49 CFR §172.800 security plan requirements for regulated chemical transport.'
}

Table logistics.freight_order {
  freight_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the freight order.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Freight order reporting and cost allocation per customer account requires direct account reference; enables financial reconciliation and compliance reporting.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the carrier selected to execute the shipment.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Freight orders must reference the chemical product to calculate hazardous‑material surcharges and produce compliance reports.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Freight order budgeting is recorded against the responsible cost center for budgeting and variance analysis.']
  delivery_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Freight order execution in chemical manufacturing requires a designated customer contact for delivery scheduling, dock access coordination, and hazmat receiving confirmation. The contact\'s ehs_contact']
  destination_location_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the location where the shipment is to be delivered.']
  freight_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.freight_rate. Business justification: A freight order is executed at a specific contracted or spot freight rate. freight_order currently has freight_rate (DECIMAL) as a denormalized rate value. Adding freight_rate_id FK to the freight_rat']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: freight_order.total_amount and accessorial_charges must be posted to GL accounts for freight expense recognition. In chemical manufacturing, freight order GL assignment drives accounts payable posting']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Internal order tracking ties each freight procurement to an internal order for approval workflow and spend control.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Freight Order Planning uses material master data to determine carrier selection, hazmat handling, and temperature requirements; linking to material_master provides the necessary material specification']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_order. Business justification: Freight orders are created to execute customer product orders in chemical manufacturing TMS. freight_order has purchase_order_id (supply side) but no sales product_order link. Direct FK enables freigh']
  origin_location_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the location where the shipment originates.']
  outbound_delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.outbound_delivery. Business justification: Freight orders execute the physical movement initiated by outbound deliveries in chemical manufacturing ERP-TMS integration. Direct FK enables freight cost allocation to specific deliveries, delivery ']
  parent_logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: A freight order is tendered for a specific shipment; linking to shipment provides the parent reference.']
  primary_freight_site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shipper (internal customer) that owns the freight order.']
  process_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.process_order. Business justification: Freight orders for finished chemicals are initiated upon process order completion. Linking freight_order to process_order enables cost allocation to the correct production order, supports shipment-to-']
  production_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.production_plan. Business justification: Freight Order Generation Process creates freight orders only after a Production Plan is firmed.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Freight orders represent revenue-generating logistics activity in chemical manufacturing. Profit center assignment enables freight cost and revenue attribution by business segment for segment reportin']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Required for generating freight orders from purchase orders; enables carrier tendering and freight cost tracking per PO.']
  shipment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment_plan. Business justification: A freight order is executed against a shipment plan that defines the route, load, and cost parameters. freight_order already links to shipment (header) but lacks a direct FK to the shipment_plan that ']
  supply_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.supply_plan. Business justification: Freight orders are created to execute supply plan commitments in chemical manufacturing — supply planners monitor freight order status to confirm supply plan execution and identify gaps. Supply plan e']
  tank_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.tank. Business justification: Bulk liquid chemical freight orders originate from specific tank farm tanks. Logistics coordinators must reference the source tank to schedule tanker truck loading, verify product compatibility, and c']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.vehicle. Business justification: A freight order is tendered to a carrier for execution using a specific vehicle or trailer. freight_order currently has trailer_number (STRING) and equipment_type (STRING) as denormalized references. ']
  accessorial_charges decimal(12,2) [note: 'Additional charges (e.g., liftgate, detention) applied to the freight order.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the electronic shipping notice sent to the receiver.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Document number issued by the carrier confirming receipt of the shipment.']
  carrier_acceptance_status varchar [note: 'Current acceptance response from the carrier.']
  carrier_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transport used by the carrier for this freight order.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance assessment of the freight order with DOT/EPA regulations.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier contract governing rates and terms.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was first captured in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary amounts.']
  delivery_window_end timestamp [note: 'Latest time the carrier must complete delivery at the destination.']
  delivery_window_start timestamp [note: 'Earliest time the carrier may deliver to the destination.']
  dot_regulation_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing applicable DOT transportation regulations for the shipment.']
  epa_regulation_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing applicable EPA environmental regulations for the shipment.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of trailer or equipment required for the shipment.']
  freight_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the freight order.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains hazardous materials subject to DOT regulations.']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'DOT classification of hazardous material (e.g., 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 9).']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this freight order record.']
  load_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shipment load based on size, hazard, and temperature requirements.']
  number_of_pallets varchar [note: 'Count of pallets included in the freight order.']
  order_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the freight order was created in the TMS.']
  pickup_window_end timestamp [note: 'Latest time the carrier must complete loading at the origin.']
  pickup_window_start timestamp [note: 'Earliest time the carrier may begin loading at the origin.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Customer purchase order linked to this freight shipment.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the freight order meets all required regulatory checks.']
  temperature_requirement_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Required temperature control for temperature-sensitive chemicals.']
  tender_method varchar [note: 'Method used to tender the freight order to carriers.']
  total_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount payable for the freight order (rate + accessorials).']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific trailer assigned to the freight order.']
  volume_cubic_m decimal(12,3) [note: 'Aggregate volume of the freight order in cubic meters.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate weight of the freight order in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Operational freight order tendered to a carrier for execution of a shipment or consolidated load. Captures freight order number, tendering method (spot/contract/load board), carrier acceptance status, agreed freight rate, accessorial charges, pickup and delivery windows, load type, temperature requirements for temperature-sensitive chemicals (e.g., epoxy resins, catalysts), and TMS order reference. Represents the contractual execution unit between Chemical Mfg and the carrier per DOT 49 CFR §172.204 shipper certification requirements. Supports multi-stop and pool-point distribution common in chemical distribution networks.'
}

Table logistics.delivery_confirmation {
  delivery_confirmation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the delivery confirmation record.']

  freight_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.freight_order.   logistics_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer or receiving party that accepted the shipment.']

  order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_order. Business justification: Delivery confirmation closes the order-to-cash cycle. In chemical manufacturing, POD (proof of delivery) is matched to the product order to trigger invoicing and revenue recognition. Direct FK enables']
  outbound_delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.outbound_delivery. Business justification: Delivery confirmation is the downstream event that closes an outbound delivery in chemical manufacturing logistics. One outbound delivery generates one delivery confirmation (POD). This link is requir']
  parent_logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Delivery confirmation records proof of delivery for a shipment; linking to shipment ties the POD to its shipment.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Delivery confirmation triggers revenue recognition in chemical manufacturing.   receiver_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Linking delivery_confirmation to customer.contact ']
  receiver_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.site. Business justification: Proof-of-delivery reconciliation in chemical manufacturing requires matching the delivery to the specific customer site (dock restrictions, hazmat receiving capability, COA/SDS requirements per site).']
  condition_on_arrival varchar [note: 'Observed condition of the goods at the time of receipt.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the proof‑of‑delivery document.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delivery confirmation record was first created in the system.']
  delivered_quantity decimal(20,4) [note: 'Quantity of product actually delivered to the receiver.']
  delivery_confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the delivery confirmation.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shipment was physically received by the customer.']
  discrepancy_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes describing any shortages, damages, or other issues noted on receipt.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the type of exception, if any, associated with the delivery.']
  hazardous_material_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the shipment contains hazardous materials subject to DOT 49 CFR.']
  humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity percentage measured at the delivery location.']
  pod_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored electronic proof‑of‑delivery document.']
  quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the delivered quantity.']
  receiver_signature varchar [note: 'Electronic representation of the signature captured from the receiver as proof of delivery.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery met all applicable regulatory requirements.']
  temperature_c decimal(10,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature recorded at the delivery location, in degrees Celsius.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for the shipment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the delivery confirmation record.']

  Note: 'Record of proof-of-delivery (POD) for completed chemical shipments. Captures actual delivery date and time, receiver name and signature, delivery location, condition of goods on arrival, any noted discrepancies (shortage, damage, contamination), exception codes, and electronic POD document reference. Triggers downstream billing and COA release workflows.'
}

Direct FK to chemical_product enables product-level hazmat compliance reporting and regulatory audit without joini']

  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.functional_location. Business justification: PSM-covered chemical plants require hazmat declarations to reference the functional location (loading bay, chemical transfer area) where hazardous materials are handled. This supports OSHA PSM complia']

Business justification: Hazmat compliance costs (PPE, emergency response, regulatory fees) in chemical manufacturing are posted to specific EHS GL accounts. GL assignment on hazmat_declaration enables EHS cost tracking and r']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Hazmat compliance programs in chemical manufacturing track costs (training, PPE, emergency response planning) against internal orders for EHS budget management and regulatory audit trails. Internal or']
  loading_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.equipment. Business justification: OSHA PSM and EPA RMP require documentation of specific loading/filling equipment used when transferring hazardous chemicals. Hazmat declarations must reference the pump, loading arm, or filling statio']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Hazmat Declaration must reference the exact material to populate UN number, hazard class, and safety data; a material_master_id FK provides the authoritative source.']
  origin_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.facility. Business justification: SARA Title III and EPA TRI reporting require aggregating hazmat shipment quantities by originating facility annually. Chemical manufacturing EHS teams link hazmat declarations to the EHS facility reco']
  process_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.process_order. Business justification: Hazmat declarations under DOT 49 CFR and IATA DGR must be reconcilable with the process order that produced the chemical, confirming hazard classification, UN number, and packing group match productio']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability analysis includes hazmat handling fees allocated to the profit center of the product line.']
  regulatory_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.regulatory_profile. Business justification: DOT/IATA hazmat declaration generation in chemical manufacturing must be validated against the receiving customer\'s regulatory profile (end-use declaration, UN number, hazmat transport class, REACH/TS']
  safety_data_sheet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.safety_data_sheet. Business justification: DOT 49 CFR and OSHA HazCom require the SDS to be the authoritative source for proper shipping name, hazard class, and emergency response info on hazmat declarations. Chemical manufacturing compliance ']
  sds_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sds. Business justification: The SDS is the authoritative source for hazmat declaration data: UN number, hazard class, packing group, proper shipping name, emergency response. Regulatory traceability from hazmat declaration to th']
  sds_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.sds_record. Business justification: DOT/IATA/IMDG regulations require hazmat declarations to be backed by a current SDS for proper shipping name, UN number, hazard class, packing group, and emergency response data. Chemical logistics ex']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shipment to which this hazmat declaration belongs.']
  shipment_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment_line. Business justification: The hazmat_declaration product description explicitly states it is a \'consolidated hazardous materials compliance record for each regulated chemical shipment LINE.\' The existing FK hazmat_declaration.']
  applicable_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework governing the shipment (DOT, IATA, or IMDG).']
  compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date when the declaration was last reviewed for compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the declaration record was created.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the hazmat declaration becomes effective.']
  emergency_response_guide_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the applicable Emergency Response Guide.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the declaration is no longer valid (if applicable).']
  hazard_class varchar [note: 'Hazard class of the material (e.g., 1, 2, 3, …). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|2|3|4|5|6|7|8|9 — promote to reference product]']
  hazard_division varchar [note: 'Division within the hazard class, if applicable (e.g., 1A, 1B).']
  hazmat_declaration_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the hazmat declaration.']
  is_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates if the shipment is exempt from certain regulations.']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is classified as hazardous.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number of the declaration within the shipment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any additional information or comments.']
  packaging_type varchar [note: 'Type of packaging used for the hazardous material.']
  packing_group varchar [note: 'Packing group indicating the degree of danger (I, II, or III).']
  ppe_requirements varchar [note: 'Required PPE for personnel handling the shipment.']
  proper_shipping_name varchar [note: 'Official shipping name as defined in the UN Model Regulations.']
  quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Amount of hazardous material declared.']
  quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity (kilograms, liters, gallons, pounds).']
  regulation_section varchar [note: 'Specific section of the regulation that applies (e.g., 49 CFR 172.200).']
  segregation_requirements varchar [note: 'Special segregation or incompatibility requirements for transport.']
  shipper_certification varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the shipper is certified for the material.']
  spill_response_instructions varchar [note: 'Procedures to follow in case of a spill during transport.']
  temperature_limit_max decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable temperature for transport.']
  temperature_limit_min decimal(8,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable temperature for transport.']
  temperature_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for temperature limits (Celsius or Fahrenheit).']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transport used for the shipment.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'UN numeric identifier for the hazardous material.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the declaration.']

  Note: 'Consolidated hazardous materials compliance record for each regulated chemical shipment line, combining SDS-derived transport requirements with DOT 49 CFR, IATA DGR, and IMDG Code shipping declarations. Records UN number, proper shipping name, hazard class and division, packing group, quantity and type of packaging, emergency response guide number (ERG), shipper certification, segregation requirements for incompatible chemicals, temperature limits during transport, PPE requirements for drivers, spill response instructions, and applicable transport regulation. Serves as the single source of truth for all hazmat transport compliance data — from SDS-sourced chemical properties through to regulatory declaration filing. Mandatory for all chemical shipments involving hazardous materials per DOT 49 CFR §172.200-204.'
}

Table logistics.shipment_plan {
  shipment_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for shipment_plan']
  billing_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_schedule. Business justification: Contract/milestone billing alignment: chemical manufacturers with contract customers bill on schedules tied to planned shipments. Linking shipment_plan to billing_schedule enables milestone billing tr']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.campaign. Business justification: Chemical manufacturers plan carrier capacity and route optimization at the campaign level — a production campaign produces a defined volume that drives bulk shipment planning. Linking shipment_plan to']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier assigned to the plan.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Planning costs (fuel surcharge, route optimization) are budgeted against the cost center owning the shipment.']
  freight_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.freight_rate. Business justification: A shipment plan is costed against a contracted or spot freight rate for the applicable lane, mode, and carrier. shipment_plan has cost_gross, cost_net, fuel_surcharge_amount as computed cost fields bu']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: shipment_plan.cost_gross/cost_net/fuel_surcharge_amount require GL account assignment for planned freight cost posting and budget vs. actual freight expense reporting in chemical manufacturing financi']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_order. Business justification: Shipment plans are created to fulfill confirmed customer product orders in chemical manufacturing. S&OP and logistics planning processes require direct traceability from shipment plan to the product o']
  origin_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.facility. Business justification: Shipment planning for chemical facilities must respect EHS facility constraints: permitted hazmat storage quantities, operating permit conditions, and Tier II threshold limits. Linking shipment_plan t']
  parent_logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Shipment plan is created to plan a particular shipment; linking to shipment establishes the parent‑child hierarchy.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.production_schedule. Business justification: Integrated Production & Logistics Scheduling aligns route plans with the Production Schedule for on‑time departures.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability analysis uses planned shipment cost data linked to the profit center of the product line.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shipper (customer) who owns the shipment.']
  supply_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.supply_plan. Business justification: Shipment plans are the logistics execution of supply plans in chemical manufacturing — when a supply plan calls for product movement, a shipment plan is created. Supply-to-logistics traceability repor']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.vehicle. Business justification: A shipment plan assigns a specific vehicle for route optimization and load planning. shipment_plan currently has vehicle_type (STRING) and vehicle_capacity_weight_kg/volume_m3 as denormalized fields. ']
  alternate_route_options varchar [note: 'Text description of alternate routes considered during planning.']
  arrival_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for shipment arrival.']
  bridge_restriction_flag boolean [note: 'True if the route is prohibited for certain hazmat due to bridge weight/height restrictions.']
  compliance_volume_limit_m3 decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable volume per DOT regulations for the selected route.']
  compliance_weight_limit_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable weight per DOT regulations for the selected route.']
  cost_adjustments decimal(12,2) [note: 'Sum of all cost adjustments (fees, discounts, surcharges) applied to the shipment.']
  cost_gross decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total gross cost of the shipment before any adjustments.']
  cost_net decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final net cost after applying adjustments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first captured in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the shipment cost.']
  departure_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for shipment departure.']
  estimated_transit_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Projected total transit time for the route, in minutes.']
  fuel_surcharge_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the fuel surcharge applied to the shipment.']
  fuel_surcharge_basis varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate fuel surcharge.']
  hazmat_route_flag boolean [note: 'True when the route is designated for hazardous material transport.']
  is_expedited boolean [note: 'True if the shipment is marked for expedited handling.']
  load_volume_m3 decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual volume loaded onto the vehicle after consolidation.']
  load_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual weight loaded onto the vehicle after consolidation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any special instructions or comments.']
  optimization_objective varchar [note: 'Primary objective used for route and load optimization.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the shipment plan.']
  plan_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment plan was generated.']
  planned_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the shipment is expected to reach the destination.']
  planned_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the shipment is expected to leave the origin.']
  restricted_route_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the route includes any regulatory restrictions (e.g., tunnel/bridge limits).']
  route_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the planned route, including waypoints.']
  route_distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total distance of the planned route, in kilometers.']
  shipment_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shipment plan.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature control requirement for the shipment load.']
  total_volume_m3 decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate volume of all items in the shipment plan.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate weight of all items in the shipment plan.']
  tunnel_restriction_flag boolean [note: 'True if the route is prohibited for certain hazmat due to tunnel restrictions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the shipment plan record.']
  vehicle_capacity_volume_m3 decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum volume the vehicle can carry, in cubic meters.']
  vehicle_capacity_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum weight the vehicle can carry, in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Consolidated shipment planning record combining route optimization and load planning for one or more shipments. Captures planned route legs, waypoints, estimated transit times, distance, fuel surcharge basis, restricted route flags (tunnel/bridge restrictions for hazmat per DOT 49 CFR §397.71), alternate route options, optimization objective (cost/time/compliance), load consolidation details, vehicle type and capacity assignment, actual loaded weight and volume, load sequence, temperature zone assignments for mixed chemical loads, and loading dock assignment. Supports DOT hazmat routing compliance, freight utilization optimization, and DOT weight compliance.'
}

Table logistics.freight_rate {
  freight_rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each freight rate record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the carrier (shipping provider) that offers this rate.']
  cost_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_element. Business justification: Freight rates are cost components in chemical manufacturing product costing. Cost element assignment on freight rate master enables freight cost to be included in standard cost rolls and activity-base']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Freight rate types (base, hazmat surcharge, fuel surcharge) map to specific GL accounts for freight expense classification in chemical manufacturing. GL assignment on rate master enables automatic acc']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: In chemical manufacturing, freight rate contracts are negotiated by customer segment (strategic accounts, hazmat-certified segments, bulk chemical segments). Segment-specific freight rates drive prici']
  commodity_class varchar [note: 'Chemical commodity classification (e.g., petrochemical, specialty, bulk) for which the rate is negotiated.']
  compliance_hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rate complies with hazardous material transport regulations.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rate contract (e.g., contracted, spot, dynamic pricing).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the freight rate record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary values.']
  destination_zone varchar [note: 'Destination geographic or tariff zone code used in rate calculations.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the rate becomes effective and may be used for cost calculations.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the rate expires; null if the rate is open‑ended.']
  freight_rate_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing special conditions, lane details, or notes.']
  freight_rate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rate record.']
  fuel_surcharge_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fuel surcharge expressed as a percentage of the base rate.']
  hazmat_surcharge decimal(12,4) [note: 'Additional surcharge for transporting hazardous chemicals, per regulatory requirements (DOT 49 CFR).']
  is_contractual boolean [note: 'True if the rate is part of a contracted agreement; false for spot rates.']
  lane_type varchar [note: 'Description of the shipment lane configuration.']
  minimum_charge decimal(12,4) [note: 'Minimum charge that applies regardless of distance or weight.']
  mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transport for which the rate applies.']
  origin_zone varchar [note: 'Origin geographic or tariff zone code used in rate calculations.']
  rate_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code used by logistics planners to reference the rate.']
  rate_source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated the rate (e.g., OTM, SAP, custom).']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rate (e.g., base, accessorial, fuel surcharge).']
  rate_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the rate expressed in the selected unit of measure.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory rule or document governing the rate (e.g., DOT 49 CFR, REACH).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the rate value is expressed (e.g., CWT per mile, flat fee).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the freight rate record.']
  volume_limit_cubic_meters decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum volume for which the rate applies; null if unlimited.']
  weight_limit_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum weight for which the rate applies; null if unlimited.']

  Note: 'Contracted or spot freight rate records negotiated with carriers for specific lanes, modes, and chemical commodity types. Stores rate type (base/accessorial/fuel surcharge), origin and destination zone, carrier, effective and expiry dates, rate per unit (CWT/mile/flat), minimum charge, hazmat surcharge, and Oracle TMS rate table reference. Drives freight cost calculation and carrier selection optimization.'
}

Table logistics.customs_declaration {
  customs_declaration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the customs declaration record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'System identifier for the carrier entity.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Customs declarations for chemical exports require product-level data: HS code, ECCN, REACH registration, hazard classification. Direct FK to chemical_product enables export control compliance reportin']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Customs duties and fees are booked to the cost center that owns the imported material.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Customs duty accrual in chemical manufacturing requires fiscal period assignment for accurate period-end financial close. Import duties must be accrued in the correct fiscal period for GAAP/IFRS compl']
  freight_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.freight_order. Business justification: A customs declaration for international chemical shipments must reference the specific freight order being cleared through customs. customs_declaration already links to shipment (header) and carrier, ']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: customs_declaration.duty_amount and freight_cost must be posted to import duty expense and freight GL accounts. In chemical manufacturing with global supply chains, customs duty GL posting is a mandat']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Customs duties are billed; linking declaration to invoice enables duty charge aggregation on the same invoice.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Chemical export/import customs declarations require lot-level data for country-of-origin certification, restricted substance declarations (REACH, RoHS), and purity verification. Customs brokers and re']
  parent_logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Customs declaration is filed for a specific shipment; linking provides direct reference to the shipment.']
  primary_customs_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the exporting party (company) responsible for the shipment.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability reporting requires customs cost allocation to the profit center generating the revenue.']
  sds_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sds. Business justification: SDS data (GHS classification, UN number, REACH registration number, hazard statements) is required input for chemical customs declarations. Regulatory traceability from customs declaration to the SDS ']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the ASN generated for the outbound shipment.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the bill of lading associated with the shipment.']
  clearance_date date [note: 'Date on which customs clearance was completed or finalized.']
  clearance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the customs clearance process.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for any additional compliance observations or remarks.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code where the chemical product was manufactured or sourced.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the customs declaration record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the declared value (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  customs_broker_name varchar [note: 'Name of the licensed customs broker handling the declaration.']
  declaration_date date [note: 'Date the customs declaration was filed with the authority.']
  declaration_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the customs declaration by the logistics or customs system.']
  declaration_status varchar [note: 'Overall processing status of the customs declaration.']
  declaration_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the declaration is for an export shipment or an import shipment.']
  declared_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value declared for customs assessment, expressed in the transaction currency.']
  destination_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code of the final destination for the shipment.']
  duty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of customs duty assessed on the shipment.']
  duty_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which the duty amount is expressed.']
  eccn varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code used by U.S. export control to classify dual‑use items.']
  freight_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total freight charge for the shipment.']
  freight_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the freight cost (ISO 4217).']
  hazardous_classification varchar [note: 'UN number or class describing the hazardous nature of the chemical.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'True if the shipment contains hazardous chemicals subject to DOT 49 CFR.']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'Six- to ten-digit code that classifies the goods for customs duties and statistical purposes.']
  import_permit_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the import permit required for regulated chemicals.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'Standard trade term defining responsibility for costs, risks, and logistics between seller and buyer.']
  is_lcl boolean [note: 'True if the shipment is less than a full container load (LCL).']
  number_of_packages varchar [note: 'Count of individual packages or containers in the shipment.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Standard packaging type used for the shipment.']
  reach_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the chemical product complies with EU REACH regulations (true = compliant).']
  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority overseeing the customs process (e.g., CBP, EPA).']
  tariff_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tariff rate expressed as a percentage of the declared value.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transport used for the shipment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the customs declaration record.']
  volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total cubic volume of the shipment in cubic meters.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Gross weight of the shipment in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Export or import customs declaration for international chemical shipments. Records declaration type (export/import), HS tariff code, country of origin, declared value, currency, Incoterms, export control classification number (ECCN), REACH compliance flag, import permit reference, customs broker details, and clearance status. Required for cross-border movement of chemicals under TSCA, REACH, and national customs regulations.'
}

Table logistics.vehicle {

  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.equipment.   cleaning_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the vehicle\'s cleaning certification.']

  cross_contamination_prevention boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cleaning procedures have been completed to prevent cross‑contamination before the next load.']

  gps_longitude double [note: 'Most recent longitude coordinate reported by the vehicle\'s GPS.']

  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent inspection.']

  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the liability insurance policy covering the vehicle.']

  maintenance_cycle_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Mileage interval after which scheduled maintenance is required.']

  registration_number varchar [note: 'U.S. Department of Transportation registration identifier for the vehicle.']
  tare_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Weight of the empty vehicle without cargo.']
  temperature_control_capability boolean [note: 'True if the vehicle can maintain a controlled temperature environment for temperature‑sensitive chemicals.']
  temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Highest temperature the vehicle can maintain when temperature control is enabled.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lowest temperature the vehicle can maintain when temperature control is enabled.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the vehicle record.']
  vehicle_code varchar [note: 'Internal alphanumeric code used to reference the vehicle within the enterprise system.']
  vehicle_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or designation for the vehicle (e.g., "West Coast Tanker 12").']
  vehicle_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the vehicle.']
  vehicle_type varchar [note: 'Category of transport equipment, aligned with DOT hazardous‑material classifications.']
  vin_number varchar [note: 'Globally unique identifier assigned by the vehicle manufacturer.']
  year_of_manufacture varchar [note: 'Calendar year the vehicle was built.']

  Note: 'Master record for vehicles and transport equipment used in chemical product logistics, including owned fleet assets and carrier-provided equipment. Stores vehicle ID, vehicle type (tanker, flatbed, dry van, ISO container, railcar, IBC chassis), license plate or equipment number, DOT registration number, hazmat placard capability per DOT 49 CFR §172.500-§172.560, last inspection date, annual DOT inspection status per 49 CFR §396.17, temperature control capability and range, dedicated/non-dedicated status, last cargo carried (for contamination prevention), cleaning certification date, tare weight, and maximum payload. Supports equipment assignment, DOT compliance tracking, and chemical cross-contamination prevention through last-cargo verification.'
}

  weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total weight of goods invoiced, expressed in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Core billing document representing a customer invoice generated from a sales order or delivery. Captures invoice number, billing date, customer account, total amount, currency, payment terms, tax totals, and lifecycle status (open, partially paid, cleared, disputed). Serves as the primary accounts receivable document and SSOT for all customer-facing billing transactions in the chemical manufacturing order-to-cash cycle. Links to invoice_line for item detail and to payment_receipt for cash application.'
}

  waste_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.waste_stream. Business justification: Waste disposal invoice lines must reference the specific waste stream being billed. Chemical manufacturers bill cost centers per waste stream for disposal services; line-level traceability is required']
  work_order_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order_operation. Business justification: Detailed Maintenance Billing Line Items: invoice lines map to specific work order operations to itemize labor and material costs.']
  accounting_date date [note: 'Fiscal date used for posting the line to the ledger.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice line record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary values on this line.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total discount value applied to this line before tax.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert the line amount to the corporate reporting currency.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the batch/lot, if applicable.']
  gl_account varchar [note: 'GL account number used for posting the line\'s financial impact.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'True if the product is classified as hazardous under regulatory standards.']
  intercompany_flag boolean [note: 'True if the line is part of an intercompany billing arrangement.']
  intercompany_invoice_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the corresponding intercompany invoice.']
  line_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Gross monetary amount for the line (quantity × unit_price).']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the invoice for ordering.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the line (e.g., open, closed, cancelled).']
  manufacturing_site varchar [note: 'Plant or site where the product batch was produced.']
  net_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net amount after tax and discount (line_amount + tax_amount – discount_amount).']
  plant varchar [note: 'SAP plant code representing the production location.']
  price_override_flag boolean [note: 'True if the unit price was manually overridden.']
  price_override_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the unit price was overridden.']
  product_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the product or service.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Amount of product or service delivered, expressed in the unit of measure.']
  rebate_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary value of the rebate applied to this line.']
  rebate_flag boolean [note: 'True if a rebate applies to this line.']
  regulatory_compliance_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating applicable regulatory requirements (e.g., REACH, GHS) for the product.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date on which revenue for this line is recognized per accounting policy.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date when the line was settled with the customer.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the financial settlement for this line.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Any special handling or shipping instructions for the line item.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary tax amount calculated for this line.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code applied to this line (e.g., VAT, GST).']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'True if the line is exempt from tax.']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price per unit of the product before taxes, discounts, or rebates.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the quantity (e.g., kilograms, liters, pieces).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the invoice line record.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail of a customer invoice representing individual chemical products, formulations, or services billed. Captures material number, product description, quantity, unit of measure, unit price, line amount, tax code, cost center, and profit center. Supports detailed revenue recognition and COGS allocation per product SKU and batch lot in SAP FI/CO.'
}

  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary values.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the adjustment becomes effective for accounting purposes.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier from an external system (e.g., ERP, EDI).']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used for any associated payment or surcharge.']
  posted_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who posted the adjustment.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the adjustment was posted to the ledger.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date the adjustment was posted to the general ledger.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the business reason for the adjustment (e.g., RETURN, PRICE_CORRECTION).']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing details of why the adjustment was issued.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'True if this adjustment reverses a previous adjustment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated the adjustment record (e.g., SAP_SD).']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the adjustment amount.']
  tax_category varchar [note: 'Classification of tax treatment for the adjustment.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code that determines the tax rate and rules applied to the adjustment.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'True if the adjustment is exempt from tax.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate expressed as a percentage (e.g., 7.25).']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary impact of the adjustment after tax (adjustment_amount + tax_amount).']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The moment the adjustment was executed (e.g., when the credit memo was posted).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the adjustment record.']

  Note: 'Financial adjustment document issued to a customer to either reduce (credit) or increase (debit) the amount owed on an existing invoice. Credit scenarios include product returns, quality disputes (OOS/OOT), pricing corrections, or volume rebate settlements. Debit scenarios include underbilling corrections, surcharges, freight adjustments, or hazardous material handling fees. Captures adjustment document number, adjustment type (credit/debit), reference invoice, reason code, adjustment amount, tax impact, and approval workflow status. Serves as the SSOT for all post-invoice billing modifications in the chemical manufacturing order-to-cash cycle. Managed in SAP SD/FI as billing document types.'
}

  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Result of matching the payment to open invoices.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that generated the payment record (e.g., SAP FI, external bank feed).']

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the payment receipt record.']

  Note: 'Record of a customer payment received against an open accounts receivable invoice. Supports payment reconciliation and AR aging in SAP FI.'
}

  Note: 'Open accounts receivable item representing an outstanding customer obligation not yet fully cleared. }

}

  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Financial period identifier (e.g., FY2023Q2) for the revenue event.']
