  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Revenue recognition events must be attributed to a legal entity for statutory financial reporting under IFRS 15/ASC 606. Chemical manufacturers with multi-entity structures require legal entity attrib']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Revenue recognition in chemical manufacturing under ASC 606/IFRS 15 is triggered by lot-level delivery acceptance — revenue is recognized when a specific lot transfers control to the customer. revenue']
  manufacturing_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.manufacturing_order. Business justification: Revenue must be recognized when a manufacturing order is completed (ASC 606/IFRS 15); linking enables accurate revenue timing.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: ASC 606/IFRS 15 compliance: revenue events tied to material deliveries need the material identifier to allocate revenue correctly.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Revenue recognition is driven by closed opportunities; linking enables ASC 606/IFRS15 reporting.']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_order. Business justification: ASC 606/IFRS 15 revenue recognition ties events to performance obligation fulfillment tracked at the order level. Revenue recognition events can exist without an invoice (bill-and-hold, consignment ar']
  outbound_delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.outbound_delivery. Business justification: Under ASC 606, revenue is recognized at transfer of control, which in chemical manufacturing occurs at point of delivery. Linking revenue_recognition_event directly to outbound_delivery enables financ']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.production_schedule. Business justification: Under ASC 606/IFRS 15, revenue recognition in chemical manufacturing is triggered by production completion milestones defined in the production schedule (e.g., batch release, campaign completion). Aud']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Revenue recognition events under ASC 606/IFRS 15 must be attributed to profit centers for segment P&L reporting in chemical manufacturing. Auditors and controllers require profit-center-level recogniz']
  revenue_recognition_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the accounting journal entry generated for this revenue event.']
  reversal_of_event_revenue_recognition_event_id varchar [note: 'If this event is a reversal, references the original revenue recognition event.']
  compliance_flag_asc606 boolean [note: 'Flag indicating compliance with ASC 606 revenue recognition criteria.']
  compliance_flag_ifrs15 boolean [note: 'Flag indicating compliance with IFRS 15 revenue recognition criteria.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the revenue recognition record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the revenue amounts.']
  deferred_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of revenue deferred (unearned) associated with this event.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Discount or rebate amount applied to the recognized revenue.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'External reference number for the revenue recognition posting event.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the revenue recognition event was triggered (e.g., delivery, billing, milestone).']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied if currency conversion was required.']
  intercompany_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the revenue event is intercompany (between legal entities).']
  is_estimated boolean [note: 'Indicates if the recognized revenue amount is estimated (e.g., for long-term contracts).']
  is_reversal boolean [note: 'Indicates if this event is a reversal of a previously posted revenue recognition.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the revenue recognition event.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Financial posting date for the revenue recognition entry.']
  recognition_end_date date [note: 'End date of the revenue recognition period (for over-time recognitions).']
  recognition_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the revenue recognition period (for over-time recognitions).']
  recognition_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether revenue is recognized at a single point or over a period.']
  recognized_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of revenue recognized in this event.']
  revenue_amount_local decimal(18,2) [note: 'Recognized revenue amount in the local currency of the transaction.']
  revenue_category varchar [note: 'Category of revenue (e.g., product sales, service, licensing).']
  revenue_line_type varchar [note: 'Type of revenue line item.']
  revenue_recognition_event_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the revenue recognition event.']
  revenue_recognition_method varchar [note: 'Accounting standard used for revenue recognition.']
  source_event_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source transaction (e.g., delivery note, invoice) that caused this revenue recognition.']
  source_event_type varchar [note: 'Source event that triggered the revenue recognition.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component included in the recognized revenue amount.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code applied to the revenue transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the revenue recognition record.']

  Note: 'Record of a revenue recognition posting event triggered by delivery, billing, or contractual milestone completion. Captures recognition event type (point-in-time, over-time), recognized revenue amount, deferred revenue amount, performance obligation reference, recognition date, and ASC 606 / IFRS 15 compliance flags. Integrates with SAP FI/CO revenue accounting and reporting (RAR) for SOX-compliant financial close.'
}

Table billing.payment_term {
  payment_term_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the payment term.']
  applicability_customer_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment code (e.g., INDUSTRIAL, RETAIL) for which this payment term is valid.']
  applicability_product_group varchar [note: 'Product group identifier (e.g., CHEMICALS, POLYMERS) to which the term applies.']
  applicability_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region (ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code) where the payment term can be used.']
  baseline_date_rule varchar [note: 'Rule indicating which document date is used as the starting point for due‑date calculation.']
  cash_flow_impact_category varchar [note: 'Categorization of the term\'s impact on cash‑flow forecasting.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason recorded for the most recent change to the payment term.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework that governs handling of the payment term.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the payment term record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment term record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code applicable to monetary amounts in the payment term.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the early‑payment discount is a percentage or a fixed monetary amount.']
  dunning_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between successive dunning notices.']
  dunning_level_start varchar [note: 'Initial dunning level triggered after the due date passes.']
  early_payment_discount_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from the baseline date during which the early‑payment discount applies.']
  early_payment_discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount offered for payment made within the qualifying early‑payment window.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the payment term becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the payment term expires; null if open‑ended.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment term in an external source system (e.g., legacy ERP).']
  fixed_discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount discount offered for early payment when discount_type is fixed_amount.']
  is_consolidated boolean [note: 'True if the term applies to intercompany or consolidated billing scenarios.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this term is the default for new customers or contracts.']
  legal_clause_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the contract clause or statutory provision governing this payment term.']
  max_discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cap on the total discount value that can be applied under this term.']
  net_due_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of days after the baseline date when full payment is due if no discount is taken.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional remarks, special conditions, or legal caveats.']
  payment_method_allowed varchar [note: 'Payment methods that may be used when this term is applied.']
  payment_term_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the payment term and its business purpose.']
  payment_term_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment term.']
  requires_approval boolean [note: 'True if usage of this payment term must be approved by a manager or finance officer.']
  tax_included_flag boolean [note: 'True if taxes are considered included in the net due amount.']
  term_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code representing the payment term (e.g., NET30, 2/10NET30).']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last updated the payment term record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the payment term record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the payment term definition for change‑management tracking.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining standard payment terms offered to customers in chemical manufacturing commercial agreements. Captures payment term code, description, baseline date calculation rule, early payment discount percentage and qualifying days, net due days, and applicability rules by customer segment, region, or product group. Examples include Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30, letter of credit terms, and consignment settlement terms. Referenced by invoices and sales orders to calculate due dates and drive AR aging, dunning triggers, and cash flow forecasting in SAP FI/SD.'
}

  variable_amount_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the billing amount can vary based on usage or other factors.']

  Note: 'Master record defining a periodic or milestone-based billing plan for long-term chemical supply contracts, toll manufacturing agreements, or take-or-pay arrangements. Captures billing plan type (periodic, milestone), billing dates, planned billing amounts, percentage of contract value, and billing status per period. Managed in SAP SD billing plan (FPLA/FPLP) for contract-driven revenue scheduling.'
}

  Note: 'Master reference table for settlement. Referenced by settlement_id.'

Table ehs.safety_incident {
  safety_incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the safety incident record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Carrier Safety Performance Tracking links safety incidents to the carrier involved, enabling carrier compliance metrics and OSHA reporting.']
  cas_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.cas_registry. Business justification: Safety incident reports must link to the CAS registry to retrieve official hazard classifications for the reported chemical.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Incident reports must link to the specific chemical product for root‑cause analysis and regulatory reporting (OSHA, EPA).']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for allocating incident-related costs to the responsible cost center for budgeting and regulatory cost reporting.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.equipment. Business justification: INCIDENT MANAGEMENT: Safety incident reports must record the equipment involved for root‑cause analysis and regulatory reporting.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.facility. Business justification: Safety incidents occur at specific facilities. safety_incident currently has no facility-level reference within the EHS domain (only functional_location_id from maintenance domain and department as a ']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical location (plant, site, area) where the incident occurred.']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.goods_receipt. Business justification: Safety incidents during hazmat receiving (chemical spills, exposure) must be linked to the specific goods receipt for OSHA recordable reporting, vendor accountability, and root cause analysis. Chemica']
  inbound_delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_delivery. Business justification: DOT/PHMSA requires safety incidents during hazmat transport and unloading to reference the inbound delivery document. Before GR posting, the inbound_delivery is the operative supply record for carrier']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Major safety incidents in chemical manufacturing trigger funded investigation and remediation projects. The safety_incident has property_damage_amount requiring a financial object for cost posting. OS']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Required for incident investigation to trace the specific lot that caused the safety incident, enabling recall and root‑cause analysis.']
  lot_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.lot_record. Business justification: Safety incidents involving specific raw material lots (worker exposure during handling of a specific batch, fire involving a specific lot) require lot-level traceability for OSHA recordkeeping, recall']
  manufacturing_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.manufacturing_order. Business justification: Safety Incident Reporting requires linking each incident to the manufacturing order that caused it for root‑cause analysis and regulatory reporting.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Incident tracking uses the material master record to access detailed specifications, storage, and compliance data for the affected raw material.']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order. Business justification: Regulatory incident reports must reference the specific sales order that caused the safety incident for traceability and liability analysis.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Root‑cause analysis ties each safety incident to the specific PO that delivered the offending material, supporting supplier audits and compliance reporting.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.vehicle. Business justification: Vehicle Accident Investigation requires associating safety incidents with the specific vehicle to analyze fleet safety and maintenance needs.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: Regulatory incident reports require identifying the responsible supplier of the hazardous material, enabling supplier performance tracking and corrective actions.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_location. Business justification: Safety incidents (spills, exposures, fires) occur at specific warehouse storage locations. OSHA incident reporting and root cause analysis require linking the incident to the physical hazmat storage l']
  attachments_flag boolean [note: 'True if supporting documents or media are attached to the incident record.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date for completion of the corrective action.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a corrective action plan is needed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the incident record was initially created.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department responsible for the area where the incident happened.']
  environmental_impact_flag boolean [note: 'True if the incident resulted in an environmental release or impact.']
  exposure_concentration decimal(12,4) [note: 'Measured concentration of the chemical exposure.']
  exposure_exceedance_flag boolean [note: 'True if measured concentration exceeds the regulatory threshold.']
  exposure_measurement_type varchar [note: 'Method used to measure chemical exposure.']
  exposure_threshold decimal(12,4) [note: 'Regulatory threshold (PEL/TLV) for the measured chemical.']
  exposure_units varchar [note: 'Units of the exposure concentration measurement.']
  first_aid_provided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether first‑aid was administered on site.']
  follow_up_actions varchar [note: 'Planned or completed actions to prevent recurrence.']
  incident_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the incident for external reference and tracking.']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the incident (e.g., injury, near‑miss, chemical exposure).']
  injury_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of injury or health effect resulting from the incident.']
  investigation_completed_date date [note: 'Date when the investigation was formally closed.']
  investigation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the incident investigation.']
  lost_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of full days of work lost due to the incident.']
  lost_time_hours varchar [note: 'Additional hours of work lost beyond full days.']
  medical_treatment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether professional medical treatment was required.']
  number_of_affected varchar [note: 'Count of individuals impacted (injured, ill, or exposed) by the incident.']
  occurrence_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident actually occurred.']
  osha_recordable_flag boolean [note: 'True if the incident meets OSHA recordable criteria.']
  property_damage_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary estimate of property or equipment damage caused by the incident.']
  psm_incident_flag boolean [note: 'True if the incident is a PSM‑related event (e.g., loss of containment).']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident must be reported to OSHA or other regulators.']
  report_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident was first reported into the system.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category describing the primary cause of the incident. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: equipment|human_error|procedure|material|environment|management|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the root cause analysis findings.']
  safety_incident_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the incident record.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity rating of the incident based on impact and regulatory criteria.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the incident record.']

  Note: 'Master record for all workplace safety incidents, near-misses, first-aid events, and occupational chemical exposure monitoring at Chemical Mfg facilities. Captures incident type (injury, illness, near-miss, property damage, process safety, chemical exposure), severity classification (OSHA recordable, lost-time, first-aid), incident date/time, location, affected personnel, initial description, root cause category, and regulatory reportability flags. For chemical exposure events: captures substance (CAS number), exposure measurement type (personal air sampling, area monitoring, biological monitoring), measured concentration, OSHA PEL/ACGIH TLV threshold, exceedance flag, and required follow-up. Integrates with Intelex EHS Management. Supports OSHA 300/300A recordkeeping, PSM incident reporting, and OSHA 1910.1000 air contaminants compliance.'
}

  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.facility. Business justification: Incident investigations are conducted at specific facilities. While incident_investigation links to safety_incident (which will now have facility_id), a direct facility_id on incident_investigation su']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical location where the incident occurred.']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.goods_receipt. Che']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order.   lot_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.lot_record.   material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Incident investigations involving raw material handling (spills, worker exposures, fires) must reference the material master to document the chemical involved, its hazard class, and storage conditions']

  safety_incident_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the safety incident or near‑miss that triggered this investigation.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: CORRECTIVE ACTION: Investigations often create a work order to implement corrective actions; linking enables traceability.']
  closure_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for closing the investigation (e.g., resolved, withdrawn).']

  investigation_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the investigation for tracking and reporting.']

  investigation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the investigation based on the nature of the event.']
  investigator_team_ids varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of employee IDs participating in the investigation.']
  is_closed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investigation has been formally closed.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent review of the investigation findings.']
  methodology varchar [note: 'Methodology used to conduct the root‑cause analysis.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the investigation based on risk and impact.']
  regulatory_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory submission was made, if applicable.']
  regulatory_submission_status varchar [note: 'Status of any required regulatory filing related to the investigation.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk rating derived from severity, likelihood, and exposure.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Fundamental underlying cause(s) identified through analysis.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the incident impact.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the investigation was formally started.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the investigation record.']

  Note: 'Detailed root cause investigation record linked to a safety incident or near-miss. Captures investigation team members, RCI (Root Cause Investigation) methodology used (5-Why, Fault Tree, HAZOP-based), contributing factors, direct and root causes, corrective action recommendations, investigation completion date, and regulatory submission status. Supports PSM incident investigation requirements under OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 and ISO 45001 audit trails.'
}

Linking capa_record to ']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: CAPA EXECUTION: Each CAPA may be executed via one or more work orders; linking provides execution tracking.']

  due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the CAPA must be completed.']

  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify that the CAPA was effective.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who initially created the CAPA record.']

  Note: 'Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record tracking remediation actions arising from safety incidents, near-misses, audit findings, inspection citations, or regulatory observations at Chemical Mfg facilities. Captures action description, action type (corrective vs. preventive), responsible owner, priority, due date, completion date, verification method, effectiveness review outcome, and linkage to originating incident, inspection finding, or MOC. Supports ISO 45001 continual improvement (Clause 10.2), ISO 14001 nonconformity management, OSHA PSM follow-up, and Responsible Care management system requirements.'
}

Table ehs.hazop_study {
  hazop_study_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the HAZOP study record.']
  cas_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.cas_registry. Business justification: HAZOP studies reference the CAS registry to ensure the correct chemical hazard profile is evaluated during process safety analysis.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: HAZOP studies are conducted per chemical process; linking to the product enables traceability of risk assessments.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.facility. Business justification: HAZOP studies are conducted for specific facility process units. hazop_study has plant_location (STRING) as a denormalized text reference to the facility location. Adding facility_id FK normalizes thi']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.functional_location. Business justification: HAZOP SCOPE: HAZOP studies are scoped to a functional location; linking enables location‑based risk management.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Funds Hazop studies through an internal order, linking safety analysis to the capital project financial tracking.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: HAZOP studies on process units handling raw materials require the material master for storage temperature limits, incompatibility flags, and regulatory compliance status to assess process deviations. ']
  material_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_specification. Business justification: HAZOP studies reference material specifications to define safe operating envelopes — assay limits, pH ranges, and viscosity bounds define the \'design intent\' that HAZOP deviations are measured against']
  process_safety_info_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.process_safety_info. Business justification: HAZOP studies are conducted using Process Safety Information (PSI) as the foundational input. Under OSHA PSM (29 CFR 1910.119), PHAs/HAZOPs must be based on current PSI. process_safety_info is the PSI']
  tank_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.tank. Business justification: PSM/RMP regulations (29 CFR 1910.119, 40 CFR Part 68) require HAZOP studies for processes involving tanks storing highly hazardous chemicals above threshold quantities. Direct link enables compliance ']
  action_due_date date [note: 'Target completion date for the recommended actions.']
  action_owner varchar [note: 'Individual or department responsible for implementing the action.']
  action_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the recommended action.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the study was formally approved.']
  control_measures varchar [note: 'Engineering or administrative controls in place to mitigate identified hazards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the study record was first created.']
  documentation_link varchar [note: 'URL or path to the full HAZOP report or supporting documents.']
  exposure_limit_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Permissible exposure limit for the chemical expressed in parts per million.']
  hazard_classifications varchar [note: 'GHS hazard classes and pictograms applicable to the chemical.']
  hazop_study_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the study.']
  inventory_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the maximum intended inventory.']
  last_reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who performed the most recent review of the study.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent review.']
  max_intended_inventory decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum quantity of the chemical intended to be stored in the unit (numeric value).']
  methodology varchar [note: 'Specific analytical approach applied (Guideword‑based HAZOP, What‑If, Checklist, LOPA).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or observations captured during the study.']
  overall_risk_rank varchar [note: 'Aggregated risk ranking for the study based on likelihood and severity.']
  physical_state varchar [note: 'Physical state of the chemical at operating conditions.']
  process_unit_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the process unit (e.g., reactor, distillation column) examined in the study.']
  process_unit_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the process unit covered by the study.']
  recommended_actions varchar [note: 'Summary of corrective or preventive actions proposed by the study.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory program(s) governing the study (e.g., OSHA PSM, EPA RMP).']
  revalidation_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent revalidation or periodic review of the study.']
  risk_likelihood varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (1‑5) of the likelihood of identified hazards.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated risk score (likelihood × severity) for each node or overall.']
  risk_severity varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (1‑5) of the severity of potential consequences.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the functional and physical scope of the HAZOP study.']
  study_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or reference code assigned to the study (e.g., HZP‑2024‑001).']
  study_end_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the HAZOP study activities were completed.']
  study_start_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the HAZOP study activities commenced.']
  study_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the HAZOP study.']
  study_type varchar [note: 'Methodology used for the study (HAZOP, What‑If, LOPA, Checklist).']
  team_members varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of participants (engineers, operators, safety specialists).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the study record.']

  Note: 'Process safety master record encompassing Process Safety Information (PSI), Hazard and Operability (HAZOP) studies, Process Hazard Analyses (PHA), and granular study nodes for chemical process units at Chemical Mfg plants. PSI content: chemical identity (CAS number), maximum intended inventory, physical/chemical properties, reactivity hazards, corrosivity data, thermal stability, P&ID references, equipment design specifications, safe operating limits. Study content: study scope, process unit reference, team composition, methodology (HAZOP, What-If, Checklist, LOPA), study/revalidation dates, overall risk ranking. Node-level detail: node identifier, design intent, guide words (No, More, Less, Reverse, Other Than), deviations, causes, consequences, safeguards, risk rankings (likelihood × severity), recommended actions. Required under OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119(d) and (e) and EPA RMP 40 CFR Part 68. Integrates with Intelex EHS Management.'
}

Table ehs.emission_event {
  emission_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each emission event record.']
  cas_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.cas_registry. Business justification: Emission reporting links each pollutant to its CAS registry entry for regulatory reporting and inventory reconciliation.']

  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Assigns emission monitoring and mitigation costs to the cost center responsible for the emission source.']
  emission_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.emission_source. Business justification: Each emission event originates from a specific emission source. Adding emission_source_id to emission_event provides a clear parent relationship.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.equipment. Business justification: EMISSIONS TRACKING: Emission events are typically generated by a specific piece of equipment; linking enables source‑based reporting.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.facility. Business justification: Emission events occur at specific facilities. emission_event has facility_code (BIGINT) as a denormalized reference. Adding facility_id FK to the facility master record normalizes this relationship an']
  inbound_delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_delivery. Business justification: Fugitive emission events during hazmat unloading (below injury threshold but EPA/state reportable) must reference the inbound delivery for source identification and regulatory reporting. This is disti']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Significant emission events in chemical manufacturing trigger funded cleanup and remediation projects tracked as internal orders. Environmental incident response costs (containment, cleanup, regulator']
  logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Transport Emission Tracking associates emission events with the corresponding shipment to calculate greenhouse‑gas inventory for regulatory compliance.']
  lot_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.lot_record. Business justification: Emission events from specific raw material lots (e.g., spill during unloading of a specific batch) require lot-level traceability for insurance claims, regulatory reporting, and supplier liability det']
  manufacturing_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.manufacturing_order. Business justification: Regulatory emission reports are generated per production order; linking emissions to the order enables accurate tracking and compliance.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Emission events involving raw material spills or fugitive releases during unloading must reference the material master for TRI/CERCLA regulatory reporting, emergency response planning, and root cause ']
  operating_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.operating_permit. Business justification: Emission events occur under specific operating permits and must be evaluated against permit limits. emission_event has a regulatory_permit_number (STRING) that is a denormalized reference to the opera']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order. Business justification: Emission reporting per production batch is allocated to the originating sales order to satisfy environmental permits and internal cost accounting.']
  safety_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.safety_incident. Business justification: Chemical releases and emission events in manufacturing are frequently co-reported as both environmental emission events and safety incidents (e.g., a chemical spill triggers both an emission_event rec']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.site. Business justification: Regulatory emission reports must be tied to the specific customer site where the release occurred (EPA reporting).']
  tank_farm_level_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.tank_farm_level. Business justification: EPA RMP and TRI reporting require correlating emission events (tank venting, fugitive releases) with tank inventory levels at the time of the event. Linking emission_event to tank_farm_level enables m']
  cause_of_release varchar [note: 'Primary cause that triggered the emission event.']
  cleanup_status varchar [note: 'Status of any remediation or cleanup activities.']
  containment_success_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether containment measures successfully prevented further release.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the emission event record was first created in the system.']
  data_quality_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the emission data passed quality checks.']
  data_quality_notes varchar [note: 'Comments on data quality issues or anomalies.']
  emergency_response_action varchar [note: 'Description of emergency response actions taken after the event.']
  emission_category varchar [note: 'Classification of emission as point source, fugitive, or accidental.']
  emission_event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the emission event record.']
  emission_factor decimal(20,5) [note: 'Factor used to estimate emissions when direct measurement is unavailable.']
  emission_factor_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the emission factor (e.g., kg/ton).']
  emission_quantity decimal(20,5) [note: 'Measured amount of pollutant released during the event.']
  emission_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the emission quantity.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the emission event was observed or recorded.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of the emission event such as routine monitoring, accidental release, or spill.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'State or region code where the emission source is located.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the emission source.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the emission source.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to determine the emission quantity.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the emission source is monitored.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or observations.']
  release_media varchar [note: 'Environmental medium into which the pollutant was released.']
  report_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the regulatory report.']
  report_submission_date date [note: 'Date the emission report was submitted to the regulator.']
  reportable_flag boolean [note: 'True if the emission event meets regulatory reporting thresholds.']
  reporting_requirement varchar [note: 'Regulatory reporting framework applicable (e.g., EPA 40 CFR 302, TRI).']
  source_permit_limit decimal(20,5) [note: 'Regulatory emission limit for the source.']
  source_permit_limit_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the permit limit (e.g., kg/year).']
  source_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the emission source (e.g., stack, vent, tank) that generated the event.']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical source of the emission.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the emission event record.']

  Note: 'Comprehensive environmental emissions, releases, and source inventory record for Chemical Mfg facilities. Source inventory: all permitted and tracked emission sources (stacks, vents, fugitive equipment components, storage tanks, wastewater treatment units), source type, associated process unit, geographic coordinates, permit reference, applicable emission limits, control device linkage, monitoring frequency. Routine emissions: point-source and fugitive release quantities of VOCs, HAPs, GHGs, pollutant CAS numbers, emission factors, measurement methods (CEMS, stack test, engineering estimate). Accidental releases/spills: release date/time, chemical identity, quantity released, release media (air, soil, water, drain), cause, containment effectiveness, emergency response actions, regulatory reportability (CERCLA RQ, EPCRA 304, state spill reporting), agency notifications, cleanup status. Feeds TRI annual reporting, EPA air permit compliance, NPDES reporting, and spill notification workflows.'
}

Table ehs.emission_source {
  emission_source_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the emission source record.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Permits for emission sources are tied to the specific chemical emitted, supporting permit management and reporting.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'FK to ehs.facility']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Emission sources (storage tanks, process vents, loading stations) are associated with specific raw materials being stored or processed. Linking to material_master enables permit limit tracking by mate']
  operating_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.operating_permit. Business justification: An emission source is governed by a single operating permit; many sources can share a permit. Adding operating_permit_id to emission_source creates the required inbound link.']
  process_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production unit or line associated with the emission source.']
  tank_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.tank. Business justification: Tanks are primary emission sources in chemical manufacturing (fugitive emissions, loading, venting). EPA Title V and air permit compliance requires linking each emission source record to the specific ']
  capacity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the source capacity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the emission source record was first created in the system.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicator of the confidence level in the reported measurement data.']
  emission_factor decimal(12,6) [note: 'Factor used to calculate emissions from process activity (e.g., kg pollutant per ton product).']
  emission_factor_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the emission factor.']
  emission_limit_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the emission limit (e.g., kilograms per hour).']
  emission_limit_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum allowable emission quantity for the source per reporting period.']
  emission_medium varchar [note: 'Environmental medium to which emissions are released.']
  emission_source_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the emission source, its function, and any special characteristics.']
  is_area_source boolean [note: 'True if emissions are dispersed over an area rather than a single point.']
  is_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the source has an attached emissions control device.']
  is_fugitive boolean [note: 'True if the source represents fugitive emissions (e.g., leaks, vents).']
  is_point_source boolean [note: 'True if emissions originate from a discrete point (e.g., stack).']
  is_reportable boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the source is subject to regulatory reporting (e.g., EPA, TRI).']
  last_monitoring_date date [note: 'Date the most recent monitoring measurement was recorded.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the emission source location in decimal degrees.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle stage of the source within the enterprise asset management process.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the source location (e.g., building, floor, area).']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the emission source location in decimal degrees.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to obtain emission measurements.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency for emission monitoring of the source.']
  next_monitoring_due date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required monitoring activity.']
  reporting_category varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) under which the source must be reported.']
  source_capacity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum designed throughput or flow rate of the emission source.']
  source_code varchar [note: 'Business‑assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the emission source within the enterprise.']
  source_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping or classification of related emission sources (e.g., boiler cluster).']
  source_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the emission source as used in reports and dashboards.']
  source_operating_hours_per_year varchar [note: 'Total number of hours the source operates in a calendar year.']
  source_owner varchar [note: 'Organizational unit or department responsible for the emission source.']
  source_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the emission source.']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the source (e.g., stack, vent, fugitive equipment, storage tank, wastewater unit, process unit).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the emission source record.']

  Note: 'Master record for all permitted and tracked emission sources at Chemical Mfg facilities, including stacks, vents, fugitive equipment components, storage tanks, and wastewater treatment units. Captures source ID, source type, associated process unit, geographic coordinates, permit reference, applicable emission limits, control device linkage, and monitoring frequency requirements. Serves as the SSOT for emission source inventory used in air permit compliance and TRI reporting.'
}

  Note: 'Comprehensive waste management record for Chemical Mfg operations covering waste stream master definitions, waste characterization, and individual disposal transactions. Waste stream master data: stream name, physical/chemical description, generating process unit, EPA waste codes, RCRA hazardous waste determination, typical generation rate, approved disposal methods, storage requirements, regulatory notification status. Disposal transaction data: waste characterization, quantity generated, disposal method (landfill, incineration, recycling, treatment), licensed disposal facility, manifest number, shipment date, chain-of-custody documentation, and LDR (Land Disposal Restrictions) compliance. Supports EPA RCRA hazardous waste reporting, biennial reporting, state environmental agency submissions, and ISO 14001 waste management tracking.'
}

  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Regulatory inspection findings trigger funded remediation projects in chemical manufacturing. EPA and OSHA audit-driven corrective actions require internal orders to track capital and expense spend. C']
  operating_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.operating_permit.   tank_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.tank. Business justification: Tanks in chemical manufacturing are subject to PSM mechanical integrity inspections, API 653 assessments, and environmental compliance audits. Linking inspection_audit to the specific tank asset enabl']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_location. Business justification: OSHA and EPA hazmat storage inspections are conducted at specific warehouse locations. Linking inspection_audit to warehouse_location enables inspection history tracking per storage area, drives corre']
  affected_area varchar [note: 'Physical area, process unit, or system within the facility impacted by the finding.']
  closure_status varchar [note: 'Overall status of the inspection\'s corrective action closure process.']
  corrective_actions_required varchar [note: 'Count of distinct corrective actions that must be implemented to close findings.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspection record was first entered into the system.']
  critical_findings varchar [note: 'Number of findings classified as critical severity, requiring immediate corrective action.']
  due_date date [note: 'Latest date by which all corrective actions for the inspection must be completed.']
  high_findings varchar [note: 'Number of findings classified as high severity.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned identifier for the inspection, often used in reports and regulatory filings.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the inspection record.']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection was actually performed.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Category of the inspection, indicating the governing standard or agency (e.g., OSHA compliance audit, EPA facility inspection).']
  low_findings varchar [note: 'Number of findings classified as low severity.']
  medium_findings varchar [note: 'Number of findings classified as medium severity.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text comments captured by the inspector, including observations not captured in structured fields.']
  regulatory_standard_referenced varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory clause, standard, or guideline cited for each finding (e.g., OSHA 1910.119, EPA 40 CFR Part 61).']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the inspection scope, including processes, areas, and systems examined.']
  total_findings varchar [note: 'Total number of findings (citations, observations, non‑conformances) recorded during the inspection.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the inspection record.']

  Note: 'EHS inspection, audit, and findings record for internal safety audits, regulatory agency inspections, and third-party compliance assessments at Chemical Mfg facilities. Captures inspection type (OSHA compliance audit, EPA facility inspection, ISO 14001/45001 internal audit, Responsible Care verification), inspection date, inspector/auditor identity, scope, and individual findings (citations, observations, nonconformances, severity, regulatory standard referenced, affected area, required corrective action, due date, closure status). Supports ISO 14001 Clause 9.2 internal audit, OSHA PSM compliance audit requirements, and drives CAPA generation from findings.'
}

Table ehs.process_safety_info {
  process_safety_info_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Process Safety Information record.']
  cas_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.cas_registry. Business justification: PSI documents are substance-specific and identified by CAS number per OSHA PSM standard (29 CFR 1910.119). Linking to cas_registry enables substance-level PSI lookups and eliminates denormalized physi']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: PSI documents are product‑specific, linking ensures consistent safety data across processes.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: PSM-covered chemicals under OSHA 1910.119 are stored in specific fixed assets (pressure vessels, reactors, storage tanks). Linking PSI to fixed assets enables asset-level PSM compliance tracking, supp']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: PSM/RMP compliance requires PSI documentation to reference the specific raw material master for storage conditions, incompatibility flags, and regulatory status. Chemical engineers building PSI packag']
  compatible_materials varchar [note: 'Materials that are safe to store or transport the chemical with.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the PSI record was initially created.']
  density_kg_per_m3 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Mass per unit volume of the chemical at standard temperature and pressure.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier for the linked safety data sheet or regulatory document.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person to contact in case of an incident involving the chemical.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the emergency contact.']
  emergency_procedure varchar [note: 'Brief description of the emergency response steps for this chemical.']
  environmental_hazards varchar [note: 'Key environmental risk categories for the chemical.']
  hazard_classification varchar [note: 'Primary GHS hazard class for the chemical.']
  health_hazards varchar [note: 'Primary health hazard categories associated with the chemical.']
  incompatible_materials varchar [note: 'Materials that must not contact the chemical due to reactive hazards.']
  inventory_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the maximum inventory amount.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the PSI record was last reviewed for accuracy.']
  max_inventory_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum quantity of the chemical intended to be stored on site.']
  next_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time for the next periodic review.']
  ph decimal(4,2) [note: 'Acidity/alkalinity of the chemical in aqueous solution.']
  process_safety_info_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the PSI record.']
  psm_covered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the chemical is covered under OSHA Process Safety Management.']
  reactivity_hazards varchar [note: 'Reactivity hazard classifications indicating dangerous reactions.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status with applicable regulations (OSHA, EPA, REACH, etc.).']
  rmp_covered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the chemical is covered under EPA Risk Management Plan requirements.']
  safety_data_sheet_url varchar [note: 'Web address where the current SDS for the chemical is stored.']
  solubility_water varchar [note: 'Qualitative description of solubility in water (e.g., miscible, limited, insoluble).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system (e.g., SAP PM, Intelex EHS).']
  storage_pressure_bar decimal(8,2) [note: 'Pressure range recommended for safe storage.']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(8,2) [note: 'Temperature range recommended for safe storage.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the PSI record.']

  Note: 'Process Safety Information (PSI) master record for highly hazardous chemicals and covered processes at Chemical Mfg facilities under OSHA PSM. Captures chemical identity (CAS number), maximum intended inventory, physical/chemical properties, reactivity hazards, corrosivity data, thermal stability data, P&ID reference, equipment design specifications, and safe operating limits. Required documentation under OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119(d) and EPA RMP 40 CFR Part 68.'
}

Business justification: EHS-managed SDSs for raw materials must be linked to the material master for incident response, incoming inspection, and GHS labeling compliance. EHS teams need this link to retrieve the correct SDS d']
  process_safety_info_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.process_safety_info. Business justification: Safety Data Sheet records describe the same chemical identified in Process Safety Info. Linking SDS to PSI via process_safety_info_id removes duplicate chemical identifiers.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: SDSs in chemical manufacturing are authored and provided by the raw material vendor/supplier. Linking SDS to the vendor enables procurement to verify current SDS versions during vendor qualification, ']
  accidental_release_measures varchar [note: 'Steps to contain and clean up accidental releases.']

  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the SDS version became effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the SDS is no longer valid (if applicable).']
  exposure_controls varchar [note: 'Engineering controls and personal protective equipment (PPE) requirements.']
  firefighting_measures varchar [note: 'Procedures and extinguishing media for fire involving the substance.']
  first_aid_measures varchar [note: 'Recommended first‑aid actions in case of exposure.']

  personal_protective_equipment varchar [note: 'Specific PPE recommended for handling the substance.']
  ph decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acidity/alkalinity of the substance in aqueous solution.']

  pictograms varchar [note: 'List of GHS pictogram codes applicable to the substance.']
  precautionary_statements varchar [note: 'Standard GHS precautionary statements for handling the substance.']
  reach_registration_number varchar [note: 'ECHA registration identifier for the substance.']
  regulatory_information varchar [note: 'Key regulatory status notes (e.g., REACH, TSCA, OSHA).']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent revision to the SDS content.']
  sds_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the SDS record.']
  sds_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the SDS document, e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.1\'.']
  signal_word varchar [note: 'GHS signal word indicating severity of hazards.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the SDS data.']
  toxicological_information varchar [note: 'Summary of acute, chronic, and specific toxicity data.']
  tsca_sara_313_status varchar [note: 'U.S. TSCA SARA 313 reporting requirement status.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the SDS record.']
  vapor_pressure_kpa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Equilibrium vapor pressure at 20 °C expressed in kilopascals.']

  Note: 'Safety Data Sheet (SDS/MSDS) master record for all chemical substances handled, produced, or distributed by Chemical Mfg. Captures SDS version, GHS classification (hazard class, category, signal word, pictograms), CAS number, chemical composition, first-aid measures, firefighting measures, exposure controls, PPE requirements, physical/chemical properties, stability/reactivity data, toxicological information, and regulatory status (REACH, TSCA, SARA 313). Integrates with product and raw material domains via CAS number.'
}

Table ehs.emergency_response_plan {
  emergency_response_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the emergency response plan record.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: ERPs are drafted for specific chemicals; linking supports drill planning and regulatory compliance.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.facility. Business justification: Emergency response plans are developed for specific facilities. emergency_response_plan has facility_code (BIGINT) as a denormalized reference. Adding facility_id FK normalizes this to the EHS facilit']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Geographic location identifier (e.g., site, campus) for the plan.']
  hazop_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.hazop_study. Business justification: Emergency response plans are developed based on hazard analyses identified in HAZOP studies. The covered_hazards field on emergency_response_plan directly corresponds to hazards identified in HAZOP st']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Emergency response plan development, annual drills, and equipment maintenance are funded via internal orders in chemical manufacturing. OSHA PSM and EPA RMP require documented drill programs with asso']
  operating_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.operating_permit. Business justification: Emergency response plans are tied to the operating permit that authorizes the facility\'s emissions. Adding operating_permit_id to emergency_response_plan links the plan to its governing permit.']
  process_safety_info_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.process_safety_info. Business justification: Emergency response plans for chemical facilities must reference the Process Safety Information for the covered hazardous chemicals. The covered_hazards field on emergency_response_plan corresponds to ']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.site. Business justification: Emergency response plans are site‑specific; linking supports drill scheduling, compliance checks, and incident coordination.']
  community_notification_thresholds varchar [note: 'Quantitative or situational thresholds that trigger external community alerts.']
  covered_hazards varchar [note: 'Narrative list of specific chemicals, processes, or scenarios addressed by the plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the plan record was first created in the system.']
  drill_completion_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent drill.']
  drill_observations varchar [note: 'Narrative observations, performance notes, and corrective actions identified during the drill.']
  drill_schedule varchar [note: 'Planned frequency and timing of emergency response drills (e.g., quarterly, annual).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the plan becomes effective and binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the plan expires or is superseded; null for open‑ended plans.']
  emergency_coordinator_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the emergency coordinator.']
  emergency_coordinator_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the emergency coordinator.']
  evacuation_routes_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of evacuation routes and assembly points.']
  last_drill_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent emergency response drill.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the plan was last formally reviewed and approved.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the plan in its lifecycle.']
  plan_category varchar [note: 'Broad hazard category covered (e.g., chemical release, fire, explosion, natural disaster, environmental emergency).']
  plan_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored electronic copy of the full emergency response plan.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the emergency response plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the plan as required by OSHA, EPA, or EPCRA.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version label or number for the plan (e.g., v1.2, 2023‑Q2).']
  regulatory_submission_date date [note: 'Date the plan was submitted to the applicable regulatory agency.']
  regulatory_submission_status varchar [note: 'Current status of required regulatory filings for the plan.']
  shelter_in_place_procedures varchar [note: 'Procedures to be followed when shelter‑in‑place is required.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the plan record.']

  Note: 'Emergency response plan master record for Chemical Mfg facilities covering chemical release, fire, explosion, natural disaster, and environmental emergency scenarios. Captures plan type (OSHA EAP, EPA RMP Emergency Response Program, SPCC Plan, EPCRA LEPC notification), covered hazards, emergency coordinator contacts, evacuation routes, shelter-in-place procedures, community notification thresholds, drill schedule and completion history, performance observations from exercises, last review date, and regulatory submission status. Required under OSHA 29 CFR 1910.38, EPA RMP 40 CFR Part 68, and EPCRA Section 304. Now includes drill/exercise tracking previously in emergency_drill.'
}

  Note: 'Master reference table for facility. Referenced by facility_id.'
}

Table ehs.emission_reporting_line_item {
  emission_reporting_line_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each emission reporting line item record']
  emission_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the emission event being reported in this submission']
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory submission that includes this emission event']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the reported quantity for this line item (e.g., direct measurement, emission factor, mass balance, engineering estimate)']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes or explanations about this specific line item inclusion']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this emission reporting line item record was first created in the system']
  data_quality_code varchar [note: 'Quality indicator for the reported data on this line item (e.g., measured, calculated, estimated, default)']
  form_section_reference varchar [note: 'Specific section or line number on the regulatory form where this emission is reported (e.g., TRI Form R Section 5.1, Part II Line 3)']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'The specific regulatory requirement or citation that mandates reporting this emission in this submission (e.g., EPCRA Section 313, 40 CFR Part 98 Subpart C, Title V permit condition)']
  reported_quantity decimal(20,5) [note: 'The specific quantity of the emission reported in this submission, which may differ from the actual emission quantity due to regulatory calculation methods, rounding rules, or reporting period allocation']
  reporting_period_allocation varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate the emission event to the reporting period (e.g., full allocation, prorated, annualized) when the event spans multiple reporting periods or requires adjustment']
  submission_line_item_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this specific line item within the submission (draft, submitted, amended, corrected, withdrawn)']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this emission reporting line item record']

  Note: 'This association product represents the documented inclusion of a specific emission event in a specific regulatory submission. It captures the regulatory reporting relationship between emission events and formal agency filings. Each record links one regulatory submission to one emission event with attributes that exist only in the context of regulatory reporting (reported quantity, period allocation, regulatory basis, and line item status).. Existence Justification: In chemical manufacturing EHS compliance, regulatory submissions (TRI Form R, Title V, EPCRA reports) aggregate emission events across multiple reporting frameworks and time periods. A single emission event can legitimately appear in multiple submissions (e.g., the same VOC release reported in both TRI annual reporting and Title V semi-annual monitoring reports), and each submission covers many emission events. The business actively manages these reporting line items with submission-specific attributes like reported quantity (which may differ from actual due to regulatory calculation rules), regulatory basis, and form section references.'
}

Table maintenance.equipment {
  equipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each maintainable plant asset in the equipment master registry. Primary key for the equipment data product.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Asset ownership tracking; required for customer asset registers and billing reports linking equipment to the owning account.']
  customer_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.site. Business justification: Required for Service Contract Management: links each equipment to the owning customer\'s site for billing, compliance, and maintenance scheduling.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Chemical plant equipment (reactors, compressors, heat exchangers) are capitalized fixed assets. Linking equipment to its fixed_asset record enables depreciation tracking, net book value reporting, ass']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.functional_location. Business justification: Equipment must be assigned to a functional location for hierarchy and maintenance planning.']
  maintenance_plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or facility where this equipment is physically installed and operated.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Equipment spec sheets require exact material grade for construction; regulatory compliance (PSM, ASME) and corrosion tracking depend on this link.']
  process_safety_info_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.process_safety_info. Business justification: OSHA PSM standard mandates that process safety information (PSI) be accessible for covered equipment. Maintenance technicians performing work on PSM-covered equipment must reference the PSI document f']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: In chemical manufacturing, equipment is assigned to profit centers (e.g., Specialty Chemicals unit, Plastics division) for segment P&L reporting and maintenance cost allocation. Finance controllers re']
  sds_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.sds_record. Business justification: In chemical manufacturing PSM environments, equipment handling hazardous chemicals must be associated with the governing SDS for emergency response planning, MOC reviews, and hazardous area classifica']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: Needed for equipment procurement, warranty, recall, and regulatory compliance tracking of the supplier of each equipment.']
  vendor_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor_site. Business justification: Equipment traceability to originating vendor manufacturing site is required for warranty claims, spare parts sourcing, and PSM/regulatory audits in chemical manufacturing. vendor_id alone is insuffici']
  asset_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI-AA fixed asset number linked to this equipment for financial accounting, depreciation calculation, and CapEx tracking. Bridges the maintenance and finance domains.']
  asset_tag_number varchar [note: 'Physical asset tag or nameplate number affixed to the equipment in the field. Used for physical identification during inspections, maintenance activities, and fixed asset accounting reconciliation.']
  calibration_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this equipment (typically instrumentation) requires periodic calibration to maintain measurement accuracy and regulatory compliance. True=calibration program applies; False=not applicable.']
  capacity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the equipment capacity value (e.g., m3, m3/hr, kW, kg/hr, bar). Follows SI unit conventions for chemical process engineering.']
  capacity_value decimal(14,4) [note: 'Principal rated capacity or throughput of the equipment (e.g., volumetric capacity for vessels in m³, flow rate for pumps in m³/hr, heat duty for heat exchangers in kW). The unit is specified in capacity_unit.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date the equipment was formally commissioned and accepted into operational service following installation, pre-commissioning checks, and startup testing. Distinct from installation date.']
  construction_year varchar [note: 'Year in which the equipment was manufactured or constructed. Used for age-based risk assessment, remaining useful life estimation, and regulatory inspection scheduling.']
  corrosion_allowance_mm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Designed corrosion allowance in millimeters added to the minimum required wall thickness to account for expected corrosion over the equipment\'s design life. Used in fitness-for-service assessments and remaining life calculations.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'SAP CO cost center to which maintenance costs for this equipment are charged. Used for maintenance cost allocation, OpEx reporting, and budget variance analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this equipment master record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for record origination and supports data lineage tracking in the Silver layer.']
  criticality_class varchar [note: 'Equipment criticality ranking used to prioritize maintenance resources and inspection frequency. A=Safety-critical/production-critical (highest priority, PSM-covered); B=Important but has redundancy or workaround; C=Non-critical/low impact on safety or production.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date the equipment was permanently removed from service and decommissioned. Null for active equipment. Required for fixed asset retirement accounting and regulatory closure documentation.']
  design_pressure_kpa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable working pressure (MAWP) for which the equipment was designed, expressed in kilopascals (kPa). Critical nameplate data for pressure vessel inspection, relief valve sizing, and PSM mechanical integrity documentation.']
  design_temperature_c decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable design temperature for which the equipment was designed, expressed in degrees Celsius. Used for material of construction verification, thermal stress analysis, and PSM mechanical integrity records.']
  equipment_category varchar [note: 'SAP PM equipment category code classifying the asset type: M=Mechanical, E=Electrical, I=Instrumentation, P=Piping, V=Vessel/Reactor. Drives maintenance planning strategy and inspection requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: M|E|I|P|V|C|R — promote to reference product]']
  equipment_class varchar [note: 'Detailed equipment classification within the category (e.g., Centrifugal Pump, Reciprocating Compressor, Shell-and-Tube Heat Exchanger, Pressure Vessel, Control Valve, Thermocouple). Aligns with ISO 14224 equipment taxonomy for reliability data collection.']
  equipment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name or short description of the equipment asset (e.g., \'Reactor Feed Pump\', \'Distillation Column Overhead Condenser\'). Corresponds to SAP PM equipment description field.']
  equipment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric equipment number assigned in SAP PM (EQUNR). Serves as the primary business identifier used across maintenance work orders, inspection records, and P&ID cross-references.']
  equipment_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the equipment asset. Active=in normal service; Inactive=installed but not in service; Decommissioned=permanently removed from service; Standby=available as backup; Under_Maintenance=currently undergoing planned or corrective maintenance.']
  hazardous_area_classification varchar [note: 'Electrical area classification of the location where this equipment is installed, per NEC/IEC standards. Determines requirements for explosion-proof or intrinsically safe electrical equipment. Zone 0/1/2 per IEC 60079; Division 1/2 per NEC Article 500.']
  inspection_interval_months varchar [note: 'Required inspection frequency in months as determined by regulatory requirements, risk-based inspection (RBI) analysis, or manufacturer recommendations. Used to auto-calculate next_inspection_date.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the equipment was physically installed at its current functional location. Marks the start of the equipment\'s operational lifecycle at this site for maintenance history and warranty tracking.']
  insulation_type varchar [note: 'Type of thermal or acoustic insulation applied to the equipment (e.g., mineral wool, calcium silicate, cellular glass, none). Relevant for corrosion under insulation (CUI) inspection programs and energy efficiency assessments.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recently completed inspection for this equipment. Used to calculate inspection intervals, assess compliance with regulatory inspection frequency requirements, and support risk-based inspection (RBI) programs.']
  maintenance_planner_group varchar [note: 'SAP PM planner group code responsible for planning and scheduling maintenance activities for this equipment. Determines which maintenance team owns the equipment\'s work order planning.']
  maintenance_strategy varchar [note: 'Assigned maintenance strategy for this equipment asset: Time_Based=fixed interval preventive maintenance; Condition_Based=maintenance triggered by condition monitoring thresholds; Predictive=data-driven prediction of failure; Run_To_Failure=no planned maintenance; Reliability_Centered=RCM-derived strategy.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the original equipment manufacturer (OEM). Used for spare parts sourcing, warranty claims, and technical support escalation.']
  moc_status varchar [note: 'Current Management of Change (MOC) workflow status for any pending or recent modification to this equipment. Not_Required=no active MOC; Pending=MOC initiated and under review; Approved=MOC approved, change authorized; Closed=MOC completed and documented.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s model or part number for this equipment type. Used for spare parts procurement, technical documentation retrieval, and like-for-like replacement identification.']
  next_calibration_date date [note: 'Date by which the next calibration of this instrument or measurement device must be completed to maintain traceability and compliance. Null if calibration_required is False.']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the next mandatory regulatory or internal inspection due for this equipment. Drives inspection scheduling for PSM mechanical integrity, API 510/570/653 inspections, and insurance surveys.']
  operating_pressure_kpa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Normal operating pressure of the equipment under standard process conditions, expressed in kilopascals (kPa). Used for process monitoring, deviation detection, and SPC control limit setting.']
  operating_temperature_c decimal(8,2) [note: 'Normal operating temperature of the equipment under standard process conditions, expressed in degrees Celsius. Used for process monitoring, heat balance calculations, and corrosion rate estimation.']
  pi_tag_reference varchar [note: 'Aveva PI System historian tag name or tag pattern associated with this equipment for real-time process data retrieval (e.g., temperature, pressure, flow readings). Enables linkage between equipment master data and real-time operational data.']
  pid_tag varchar [note: 'The P&ID tag number identifying this equipment on process engineering drawings (e.g., P-101A, E-201, V-301). Used for cross-referencing with Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams, HAZOP studies, and PSM documentation.']
  psm_covered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this equipment is subject to OSHA Process Safety Management (PSM) mechanical integrity requirements under 29 CFR 1910.119. True=PSM-covered; requires documented inspection, testing, and quality assurance program.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned serial number for the equipment unit. Required for warranty tracking, OEM service records, and regulatory inspection documentation.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dry operating weight of the equipment in kilograms. Used for structural load calculations, rigging and lifting plans, and transportation logistics for equipment removal or replacement.']
