  technician_certification_number varchar [note: 'Certification or qualification number of the technician performing calibration, demonstrating competency for this calibration type.']
  tolerance_lower_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Minimum acceptable value for calibration pass criteria. Defines lower bound of acceptable measurement range.']
  tolerance_upper_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Maximum acceptable value for calibration pass criteria. Defines upper bound of acceptable measurement range.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Engineering unit for calibration values (e.g., PSI, bar, degC, degF, pH, mA, mV, GPM, kg/h). Applies to as-found, as-left, target, and tolerance values.']
  vendor_lab_accreditation varchar [note: 'Accreditation body and certificate number for external calibration laboratory (e.g., A2LA, ANAB, UKAS). Ensures vendor competency.']

  Note: 'Calibration record for instrumentation and measurement devices (transmitters, analyzers, gauges, safety valves) documenting as-found and as-left conditions, calibration procedure, tolerance limits, pass/fail status, calibration interval, and next due date. Supports PSM mechanical integrity requirements, ISO 9001 instrument calibration compliance, and LIMS integration for laboratory instrument calibration.'
}

Table maintenance.breakdown_event {
  breakdown_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the breakdown event record. Primary key for the breakdown event entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Breakdown events incur repair and downtime costs; assigning a cost center is required for loss analysis and financial impact reporting.']
  emission_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.emission_event. Business justification: Equipment breakdowns in chemical manufacturing frequently cause unplanned emission events (e.g., seal failure causing fugitive release). Linking breakdown_event to emission_event supports environmenta']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment asset that experienced the breakdown failure.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Breakdown events in chemical plants directly trigger fixed asset impairment assessments (IAS 36) and insurance claims. Finance requires breakdown events linked to fixed assets to evaluate asset write-']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the functional location where the breakdown occurred within the plant hierarchy.']
  maintenance_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.plant. Business justification: Breakdown event occurs at a plant; replace plant code with FK.']
  pm_notification_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the PM notification that reported this breakdown event. Links to the maintenance notification system.']
  production_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.production_plan. Business justification: Breakdown events cause production plan deviations requiring replanning. Planners need to link breakdown events to affected production plans for production impact assessment, regulatory reporting (PSM)']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.production_schedule. Business justification: Breakdown events directly disrupt specific production schedule slots. affected_production_unit is a denormalized plain-text reference to the production schedule context. Linking to production_schedule']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Breakdown events trigger emergency procurement (spare parts, external repair services) in chemical plants. Linking breakdown_event to the resulting emergency PO enables breakdown-to-spend analysis, em']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corrective work order created to address this breakdown event.']
  breakdown_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total elapsed time in hours from breakdown start to restoration. Used for Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) calculation and downtime analysis.']
  breakdown_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the equipment was restored to operational status following the breakdown event.']
  breakdown_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the breakdown event, typically auto-generated or manually assigned for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  breakdown_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the equipment breakdown or failure was first detected or occurred. Critical for MTBF and MTTR calculations.']
  breakdown_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the breakdown event indicating progression from detection through resolution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the breakdown event record was first created in the maintenance management system.']
  damage_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code classifying the type of damage observed on the equipment, such as crack, deformation, erosion, or contamination.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the breakdown event was first detected, such as operator observation, automated alarm, scheduled inspection, or predictive analytics.']
  ehs_incident_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the associated EHS incident report if the breakdown event had safety or environmental implications.']
  environmental_release_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the breakdown event resulted in an unplanned release of chemicals or hazardous materials to the environment.']
  estimated_repair_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost to repair or restore the equipment following the breakdown event, including labor, materials, and contractor costs.']
  failure_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the root cause of the breakdown, such as wear, corrosion, operator error, design defect, or inadequate maintenance.']
  failure_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the root cause analysis findings for the breakdown event. Supports RCI (Root Cause Investigation) documentation.']
  failure_mode varchar [note: 'Description of how the equipment failed, such as mechanical fracture, electrical short, seal leak, bearing seizure, or control system fault. Supports failure mode and effects analysis (FMEA).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the breakdown event record was last updated or modified.']
  long_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the breakdown event, including symptoms, observations, and contextual information.']
  malfunction_start_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this breakdown event marks the initial onset of a malfunction or is a continuation of a previous issue.']
  moc_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the MOC workflow initiated to implement corrective actions for this breakdown event.']
  moc_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether corrective actions for this breakdown require a formal Management of Change process due to process safety implications.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Priority classification for the breakdown event response, determining urgency of corrective action and resource allocation.']
  production_impact_severity varchar [note: 'Categorical assessment of the severity of production impact caused by the breakdown, ranging from critical plant shutdown to minimal disruption.']
  production_loss_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantified amount of production output lost due to the breakdown event, measured in the unit of measure specified.']
  production_loss_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the production loss quantity, such as kilograms, liters, tons, or batch count.']
  psm_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the breakdown event involved PSM-covered equipment requiring mechanical integrity documentation and regulatory reporting.']
  rci_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the formal root cause investigation conducted for this breakdown event.']
  rci_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the breakdown event severity or recurrence pattern requires a formal root cause investigation.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the breakdown event was formally reported into the maintenance management system.']
  response_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Elapsed time in hours from breakdown detection to initiation of corrective action. Key metric for maintenance responsiveness.']
  restoration_method varchar [note: 'Method used to restore equipment to operational status, such as on-site repair, component replacement, temporary fix, or operational workaround.']
  safety_incident_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the breakdown event resulted in or was associated with a safety incident requiring EHS reporting.']
  short_description varchar [note: 'Brief summary description of the breakdown event for quick identification and reporting purposes.']

  Note: 'Unplanned equipment breakdown or failure event record capturing the onset of failure, failure mode, failure cause, affected production unit, production loss quantity, safety implications, and time to restore. Tracks breakdown start and end timestamps, breakdown duration, malfunction start indicator, and linkage to corrective work order and PM notification. Supports MTBF/MTTR calculation, RCI investigations, and PSM incident reporting.'
}

Table maintenance.permit_to_work {
  permit_to_work_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the permit to work record. Primary key for the permit to work entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Permit issuance often includes fees and resource allocation; linking to cost center supports permit cost accounting and audit trails.']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific equipment or asset on which the permitted work will be performed.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the functional location where the permitted work will be performed. Identifies the specific plant area or process unit.']
  hazop_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ehs.hazop_study. Business justification: High-hazard maintenance work (confined space entry, hot work near flammables) in PSM facilities must reference the governing HAZOP study to ensure identified hazard controls are applied during the job']
  maintenance_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.plant. Business justification: Permit to work is issued for a specific plant; replace plant code with FK.']
  notification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.notification. Business justification: A Permit to Work (PTW) in chemical plant operations is typically issued in response to a maintenance notification that identified the need for hazardous work. permit_to_work already links to work_orde']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.production_schedule. Business justification: PTW issuance requires verification that the target equipment is not actively scheduled for production. In chemical manufacturing, PTW systems are integrated with production scheduling — a PTW cannot b']
  sds_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.sds_record. Business justification: OSHA PSM (29 CFR 1910.119) and COSHH require that Permits to Work for maintenance on chemical-handling equipment reference the current SDS of the hazardous chemical involved. PTW issuers must confirm ']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work center responsible for executing the permitted work.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work order that this permit authorizes. Links permit to the underlying maintenance activity.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation if the permit was cancelled before work completion. Includes reason code and detailed justification.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the permit was formally closed after work completion and area inspection confirmed safe conditions restored.']
  closure_confirmation varchar [note: 'Documentation confirming that all work is complete, all tools and materials removed, all isolations restored, area cleaned, and safe conditions verified before permit closure.']
  confined_space_entry_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the work involves entry into a permit-required confined space as defined by OSHA 1910.146. Triggers additional safety requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the permit record was first created in the system.']
  fire_watch_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a dedicated fire watch person must be present during hot work operations to monitor for ignition and respond to fire emergencies.']
  gas_test_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether atmospheric testing for oxygen, flammable gases, and toxic gases is required before and during the work.']
  gas_test_results varchar [note: 'Results of atmospheric testing including oxygen percentage, lower explosive limit (LEL) readings, and toxic gas concentrations. Recorded before work begins and at specified intervals.']
  hazard_identification varchar [note: 'Comprehensive identification of all hazards present in the work area including chemical, physical, biological, and process hazards. Critical for risk assessment and control measures.']
  isolation_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific isolation and lockout/tagout requirements for the work including electrical isolation, mechanical isolation, line blanking, and energy isolation procedures.']
  issued_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the permit was officially issued and approved by the issuing authority.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the permit record was last updated in the system.']
  lel_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured flammable gas concentration as percentage of lower explosive limit. Must be below threshold (typically <10% LEL) for hot work authorization.']
  moc_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the associated Management of Change document if MOC review was required for this work.']
  moc_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the work requires a Management of Change review because it involves modifications to process equipment, procedures, or chemicals.']
  oxygen_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured oxygen concentration in the work area atmosphere expressed as percentage. Must be within safe range (typically 19.5% to 23.5%) for entry.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique permit number assigned to this work authorization. Used for tracking and reference in safety documentation.']
  permit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the permit to work. Tracks the permit from initial request through closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|suspended|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  permit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the permit based on the nature of hazardous work being authorized. Determines specific safety protocols and approval requirements.']
  ppe_requirements varchar [note: 'Mandatory personal protective equipment required for the permitted work including respirators, protective clothing, gloves, eye protection, and fall protection.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Priority level of the permitted work indicating urgency and scheduling precedence.']
  psm_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the work is on PSM-covered equipment or processes that contain highly hazardous chemicals above threshold quantities.']
  rescue_plan varchar [note: 'Documented rescue and emergency response plan for confined space entry or other high-risk work. Includes rescue equipment, procedures, and emergency contacts.']
  shutdown_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the work requires a process unit shutdown or can be performed during normal operations.']
  special_precautions varchar [note: 'Additional safety precautions, restrictions, or conditions specific to this permit beyond standard requirements. May include weather restrictions, simultaneous operations restrictions, or special monitoring requirements.']
  toxic_gas_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured concentration of toxic gases in parts per million. Must be below permissible exposure limits for the specific chemicals present.']
  valid_from_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the permit becomes effective and work is authorized to begin. Marks the start of the permits validity period.']
  valid_until_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the permit expires and work must cease. Permits typically have limited duration (e.g., 8 hours, 12 hours, or one shift) and must be renewed if work continues.']
  work_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the maintenance or construction activity to be performed under this permit. Includes scope, methods, and expected outcomes.']

  Note: 'Permit to Work (PTW) record authorizing maintenance and construction activities in hazardous areas of a chemical plant. Captures permit type (hot work, confined space entry, electrical isolation, line breaking, excavation), issuing authority, work description, hazard identification, isolation requirements, PPE requirements, gas test results, validity period, and closure confirmation. Supports OSHA PSM, EHS compliance, and safe work practices.'
}

Table maintenance.task_list {
  task_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each maintenance task list template.']
  predecessor_task_list_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on task_list (predecessor_task_list_id)']

  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the task list revision was approved.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for cost estimates.']
  craft_skill_required varchar [note: 'Skill or qualification code required for personnel performing the operation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the task list record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the task list is no longer valid (null for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the task list is valid for use.']
  equipment_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of equipment for which the task list is applicable (e.g., reactor, pump).']
  estimated_labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary estimate of labor cost for the task list.']

  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent work order generation from this task list.']
  lockout_tagout_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether lockout/tagout procedures are mandatory for the operation.']
  material_requirements varchar [note: 'List of materials and quantities needed for the operation.']
  moc_reference_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associated MOC document, if applicable.']
  moc_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Management of Change (MOC) must be completed before execution.']
  operation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the work to be performed in this step.']
  operation_step_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the operation within the task list.']
  planned_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated labor time required for the operation.']
  planned_hours_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for planned hours.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the task list for planning purposes.']
  psm_critical_flag boolean [note: 'True if the task list is subject to PSM mechanical integrity requirements.']
  regulatory_requirement_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing applicable regulatory or compliance requirement (e.g., OSHA, EPA).']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date when the current revision was released.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number for change control.']
  safety_precautions varchar [note: 'Safety instructions, PPE, and lockout/tagout notes for the operation.']
  task_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of maintenance activity the task list supports.']
  task_list_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code (e.g., PLKO) used in SAP PM to reference the task list.']
  task_list_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the purpose and scope of the task list.']
  task_list_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the task list used by planners and technicians.']
  task_list_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the task list.']
  task_list_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the task list: equipment‑specific, functional‑location‑specific, or general.']
  tool_requirements varchar [note: 'List of tools or equipment required to complete the operation.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the task list has been used to generate work orders.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for change management.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center responsible for executing the operation.']

  Note: 'Reusable maintenance task list (job plan/procedure template) defining standardized sequences of operations, required materials, tools, safety precautions, and estimated labor for recurring maintenance activities. Serves as the master template from which work order operations are generated, ensuring consistent execution quality across crews and shifts. Captures task list type (equipment-specific, functional-location-specific, general), group/counter structure, operation steps with detailed descriptions, work center assignments, planned hours per operation, required craft qualifications, material/tool requirements with quantities, and safety/lockout-tagout instructions. Referenced by pm_plan for automated work order generation. Aligns with SAP PM Task List (IA01-IA03/PLKO) and supports PSM mechanical integrity procedure documentation, OSHA-required safe work procedures, craft standardization, and audit-ready maintenance procedure records.'
}

Table maintenance.calibration_procedure {

  calibration_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the certificate issued after successful calibration.']
  calibration_interval_days varchar [note: 'Standard interval, in days, between required calibrations for equipment using this procedure.']
  calibration_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or facility where the calibration is performed.']
  calibration_procedure_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the calibration procedure.']
  calibration_procedure_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the calibration procedure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the calibration procedure record was created.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to supporting documentation or SOP for the procedure.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the calibration procedure becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the calibration procedure expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  equipment_category varchar [note: 'Category of equipment to which the procedure applies.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the procedure is mandatory for the equipment class.']
  last_calibrated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent calibration performed using this procedure.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the primary calibration value.']
  method varchar [note: 'Technique used to perform the calibration.']
  next_due_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp for the next required calibration.']
  procedure_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code used to reference the calibration procedure.']
  procedure_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the calibration procedure.']
  procedure_type varchar [note: 'Category of measurement the procedure addresses.']
  regulatory_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework that mandates the calibration procedure.']
  required_by_regulation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the procedure is mandated by regulatory standards.']
  tolerance_unit varchar [note: 'Unit for the tolerance value.']
  tolerance_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable deviation from the target value.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the calibration procedure record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the procedure definition.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for calibration_procedure. Referenced by calibration_procedure_id.'
}

Table maintenance.maintenance_plant {
  maintenance_plant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for plant']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Each chemical manufacturing plant belongs to a legal entity for statutory financial reporting, tax compliance, environmental permit registration, and intercompany cost allocation. Maintenance plant ma']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the plant.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, building, etc.).']
  area_sq_m decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total built‑up area of the plant in square metres.']
  capacity_tons_per_year decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount of product the plant can produce in a year.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the plant is located.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost‑center identifier for budgeting and charge‑back.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the plant location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the plant record was first created in the system.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date the plant was or will be retired from service (nullable).']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the designated emergency contact for the plant.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the emergency contact.']
  environmental_permit_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary environmental compliance permit.']
  establishment_date date [note: 'Date the plant began operations.']
  is_certified_iso14001 boolean [note: 'Indicates ISO 14001 environmental management certification status.']
  is_certified_iso9001 boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plant holds ISO 9001 quality certification.']
  is_certified_ohsas18001 boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plant is certified to OHSAS 18001 occupational health and safety standard.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Most recent date a safety or regulatory inspection was performed.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the plant in decimal degrees.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the plant in decimal degrees.']
  maintenance_strategy varchar [note: 'Approach used for maintaining plant equipment.']
  manager_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the plant manager.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the plant manager.']
  manager_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number of the plant manager.']
  mtbf_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average operating time between equipment failures.']
  mttr_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average time required to repair equipment after a failure.']
  number_of_employees varchar [note: 'Total staff employed at the plant.']
  number_of_units varchar [note: 'Count of major equipment units (reactors, vessels, etc.) at the plant.']
  operating_company varchar [note: 'Legal entity that owns or operates the plant.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'External business code or short identifier assigned to the plant.']
  plant_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form textual description of the plant, its purpose, and notable characteristics.']
  plant_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the plant used in reports and UI.']
  plant_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the plant.']
  plant_type varchar [note: 'Category of the plant based on its primary function.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code for the plant address.']
  regulatory_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the plant\'s regulatory compliance.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Overall safety performance rating of the plant.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the plant location.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the plant record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for plant. Referenced by plant_id.'
}

Table finance.cost_center {
  cost_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each cost center record.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Cost centers in SAP CO are assigned to company codes (legal entities). This relationship is essential for legal entity-level cost reporting, statutory reporting, and SOX compliance. Chemical manufactu']
  parent_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent cost center in the hierarchy.']
  actual_spend_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total actual expenses recorded against the cost center for the fiscal year.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to allocate costs to the center.']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned expense amount allocated to the cost center for the budget year.']
  budget_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the budget amounts apply.']
  cost_center_category varchar [note: 'High‑level business category for cost allocation.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier used in accounting and reporting (e.g., "CC‑0012").']
  cost_center_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context.']
  cost_center_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level indicating position in multi‑level hierarchy.']
  cost_center_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the cost center (e.g., "North Plant Operations").']
  cost_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost center.']
  cost_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost center by organizational role.']
  cost_driver varchar [note: 'Primary driver used for allocating costs (e.g., labor hours, machine hours).']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the cost center operates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cost center record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code used for budgeting and reporting (e.g., "USD").']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost center in external ERP or legacy systems.']
  group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of cost centers for consolidated reporting.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Depth of the cost center within the organizational hierarchy (1 = top level).']
  is_capex boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost center is used for capital expenditures.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the cost center was last reviewed for relevance and accuracy.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Name of the department that owns the cost center.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region identifier (e.g., "North America").']
  reporting_code varchar [note: 'Code used for statutory reporting and SOX audit trails.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the cost center is reviewed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the cost center record.']
  variance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Difference between budgeted and actual spend (budget – actual).']
  variance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage variance calculated as (variance_amount / budget_amount) * 100.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date when the cost center becomes effective for accounting.']
  valid_to date [note: 'Date when the cost center is retired or no longer valid (nullable).']

  Note: 'Master record for organizational cost centers used in management accounting and cost controlling. Represents a plant, department, or functional unit to which operating costs are allocated. Tracks hierarchy position, responsible manager, profit center assignment, currency, validity period, and budget ownership. Serves as the primary cost collection point for OpEx tracking, overhead allocation, and variance-to-budget reporting across chemical manufacturing facilities including production plants, R&D labs, and shared services.'
}

This relationship enables legal entity-level P&L reporting and supports EBITDA analysis per legal ent']

  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the profit center is retired or becomes inactive (null if open‑ended).']

  segment varchar [note: 'High‑level market segment to which the profit center belongs.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the profit center record.']

  Note: 'Master record for profit centers representing business segments, product lines, or plant units for which profitability is measured independently. Tracks profit center hierarchy, controlling area, responsible manager, currency, segment classification, and validity dates. Supports EBITDA reporting, segment profitability analysis under FASB ASC 280, and statutory reporting for chemical product lines including specialty chemicals, performance materials, and agricultural solutions.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry {
  journal_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the journal entry record.']
  business_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the business unit or division responsible for the entry.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Cost center to which the cost of the transaction is charged.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Journal entries belong to a fiscal period; linking to the fiscal_period master enables period-based reporting and removes the redundant string field.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity (company code) that owns the transaction.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Profit center that receives the revenue or expense impact.']
  reversed_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original journal entry that is being reversed.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who created the journal entry.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the journal entry record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the transaction amount.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'External document number assigned to the journal entry for reference and audit.']
  document_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the journal entry.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Category of the journal entry indicating its business purpose (e.g., SA=Sales, AB=Asset, AR=Accounts Receivable).']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert the transaction currency to the corporate group currency.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year to which the posting belongs.']
  gross_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value before tax, discounts, or adjustments.']
  intercompany_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the entry represents an intercompany transaction.']
  journal_entry_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing context for the posting.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle stage of the journal entry for compliance tracking.']
  local_currency_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount expressed in the legal entity\'s local currency.']
  net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount after tax and other adjustments.']
  party_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external party (customer, vendor, etc.) associated with the entry.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the journal entry is posted to the general ledger.']
  posting_key varchar [note: 'Key that determines the posting type (debit/credit) and account type.']
  posting_period varchar [note: 'Period code (e.g., "0010") indicating the posting window.']
  posting_reference varchar [note: 'Reference text used by the posting program for traceability.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number from an external system (e.g., purchase order, invoice).']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'True if this entry reverses a prior posting.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the journal entry.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code determining tax rate and jurisdiction.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the business event that generated the journal entry occurred.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who last modified the journal entry.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the journal entry.']

  Note: 'Core general ledger posting document capturing all financial transactions across the chemical manufacturing enterprise. Includes document type, posting date, fiscal period, legal entity, reference number, currency, exchange rate, reversal indicator, and audit trail fields. Represents the authoritative record for all accounting events including accruals, reversals, intercompany eliminations, period-end adjustments, tax postings, and reclassifications. Subject to SOX controls, FASB retention requirements, and statutory audit trail obligations.'
}

Table finance.cost_element {
  cost_element_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the cost element master record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost element belongs to a cost center; replace code with FK for proper hierarchy.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Cost element may be posted to a GL account; add FK for accurate accounting linkage.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate the cost element to cost objects (e.g., direct, absorption, activity‑based).']
  cost_element_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost element indicating its accounting purpose (e.g., primary cost, secondary overhead, revenue).']
  cost_element_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the cost element in financial transactions and reporting.']
  cost_element_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the cost element, including business rules and usage notes.']
  cost_element_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the cost element.']
  cost_element_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost element.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cost element record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the default rate.']
  default_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Default monetary rate applied per unit of measure when the cost element is used.']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost element is subject to tax.']
  overhead_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Default overhead rate applied when the cost element is used for overhead absorption.']
  quantity_based_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost element is calculated on a per‑quantity basis.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the cost element must be tracked for regulatory reporting (e.g., REACH, EPA).']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate (percentage) when the cost element is taxable.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure associated with the cost element (e.g., kg, hour, liter).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the cost element record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the cost element record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the cost element definition for change management and audit.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the cost element record.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date from which the cost element becomes valid for posting.']
  valid_to date [note: 'Date after which the cost element is no longer valid (null if open‑ended).']

  Note: 'Cost element master defining the nature of costs and revenues tracked in management accounting. Classifies costs as primary (directly linked to GL accounts for external reporting) or secondary (internal allocations, overhead absorption, activity-based costing). Includes cost element category, unit of measure, quantity-based costing flag, and validity period. Critical for standard costing of chemical formulations, overhead rate application, and COGS analysis across batch and continuous manufacturing processes.'
}

  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product.   material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master.   production_plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the cost applies.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: In chemical mfg, standard costs are set at the SKU level (pack size and grade combination) because filling, packaging, and labeling costs differ per SKU. Required for accurate COGS calculation and inv']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the cost record.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the cost record.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cost record received approval.']
  cost_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard cost per unit of the product, expressed in the specified currency.']
  cost_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the cost record (e.g., "Standard Cost for Polyethylene 2024 Q1").']
  cost_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost record indicating its purpose (standard, planned, budget, or actual).']
  cost_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the cost amount (e.g., kilogram, liter, unit).']
  cost_version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for revisions of the standard cost.']
  costing_lot_size decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity of product units for which the standard cost is calculated.']
  costing_method varchar [note: 'Method used to derive the standard cost (e.g., FIFO, LIFO, weighted average, standard).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cost record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the cost amount.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Internal department responsible for the cost record.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the standard cost becomes valid for valuation.']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date after which the standard cost is no longer valid (nullable for open‑ended).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the standard cost expires (mirrors effective_to).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the cost record.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when the standard cost was officially released for use in valuation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the cost data (e.g., SAP FI/CO).']
  standard_cost_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost record.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that last updated the cost record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the cost record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that created the cost record.']

  Note: 'Standard cost estimate for chemical finished goods, intermediates, and formulations used for inventory valuation and COGS determination. Captures cost component breakdown (raw materials, direct labor, energy, manufacturing overhead, depreciation), standard price per unit, costing lot size, validity period, and release status. Supports product costing for complex multi-step chemical synthesis, make-vs-buy decisions, transfer pricing, and variance analysis against actual production costs in batch and continuous process environments.'
}

Adding fiscal_period_id enables period-level budget planning and supports monthly/quarterly budget phasing. This is esse']

Business justification: Budget plans in SAP FM/CO can be linked to GL accounts for account-level budgeting and financial planning. This enables account-level budget vs. actual variance analysis and supports the chart of acco']

This relationship enables legal entity-level budget consolidation and supports FASB comp']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Budget plan also targets a profit center; FK provides direct linkage.']

  approval_outcome varchar [note: 'Enum indicating the final outcome of the budget plan approval process.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Result of the approval workflow (e.g., Pending, Approved, Rejected).']
  budget_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the budget plan (e.g., Draft, Submitted, Approved, Closed).']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the budget plan record was first created in the system.']

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the budget plan record.']
  variance_allowed decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum permissible variance (percentage) between actual spend and planned amount before alerts are raised.']

  Note: 'Annual and multi-year budget plan record for cost centers, profit centers, and internal orders. Captures budget version, fiscal year, planning period, budget type (original, supplement, return), planned amounts by cost element, currency, approval status, and planner identity. Supports financial planning, annual budgeting cycles, variance-to-budget reporting, and management reporting for chemical manufacturing operations including plant-level OpEx budgets and R&D investment planning.'
}

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the transaction record.']

  Note: 'Fixed asset transaction record capturing all asset value movements including acquisitions, retirements, transfers, write-ups, write-downs, and periodic depreciation postings. Includes transaction type, posting date, amount, document reference, asset value date, depreciation area, and GL account assignment. Provides the complete financial history of plant assets (reactors, columns, tanks, infrastructure) for FASB ASC 360 compliance, audit trails, and SOX controls.'
}

Table planning.demand_forecast {
  demand_forecast_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each demand forecast version.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Customer‑specific demand forecast drives sales planning and supply allocation; key accounts require separate forecasts for accurate production scheduling.']
  budget_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget_plan. Business justification: Demand forecasts drive revenue budget plans in chemical manufacturing. Linking demand_forecast to budget_plan supports top-down/bottom-up budget reconciliation, S&OP financial review, and revenue fore']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: REQUIRED: Demand Forecast reports are generated per chemical product; linking enables product‑level demand planning and variance analysis.']
  customer_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.site. Business justification: Site-level demand planning is standard in chemical manufacturing — bulk delivery forecasts are tied to specific customer receiving sites (tank farms, bulk terminals). Planners need site-level forecast']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Demand forecasts in chemical manufacturing are period-bound planning documents. Linking to fiscal_period enables revenue forecast-to-actual variance reporting, period-close demand reconciliation, and ']
  forecast_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.forecast_version. Business justification: Each demand forecast belongs to a specific forecast version; linking enables version control and eliminates redundant version fields.']
  grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grade. Business justification: Demand forecasts in chemical manufacturing are grade-specific — customers forecast ACS reagent grade separately from technical grade. Without a grade FK, the forecast cannot drive grade-level producti']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Demand Forecasting uses the material master to tie forecast SKUs to the authoritative material record, ensuring accurate planning and regulatory reporting.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: S&OP demand forecast uses high‑value sales opportunities to project future demand, a core process linking sales pipeline to planning.']
  organization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_organization. Business justification: In chemical manufacturing S&OP, demand forecasts are owned, submitted, and approved by sales organizations. This link is required for forecast consolidation by sales org, quota-vs-forecast variance re']
  packaging_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.packaging_config. Business justification: Demand forecasts in chemical manufacturing are often packaging-specific — customers forecast 500 drums vs. 20 IBCs separately. Without a packaging_config FK, the forecast cannot drive packaging materi']
  prior_demand_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on demand_forecast (prior_demand_forecast_id)']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Demand forecasts in chemical manufacturing are profit-center scoped for revenue planning and P&L budgeting. Linking demand_forecast to profit_center enables revenue budget alignment, profit center-lev']
  qualification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.qualification. Business justification: Demand forecasts for qualified products must reference the qualification record to enforce approved volume limits (max_approved_volume_kg, min_approved_volume_kg). Planners use qualification-constrain']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Segment-level demand forecasting is a core S&OP process in chemical manufacturing — planners build forecasts by customer segment (automotive, ag-chem, construction) to drive production planning. A dom']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: REQUIRED: SKU‑level forecasts drive packaging and logistics planning; FK ties forecast rows to the definitive SKU master.']
  adjusted_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity after consensus or manual adjustments.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the forecast was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the forecast.']
  baseline_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Statistical or algorithmic baseline quantity forecast before adjustments.']
  confidence_interval_lower decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower bound of the confidence interval for the forecast quantity.']
  confidence_interval_upper decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper bound of the confidence interval for the forecast quantity.']
  confidence_level decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence percentage (0‑100) associated with the forecast.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the forecast record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values associated with the forecast.']
  demand_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the demand signal used for the forecast.']
  forecast_accuracy decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage accuracy of past forecasts compared to actuals.']
  forecast_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of what is being forecasted.']
  forecast_method varchar [note: 'Method used to generate the forecast quantity.']
  forecast_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the forecast (e.g., "2025 Q3 Demand").']
  forecast_owner varchar [note: 'Department or team responsible for the forecast.']
  forecast_period_end date [note: 'Last calendar date of the forecasted period.']
  forecast_period_start date [note: 'First calendar date of the forecasted period.']
  forecast_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the forecast.']
  forecast_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the forecast is for demand or supply.']
  forecast_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the forecast (e.g., "V1", "V2").']
  forecast_version_date date [note: 'Calendar date when this forecast version was released.']
  is_final boolean [note: 'True when the forecast version is the definitive one for planning.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the forecast is locked for further edits.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or rationale for the forecast.']
  planning_bucket varchar [note: 'Granularity of the forecast (e.g., weekly, monthly).']
  planning_horizon varchar [note: 'Timeframe the forecast covers relative to the planning window.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number for the forecast record.']
  scenario_name varchar [note: 'Name of the scenario applied to the forecast.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for the forecast quantity.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the forecast.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the forecast record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the forecast.']

  Note: 'Master demand forecast record capturing statistical and consensus-based demand projections for chemical products and SKUs across planning horizons (short, mid, long-term). Stores forecast version, method (statistical, ML, consensus), baseline quantity, adjusted quantity, forecast accuracy metrics, planning bucket (weekly/monthly), and lifecycle status. Serves as the authoritative SSOT for demand signals feeding S&OP and MRP processes.'
}

Table planning.forecast_version {
  forecast_version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each forecast version snapshot.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Forecast versions in chemical manufacturing are created for specific fiscal periods (monthly, quarterly rolling forecasts). Linking forecast_version to fiscal_period enables period-based forecast accu']
  organization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_organization. Business justification: Forecast versions in chemical manufacturing S&OP are submitted and approved per sales organization. The approval workflow, version comparison reports, and forecast accuracy KPIs are all structured by ']
  superseded_forecast_version_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on forecast_version (superseded_forecast_version_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the forecast version.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the forecast version was approved.']
  confidence_interval_lower decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the statistical confidence interval for the forecast.']
  confidence_interval_upper decimal(5,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the statistical confidence interval for the forecast.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the forecast version record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for monetary values.']
  demand_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the data used to generate the forecast.']
  forecast_accuracy_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired forecast accuracy expressed as a percentage.']
  forecast_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the forecast purpose.']
  forecast_method varchar [note: 'Technique applied to produce the demand forecast.']
  forecast_run_date date [note: 'Date on which the demand forecast was generated.']
  forecast_version_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description providing context for the forecast version.']
  forecast_version_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the forecast version.']
  is_final_version boolean [note: 'True if this version is the definitive version for the planning cycle.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the most recent update.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations about the forecast version.']
  planning_horizon_end date [note: 'Last day of the planning horizon covered by this forecast version.']
  planning_horizon_start date [note: 'First day of the planning horizon covered by this forecast version.']
  planning_horizon_type varchar [note: 'Time granularity of the forecast horizon.']
  revision_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why a new forecast version was created.']
  scenario_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the forecasting scenario (e.g., Base, Optimistic, Pessimistic).']
  total_demand_quantity decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of forecasted demand across all items for the planning horizon.']
  total_demand_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value (in the selected currency) of the total forecasted demand.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for the forecasted quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the forecast version record.']
  version_effective_date date [note: 'Date when this forecast version becomes effective for planning purposes.']
  version_expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the forecast version is no longer valid for planning.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating the version order within a forecast cycle.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that initially created the forecast version record.']

  Note: 'Version-controlled snapshot of a demand forecast cycle, capturing the forecast run date, planning horizon start/end, version number, approval status, and the planner or system that generated it. Enables comparison of forecast versions across S&OP cycles and supports forecast accuracy tracking over time. Each version contains the full set of demand_forecast line records for that planning cycle.'
}

Table planning.mrp_run {
  mrp_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the MRP run execution.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: MRP runs in chemical manufacturing are executed within fiscal planning periods. Linking mrp_run to fiscal_period enables period-based MRP analysis, budget consumption tracking per period, and audit tr']
  prior_mrp_run_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on mrp_run (prior_mrp_run_id)']
  production_plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the MRP run was executed.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the data processing batch that loaded this MRP run record.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes associated with the MRP run.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates if the MRP run is marked as critical for production planning.']
  is_simulation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MRP run was a simulation (true) or a live run (false).']
  mrp_run_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the MRP run.']
  planning_date date [note: 'The date for which the MRP planning is performed.']
  planning_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Number of days ahead covered by the MRP planning horizon.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the MRP run record was created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the MRP run record.']
  run_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total execution time of the MRP run in seconds.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'System-generated sequential identifier for the MRP run.']
  run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the MRP run started.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the type of MRP execution: regenerative, net change, or net change within planning horizon.']
  run_user_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the user who initiated the MRP run.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of origin for the MRP run data.']
  total_exception_messages varchar [note: 'Count of exception messages produced during the MRP run.']
  total_planned_orders varchar [note: 'Number of production orders generated by the MRP run.']
  total_planned_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Aggregate quantity (in base unit) of all planned material requirements.']
  total_purchase_requisitions varchar [note: 'Number of purchase requisitions generated by the MRP run.']

  Note: 'Material Requirements Planning (MRP) run master record capturing each MRP execution event in SAP PP/MRP. Records run ID, run type (regenerative, net change, net change in planning horizon), plant scope, planning date, run status, total planned orders generated, total purchase requisitions generated, and exception message count. Serves as the audit header for all MRP-generated planning elements.'
}

  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the plan (e.g., Master Production Schedule, Material Requirements Planning, Capacity Planning, Scheduling).']
  planned_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Authorized quantity of product to be produced within the planning horizon.']
  planning_horizon_end date [note: 'Last date of the planning horizon covered by this plan.']
  planning_horizon_start date [note: 'First date of the planning horizon covered by this plan.']
  planning_period varchar [note: 'Granularity of the plan (e.g., daily, weekly).']
  production_method varchar [note: 'Process mode used to manufacture the product.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the plan complies with all applicable chemical regulations (EPA, REACH, etc.).']
  safety_stock decimal(18,3) [note: 'Buffer inventory quantity to protect against demand variability.']
  scheduling_priority varchar [note: 'Priority used by MRP/scheduling engines to allocate capacity.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the planned quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the plan.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the plan for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Master Production Schedule (MPS) and production plan record defining the authorized production quantities for finished chemical products and key intermediates over a planning horizon. Captures product, plant, planning period, planned production quantity, UOM, production method, scheduling priority, freeze horizon flag, and plan status. Bridges the S&OP consensus plan and detailed MRP/scheduling execution in chemical manufacturing.'
}

Serves as the supply-side counterpart to the demand forecast in S&OP balancing.'
}

Supports campaign-based scheduling optimization common in specialty chemical and agrochemical manufacturing.'
}

Business justification: Allocation rules in chemical manufacturing are product-specific — a rule defines how available supply of a specific chemical product is distributed among customers during shortage. Without a chemical_']
  demand_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.demand_forecast. Business justification: Allocation rules are triggered by demand exceeding constrained supply. Linking allocation_rule to the demand_forecast that revealed the supply-demand gap provides the demand context for the allocation']
  fallback_allocation_rule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on allocation_rule (fallback_allocation_rule_id)']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Allocation rules in chemical manufacturing are effective for specific planning periods aligned to fiscal periods. Period-based allocation reporting and financial reconciliation of allocated vs. actual']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Allocation rules in chemical mfg govern how limited supply of a specific material is distributed across customers or plants. A material-specific allocation rule (e.g., for a constrained specialty chem']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Allocation rules in chemical manufacturing are defined at segment level during supply constraints — strategic segments (e.g., automotive, pharma) receive priority allocation. Segment-based allocation ']
  supply_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.supply_plan. Business justification: An allocation_rule is created specifically when supply is constrained — it defines how the constrained supply from a supply_plan is distributed across customers. Linking allocation_rule to the supply_']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Territory-scoped allocation rules are standard in chemical manufacturing S&OP — regional supply constraints require allocation rules specific to sales territories. Planners and sales managers jointly ']
  allocation_basis varchar [note: 'Basis on which supply is allocated (e.g., historical share, contract priority, strategic customer tier).']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate allocations (proportional, fixed quantity, or priority‑based).']
  allocation_period_end date [note: 'Last date of the period for which the rule applies.']
  allocation_period_start date [note: 'First date of the period for which the rule applies.']
  allocation_rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the intent and application of the allocation rule.']
  allocation_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  allocation_scope varchar [note: 'Scope level at which the rule is applied: per customer, geographic region, or sales channel.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the rule.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rule was approved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rule record was created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the rule becomes effective for operational use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the rule expires; null if open‑ended.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule exclusively governs allocation (true) or can be combined with other rules (false).']
  max_allocation_per_customer decimal(20,4) [note: 'Upper limit of quantity that any single customer can receive under this rule.']
  min_allocation_per_customer decimal(20,4) [note: 'Lower bound of quantity guaranteed to each eligible customer under this rule.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments related to the rule.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Numeric priority of the rule; lower numbers indicate higher priority when multiple rules apply.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the rule in external systems and documents.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the purpose of the rule.']
  total_available_quantity decimal(20,4) [note: 'Total quantity of the product available for allocation during the rule period.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity fields (e.g., kilograms, liters).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the rule.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the rule to support change management.']
  version_effective_date date [note: 'Date when this version of the rule becomes effective.']
  version_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this version of the rule expires; null if still active.']

  Note: 'Product allocation rule master record defining how constrained supply of a chemical product is allocated across customers, regions, or sales channels during shortage situations. Captures allocation rule ID, product, allocation basis (historical share, contract priority, strategic customer tier), allocation period, total available quantity, allocation method, and approval status. Governs fair and strategic supply allocation during chemical supply shortages or plant outages.'
}

Table planning.customer_allocation {
  customer_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the customer allocation record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer receiving the allocation.']
  allocation_rule_id varchar [note: 'Code identifying the allocation rule applied to this record.']
  chemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the chemical product being allocated.']
  customer_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.site. Business justification: Allocations in chemical manufacturing are fulfilled to specific customer delivery sites. Site-level allocation tracking is required for logistics scheduling, EHS compliance (hazmat receiving capabilit']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Customer allocations in chemical manufacturing are period-specific. Linking to fiscal_period enables period-end allocation revenue recognition, financial reconciliation of allocated vs. invoiced volum']
  grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grade. Business justification: Customer allocations in chemical manufacturing are grade-specific — a customer allocated 10 MT may specifically need USP grade, not technical grade. Without a grade FK, allocation cannot enforce grade']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: During supply constraints in chemical manufacturing, customer allocations are prioritized by opportunity stage and strategic value. S&OP allocation decisions require tracing which opportunity drives e']
  price_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.price_agreement. Business justification: Price agreements in chemical manufacturing define contracted volume commitments that directly govern customer allocation entitlements. Allocation planners must reference the price agreement to enforce']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Customer allocations in chemical manufacturing generate revenue attributed to profit centers for P&L reporting. Linking customer_allocation to profit_center enables revenue allocation reporting by pro']
  qualification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.qualification. Business justification: In chemical manufacturing, product allocations must be gated by customer qualification records — a customer can only receive an allocated product/grade they are qualified for. This qualification-gated']
  quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_specification. Business justification: Customer allocations in specialty chemical manufacturing are grade/spec-specific — pharmaceutical-grade vs. industrial-grade product is allocated to different customers under different quality specifi']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Customer allocations are naturally at the SKU level in chemical manufacturing — a customer is allocated a specific pack size and grade combination. The existing chemical_product_id FK is too coarse; S']
  split_from_customer_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on customer_allocation (split_from_customer_allocation_id)']
  supply_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to planning.supply_plan. Business justification: customer_allocation is the transactional record assigning allocated quantities to specific customers from a constrained supply. Linking customer_allocation to the supply_plan it draws from closes the ']
  allocated_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of product allocated to the customer.']
  allocated_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the allocated quantity.']
  allocated_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the allocated quantity expressed in the specified currency.']
  allocated_value_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the allocated monetary value.']
  allocation_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the allocation expires or is scheduled to end.']
  allocation_rule_version varchar [note: 'Version of the allocation rule applied to this record.']
  allocation_source varchar [note: 'Source system or actor that created the allocation.']
  allocation_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the allocation becomes effective.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the allocation.']
  allocation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the allocation origin or methodology.']
  allocation_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the allocation rule instance.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User or authority that approved the allocation.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the allocation was approved for execution.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier associated with the allocated product.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the allocation complies with relevant chemical regulations.']
  consumed_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of the allocation that has been consumed by the customer.']
  consumed_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the consumed quantity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the allocation record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for monetary values associated with the allocation.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the allocation became effective (may include time of day).']
  ehs_category varchar [note: 'Environmental, Health, and Safety classification of the allocated product.']
  expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the allocation expires.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the allocation is locked from further changes.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for the batch of product allocated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or business notes.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the allocation for fulfillment sequencing.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory compliance status of the allocation.']
  remaining_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of the allocation that remains unconsumed.']
  remaining_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the remaining quantity.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number for changes to the allocation record.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or code describing why the allocation is in its current status.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or process identifier that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the allocation record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or process identifier that created the allocation record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record assigning a specific allocated quantity of a constrained chemical product to a customer account or distribution channel for a defined period. Captures allocation ID, allocation rule reference, customer account, product, allocated quantity, consumed quantity, remaining quantity, allocation period start/end, and allocation status. Enables order management to enforce supply allocation limits during chemical product shortages.'
}
